The Sound of Essentials

Rhythm Quest: Essential Picture Dictionary

4,000+ Essential Words for Family Literacy

Mapped across the 7 Lands of the Rhythm Quest Universe

THE 7 LANDS

🎵 Harmonia — Kenji & Aiko

🔢 Numeria — Kwame & Octavia

🌿 Terrasol — Silas & Vesta

🌊 Aquaria — Ronan & Nerissa

🤸 Vitalis — Felix & Amara

🏛️ Sophia — Ezra & Athena

⏰ Celestia — Elias & Selene

"Calm. Focused. Essential."

Adjacent in scope to the Oxford Picture Dictionary (OPD)
Designed for ESOL Families & Early Childhood Learners

How to Use This Book

A guide for parents, teachers, tutors, and learners

For Families: Shared Reading

This dictionary is designed for parents and children to explore together. Each page features a thematic scene from one of the 7 Lands of the Rhythm Quest. Point to each numbered item in the illustration, say the word aloud, then find it in the vocabulary table below.

  1. LOOK Find the numbered items in the scene illustration at the top of each page.
  2. SAY Read the word aloud. Use the phonetic guide (/fuh-NEH-tik/) to help with pronunciation.
  3. CONNECT Read the sentence context to see how the word is used in the Rhythm Quest story.
  4. TRANSLATE Write the word in your home language in the translation column. This makes the book yours!
  5. PLAY Look for the character tips from the hero duos. They offer fun activities to practice each group of words.

Reading the Vocabulary Table

# The item number matching the illustration callout
Word The English vocabulary word in bold
/Phonetic/ Pronunciation guide using simple syllable breaks
In the Story… A sentence showing how the word connects to the SOE characters
My Language Write the word in your home language here

For Teachers & Programs

This dictionary is aligned with the thematic scope of the Oxford Picture Dictionary and supports Title III (English Language Acquisition) programming. Each Land maps to standard ESOL survival vocabulary topics. The sub-scenes can be used as standalone lesson units.

💡 Print Referencing Tip

When reading with a child, point to each word as you say it. Tap the number in the illustration, then trace your finger to the matching number in the table. This builds the crucial connection between spoken and written language.

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 15: Body Language & Gestures

In the Harmonia Mirror Room, Kenji and Aiko learn that communication goes beyond words.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Nod /NOD/ Nod your head up and down to mean "yes." Fingerspell NOD
2 Shake head /SHAYK HED/ Shake your head side to side to mean "no." Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Point /POYNT/ Point to the picture you want to talk about. Fingerspell POINT
4 Shrug /SHRUG/ Shrug your shoulders to say "I don't know." Fingerspell SHRUG
5 Thumbs up /THUMZ UP/ A thumbs up means "good job" or "yes." Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Thumbs down /THUMZ DOWN/ A thumbs down means "no" or "I don't like it." Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Clap /KLAP/ Clap your hands to show you enjoyed the show. Fingerspell CLAP
8 Gesture /JES-chur/ A gesture is a movement that communicates meaning. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Eye contact /EYE KON-takt/ Make eye contact when someone is talking to you. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Facial expression /FAY-shul ek-SPRESH-un/ A smile is a facial expression that means "I'm happy." Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Frown /FROWN/ A frown shows you are unhappy or worried. Fingerspell FROWN
12 Wink /WINK/ A friendly wink means "I'm joking with you." Fingerspell WINK
13 Yawn /YAWN/ If you yawn, cover your mouth with your hand. Fingerspell YAWN
14 Stretch /STRECH/ Stretch your arms after sitting for a long time. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Cross arms /KROS ARMZ/ Crossing your arms can mean you are cold or upset. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 High five /HY FYV/ Give your teammate a high five after a great play. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Fist bump /FIST BUMP/ A fist bump is a friendly greeting between friends. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Shake hands /SHAYK HANDZ/ Shake hands firmly when meeting someone new. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Bow /BOW/ In many cultures, a bow is a sign of respect. Bend at waist with hands at sides
20 Tone of voice /TOHN ov VOYS/ Your tone of voice tells people how you feel. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Whisper /WIS-per/ Whisper quietly in the library. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Shout /SHOWT/ Shout for help if you are in danger. Fingerspell SHOUT
23 Body language /BOD-ee LANG-gwij/ Body language speaks louder than words sometimes. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Personal space /PUR-sun-ul SPAYS/ Respect other people's personal space. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Posture /POS-chur/ Good posture means sitting and standing up straight. Fingerspell or sign each word
🪞

Kenji's Tip:

"Stand in front of a mirror and practice! SMILE — that's happy. FROWN — that's sad. NOD — that's yes. SHRUG — that's 'I don't know.' Your BODY speaks English too!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 18: Celebrations & Traditions

In Harmonia, every culture brings its own special celebrations. Kenji and Aiko celebrate them all!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Holiday /HOL-ih-day/ Our favorite holiday is Thanksgiving. Thumbs at armpits, wiggle fingers (VACATION)
2 Tradition /truh-DISH-un/ A tradition is something families do year after year. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Celebration /sel-eh-BRAY-shun/ The celebration includes food, music, and dancing. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Festival /FES-tih-vul/ The community festival has games and live music. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Parade /puh-RAYD/ Marching bands walk in the parade down Main Street. Fingerspell PARADE
6 Fireworks /FYR-wurks/ Colorful fireworks light up the sky at midnight. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Candle /KAN-dul/ Light a candle on the birthday cake. Fingerspell CANDLE
8 Decoration /dek-oh-RAY-shun/ Hang the decoration on the wall for the party. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Costume /KOS-toom/ Children wear a costume on Halloween. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Gift /GIFT/ A gift is something you give to show you care. Fingerspell GIFT
11 Wrap /RAP/ Wrap the gift in colorful paper. Fingerspell WRAP
12 Card /KARD/ Write a birthday card for your friend. Fingerspell CARD
13 Feast /FEEST/ The family prepared a feast for the holiday meal. Fingerspell FEAST
14 Toast /TOHST/ Raise your glass and make a toast to the new year. Fingerspell TOAST
15 Reunion /ree-YOON-yun/ The family reunion brings everyone together. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Ceremony /SAIR-eh-moh-nee/ A wedding ceremony celebrates love. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Custom /KUS-tum/ Every country has its own customs and practices. Fingerspell CUSTOM
18 Heritage /HAIR-ih-tij/ Share your cultural heritage with your classmates. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Feast day /FEEST DAY/ A feast day is a special meal for a holiday. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Ancestor /AN-ses-ter/ An ancestor is a family member from long ago. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Gathering /GATH-er-ing/ A gathering is when people come together. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Potluck /POT-luk/ Everyone brings a dish to the potluck dinner. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Invitation /in-vih-TAY-shun/ Send an invitation to your friends for the party. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Guest /GEST/ A guest is someone you invite to your home. Fingerspell GUEST
25 Host /HOHST/ The host welcomes everyone to the party. Fingerspell HOST
🎉

Kenji's Tip:

"What celebrations does YOUR family have? Say: 'My family celebrates ___. We eat ___. We wear ___. We listen to ___.' Sharing YOUR traditions in English is beautiful!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 6: Clothing & Getting Dressed

Every morning, Kenji and Aiko get dressed in their Harmonia outfits. Every piece of clothing has a name.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Shirt /SHURT/ Kenji puts on a clean white shirt. Pinch and pull shirt fabric at chest
2 T-shirt /TEE-shurt/ Aiko wears a purple T-shirt on casual days. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Blouse /BLOWZ/ The blouse has small buttons down the front. Fingerspell BLOUSE
4 Pants /PANTS/ Kenji pulls on a pair of dark blue pants. Both hands pat thighs, then pull up at waist
5 Jeans /JEENZ/ Aiko's jeans are comfortable for running and playing. Fingerspell JEANS
6 Shorts /SHORTS/ In summer, Kenji wears shorts to stay cool. Bent hand pushes down (repeated)
7 Skirt /SKURT/ Aiko twirls in her colorful skirt. 5-hands brush down from waist
8 Dress /DRES/ The flowered dress is for special occasions. 5-hands brush down chest (showing garment)
9 Sweater /SWET-er/ A warm sweater keeps you cozy on chilly days. Claw hands mime pulling sweater on
10 Jacket /JAK-it/ Kenji zips up his jacket before going outside. Sign COAT (same motion)
11 Coat /KOHT/ The long winter coat keeps out the cold wind. A-hands trace lapels to shoulders
12 Raincoat /RAYN-koht/ Aiko grabs her yellow raincoat when it's raining. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Hoodie /HOO-dee/ Kenji pulls the hoodie over his head. Fingerspell HOODIE
14 Vest /VEST/ The vest adds a layer without long sleeves. Fingerspell VEST
15 Suit /SOOT/ Father wears a suit to important meetings. Fingerspell SUIT
16 Tie /TY/ The colorful tie goes around the collar. Mime tying a necktie
17 Uniform /YOO-nih-form/ Students at Harmonia School wear a uniform. U-hands brush down chest
18 Underwear /UN-der-wair/ Put on clean underwear first thing every morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Socks /SOKS/ The socks keep your feet warm inside your shoes. Index fingers rub together pointing down
20 Shoes /SHOOZ/ Tie your shoes before you go outside. Both S-fists tap together sideways
21 Sneakers /SNEE-kerz/ Aiko runs fastest in her new sneakers. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Boots /BOOTS/ Rain boots protect your feet from puddles. B-hands stamp alternately
23 Sandals /SAN-dulz/ Sandals are open shoes for warm weather. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Slippers /SLIP-erz/ Wear slippers inside the house. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Hat /HAT/ Kenji wears a hat to shade his face from the sun. Pat top of head
26 Cap /KAP/ The baseball cap goes forwards or backwards. Fingerspell CAP
27 Scarf /SKARF/ A scarf wraps around the neck to stay warm. Mime wrapping scarf around neck
28 Gloves /GLUVZ/ Warm gloves protect your fingers from the cold. One hand slides over other like putting on glove
29 Belt /BELT/ The belt keeps Kenji's pants from falling down. Both hands trace belt around waist
30 Button /BUT-un/ Button your shirt from the bottom up. F-hand taps down chest in spots
31 Zipper /ZIP-er/ Zip the zipper all the way up on cold days. Mime pulling zipper up chest
32 Pocket /POK-it/ Kenji keeps his key in his front pocket. Fingerspell POCKET
33 Sleeve /SLEEV/ Roll up your sleeves when you wash dishes. Fingerspell SLEEVE
34 Collar /KOL-er/ The collar of the shirt goes around the neck. Fingerspell COLLAR
35 Laundry /LAWN-dree/ Put your dirty clothing in the laundry basket. Fingerspell or sign each word
36 Iron /EYE-urn/ Iron the wrinkled shirt until it's smooth. Fingerspell IRON
37 Fold /FOHLD/ Fold the clean clothes and put them away. Fingerspell FOLD
38 Hanger /HANG-er/ Hang the jacket on a hanger in the closet. Fingerspell HANGER
39 Size /SYZ/ What size shirt do you wear? Small, medium, or large? Fingerspell SIZE
40 Try on /TRY ON/ Try on the shoes to see if they fit. Fingerspell TRY ON
👗

Aiko's Tip:

"Look at what you're wearing right now! Name each item: shirt, pants, socks, shoes. Now describe the color: 'I'm wearing a BLUE shirt.' You just made a complete sentence!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 7: Colors & Patterns

The Harmonia Paint Studio is where Kenji and Aiko mix colors and design patterns. Art is a language too.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Red /RED/ The red paint is as bright as a cherry. Index finger strokes down lips
2 Blue /BLOO/ The sky above Harmonia is a deep blue. B-hand shakes in space
3 Yellow /YEL-oh/ The yellow sunflower turns toward the light. Y-hand shakes in space
4 Green /GREEN/ Green is the color of fresh leaves and grass. G-hand shakes in space
5 Orange /OR-inj/ Orange is a mix of red and yellow. C-hand squeezes near chin (like squeezing orange)
6 Purple /PUR-pul/ Red and blue mixed together make purple. P-hand shakes in space
7 Pink /PINK/ The pink flowers bloom in Harmonia's garden. Middle finger strokes down chin (P-hand)
8 Brown /BROWN/ The tree trunk is brown and rough. B-hand slides down cheek
9 Black /BLAK/ Kenji writes with black ink in his journal. Index finger swipes across forehead
10 White /WYT/ The white paper is ready for your drawing. 5-hand on chest pulls away to flat O
11 Gray /GRAY/ The rainy sky turns a cloudy gray. 5-hands pass through each other back and forth
12 Gold /GOHLD/ The gold trophy shines on the shelf. Fingerspell GOLD
13 Silver /SIL-ver/ The silver bracelet sparkles in the light. Fingerspell SILVER
14 Beige /BAYZH/ The beige walls make the room feel calm. Fingerspell BEIGE
15 Turquoise /TUR-koyz/ The turquoise ocean water is clear and warm. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Maroon /mah-ROON/ The maroon curtains are dark red-brown. Fingerspell MAROON
17 Navy /NAY-vee/ Kenji's navy blue uniform looks sharp. Fingerspell NAVY
18 Light /LYT/ Light blue is softer and paler than dark blue. Both flat hands rise up easily
19 Dark /DARK/ Dark green looks almost like black. Both flat hands cross in front of face
20 Bright /BRYT/ The bright yellow paint stands out on the canvas. Both flat O-hands at chest open to 5-hands
21 Striped /STRYPD/ Aiko's striped shirt has blue and white lines. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Plaid /PLAD/ The plaid blanket has a crisscross pattern. Fingerspell PLAID
23 Polka dot /POHL-kuh DOT/ The polka dot dress has small round circles. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Solid /SOL-id/ A solid color means one color with no pattern. Fingerspell SOLID
25 Pattern /PAT-ern/ The pattern repeats over and over on the fabric. Fingerspell or sign each word
🎨

Kenji's Tip:

"Look around the room. How many colors can you name in English? Point and say: 'That wall is WHITE. That chair is BROWN. My shirt is BLUE.' Colors are everywhere!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 10: Communication & Technology

Kenji and Aiko use the Harmonia Communication Hub to connect with friends across all Seven Lands.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Phone /FOHN/ Aiko calls her grandmother on the phone every week. Y-hand at ear (phone shape)
2 Cell phone /SEL FOHN/ A cell phone fits in your pocket. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Call /KAWL/ I will call you at five o'clock. Fingerspell CALL
4 Text message /TEKST MES-ij/ Send a text message if you can't call. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Internet /IN-ter-net/ The internet connects people around the world. 5-hands, middle fingers touch, twist
6 Website /WEB-syt/ Visit the school website for announcements. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Password /PAS-wurd/ Keep your password private and secure. P-hand taps opposite palm (SECRET + WORD)
8 Username /YOO-zer-naym/ Your username identifies you online. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Download /DOWN-lohd/ Download the file to your computer. Claw hand pulls down to S-fist
10 Upload /UP-lohd/ Upload your photo to the class album. S-fist pushes up, opens to claw
11 Search /SURCH/ Search the internet for the answer. Fingerspell SEARCH
12 Type /TYP/ Kenji types quickly on the keyboard. Fingerspell TYPE
13 Click /KLIK/ Click the blue button to open the page. Fingerspell CLICK
14 Screen /SKREEN/ The screen shows the video clearly. Both flat hands outline rectangle
15 Keyboard /KEE-bord/ The keyboard has letters, numbers, and symbols. Fingers mime typing
16 Mouse /MOWS/ Move the mouse to select the correct answer. Mime clicking mouse button
17 Printer /PRIN-ter/ The printer makes paper copies of documents. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Charger /CHAR-jer/ Plug in the charger when the battery is low. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Headphones /HED-fohnz/ Wear headphones to listen without disturbing others. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Speaker /SPEE-ker/ The speaker plays music for everyone to hear. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Camera /KAM-er-uh/ Aiko takes a photo with her camera. Both L-hands frame box at eye, click
22 Video /VID-ee-oh/ The video shows step-by-step instructions. 5-hand at temple, mime projecting
23 App /AP/ Open the dictionary app on your tablet. Fingerspell APP
24 Wi-Fi /WY-fy/ Connect to the Wi-Fi to go online. Fingerspell WI-FI
25 Letter /LET-er/ Kenji writes a letter to his pen pal in Land 4. Fingerspell LETTER
26 Envelope /EN-veh-lohp/ Put the letter in the envelope and seal it. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Stamp /STAMP/ Stick a stamp on the envelope before mailing. Fingerspell STAMP
28 Post office /POHST OF-is/ Take the package to the post office to mail it. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Newspaper /NOOZ-pay-per/ Grandfather reads the newspaper every morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Magazine /mag-uh-ZEEN/ The magazine has pictures and articles about nature. Fingerspell or sign each word
📱

Aiko's Tip:

"Technology words are EVERYWHERE! The next time you use a phone, tablet, or computer, say the English word for what you are doing: 'I click. I type. I search. I download.' You are learning English every time you use technology!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 12: Daily Routines

Follow Kenji and Aiko through one full day in Harmonia — from sunrise to bedtime.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Wake up /WAYK UP/ Kenji wakes up at six o'clock every morning. Pinch at eyes, flick open
2 Get up /GET UP/ Get up when the alarm rings. Fingerspell GET UP
3 Brush teeth /BRUSH TEETH/ Brush your teeth for two minutes morning and night. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Wash face /WASH FAYS/ Aiko washes her face with warm water. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Comb hair /KOHM HAIR/ Comb your hair before you leave the house. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Get dressed /GET DRESD/ Get dressed and put on your shoes. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Eat breakfast /EET BREK-fust/ Eat a healthy breakfast to start the day. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Pack lunch /PAK LUNCH/ Pack your lunch in a bag with an ice pack. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Leave home /LEEV HOHM/ Kenji leaves home at seven thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Walk to school /WAWK too SKOOL/ Aiko walks to school with her neighbor. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Arrive /uh-RYV/ The students arrive before the bell rings. Fingerspell ARRIVE
12 Study /STUD-ee/ Study your vocabulary words every day. 5-hand wiggles fingers at opposite palm
13 Take a break /TAYK uh BRAYK/ Take a break and stretch your legs. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Eat lunch /EET LUNCH/ Students eat lunch in the cafeteria at noon. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Come home /KUM HOHM/ Kenji comes home at three thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Do homework /DOO HOHM-wurk/ Do your homework before you play outside. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Practice /PRAK-tis/ Aiko practices piano for thirty minutes. A-hand brushes back and forth on opposite index
18 Play /PLAY/ Children play in the yard after school. Y-hands shake in front
19 Eat dinner /EET DIN-er/ The family eats dinner together at six o'clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Watch TV /WOCH TEE-VEE/ Kenji watches TV for one hour before bed. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Take a bath /TAYK uh BATH/ Take a warm bath before bedtime. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Read a book /REED uh BOOK/ Read a book in bed to relax your mind. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Say goodnight /SAY GOOD-nyt/ Say goodnight to everyone in the house. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Turn off the light /TURN OFF thuh LYT/ Turn off the light and close your eyes. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Go to sleep /GOH too SLEEP/ Aiko goes to sleep at nine o'clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Alarm clock /uh-LARM KLOK/ The alarm clock rings at six every morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Routine /roo-TEEN/ A routine is a set of things you do every day. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Schedule /SKED-yool/ Follow your schedule so you have time for everything. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
29 On time /ON TYM/ Being on time shows you respect other people. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Late /LAYT/ If you are late, say "I'm sorry I'm late." Hand waves downward beside hip
31 Early /UR-lee/ Aiko likes to arrive early to help set up. Middle finger slides over fist knuckle
32 Habit /HAB-it/ A good habit makes your life easier and better. Fingerspell HABIT
33 Chores /CHORZ/ Everyone in the family helps with chores. Fingerspell CHORES
34 Make the bed /MAYK thuh BED/ Make the bed first thing when you wake up. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Set the table /SET thuh TAY-bul/ Set the table with plates, forks, and napkins. Fingerspell or sign each word
36 Clean up /KLEEN UP/ Clean up your room before going outside. Fingerspell or sign each word
37 Take out trash /TAYK OWT TRASH/ Take out the trash on Tuesday nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
38 Feed the pet /FEED thuh PET/ Feed the pet before you eat your own breakfast. Fingerspell or sign each word
39 Water plants /WAW-ter PLANTS/ Water the plants every other day. Fingerspell or sign each word
40 Bedtime /BED-tym/ Bedtime is at nine o'clock on school nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
⏰

Aiko's Tip:

"Make YOUR daily routine list! Write what you do from morning to night: 'I wake up. I brush my teeth. I eat breakfast.' Now you've written TEN English sentences about YOUR life!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 9: Days, Months & Numbers

On the Harmonia Calendar Wall, Kenji and Aiko track each day, month, and milestone.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Sunday /SUN-day/ Sunday is the first day of the week. Fingerspell SUNDAY
2 Monday /MUN-day/ Monday is when the school week begins. Fingerspell MONDAY
3 Tuesday /TOOZ-day/ Kenji has music practice every Tuesday. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Wednesday /WENZ-day/ Wednesday is in the middle of the week. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Thursday /THURZ-day/ Aiko visits the library every Thursday. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Friday /FRY-day/ Friday is the last school day of the week! Fingerspell FRIDAY
7 Saturday /SAT-er-day/ Saturday is for rest and play. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 January /JAN-yoo-air-ee/ January is the first month of the new year. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 February /FEB-roo-air-ee/ February has only twenty-eight or twenty-nine days. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 March /MARCH/ Spring flowers start to bloom in March. Fingerspell MARCH
11 April /AY-prul/ April showers bring May flowers. Fingerspell APRIL
12 May /MAY/ May is the last full month of spring. Fingerspell MAY
13 June /JOON/ School usually ends in June. Fingerspell JUNE
14 July /joo-LY/ July is the hottest month of summer. Fingerspell JULY
15 August /AW-gust/ In August, families prepare for back-to-school. Fingerspell AUGUST
16 September /sep-TEM-ber/ September is the start of fall. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 October /ok-TOH-ber/ The leaves change color in October. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 November /noh-VEM-ber/ In November, families gather for Thanksgiving. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 December /dih-SEM-ber/ December is the last month of the year. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Today /too-DAY/ Today is a great day to learn something new. Both Y-hands drop in front of body
21 Tomorrow /too-MOR-oh/ Tomorrow we will practice our greetings. Thumb of A-hand bounces forward from cheek
22 Yesterday /YES-ter-day/ Yesterday Kenji learned ten new words. Y-hand or A-thumb touches cheek, then ear
23 Week /WEEK/ There are seven days in one week. Index moves across opposite flat palm (7 days)
24 Month /MUNTH/ A month has about thirty days. Index slides down opposite index once
25 Year /YEER/ A year has twelve months and 365 days. S-fists circle each other, then stack
26 Calendar /KAL-en-der/ Mark important dates on the calendar. C-hands mime turning page
27 Birthday /BURTH-day/ Today is Aiko's birthday! She is eleven. Middle finger touches chin, then chest
28 Holiday /HOL-ih-day/ A holiday is a special day of celebration. Thumbs at armpits, wiggle fingers (VACATION)
29 Date /DAYT/ What is the date today? It is March second. Fingerspell DATE
30 Season /SEE-zun/ There are four seasons: spring, summer, fall, winter. Fingerspell SEASON
📅

Kenji's Tip:

"Sing the days of the week! Sunday, Monday, Tuesday... make it a song with a rhythm. Now sing the months! January, February, March... music makes memorization EASY."

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 5: Emotions & Feelings

In the Harmonia Mood Garden, each flower represents a different emotion. Kenji and Aiko walk through and name what they feel.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Happy /HAP-ee/ Kenji feels happy when he plays his instrument. Flat hand brushes up chest twice
2 Sad /SAD/ Aiko feels sad when her friend moves away. Both open hands slide down face
3 Angry /ANG-gree/ It's okay to feel angry, but use your words. Claw hand pulls away from face
4 Scared /SKAIRD/ The loud thunder made the child feel scared. Both fists open outward near chest (startle)
5 Surprised /ser-PRYZD/ Kenji was surprised by the birthday party. Pinch fingers near eyes, flick open
6 Excited /ek-SY-ted/ Aiko is excited about the field trip tomorrow. Middle fingers brush up chest alternately
7 Nervous /NUR-vus/ Kenji feels nervous before his first performance. Both 5-hands shake in front
8 Calm /KAHM/ Take a deep breath and feel calm. Both flat hands push down slowly
9 Tired /TY-erd/ After running all day, Aiko feels tired. Bent hands on chest, fall forward
10 Bored /BORD/ Kenji feels bored when there is nothing to do. Index finger twists on nose
11 Confused /kun-FYOOZD/ The difficult question left Aiko feeling confused. Claw hands near temples circle in opposite directions
12 Proud /PROWD/ Kenji is proud of the song he wrote. Thumb slides up chest
13 Frustrated /FRUS-tray-ted/ Aiko feels frustrated when she can't solve the puzzle. Back of hand hits chin upward
14 Shy /SHY/ The new student feels shy on the first day. Knuckles brush up cheek, head turns
15 Brave /BRAYV/ Aiko is brave when she speaks in front of the class. Claw hands at shoulders pull to fists at chest
16 Lonely /LOHN-lee/ Kenji feels lonely when his friends are away. Index finger circles in front of face
17 Grateful /GRAYT-ful/ I am grateful for my family and friends. Flat hand from chin sweeps forward
18 Jealous /JEL-us/ It's natural to feel jealous sometimes, but kindness is stronger. J-hand draws J from corner of mouth
19 Worried /WUR-eed/ Aiko is worried about the math test. W-hands circle in front of forehead
20 Hopeful /HOHP-ful/ Kenji feels hopeful that tomorrow will be a good day. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Disappointed /dis-uh-POYNT-ed/ The cancelled trip made Aiko feel disappointed. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Embarrassed /em-BAIR-usd/ Kenji felt embarrassed when he tripped on stage. Both hands spread up cheeks (blushing)
23 Curious /KYOOR-ee-us/ Aiko is curious about how instruments are made. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Comfortable /KUM-fer-tuh-bul/ The cozy room makes everyone feel comfortable. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Overwhelmed /oh-ver-WELMD/ Too many tasks at once can make you feel overwhelmed. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Relaxed /rih-LAKSD/ After yoga, Kenji feels completely relaxed. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Content /kun-TENT/ Aiko feels content sitting by the river reading. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Anxious /ANG-shus/ Kenji feels anxious before meeting new people. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Loved /LUVD/ Everyone deserves to feel loved. Fingerspell LOVED
30 Safe /SAYF/ Kenji feels safe when he is with his family. S-hands cross at wrists, then pull apart
💛

Kenji's Tip:

"How do you feel right now? Say it: 'I feel ___.' Happy? Tired? Curious? Naming a feeling is the FIRST STEP to understanding it."

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 3: Family & Relationships

In the Harmonia Village, families gather for the weekly circle. Kenji introduces his family one by one.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Family /FAM-uh-lee/ Kenji's family sits together at the long table. F-hands circle outward from center
2 Mother /MUTH-er/ His mother makes rice and fish every Sunday. Open 5-hand, thumb taps chin
3 Father /FAH-ther/ His father teaches music in the village school. Open 5-hand, thumb taps forehead
4 Parent /PAIR-unt/ A parent takes care of their children. Open 5 at forehead, then chin
5 Son /SUN/ Kenji is the eldest son in his family. Sign BOY + BABY (rock arms)
6 Daughter /DAW-ter/ Aiko is the youngest daughter. Sign GIRL + BABY (rock arms)
7 Brother /BRUTH-er/ Kenji has one older brother who plays drums. A-hand forehead to L-hand (BOY + SAME)
8 Sister /SIS-ter/ Aiko's sister teaches her new songs. A-hand chin to L-hand (GIRL + SAME)
9 Grandmother /GRAND-muth-er/ Grandmother tells stories by the fire. Open 5 at chin, arc forward twice
10 Grandfather /GRAND-fah-ther/ Grandfather built their house with his own hands. Open 5 at forehead, arc forward twice
11 Grandparent /GRAND-pair-unt/ A grandparent is the parent of your parent. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Aunt /ANT/ Aiko's aunt brings fresh flowers every week. A-hand shakes near cheek
13 Uncle /UNG-kul/ Kenji's uncle is a fisherman. U-hand shakes near forehead
14 Cousin /KUZ-in/ My cousin and I are the same age. C-hand shakes near ear
15 Niece /NEES/ My niece is my sister's daughter. Fingerspell NIECE
16 Nephew /NEF-yoo/ My nephew is my brother's son. Fingerspell NEPHEW
17 Husband /HUZ-bund/ Kenji's father is his mother's husband. Clasp hands (MARRY) + BOY sign
18 Wife /WYF/ Kenji's mother is his father's wife. Clasp hands (MARRY) + GIRL sign
19 Baby /BAY-bee/ The baby sleeps in a small crib. Rock arms like cradling baby
20 Toddler /TOD-ler/ The toddler is learning to walk. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Child /CHYLD/ Every child in Harmonia learns music first. Pat downward in air (showing height)
22 Teenager /TEEN-ay-jer/ The teenager helps younger children with homework. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Adult /uh-DULT/ An adult has more responsibilities than a child. A-hand moves from forehead to chest
24 Elder /EL-der/ The village elder shares wisdom from long ago. Fingerspell ELDER
25 Stepmother /STEP-muth-er/ A stepmother joins the family through marriage. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Stepfather /STEP-fah-ther/ A stepfather loves his stepchildren as his own. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Half-brother /HAF-bruth-er/ A half-brother shares one parent with you. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Half-sister /HAF-sis-ter/ A half-sister shares one parent with you. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Foster child /FOS-ter CHYLD/ A foster child lives with a caring family temporarily. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Adopted /uh-DOP-ted/ An adopted child is chosen and loved into a family. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Generation /jen-er-AY-shun/ Three generations live together in Harmonia. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Twin /TWIN/ The twins look exactly alike! Fingerspell TWIN
33 Sibling /SIB-ling/ A sibling is a brother or sister. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Relative /REL-uh-tiv/ A relative is someone in your extended family. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Gather /GATH-er/ The family gathers together for holidays. Fingerspell GATHER
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

Kenji's Tip:

"Draw a family tree! Put your grandparents at the top, your parents in the middle, and you at the bottom. Write each person's family word: MOTHER, FATHER, GRANDMOTHER. That's YOUR family tree in English!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 1: Greetings & Introductions

Kenji and Aiko stand at the gates of Harmonia, welcoming new visitors. Every journey begins with a greeting.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Hello /heh-LOH/ Kenji says hello to every person who arrives. Flat hand, salute from forehead outward
2 Goodbye /good-BY/ Aiko waves goodbye as the visitors leave. Open hand, wave fingers down repeatedly
3 Good morning /good MOR-ning/ Good morning! The sun rises over Harmonia. Sign GOOD + MORNING (arm rises like sun)
4 Good afternoon /good af-ter-NOON/ Good afternoon! It's time for lunch. Sign GOOD + AFTERNOON (forearm tilts forward)
5 Good evening /good EEV-ning/ Good evening! The stars are coming out. Sign GOOD + EVENING (hand curves down like setting sun)
6 Good night /good NYT/ Good night! Time to rest and sleep. Sign GOOD + NIGHT (dominant hand drops over non-dominant)
7 Please /PLEEZ/ Please pass the water, Kenji says politely. Flat hand circles on chest
8 Thank you /THANK yoo/ Thank you for sharing, Aiko says with a smile. Flat hand from chin outward
9 You're welcome /yor WEL-kum/ You're welcome! It's nice to help a friend. Flat hand moves from mouth forward and down
10 Excuse me /ek-SKYOOZ mee/ Excuse me, may I sit here? Fingertips brush opposite palm forward twice
11 I'm sorry /eym SOR-ee/ I'm sorry, I didn't mean to bump into you. Fist circles on chest (sign SORRY)
12 Yes /YES/ Yes, I would like to learn a new word today. S-hand nods up and down
13 No /NOH/ No, thank you, I'm not hungry right now. Index and middle finger snap to thumb
14 Name /NAYM/ My name is Kenji. What is your name? Two H-fingers tap crosswise
15 First name /FURST NAYM/ Write your first name on this line. Sign FIRST (index touches thumb-side palm) + NAME
16 Last name /LAST NAYM/ Your last name is your family name. Sign LAST (pinky strikes opposite pinky) + NAME
17 Nice to meet you /NYS too MEET yoo/ Nice to meet you! Welcome to Harmonia. Both flat hands meet in middle
18 How are you? /HOW ar yoo/ How are you today? I am doing well. Curved hands on chest flip outward (HOW) + point
19 Fine /FYN/ I'm fine, thank you for asking. Thumb of 5-hand taps chest
20 Friend /FREND/ Aiko introduces her new friend to the group. Hook index fingers together, then reverse
21 Handshake /HAND-shayk/ A firm handshake shows respect. Clasp hands together, mime shaking
22 Wave /WAYV/ Kenji gives a friendly wave from across the garden. Open hand waves side to side
23 Bow /BOW/ In some cultures, people bow to greet each other. Bend at waist with hands at sides
24 Hug /HUG/ Aiko gives her grandmother a warm hug. Cross arms over chest, squeeze
25 Smile /SMYL/ A smile is a greeting that everyone understands. Index fingers draw smile on cheeks
26 Welcome /WEL-kum/ Welcome to our village! Come inside. Open hand sweeps inward toward body
27 Introduce /in-troh-DOOS/ Let me introduce you to my teacher. Both flat hands brought together from sides
28 Conversation /kon-ver-SAY-shun/ A good conversation has listening AND speaking. Both C-hands move alternately near mouth
29 Polite /poh-LYT/ It is polite to say "please" and "thank you." Thumb of 5-hand taps chest gently
30 Respect /rih-SPEKT/ Respect means treating others the way you want to be treated. R-hand arcs from forehead outward
🎵

Kenji's Tip:

"Practice these greetings with your family. Say: 'Good morning, Mom. How are you?' Listen for the answer. Now YOU are using everyday English!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 13: Hobbies & Recreation

In the Harmonia Fun House, Kenji and Aiko explore all the ways people spend their free time.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Hobby /HOB-ee/ A hobby is something you enjoy doing in your free time. Fingerspell HOBBY
2 Read /REED/ Aiko loves to read adventure stories. V-fingers move down opposite flat palm
3 Draw /DRAW/ Kenji draws pictures of animals in his sketchbook. Fingerspell DRAW
4 Paint /PAYNT/ Aiko paints landscapes with watercolors. Fingerspell PAINT
5 Sing /SING/ Kenji sings with the Harmonia choir every Saturday. Fingerspell SING
6 Dance /DANS/ Aiko loves to dance to her favorite songs. Fingerspell DANCE
7 Cook /KOOK/ Kenji helps his mother cook dinner on Sundays. Flip hand on opposite palm (like flipping pancake)
8 Bake /BAYK/ Aiko bakes cookies for her classmates. Fingerspell BAKE
9 Garden /GAR-den/ Many people garden to grow flowers and vegetables. G-hands outline garden plot
10 Swim /SWIM/ Kenji swims at the community pool on hot days. Fingerspell SWIM
11 Ride a bike /RYD uh BYK/ Aiko rides her bike to the park every weekend. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Skateboard /SKAYT-bord/ Kenji practices tricks on his skateboard. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Soccer /SOK-er/ Soccer is a popular sport played with a round ball. Mime kicking ball to side
14 Basketball /BAS-ket-bawl/ Shoot the basketball through the hoop! Both hands mime shooting basketball
15 Baseball /BAYS-bawl/ Baseball uses a bat, a ball, and a glove. Both hands mime swinging bat
16 Tennis /TEN-is/ Hit the tennis ball over the net. Mime swinging tennis racket
17 Board game /BORD GAYM/ The family plays a board game on rainy days. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Video game /VID-ee-oh GAYM/ Kenji plays video games for one hour after homework. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Puzzle /PUZ-ul/ Aiko works on a 500-piece puzzle of the ocean. Fingerspell PUZZLE
20 Collect /kuh-LEKT/ Some people collect stamps, coins, or stickers. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Photography /foh-TOG-ruh-fee/ Photography captures special moments in pictures. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Sew /SOH/ Grandmother teaches Aiko to sew a button. Fingerspell SEW
23 Knit /NIT/ Kenji's mother knits warm scarves in winter. Fingerspell KNIT
24 Camp /KAMP/ The family goes camping in the mountains each summer. Fingerspell CAMP
25 Hike /HYK/ Hike along the trail to see the waterfall. Fingerspell HIKE
26 Fish /FISH/ Grandfather fishes at the lake early in the morning. Flat hand swims forward like fish
27 Instrument /IN-stroo-ment/ Kenji plays three musical instruments. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Piano /pee-AN-oh/ Aiko practices piano chords every evening. Fingerspell PIANO
29 Guitar /gih-TAR/ Kenji strums his guitar and sings along. Fingerspell GUITAR
30 Drum /DRUM/ The drum keeps the beat steady for the whole band. Fingerspell DRUM
31 Yoga /YOH-guh/ Yoga helps your body stretch and your mind relax. Fingerspell YOGA
32 Martial arts /MAR-shul ARTS/ Martial arts teaches discipline and self-defense. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Volunteer /vol-un-TEER/ Kenji volunteers at the food bank every month. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Free time /FREE TYM/ What do you like to do in your free time? Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Fun /FUN/ Having fun is important for a happy life. Fingerspell FUN
🎸

Kenji's Tip:

"What is YOUR hobby? Say: 'My hobby is ___. I like to ___ because ___.' Now ask a friend: 'What is YOUR hobby?' You just had a REAL conversation in English!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 11: Manners & Politeness

At the Harmonia Etiquette Garden, Kenji and Aiko practice the gentle words that build bridges between people.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Polite /poh-LYT/ Polite words open doors that rudeness cannot. Thumb of 5-hand taps chest gently
2 Rude /ROOD/ Speaking rudely hurts people's feelings. Fingerspell RUDE
3 Manners /MAN-erz/ Good manners show respect for others. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 May I /MAY eye/ May I please borrow your pencil? Fingerspell MAY I
5 Pardon /PAR-dun/ Pardon me, could you repeat that? Fingerspell PARDON
6 After you /AF-ter yoo/ After you — please go first through the door. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Bless you /BLES yoo/ Bless you! Kenji says after Aiko sneezes. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Congratulations /kun-grat-yoo-LAY-shunz/ Congratulations on winning the spelling bee! Fingerspell or sign each word
9 I appreciate it /eye uh-PREE-shee-ayt it/ I appreciate your help with my homework. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 No problem /noh PROB-lum/ No problem! I'm happy to help you. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Certainly /SUR-ten-lee/ Certainly! I can show you the way. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Of course /ov KORS/ Of course you can sit with us at lunch. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Patience /PAY-shunss/ Patience means waiting without getting upset. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Kind /KYND/ A kind word can change someone's whole day. Fingerspell KIND
15 Gentle /JEN-tul/ Speak in a gentle voice when someone is sad. Fingerspell GENTLE
16 Share /SHAIR/ Kenji shares his crayons with the new student. Flat hand slides between opposite thumb and index
17 Take turns /TAYK TURNZ/ Take turns so everyone gets a chance to play. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Interrupt /in-ter-UPT/ Don't interrupt when someone else is talking. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Listen /LIS-en/ Listen carefully when your teacher speaks. Cup hand behind ear
20 Apologize /uh-POL-oh-jyz/ Apologize sincerely when you make a mistake. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Forgive /for-GIV/ Forgive your friend and start fresh. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Compliment /KOM-plih-ment/ Give a compliment: "I like your drawing!" Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Gratitude /GRAT-ih-tood/ Gratitude means being thankful for what you have. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Considerate /kun-SID-er-it/ Being considerate means thinking about how others feel. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Courteous /KUR-tee-us/ A courteous person holds the door open for others. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌸

Kenji's Tip:

"Magic words unlock the world! Practice every day: 'Please. Thank you. Excuse me. May I?' These four phrases will take you ANYWHERE."

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 16: Music & Instruments

The Harmonia Music Hall is alive with sound! Kenji teaches Aiko the names of every instrument.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Piano /pee-AN-oh/ She plays the piano beautifully. Fingerspell PIANO
2 Guitar /gih-TAR/ Kenji strums the guitar and sings a song. Fingerspell GUITAR
3 Drum /DRUM/ Beat the drum to keep the rhythm. Fingerspell DRUM
4 Violin /vy-oh-LIN/ The violin makes a sweet, high sound. Fingerspell VIOLIN
5 Flute /FLOOT/ The flute is a wind instrument made of metal. Fingerspell FLUTE
6 Trumpet /TRUM-pit/ The trumpet plays a bright, bold melody. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Tambourine /TAM-buh-reen/ Shake the tambourine to add rhythm. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Xylophone /ZY-loh-fohn/ Each bar of the xylophone plays a different note. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Melody /MEL-oh-dee/ A melody is the main tune of a song. Fingerspell MELODY
10 Rhythm /RITH-um/ Clap the rhythm with your hands. R-hand bounces forward rhythmically
11 Beat /BEET/ The beat is the steady pulse of the music. Fist taps chest rhythmically
12 Lyrics /LEER-iks/ The lyrics are the words of a song. Fingerspell LYRICS
13 Choir /KWY-er/ The school choir sings in four-part harmony. Fingerspell CHOIR
14 Orchestra /OR-kes-truh/ An orchestra has many different instruments. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Concert /KON-sert/ We went to a concert in the park last night. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Note /NOHT/ Each note has a different pitch and length. Fingerspell NOTE
17 Tempo /TEM-poh/ The tempo tells you how fast to play. Index bounces at varying speeds
18 Volume /VOL-yoom/ Turn down the volume — it's too loud. Fingerspell VOLUME
19 Harmony /HAR-moh-nee/ Harmony is when different notes sound good together. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Microphone /MY-kroh-fohn/ Speak into the microphone so everyone can hear. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Headphones /HED-fohnz/ Put on your headphones to listen to the recording. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Record /rih-KORD/ Let's record our song and share it. Fingerspell RECORD
23 Practice /PRAK-tis/ Practice the piano for thirty minutes every day. A-hand brushes back and forth on opposite index
24 Performer /per-FOR-mer/ The performer bowed after the standing ovation. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Audience /AW-dee-ents/ The audience clapped at the end of the concert. Fingerspell or sign each word
🎵

Kenji's Tip:

"Music IS language! Every song has LYRICS, MELODY, and RHYTHM. Play your favorite English song and follow the LYRICS — you'll learn new words while having fun!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 2: Personal Information

At the Harmonia Registration Hall, newcomers fill out their personal information cards. Kenji and Aiko show them how.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Address /AD-res/ Write your address so people know where you live. A-hands move up chest (like suspenders)
2 Street /STREET/ Kenji lives on Maple Street in Harmonia. Both S-hands move forward parallel
3 City /SIT-ee/ The city is a large place with many buildings. Triangle rooftops with both hands, twist
4 State /STAYT/ Each state has its own capital city. S-hand taps opposite flat palm
5 Zip code /ZIP kohd/ The zip code helps the mail find your house. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Country /KUN-tree/ People in Harmonia come from every country. Y-hand circles elbow
7 Phone number /FOHN NUM-ber/ Write your phone number on the line. Sign PHONE + NUMBER
8 Email /EE-mayl/ Send me an email if you have any questions. Index shoots into opposite C-hand (mail + computer)
9 Date of birth /DAYT ov BURTH/ Your date of birth is the day you were born. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Age /AYJ/ Kenji is ten years of age. Pull chin down with C-hand
11 Male /MAYL/ Check the box that says male or female. Fingerspell MALE
12 Female /FEE-mayl/ The form asks if you are male or female. Fingerspell FEMALE
13 Signature /SIG-nuh-chur/ Write your signature at the bottom of the form. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Social Security number /SOH-shul sih-KYOOR-ih-tee NUM-ber/ A Social Security number is private information. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Identification /eye-den-tih-fih-KAY-shun/ Bring your identification to the appointment. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Driver's license /DRY-verz LY-sens/ A driver's license has your photo and address. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Passport /PAS-port/ A passport lets you travel to other countries. P-hand opens like small book
18 Apartment number /uh-PART-ment NUM-ber/ The apartment number is 4B on the second floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Marital status /MAIR-ih-tul STAT-us/ Marital status means single, married, or divorced. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Single /SING-gul/ Single means you are not married. Fingerspell SINGLE
21 Married /MAIR-eed/ Married means you have a husband or wife. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Emergency contact /ih-MUR-jun-see KON-takt/ Write the name of your emergency contact. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Form /FORM/ Fill out the form with a dark pen. Fingerspell FORM
24 Application /ap-lih-KAY-shun/ The job application asks about your work experience. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Print /PRINT/ Please print your name clearly in block letters. Fingerspell PRINT
26 Spell /SPEL/ Can you spell your last name for me? Fingerspell SPELL
27 Citizen /SIT-ih-zen/ A citizen has the right to vote. Both C-hands touch shoulders then meet at center
28 Resident /REZ-ih-dent/ A resident is someone who lives in a place. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Birth certificate /BURTH ser-TIF-ih-kit/ A birth certificate proves your date and place of birth. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Legal /LEE-gul/ A legal document is recognized by the government. Fingerspell LEGAL
📋

Aiko's Tip:

"Practice filling out forms! Write your name, address, and phone number. Do it until it feels easy. These words keep you safe and connected."

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 20: Pets at Home

Kenji and Aiko visit the Harmonia Pet Shop. Caring for a pet teaches responsibility — and lots of vocabulary!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Pet /PET/ A pet is an animal you keep at home. Stroke back of hand (petting motion)
2 Dog /DOG/ The dog wags its tail when it is happy. Snap fingers + pat thigh
3 Cat /KAT/ The cat purrs when you scratch behind its ears. F-hand pulls from cheek (whiskers)
4 Fish /FISH/ The goldfish swims in a round bowl. Flat hand swims forward like fish
5 Bird /BURD/ The bird sings a cheerful song every morning. G-hand opens and closes at mouth (beak)
6 Hamster /HAM-ster/ The hamster runs on its wheel at night. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Turtle /TUR-tul/ The turtle moves slowly but lives a long time. A-hand under opposite cupped hand, thumb peeks
8 Rabbit /RAB-it/ The rabbit hops around the yard eating clover. Both H-hands at head, wiggle (long ears)
9 Collar /KOL-er/ Put a collar and tag on your dog. Fingerspell COLLAR
10 Leash /LEESH/ Walk the dog on a leash for safety. Fingerspell LEASH
11 Bowl /BOHL/ Fill the water bowl for the cat. Both cupped hands form bowl shape
12 Cage /KAYJ/ The bird lives in a large cage with perches. Fingerspell CAGE
13 Veterinarian /vet-er-ih-NAIR-ee-un/ Take your pet to the veterinarian for checkups. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Feed /FEED/ Feed the fish twice a day. Fingerspell FEED
15 Groom /GROOM/ Groom the dog by brushing its fur. Fingerspell GROOM
16 Train /TRAYN/ Train the puppy to sit and stay. H-hands slide along each other (tracks)
17 Walk /WAWK/ Walk the dog around the block every morning. Both flat hands walk forward alternately
18 Adopt /uh-DOPT/ Adopt a pet from the animal shelter. Fingerspell ADOPT
19 Shelter /SHEL-ter/ The animal shelter cares for homeless pets. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Loyal /LOY-ul/ Dogs are loyal companions to their owners. Fingerspell LOYAL
21 Playful /PLAY-ful/ The playful kitten chases a ball of yarn. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Bark /BARK/ The dog barks when someone knocks on the door. Fingerspell BARK
23 Purr /PUR/ The cat purrs softly while sleeping on my lap. Fingerspell PURR
24 Fetch /FECH/ Throw the ball and the dog will fetch it. Fingerspell FETCH
25 Companion /kum-PAN-yun/ A pet is a loving companion for the whole family. Fingerspell or sign each word
🐾

Kenji's Tip:

"Do you have a pet? Describe it! 'My ___ is ___. It likes to ___. I feed it ___.' If you don't have a pet, describe your DREAM pet in English!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 17: School Events & Activities

The Harmonia School Calendar is packed with exciting events all year long!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Assembly /uh-SEM-blee/ The whole school meets in the gym for assembly. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Field trip /FEELD TRIP/ Our field trip to the zoo is next Wednesday. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Science fair /SY-ents FAIR/ Aiko won first place at the science fair. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Talent show /TAL-ent SHOH/ Students sing and dance at the talent show. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Picture day /PIK-chur DAY/ Wear your nicest clothes for picture day. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Spirit week /SPIR-it WEEK/ During spirit week, everyone wears school colors. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Book fair /BOOK FAIR/ Buy new books at the school book fair. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Fire drill /FYR DRIL/ During a fire drill, walk quickly to the exit. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Pep rally /PEP RAL-ee/ The pep rally gets everyone excited for the game. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Graduation /graj-oo-AY-shun/ Graduation is a proud day for students and families. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Award /uh-WORD/ She received an award for perfect attendance. Fingerspell AWARD
12 Certificate /ser-TIF-ih-kit/ The certificate shows you completed the program. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Ceremony /SAIR-eh-moh-nee/ The ceremony will be held in the auditorium. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Auditorium /aw-dih-TOR-ee-um/ The auditorium has seats for five hundred people. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Stage /STAYJ/ The actors perform on the stage. Fingerspell STAGE
16 Cafeteria /kaf-eh-TEER-ee-uh/ We eat lunch in the cafeteria every day. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Gymnasium /jim-NAY-zee-um/ The basketball game is in the gymnasium. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Recess /REE-ses/ Recess is the best part of the school day! Fingerspell RECESS
19 Semester /seh-MES-ter/ Each school year has two semesters. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Yearbook /YEER-book/ Sign my yearbook on the last day of school! Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Clubs /KLUBZ/ Join clubs to make friends who share your interests. Fingerspell CLUBS
22 Team /TEEM/ Our soccer team won the championship. Fingerspell TEAM
23 Coach /KOHCH/ The coach teaches the team how to play. C-hand taps shoulder (CAPTAIN)
24 Captain /KAP-tin/ The team captain leads warm-ups before practice. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Tryout /TRY-owt/ Tryouts for the basketball team are on Monday. Fingerspell TRYOUT
🏫

Aiko's Tip:

"School is MORE than homework! Say these words: ASSEMBLY, FIELD TRIP, TALENT SHOW. Which one is YOUR favorite? Tell a friend in English!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 4: The Classroom

The Harmonia Village School is alive with music and learning. Every subject begins with rhythm.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 School /SKOOL/ Aiko walks to school every morning at eight o'clock. Clap hands twice
2 Classroom /KLAS-room/ The classroom has desks, a whiteboard, and lots of books. Sign CLASS (C-hands circle) + ROOM
3 Teacher /TEE-cher/ The teacher writes the lesson on the board. Both flat hands at temples, move outward + PERSON
4 Student /STOO-dent/ Each student has a notebook and a pencil. Flat hand lifts from opposite palm + PERSON
5 Desk /DESK/ Kenji keeps his books inside his desk. Sign TABLE (forearms stacked, then flat)
6 Chair /CHAIR/ Push your chair in when you leave the desk. Two bent fingers sit on opposite two fingers
7 Whiteboard /WYT-bord/ The teacher draws a diagram on the whiteboard. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Marker /MAR-ker/ Use the blue marker to underline the key words. Fingerspell MARKER
9 Eraser /ih-RAY-ser/ The eraser removes mistakes from the whiteboard. Fingerspell ERASER
10 Pencil /PEN-sul/ Sharpen your pencil before the quiz. Thumb on lips, then write on palm
11 Pen /PEN/ Sign your paper with a black pen. Pinch dominant hand, write on opposite palm
12 Notebook /NOHT-book/ Aiko writes vocabulary words in her notebook. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Textbook /TEKST-book/ Open your textbook to page forty-two. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Paper /PAY-per/ Take a piece of paper for the drawing activity. Both flat hands brush past each other
15 Folder /FOHL-der/ Keep your homework in the green folder. Fingerspell FOLDER
16 Backpack /BAK-pak/ Kenji puts everything in his backpack. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Ruler /ROO-ler/ Use the ruler to draw a straight line. R-hands outline ruler
18 Scissors /SIZ-erz/ Cut along the dotted line with scissors. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Glue /GLOO/ Glue the picture onto the poster board. Fingerspell GLUE
20 Tape /TAYP/ Use tape to fix the torn page. Fingerspell TAPE
21 Calculator /KAL-kyoo-lay-ter/ The calculator helps you solve math problems quickly. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Computer /kum-PYOO-ter/ Students use the computer for research projects. C-hand bounces up opposite forearm
23 Laptop /LAP-top/ Aiko types her essay on a laptop. Fingerspell LAPTOP
24 Tablet /TAB-let/ The teacher shows a video on the tablet. Fingerspell TABLET
25 Projector /proh-JEK-ter/ The projector displays the lesson on the big screen. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Globe /GLOHB/ Spin the globe and point to a country. Fingerspell GLOBE
27 Map /MAP/ The map on the wall shows all seven continents. M-hands trace on flat surface
28 Clock /KLOK/ The clock on the wall says it's three fifteen. Point to wrist + mime clock hands
29 Bell /BEL/ The bell rings at the end of each class. Fingerspell BELL
30 Homework /HOHM-wurk/ Finish your homework before you watch television. Sign HOME + WORK
31 Test /TEST/ Study hard for the spelling test on Friday. X-hands draw question marks in air, then move down
32 Quiz /KWIZ/ The short quiz has only ten questions. Fingerspell QUIZ
33 Grade /GRAYD/ Kenji earned a good grade on his project. Fingerspell GRADE
34 Report card /rih-PORT KARD/ The report card shows your grades for the semester. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Recess /REE-ses/ At recess, children play outside on the field. Fingerspell RECESS
36 Lunch period /LUNCH PEER-ee-ud/ The lunch period is thirty minutes long. Fingerspell or sign each word
37 Library /LY-brair-ee/ The library has thousands of books to borrow. L-hand circles in air
38 Gymnasium /jim-NAY-zee-um/ Students play basketball in the gymnasium. Fingerspell or sign each word
39 Cafeteria /kaf-uh-TEER-ee-uh/ Aiko eats lunch in the school cafeteria. Fingerspell or sign each word
40 Principal /PRIN-suh-pul/ The principal greets every student by name. Fingerspell or sign each word
41 Counselor /KOWN-sel-er/ The school counselor helps students with problems. Fingerspell or sign each word
42 Attendance /uh-TEN-duns/ The teacher takes attendance each morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
43 Absent /AB-sent/ Kenji was absent because he had a cold. Fingerspell ABSENT
44 Present /PREZ-ent/ "Present!" Aiko calls out when her name is called. Fingerspell or sign each word
45 Field trip /FEELD TRIP/ The class goes on a field trip to the museum. Fingerspell or sign each word
📚

Aiko's Tip:

"Point at everything in YOUR classroom and say the word in English. Desk! Chair! Whiteboard! Pencil! The more you say it, the more you remember it."

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 8: The Home

Kenji and Aiko give a tour of their home in Harmonia. Every room has a purpose, and every object has a name.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 House /HOWS/ The house has a front yard and a back door. Both hands form roof peak, then walls
2 Apartment /uh-PART-ment/ An apartment is a home inside a larger building. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Room /ROOM/ Each room in the house has a different purpose. R-hands form box shape
4 Kitchen /KICH-en/ The kitchen is where the family cooks meals. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Living room /LIV-ing ROOM/ The living room has a sofa, a TV, and bookshelves. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Bedroom /BED-room/ Kenji reads books in his bedroom before sleep. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Bathroom /BATH-room/ Wash your hands in the bathroom after playing. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Dining room /DY-ning ROOM/ The family eats dinner together in the dining room. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Garage /guh-RAHJ/ The car is parked in the garage. Fingerspell GARAGE
10 Basement /BAYS-ment/ The basement is the room below the ground floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Attic /AT-ik/ Old photo albums are stored in the attic. Fingerspell ATTIC
12 Hallway /HAWL-way/ The hallway connects the bedrooms to the living room. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Door /DOR/ Close the door quietly when someone is sleeping. Both B-hands: one swings open from other
14 Window /WIN-doh/ Open the window to let in fresh air. Both flat hands stack, top slides open
15 Roof /ROOF/ The roof keeps rain and snow out of the house. Fingerspell ROOF
16 Floor /FLOR/ Sweep the floor before setting the table. Both flat hands horizontal, slide apart
17 Ceiling /SEE-ling/ The ceiling fan cools the room in summer. Flat hand above, pats up
18 Wall /WAWL/ Aiko hangs her paintings on the wall. Both flat hands vertical, parallel
19 Stairs /STAIRZ/ Walk carefully going up and down the stairs. Fingerspell STAIRS
20 Closet /KLOZ-it/ Keep your coats and shoes in the closet. Fingerspell CLOSET
21 Furniture /FUR-nih-chur/ A table, chairs, and a bed are all furniture. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Sofa /SOH-fuh/ The whole family sits on the sofa for movie night. Fingerspell SOFA
23 Table /TAY-bul/ Set the table with plates, cups, and napkins. Both forearms stack, form flat surface
24 Bed /BED/ Make your bed every morning when you wake up. Fingerspell BED
25 Pillow /PIL-oh/ Rest your head on the soft pillow. Fingerspell PILLOW
26 Blanket /BLANG-kit/ Pull the warm blanket up to your chin. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Lamp /LAMP/ Turn on the lamp to read at night. Fingerspell LAMP
28 Mirror /MIR-er/ Kenji checks his hair in the mirror. Fingerspell MIRROR
29 Curtains /KUR-tunz/ Close the curtains to block the sunlight. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Rug /RUG/ The soft rug covers the cold floor. Fingerspell RUG
31 Sink /SINK/ Wash the dishes in the kitchen sink. Fingerspell SINK
32 Toilet /TOY-let/ Flush the toilet after each use. Fingerspell TOILET
33 Bathtub /BATH-tub/ The bathtub fills with warm water for bath time. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Shower /SHOW-er/ Kenji takes a quick shower before school. 5-hand above head opens/closes (water spraying)
35 Refrigerator /rih-FRIJ-er-ay-ter/ Store milk and vegetables in the refrigerator. Both hands shiver with R-hands
36 Microwave /MY-kroh-wayv/ Heat the soup in the microwave for two minutes. Fingerspell or sign each word
37 Dishwasher /DISH-wash-er/ Load the dirty dishes into the dishwasher. Fingerspell or sign each word
38 Washing machine /WASH-ing muh-SHEEN/ The washing machine cleans clothes with soap and water. Fingerspell or sign each word
39 Dryer /DRY-er/ The dryer makes wet clothes warm and dry. Fingerspell DRYER
40 Key /KEE/ Always carry your house key in a safe place. Fingerspell KEY
41 Lock /LOK/ Lock the door at night for safety. Fingerspell LOCK
42 Doorbell /DOR-bel/ Ring the doorbell and wait for someone to answer. Fingerspell or sign each word
43 Mailbox /MAYL-boks/ Aiko checks the mailbox for letters. Fingerspell or sign each word
44 Yard /YARD/ The children play in the front yard. Fingerspell YARD
45 Porch /PORCH/ Grandfather sits on the porch in his rocking chair. Fingerspell PORCH
🏠

Aiko's Tip:

"Give a tour of YOUR home in English! Walk through each room and say: 'This is the kitchen. This is the bedroom. This is the bathroom.' You are now a tour guide in your own language!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 19: The Neighborhood

Kenji and Aiko explore their neighborhood, discovering all the places and people nearby.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Neighbor /NAY-ber/ A neighbor is someone who lives next to you. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Block /BLOK/ Walk around the block for exercise. Fingerspell BLOCK
3 Corner /KOR-ner/ The bakery is on the corner of Oak Street. Fingerspell CORNER
4 Lane /LAYN/ Our house is on a quiet lane with big trees. Fingerspell LANE
5 Avenue /AV-eh-noo/ The parade goes down the main avenue. Fingerspell AVENUE
6 Apartment /uh-PART-ment/ We live in an apartment on the third floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Townhouse /TOWN-hows/ A townhouse shares walls with its neighbors. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Yard /YARD/ The children play in the front yard. Fingerspell YARD
9 Community /kuh-MYOO-nih-tee/ Our community has a park, library, and school. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Park /PARK/ The park has swings, slides, and a basketball court. Modified P-hand taps S-hand on top
11 Library /LY-brair-ee/ Borrow books for free at the public library. L-hand circles in air
12 Church /CHURCH/ The church opens its doors for community meals. C-hand taps back of S-fist (building on rock)
13 Mosque /MOSK/ The mosque is a place of worship and community. Fingerspell MOSQUE
14 Temple /TEM-pul/ The temple hosts cultural celebrations. Fingerspell TEMPLE
15 Grocery store /GROH-ser-ee STOR/ The grocery store is three blocks from our house. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Laundromat /LAWN-droh-mat/ Wash your clothes at the laundromat. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Pharmacy /FAR-muh-see/ The pharmacy is next to the doctor's office. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Gas station /GAS STAY-shun/ Fill up the tank at the gas station. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Barbershop /BAR-ber-shop/ Get a haircut at the barbershop on Main Street. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Salon /suh-LON/ She gets her hair done at the salon. Fingerspell SALON
21 Daycare /DAY-kair/ Drop off the baby at daycare before work. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Clinic /KLIN-ik/ The community clinic provides low-cost health care. Fingerspell CLINIC
23 Senior center /SEEN-yer SEN-ter/ The senior center offers activities for older adults. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Playground /PLAY-grownd/ Meet me at the playground after school. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Community garden /kuh-MYOO-nih-tee GAR-den/ Grow vegetables in the community garden. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏘️

Aiko's Tip:

"Take a 'neighborhood vocabulary walk!' Name every place you pass: PHARMACY, LIBRARY, PARK, LAUNDROMAT. If you can name 20 places, you already know your neighborhood in English!"

Land 1 of 7

🎵 Harmonia

Guided by Kenji the Rhythm Keeper & Aiko the Melody Weaver

Scene 14: The Playground & Recess

The Harmonia Schoolyard is alive with laughter and games. Kenji and Aiko join their friends.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Playground /PLAY-grownd/ The playground has swings, slides, and monkey bars. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Swing /SWING/ Aiko pumps her legs high on the swing. Fingerspell SWING
3 Slide /SLYD/ Go down the slide feet-first for safety. Fingerspell SLIDE
4 Seesaw /SEE-saw/ Two friends go up and down on the seesaw. Fingerspell SEESAW
5 Monkey bars /MUN-kee BARZ/ Kenji crosses the monkey bars hand over hand. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Sandbox /SAND-boks/ The sandbox is for building castles and digging. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Jump rope /JUMP ROHP/ The girls jump rope and count to one hundred. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Hopscotch /HOP-skoch/ Draw the hopscotch squares with chalk. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Tag /TAG/ "You're it!" Kenji shouts as they play tag. Fingerspell TAG
10 Hide and seek /HYD and SEEK/ In hide and seek, one person counts while others hide. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Catch /KACH/ Throw the ball and catch it with both hands. Fingerspell CATCH
12 Throw /THROH/ Throw the ball gently to your partner. Fingerspell THROW
13 Kick /KIK/ Kick the soccer ball toward the goal. Fingerspell KICK
14 Run /RUN/ Run fast in the relay race! L-hands, index pulls on opposite thumb, move forward
15 Jump /JUMP/ Jump over the puddle so your shoes stay dry! Fingerspell JUMP
16 Climb /KLYM/ Climb the rock wall one hand at a time. Fingerspell CLIMB
17 Skip /SKIP/ Skip along the path and sing a song. Fingerspell SKIP
18 Race /RAYS/ Ready, set, go! It's a running race. Fingerspell RACE
19 Team /TEEM/ Pick your team and choose who plays first. Fingerspell TEAM
20 Fair /FAIR/ Play fair so everyone has a good time. Fingerspell FAIR
21 Winner /WIN-er/ The winner shakes hands with the other players. Fingerspell WINNER
22 Loser /LOO-zer/ A gracious loser says "good game" and tries again. Fingerspell LOSER
23 Score /SKOR/ The score is five to three — we're winning! Both hands tally in air
24 Whistle /WIS-ul/ The teacher blows the whistle to end recess. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Line up /LYN UP/ Line up quietly to go back inside. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Bench /BENCH/ Sit on the bench if you need to rest. Fingerspell BENCH
27 Shade /SHAYD/ Find the shade under the tree on hot days. Fingerspell SHADE
28 Water fountain /WAW-ter FOWN-tun/ Get a drink from the water fountain. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Recess /REE-ses/ Recess is everybody's favorite time of day. Fingerspell RECESS
30 Outdoor /OWT-dor/ Outdoor games are best on sunny days. Fingerspell or sign each word
🤸

Aiko's Tip:

"Name the ACTIONS you do at recess: 'I RUN. I JUMP. I CLIMB. I THROW.' Action words (verbs!) are the most powerful words in any language!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 2: Basic Math Operations

In the Numeria Calculation Chamber, Kwame and Octavia solve problems on the giant chalkboard.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Add /AD/ Add three and four to get seven. Flat O-hands come together (combining)
2 Addition /uh-DISH-un/ Addition means putting numbers together. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Plus /PLUS/ Three plus two equals five. Fingerspell PLUS
4 Subtract /sub-TRAKT/ Subtract five from ten to get five. Bent hand swipes across opposite palm down
5 Subtraction /sub-TRAK-shun/ Subtraction means taking a number away. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Minus /MY-nus/ Ten minus three equals seven. Fingerspell MINUS
7 Multiply /MUL-tih-ply/ Multiply four times three to get twelve. V-hands cross in front
8 Multiplication /mul-tih-plih-KAY-shun/ Multiplication is repeated addition. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Times /TYMZ/ Six times two equals twelve. Index taps wrist (watch location) (repeated)
10 Divide /dih-VYD/ Divide twelve by three to get four. Flat hands slide apart diagonally
11 Division /dih-VIZH-un/ Division splits a number into equal groups. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Equals /EE-kwulz/ Two plus two equals four. Both bent hands tap together evenly (repeated)
13 Sum /SUM/ The sum of five and three is eight. Fingerspell SUM
14 Difference /DIF-er-ens/ The difference between ten and six is four. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Product /PROD-ukt/ The product of three and four is twelve. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Quotient /KWOH-shunt/ The quotient of twenty divided by five is four. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Remainder /rih-MAYN-der/ When you divide seven by two, the remainder is one. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Fraction /FRAK-shun/ One half is a fraction written as 1/2. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Decimal /DES-ih-mul/ The number 3.5 has a decimal point. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Percent /per-SENT/ Fifty percent means half. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Equation /ih-KWAY-zhun/ An equation shows that two sides are equal. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Problem /PROB-lem/ Kwame solves one math problem at a time. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Answer /AN-ser/ The answer to five plus five is ten. Index from chin points forward
24 Estimate /ES-tih-mayt/ Estimate how many marbles are in the jar. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Calculate /KAL-kyoo-layt/ Octavia can calculate the total in her head. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Greater than /GRAY-ter THAN/ Ten is greater than five. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Less than /LES THAN/ Three is less than seven. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Equal to /EE-kwul TOO/ Six is equal to three plus three. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Average /AV-er-ij/ The average of two, four, and six is four. Flat hand levels across opposite hand
30 Total /TOH-tul/ The total cost of the three items is fifteen dollars. Fingerspell TOTAL
➕

Octavia's Tip:

"Math has its own vocabulary! Learn these words and math class gets easier. Say: 'I ADD. I SUBTRACT. I MULTIPLY. I DIVIDE.' Now say it with numbers: 'Two PLUS three EQUALS five!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 17: Budget & Savings

At the Numeria Savings Bank, Kwame learns every penny counts

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Budget /BUJ-it/ Make a budget to track your money. B-hands on opposite palm (MONEY + PLAN)
2 Savings /SAY-vingz/ Put money in savings every month. Both hands scoop toward body (gathering)
3 Income /IN-kum/ Income is the money you earn. Hand scoops money toward body
4 Expenses /ek-SPEN-sez/ Rent and food are monthly expenses. Hand pushes money away from body
5 Afford /uh-FORD/ Can we afford a new computer? Fingerspell AFFORD
6 Borrow /BOR-oh/ She borrowed money to pay the bill. Fingerspell BORROW
7 Lend /LEND/ Will you lend me ten dollars? Fingerspell LEND
8 Owe /OH/ I owe you five dollars from lunch. Fingerspell OWE
9 Debt /DET/ Pay off your debt quickly. Fingerspell DEBT
10 Interest /IN-ter-est/ The bank pays interest on savings. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Invest /in-VEST/ Invest your money in stocks or bonds. Fingerspell INVEST
12 Profit /PROF-it/ The store made a thousand dollar profit. Fingerspell PROFIT
13 Loss /LOS/ The business had a loss last quarter. Fingerspell LOSS
14 Tax /TAKS/ Sales tax adds eight percent. T-hand or X-hand taps opposite palm
15 Tip /TIP/ Leave a tip for the waiter. Fingerspell TIP
16 Discount /DIS-kownt/ Use this coupon for ten percent discount. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Free /FREE/ Free admission on Sundays. F-hands cross, then spread apart
18 Charge /CHARJ/ Is there a charge for parking? Fingerspell CHARGE
19 Fee /FEE/ The doctor charges a co-pay fee. Fingerspell FEE
20 Rent /RENT/ Rent is due on the first. Fingerspell RENT
21 Bill /BIL/ Pay the electric bill on time. Fingerspell BILL
22 Receipt /rih-SEET/ Keep the receipt for tax records. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Wallet /WOL-it/ Put your money in your wallet. Fingerspell WALLET
24 Piggy bank /PIG-ee BANK/ Save coins in a piggy bank. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Goal /GOHL/ Set a savings goal and work toward it. Fingerspell GOAL
M

Kwame's Tip:

"English + Math = MONEY SKILLS! INCOME, EXPENSES, SAVE. Say it, DO it!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 18: Calendar Math

At the Numeria Time Lab, Octavia maps out every calendar calculation

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Date /DAYT/ What is today's date? Fingerspell DATE
2 Day /DAY/ There are seven days in a week. Index arm rests on opposite, arcs like sun
3 Week /WEEK/ The project is due next week. Index moves across opposite flat palm (7 days)
4 Month /MUNTH/ January is the first month. Index slides down opposite index once
5 Year /YEER/ A year has three hundred sixty-five days. S-fists circle each other, then stack
6 Decade /DEK-ayd/ A decade is ten years. Fingerspell DECADE
7 Century /SEN-chur-ee/ A century is one hundred years. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Leap year /LEEP YEER/ A leap year has three sixty-six days. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Quarter /KWOR-ter/ Each quarter has three months. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Semester /seh-MES-ter/ Fall semester begins in August. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Weekend /WEEK-end/ Saturday and Sunday are the weekend. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Weekday /WEEK-day/ School is open on weekdays only. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Yesterday /YES-ter-day/ Yesterday was Tuesday. Y-hand or A-thumb touches cheek, then ear
14 Today /too-DAY/ Today is Wednesday. Both Y-hands drop in front of body
15 Tomorrow /too-MOR-oh/ Tomorrow will be Thursday. Thumb of A-hand bounces forward from cheek
16 Morning /MOR-ning/ Class starts at eight in the morning. Arm rises from opposite forearm (sunrise)
17 Afternoon /af-ter-NOON/ The meeting is at two in the afternoon. Forearm tilts forward from opposite forearm
18 Evening /EEV-ning/ We eat dinner in the evening. Hand curves down on opposite forearm (sunset)
19 Midnight /MID-nyt/ The clock struck midnight. Hand drops below opposite forearm (sun below)
20 Noon /NOON/ Lunch is served at noon. Forearm straight up on opposite forearm
21 Dawn /DAWN/ The sun rises at dawn. Fingerspell DAWN
22 Dusk /DUSK/ The sky turns orange at dusk. Fingerspell DUSK
23 Annual /AN-yoo-ul/ The annual parade is on July fourth. Fingerspell ANNUAL
24 Biweekly /by-WEEK-lee/ She gets paid biweekly. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Schedule /SKEJ-ool/ Check the bus schedule. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
C

Octavia's Tip:

"YESTERDAY, TODAY, TOMORROW. Three sentences, three time words, three steps to fluency!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 8: Charts & Graphs

In the Numeria Data Center, Kwame and Octavia read and build graphs to tell stories with numbers.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Graph /GRAF/ A graph shows information visually. 4-hand moves along opposite palm stepping up
2 Bar graph /BAR GRAF/ The bar graph shows how many books each student read. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Pie chart /PY CHART/ The pie chart shows what percentage of students prefer each color. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Line graph /LYN GRAF/ The line graph shows temperature changes over a week. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Data /DAY-tuh/ Data is information collected from research or observation. D-hand moves along opposite palm (reading data)
6 Table /TAY-bul/ The table organizes data into rows and columns. Both forearms stack, form flat surface
7 Row /ROH/ Read across the row to find the answer. Fingerspell ROW
8 Column /KOL-um/ Each column represents a different category. Fingerspell COLUMN
9 Title /TY-tul/ The title of the graph tells you what it's about. Fingerspell TITLE
10 Label /LAY-bul/ Each axis needs a label so readers understand. Fingerspell LABEL
11 Axis /AK-sis/ The x-axis goes left to right, the y-axis goes up and down. Fingerspell AXIS
12 Survey /SUR-vay/ Octavia conducts a survey to find everyone's favorite fruit. Fingerspell SURVEY
13 Result /rih-ZULT/ The survey result shows that apples are most popular. Fingerspell RESULT
14 Compare /kum-PAIR/ Compare the two bars to see which is taller. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Increase /in-KREES/ The graph shows an increase in temperature. H-hand rises up opposite index
16 Decrease /dih-KREES/ There was a decrease in rainfall this month. H-hand drops down opposite index
17 Pattern /PAT-ern/ Can you find a pattern in these numbers? Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Predict /prih-DIKT/ Use the data to predict what will happen next. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Tally /TAL-ee/ Make a tally mark for each person's answer. Fingerspell TALLY
20 Chart /CHART/ The chart makes the information easy to understand. Index draws chart lines on opposite palm
📊

Octavia's Tip:

"YOU can make a graph! Ask five people: 'What is your favorite color?' Write the answers, then draw a bar graph. You just became a data scientist!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 14: Cooking Measurements

In the Numeria Kitchen Lab, Kwame and Octavia bake a cake while learning to measure ingredients precisely.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Recipe /RES-ih-pee/ Follow the recipe step by step. Fingerspell RECIPE
2 Ingredient /in-GREE-dee-ent/ Flour is an important ingredient in bread. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Measure /MEZH-er/ Measure the sugar carefully before adding it. Both Y-hands tap together (SAME SIZE)
