4 Cup /KUP/ Add one cup of milk to the batter. C-hand taps opposite flat palm (cup on saucer)
5 Tablespoon /TAY-bul-spoon/ Use two tablespoons of butter. Fingerspell T-B-S or sign BIG + SPOON
6 Teaspoon /TEE-spoon/ Add half a teaspoon of vanilla extract. Fingerspell T-S-P or sign SMALL + SPOON
7 Pinch /PINCH/ A pinch of salt is a tiny amount between two fingers. Fingerspell PINCH
8 Dash /DASH/ A dash of pepper adds flavor to the soup. Fingerspell DASH
9 Stir /STUR/ Stir the batter until it is smooth. Fingerspell STIR
10 Mix /MIKS/ Mix the dry ingredients together first. Fingerspell MIX
11 Pour /POR/ Pour the batter into the baking pan. Fingerspell POUR
12 Preheat /pree-HEET/ Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Bake /BAYK/ Bake the cookies for twelve minutes. Fingerspell BAKE
14 Cool /KOOL/ Let the cake cool before adding frosting. Both open hands fan at body
15 Serving /SUR-ving/ One serving of rice is one half cup. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Serving size /SUR-ving SYZ/ The serving size is listed on the label. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Yield /YEELD/ This recipe yields twenty-four cookies. Fingerspell YIELD
18 Double /DUB-ul/ Double the recipe to make enough for everyone. Fingerspell DOUBLE
19 Halve /HAV/ Halve the recipe if you only need a small batch. Fingerspell HALVE
20 Level /LEV-ul/ Use a level cup of flour, not a heaping one. Fingerspell LEVEL
21 Heaping /HEEP-ing/ A heaping tablespoon is piled above the rim. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Sift /SIFT/ Sift the flour to remove lumps. Fingerspell SIFT
23 Whisk /WISK/ Whisk the eggs until they are fluffy. Fingerspell WHISK
24 Grease /GREES/ Grease the pan so the cake doesn't stick. Fingerspell GREASE
25 Batch /BACH/ The first batch of cookies is ready! Fingerspell BATCH
26 Timer /TY-mer/ Set the timer so the bread doesn't burn. T-hand taps wrist
27 Degrees /dih-GREEZ/ Bake at three hundred fifty degrees Fahrenheit. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Measuring cup /MEZH-er-ing KUP/ Use a measuring cup for liquid ingredients. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Measuring spoon /MEZH-er-ing SPOON/ The measuring spoon set has four sizes. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Spatula /SPACH-uh-luh/ Use a spatula to scrape the bowl clean. Fingerspell or sign each word
🧁

Octavia's Tip:

"Cooking IS math! When you MEASURE, you use fractions. When you DOUBLE a recipe, you multiply. When you HALVE it, you divide. Bake a cake and do math at the same time!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 19: Data & Statistics

In the Numeria Data Center, Kwame reads numbers that tell stories

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Data /DAY-tuh/ Collect data by counting and measuring. D-hand moves along opposite palm (reading data)
2 Survey /SUR-vay/ Take a survey to find opinions. Fingerspell SURVEY
3 Result /rih-ZULT/ The results were surprising. Fingerspell RESULT
4 Percentage /per-SEN-tij/ Eighty percent of students passed. Draw percent sign in air (O / O)
5 Increase /in-KREES/ Temperature increased today. H-hand rises up opposite index
6 Decrease /dee-KREES/ Rainfall decreased this month. H-hand drops down opposite index
7 Average /AV-er-ij/ The average score was eighty-five. Flat hand levels across opposite hand
8 Rank /RANK/ Our school ranks third. Fingerspell RANK
9 Score /SKOR/ What was your math score? Both hands tally in air
10 Total /TOH-tul/ Total number of students is two hundred. Fingerspell TOTAL
11 Half /HAF/ Half of the class speaks Spanish. Fingerspell HALF
12 Double /DUB-ul/ Double the recipe for more people. Fingerspell DOUBLE
13 Triple /TRIP-ul/ Sales tripled during the holidays. Fingerspell TRIPLE
14 Majority /muh-JOR-ih-tee/ The majority voted yes. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Minority /my-NOR-ih-tee/ A minority chose option B. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Frequent /FREE-kwent/ Rain is frequent in April. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Rare /RAIR/ Snow in Florida is very rare. Fingerspell RARE
18 Compare /kum-PAIR/ Compare last month to this month. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Predict /prih-DIKT/ Can you predict tomorrow's weather? Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Trend /TREND/ The trend shows sales going up. Fingerspell TREND
21 Chart /CHART/ Draw a bar chart to show results. Index draws chart lines on opposite palm
22 Graph /GRAF/ The line graph shows temperature. 4-hand moves along opposite palm stepping up
23 Table /TAY-bul/ Organize data in a table. Both forearms stack, form flat surface
24 Row /ROH/ Each row shows one student. Fingerspell ROW
25 Column /KOL-um/ The first column shows the names. Fingerspell COLUMN
G

Kwame's Tip:

"Numbers tell STORIES! What INCREASED? What DECREASED? You are a DATA detective!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 16: Distance & Speed

At the Numeria Racetrack, Octavia calculates how fast and how far

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Speed /SPEED/ The speed limit is thirty-five miles per hour. Both S-hands flick to 5-hands quickly
2 Distance /DIS-tents/ The distance from home to school is two miles. A-hand pushes forward from opposite A-hand
3 Far /FAR/ How far is the nearest gas station? Fingerspell FAR
4 Near /NEER/ The park is near our house. Fingerspell NEAR
5 Close /KLOHS/ Please sit close to the front. B-hands apart, swing together
6 Fast /FAST/ The train moves very fast. L-hands pull back quickly (snapping)
7 Slow /SLOH/ Drive slow in the school zone. Hand drags slowly up opposite forearm
8 Rate /RAYT/ The rate of speed increased. Fingerspell RATE
9 Per /PUR/ The car travels sixty miles per hour. Fingerspell PER
10 Direction /dih-REK-shun/ Turn left at the next direction sign. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Route /ROOT/ Take the shortest route to the airport. Fingerspell ROUTE
12 Map /MAP/ Follow the map to find the treasure. M-hands trace on flat surface
13 GPS /JEE-PEE-ES/ GPS says we arrive in twenty minutes. Fingerspell GPS
14 Navigate /NAV-ih-gayt/ Navigate to the restaurant using your phone. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Step /STEP/ I walked ten thousand steps today. Fingerspell STEP
16 Lap /LAP/ She ran four laps around the track. Fingerspell LAP
17 Marathon /MAIR-uh-thon/ A marathon is twenty-six miles. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Accelerate /ak-SEL-er-ayt/ The car accelerates quickly. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Brake /BRAYK/ Press the brake to slow down. Fingerspell BRAKE
20 Timer /TY-mer/ Set the timer for ten minutes. T-hand taps wrist
21 Stopwatch /STOP-wotch/ Use the stopwatch to time the race. Both S-hands: one taps other (START + WATCH)
22 Record /REK-ord/ She broke the school record. Fingerspell RECORD
23 Average /AV-er-ij/ Average speed was forty-five mph. Flat hand levels across opposite hand
24 Maximum /MAK-sih-mum/ Maximum speed on the highway is sixty-five. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Minimum /MIN-ih-mum/ The minimum age to drive is sixteen. Fingerspell or sign each word
F

Octavia's Tip:

"How FAST can you say these words? Time yourself with a STOPWATCH!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 13: Estimation & Comparison

At the Numeria Guessing Fair, Kwame and Octavia estimate, compare, and check their answers.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Estimate /ES-tih-mayt/ Estimate how many beans are in the jar. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 About /uh-BOWT/ There are about fifty people in the room. Fingerspell ABOUT
3 Approximately /uh-PROK-sih-mut-lee/ The trip takes approximately two hours. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Round /ROWND/ Round 47 to the nearest ten — that's 50. Fingerspell ROUND
5 Nearest /NEER-est/ Round to the nearest hundred. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 More than /MOR THAN/ Fifty is more than thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Less than /LES THAN/ Twelve is less than twenty. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Equal to /EE-kwul TOO/ Ten dimes is equal to one dollar. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Between /bih-TWEEN/ The answer is between twenty and thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Close to /KLOHS TOO/ My estimate was close to the real answer! Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Exactly /eg-ZAKT-lee/ There are exactly forty-two marbles. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Enough /ih-NUF/ Do we have enough money to buy the book? Fingerspell ENOUGH
13 Too many /TOO MEN-ee/ There are too many apples for one bag. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Too few /TOO FYOO/ We have too few chairs for all the guests. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Most /MOHST/ Blue got the most votes in the survey. Fingerspell MOST
16 Least /LEEST/ Yellow got the least number of votes. Fingerspell LEAST
17 Same /SAYM/ Both cups hold the same amount of water. Y-hand: index fingers come together
18 Different /DIF-er-ent/ These two shapes are different sizes. Crossed index fingers pull apart
19 Bigger /BIG-er/ The watermelon is bigger than the orange. Fingerspell BIGGER
20 Smaller /SMAWL-er/ A grape is smaller than a plum. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Taller /TAWL-er/ The giraffe is taller than the horse. Fingerspell TALLER
22 Shorter /SHOR-ter/ The cat is shorter than the dog. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Heavier /HEV-ee-er/ The bowling ball is heavier than the tennis ball. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Lighter /LYT-er/ A feather is lighter than a book. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Reasonable /REE-zun-uh-bul/ Is your estimate reasonable? Check your work. Fingerspell or sign each word
🎯

Kwame's Tip:

"Before you solve a math problem, ESTIMATE the answer first! Then solve it exactly. Were you close? Estimation is a SUPERPOWER that checks your work!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 11: Fractions & Decimals

In the Numeria Splitting Station, Kwame and Octavia learn to divide things into equal parts.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Fraction /FRAK-shun/ A fraction shows a part of a whole. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Numerator /NOO-mer-ay-ter/ The numerator is the top number in a fraction. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Denominator /dih-NOM-ih-nay-ter/ The denominator is the bottom number in a fraction. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 One half /WUN HAF/ One half means one of two equal parts. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 One third /WUN THURD/ One third of a pie is a small slice. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 One quarter /WUN KWOR-ter/ One quarter is the same as one fourth. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Three quarters /THREE KWOR-terz/ Three quarters of the tank is almost full. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Whole /HOHL/ A whole means all the parts together. Fingerspell WHOLE
9 Part /PART/ Each part of the pie is the same size. Fingerspell PART
10 Equal parts /EE-kwul PARTS/ Divide the chocolate bar into equal parts. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Decimal /DES-ih-mul/ A decimal uses a dot to show values less than one. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Decimal point /DES-ih-mul POYNT/ Put the decimal point between dollars and cents. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Tenth /TENTH/ 0.1 is one tenth. Fingerspell TENTH
14 Hundredth /HUN-dredth/ 0.01 is one hundredth. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Percent /per-SENT/ Fifty percent means half of something. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Percentage /per-SEN-tij/ The percentage of students who passed is ninety. Draw percent sign in air (O / O)
17 Equivalent /ih-KWIV-uh-lent/ One half and two quarters are equivalent fractions. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Mixed number /MIKSD NUM-ber/ Two and a half is a mixed number. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Improper fraction /im-PROP-er FRAK-shun/ Five thirds is an improper fraction. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Reduce /rih-DOOS/ Reduce two fourths to one half. Fingerspell REDUCE
21 Simplify /SIM-plih-fy/ Simplify the fraction to its smallest form. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Convert /kun-VERT/ Convert the fraction to a decimal. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Ratio /RAY-shee-oh/ The ratio of boys to girls is three to two. Fingerspell RATIO
24 Proportion /proh-POR-shun/ A proportion shows two equal ratios. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Pie /PY/ Cut the pie into eight equal slices. Fingerspell PIE
🥧

Kwame's Tip:

"Fractions are REAL LIFE! When you share a pizza equally with a friend, each person gets ONE HALF. That's 1/2. Now share with three friends — each gets ONE QUARTER. That's 1/4!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 15: Measurement at Home

Kwame and Octavia measure everything in the Numeria Kitchen

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Tablespoon /TAY-bul-spoon/ Add one tablespoon of butter. Fingerspell T-B-S or sign BIG + SPOON
2 Teaspoon /TEE-spoon/ One teaspoon of salt is enough. Fingerspell T-S-P or sign SMALL + SPOON
3 Cup /KUP/ Use one cup of flour for the cake. C-hand taps opposite flat palm (cup on saucer)
4 Pint /PYNT/ A pint of milk equals two cups. Fingerspell PINT
5 Quart /KWORT/ A quart is four cups of liquid. Fingerspell QUART
6 Gallon /GAL-un/ Buy a gallon of water for camping. G-hand large volume shape
7 Ounce /OWNS/ The recipe calls for eight ounces. Fingerspell OUNCE
8 Pound /POWND/ Buy one pound of ground beef. P-hand drops firmly
9 Gram /GRAM/ One hundred grams of rice is one serving. Fingerspell GRAM
10 Kilogram /KIL-oh-gram/ A kilogram is more than two pounds. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Liter /LEE-ter/ Drink two liters of water daily. Fingerspell LITER
12 Milliliter /MIL-ih-lee-ter/ The bottle says twenty-five milliliters. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Inch /INCH/ The board is twelve inches long. Index and thumb pinch close together
14 Foot /FOOT/ The table is three feet tall. Point to foot
15 Yard /YARD/ The fabric costs five dollars per yard. Fingerspell YARD
16 Mile /MYL/ The school is two miles from home. M-hand moves forward
17 Centimeter /SEN-tih-mee-ter/ Measure the line in centimeters. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Meter /MEE-ter/ The pool is fifty meters long. Fingerspell METER
19 Kilometer /KIL-oh-mee-ter/ A kilometer is more than half a mile. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Temperature /TEM-per-uh-chur/ Check the temperature on the weather app. Index slides up/down opposite index (thermometer)
21 Fahrenheit /FAIR-en-hyt/ Water boils at two hundred twelve Fahrenheit. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Celsius /SEL-see-us/ Water boils at one hundred Celsius. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Ruler /ROO-ler/ Use a ruler to draw a straight line. R-hands outline ruler
24 Tape measure /TAYP MEZH-er/ A tape measure can measure curves. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Scale /SKAYL/ Step on the scale to see your weight. Both flat hands balance like scale
R

Kwame's Tip:

"Measurements are everywhere! CUP, TABLESPOON, INCH, POUND. Kitchen English IS real!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 3: Money & Currency

Kwame and Octavia visit the Numeria Treasury where they learn about coins, bills, and the value of every cent.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Money /MUN-ee/ Money is used to buy things you need. Flat O taps opposite palm twice
2 Dollar /DOL-er/ One dollar equals one hundred cents. Flat hand grasps and slides off opposite hand
3 Cent /SENT/ A cent is the smallest unit of money. Fingerspell CENT
4 Penny /PEN-ee/ A penny is worth one cent and is copper-colored. Fingerspell PENNY
5 Nickel /NIK-ul/ A nickel is worth five cents. Fingerspell NICKEL
6 Dime /DYM/ A dime is worth ten cents and is the smallest coin. Fingerspell DIME
7 Quarter /KWOR-ter/ A quarter is worth twenty-five cents. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Bill /BIL/ A one-dollar bill is green and paper. Fingerspell BILL
9 Five-dollar bill /FYV-DOL-er BIL/ Kwame has a five-dollar bill in his wallet. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Ten-dollar bill /TEN-DOL-er BIL/ The ten-dollar bill has a picture of Alexander Hamilton. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Twenty-dollar bill /TWEN-tee-DOL-er BIL/ Octavia pays with a twenty-dollar bill. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Change /CHAYNJ/ The cashier gives back change after you pay. A-fists switch positions (swap)
13 Wallet /WAL-it/ Keep your money safe in your wallet. Fingerspell WALLET
14 Purse /PURS/ Octavia keeps her wallet inside her purse. Fingerspell PURSE
15 Cash /KASH/ Some stores only accept cash, not cards. Fingerspell CASH
16 Credit card /KRED-it KARD/ A credit card lets you buy now and pay later. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Debit card /DEB-it KARD/ A debit card takes money directly from your bank. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Check /CHEK/ Write a check to pay the rent. Fingerspell CHECK
19 Coins /KOYNZ/ The coins jingle in Kwame's pocket. Index traces circle on opposite palm (repeated)
20 Price /PRYS/ The price tag shows how much something costs. F-hand strikes opposite palm downward
21 Cost /KOST/ How much does this book cost? X-hand slides down opposite palm
22 Expensive /ek-SPEN-siv/ The bicycle is too expensive at three hundred dollars. Flat O on palm, flick open going up
23 Cheap /CHEEP/ The notebook is cheap at only two dollars. B-hand curves down off opposite palm
24 Affordable /uh-FOR-duh-bul/ Find an affordable phone plan that fits your budget. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Budget /BUJ-it/ A budget helps you plan how to spend your money. B-hands on opposite palm (MONEY + PLAN)
26 Save /SAYV/ Save a little money each week for emergencies. Fingerspell SAVE
27 Spend /SPEND/ Don't spend more money than you earn. Fingerspell SPEND
28 Earn /URN/ Kwame earns money by helping in the garden. Fingerspell EARN
29 Borrow /BOR-oh/ Borrow money only when you know you can pay it back. Fingerspell BORROW
30 Owe /OH/ If you borrow ten dollars, you owe ten dollars. Fingerspell OWE
31 Tip /TIP/ A tip is extra money you give for good service. Fingerspell TIP
32 Receipt /rih-SEET/ Keep the receipt in case you need to return the item. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Tax /TAKS/ Sales tax is added to the price of most items. T-hand or X-hand taps opposite palm
34 Discount /DIS-kownt/ A discount lowers the original price. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Sale /SAYL/ Everything is twenty percent off during the sale. Fingerspell SALE
💰

Kwame's Tip:

"Let's practice! If you have ONE DOLLAR and you buy something that costs 75 CENTS, how much CHANGE do you get? Count: 25 cents. A QUARTER! You just did math AND money!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 1: Numbers & Counting

Kwame and Octavia stand before the great Number Wall in Numeria. Each number has a shape and a story.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 One /WUN/ One is the first number on the wall. Hold up index finger
2 Two /TOO/ Two socks make a pair. Hold up index + middle finger
3 Three /THREE/ A triangle has three sides. Hold up thumb + index + middle
4 Four /FOR/ A table has four legs. Hold up four fingers (no thumb)
5 Five /FYV/ Kwame holds up five fingers on one hand. Open 5-hand
6 Six /SIKS/ A cube has six flat sides. Fingerspell SIX
7 Seven /SEV-en/ There are seven days in a week. Fingerspell SEVEN
8 Eight /AYT/ An octopus has eight arms. Fingerspell EIGHT
9 Nine /NYN/ Nine is one less than ten. Fingerspell NINE
10 Ten /TEN/ Count to ten on your fingers. Thumb of A-hand shakes
11 Eleven /ih-LEV-en/ Eleven comes right after ten. Fingerspell ELEVEN
12 Twelve /TWELV/ There are twelve months in a year. Fingerspell TWELVE
13 Thirteen /thur-TEEN/ Thirteen is the first "teen" number. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Twenty /TWEN-tee/ Twenty is two groups of ten. Fingerspell TWENTY
15 Thirty /THUR-tee/ There are about thirty days in a month. Fingerspell THIRTY
16 Forty /FOR-tee/ Kwame counted forty stars in the sky. Fingerspell FORTY
17 Fifty /FIF-tee/ Fifty cents equals half a dollar. Fingerspell FIFTY
18 Hundred /HUN-dred/ One hundred pennies make one dollar. Sign 1 + C (one-C)
19 Thousand /THOW-zund/ A thousand is ten groups of one hundred. Fingertips of M-hand tap opposite palm
20 Million /MIL-yun/ There are millions of stars in the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 First /FURST/ Kwame was the first to finish the puzzle. Fingerspell FIRST
22 Second /SEK-und/ Octavia was the second person in line. Index finger flicks behind opposite palm
23 Third /THURD/ The third floor is above the second floor. Fingerspell THIRD
24 Zero /ZEE-roh/ Zero means nothing, or the starting point. O-hand
25 Number /NUM-ber/ Write the number under each picture. Both flat O-hands twist together
26 Count /KOWNT/ Count the apples in the basket. Thumb and index slide up opposite palm
27 Pair /PAIR/ A pair means two of the same thing. Fingerspell PAIR
28 Dozen /DUZ-en/ A dozen means twelve. Buy a dozen eggs. Fingerspell DOZEN
29 Half /HAF/ Cut the pizza in half to share equally. Fingerspell HALF
30 Double /DUB-ul/ Double three is six. Fingerspell DOUBLE
31 Even /EE-ven/ Two, four, six, and eight are even numbers. Fingerspell EVEN
32 Odd /OD/ One, three, five, and seven are odd numbers. Fingerspell ODD
33 Digit /DIJ-it/ Each number from zero to nine is a single digit. Fingerspell DIGIT
34 Numeral /NOO-mer-ul/ A numeral is a symbol that represents a number. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Sequence /SEE-kwens/ A sequence is numbers in a specific order. Fingerspell or sign each word
🔢

Kwame's Tip:

"Let's count together! Clap your hands for every number: ONE (clap), TWO (clap), THREE (clap)... up to TEN. Rhythm plus counting equals POWER!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 12: Patterns & Sequences

The Numeria Pattern Wall is covered in beautiful designs. Kwame and Octavia decode the hidden rules.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Pattern /PAT-ern/ A pattern repeats in a predictable way. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Repeat /rih-PEET/ The pattern of colors repeats: red, blue, red, blue. Fingerspell REPEAT
3 Sequence /SEE-kwens/ A sequence has numbers or objects in a specific order. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Next /NEKST/ What comes next in the pattern: 2, 4, 6, ___? Fingerspell NEXT
5 Predict /prih-DIKT/ Predict the next shape in the sequence. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Sort /SORT/ Sort the buttons by size from smallest to largest. Fingerspell SORT
7 Classify /KLAS-ih-fy/ Classify the shapes into groups. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Arrange /uh-RAYNJ/ Arrange the numbers in order from least to greatest. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Organize /OR-guh-nyz/ Organize your data before making a chart. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Category /KAT-uh-gor-ee/ Put each animal into the correct category. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Rule /ROOL/ The rule for this pattern is "add three." Fingerspell RULE
12 Skip count /SKIP KOWNT/ Skip count by fives: 5, 10, 15, 20. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Growing pattern /GROH-ing PAT-ern/ A growing pattern gets bigger each time. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Shrinking pattern /SHRINK-ing PAT-ern/ A shrinking pattern gets smaller each time. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Symmetry /SIM-eh-tree/ This design has symmetry — both sides match. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Repeating /rih-PEE-ting/ The repeating pattern goes: star, moon, star, moon. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Core /KOR/ The core of the pattern is the part that repeats. Fingerspell CORE
18 Extend /ek-STEND/ Extend the pattern by adding more shapes. Fingerspell EXTEND
19 Function /FUNK-shun/ A function is a rule that changes one number to another. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Input /IN-poot/ The input goes into the machine. Fingerspell INPUT
21 Output /OWT-poot/ The output comes out after the rule is applied. Fingerspell OUTPUT
22 Table /TAY-bul/ Use a table to show the input and output. Both forearms stack, form flat surface
23 Variable /VAIR-ee-uh-bul/ A variable is a letter that stands for an unknown number. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Missing number /MIS-ing NUM-ber/ Find the missing number: 3 + ___ = 10. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Algebra /AL-juh-bruh/ Algebra uses letters and numbers to solve equations. Fingerspell or sign each word
🔄

Octavia's Tip:

"Patterns are MUSIC! Clap a rhythm: TAP-TAP-CLAP, TAP-TAP-CLAP. That's a pattern! Now do it with numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8 — the pattern adds TWO each time!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 10: Problem Solving & Logic

In the Numeria Puzzle Room, Kwame and Octavia work through puzzles that test reasoning, patterns, and logic.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Problem /PROB-lem/ Read the problem carefully before you solve it. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Solution /soh-LOO-shun/ Every problem has at least one solution. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Strategy /STRAT-uh-jee/ A strategy is a plan for solving a problem. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Step /STEP/ Solve the problem one step at a time. Fingerspell STEP
5 Clue /KLOO/ The clue in the sentence helps find the answer. Fingerspell CLUE
6 Pattern /PAT-ern/ Find the pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8 — what comes next? Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Sort /SORT/ Sort the shapes by color and size. Fingerspell SORT
8 Group /GROOP/ Group the animals that live in water. Fingerspell GROUP
9 Order /OR-der/ Put the numbers in order from smallest to largest. Fingerspell ORDER
10 Classify /KLAS-ih-fy/ Classify the objects as living or nonliving. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Compare /kum-PAIR/ Compare the two circles — which is bigger? Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Contrast /kun-TRAST/ Contrast the summer and winter temperatures. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Cause /KAWZ/ The cause of the flood was heavy rain. Fingerspell CAUSE
14 Effect /ih-FEKT/ The effect of the sun is warmth and light. Fingerspell EFFECT
15 Puzzle /PUZ-ul/ Kwame enjoys solving a difficult puzzle. Fingerspell PUZZLE
16 Reason /REE-zun/ Give a reason for your answer. Fingerspell REASON
17 Evidence /EV-ih-dents/ Show evidence from the text to support your answer. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Conclusion /kun-KLOO-zhun/ The conclusion summarizes what you learned. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Hypothesis /hy-POTH-ih-sis/ A hypothesis is your best guess before testing. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Experiment /ek-SPER-ih-ment/ An experiment tests whether your hypothesis is correct. Fingerspell or sign each word
🧩

Octavia's Tip:

"thinking like a mathematician means asking: What do I know? What do I need to find? What's my STRATEGY? These three questions can solve ANY problem — in math AND in life!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 9: Shapes & Geometry

The Numeria Shape Garden features every geometric form — paths, sculptures, and fountains in perfect proportions.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Circle /SUR-kul/ A circle is perfectly round with no corners. Fingerspell CIRCLE
2 Square /SKWAIR/ A square has four equal sides and four right angles. Fingerspell SQUARE
3 Rectangle /REK-tang-gul/ A rectangle is like a stretched-out square. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Triangle /TRY-ang-gul/ A triangle has three sides and three corners. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Oval /OH-vul/ An oval is shaped like an egg. Fingerspell OVAL
6 Diamond /DY-mund/ A diamond shape has four sides tilted on a point. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Star /STAR/ Kwame draws a five-pointed star. Both index fingers brush up alternately
8 Heart /HART/ A heart shape is a symbol of love. Middle finger taps left chest
9 Pentagon /PEN-tuh-gon/ A pentagon has five sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Hexagon /HEK-suh-gon/ A hexagon has six sides like a honeycomb cell. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Octagon /OK-tuh-gon/ A stop sign is an octagon with eight sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Sphere /SFEER/ A ball is a three-dimensional sphere. Fingerspell SPHERE
13 Cube /KYOOB/ A cube has six flat square faces. Fingerspell CUBE
14 Cylinder /SIL-in-der/ A can of soup is shaped like a cylinder. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Cone /KOHN/ An ice cream cone narrows to a point. Fingerspell CONE
16 Pyramid /PEER-uh-mid/ A pyramid has a flat base and triangular sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Angle /ANG-gul/ An angle is formed where two lines meet. Fingerspell ANGLE
18 Right angle /RYT ANG-gul/ A right angle is exactly 90 degrees. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Line /LYN/ A straight line goes on forever in both directions. Fingerspell LINE
20 Point /POYNT/ A point is a single exact location. Fingerspell POINT
21 Parallel /PAIR-uh-lel/ Parallel lines run side by side and never touch. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Perpendicular /pur-pen-DIK-yoo-ler/ Perpendicular lines meet at a right angle. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Symmetry /SIM-eh-tree/ A butterfly has perfect symmetry — both sides match. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Radius /RAY-dee-us/ The radius is the distance from the center to the edge of a circle. Fingerspell RADIUS
25 Diameter /dy-AM-ih-ter/ The diameter goes all the way across the circle. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Circumference /sur-KUM-fer-ens/ The circumference is the distance around the circle. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Side /SYD/ A triangle has three sides. Fingerspell SIDE
28 Corner /KOR-ner/ A square has four corners. Fingerspell CORNER
29 Face /FAYS/ A cube has six flat faces. Index finger circles face
30 Edge /EJ/ A cube has twelve edges. Fingerspell EDGE
📐

Kwame's Tip:

"Look around — shapes are EVERYWHERE! The window is a RECTANGLE. The clock is a CIRCLE. The yield sign is a TRIANGLE. Name every shape you see — that's geometry in action!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 4: The Bank

Kwame and Octavia visit the Numeria Community Bank to learn about saving and banking.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Bank /BANGK/ A bank is a safe place to keep your money. B-hand taps opposite palm repeatedly (stacking money)
2 Account /uh-KOWNT/ Open an account at the bank to save money. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Savings account /SAY-vingz uh-KOWNT/ A savings account helps your money grow. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Checking account /CHEK-ing uh-KOWNT/ Use a checking account to pay bills. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Deposit /dih-POZ-it/ Deposit your paycheck into your bank account. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Withdraw /with-DRAW/ Withdraw cash from the ATM when you need it. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 ATM /AY-TEE-EM/ The ATM machine gives cash 24 hours a day. Fingerspell ATM
8 Balance /BAL-uns/ Your balance is how much money is in your account. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Interest /IN-ter-est/ Interest is money the bank pays you for saving. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Loan /LOHN/ A loan is money you borrow and pay back over time. Fingerspell LOAN
11 Payment /PAY-ment/ Make your car payment on time each month. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Transfer /TRANS-fur/ Transfer money from savings to checking online. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Statement /STAYT-ment/ The bank statement shows all your transactions. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 PIN /PIN/ Enter your four-digit PIN to access your account. Fingerspell PIN
15 Direct deposit /dih-REKT dih-POZ-it/ Direct deposit puts your paycheck in the bank automatically. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Wire transfer /WYR TRANS-fur/ A wire transfer sends money to another bank quickly. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Online banking /on-LYN BANG-king/ Online banking lets you manage money from home. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Teller /TEL-er/ The bank teller helps customers at the counter. Fingerspell TELLER
19 Safe /SAYF/ The bank safe protects valuable items. S-hands cross at wrists, then pull apart
20 Mortgage /MOR-gij/ A mortgage is a loan to buy a house. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏦

Octavia's Tip:

"Knowing banking words protects you! Practice: 'I want to DEPOSIT money. I want to WITHDRAW money. What is my BALANCE?' These three sentences give you power at any bank."

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 5: The Grocery Store & Shopping

Kwame and Octavia push a cart through the Numeria Supermarket, buying everything on their list.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Store /STOR/ The store opens at eight in the morning. Both flat O-hands at chest flick outward (SELL)
2 Supermarket /SOO-per-mar-kit/ The supermarket has aisles of food and supplies. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Aisle /EYL/ The cereal is in aisle three. Fingerspell AISLE
4 Cart /KART/ Push the shopping cart down the aisle. Fingerspell CART
5 Basket /BAS-kit/ Use a basket if you only need a few things. Fingerspell BASKET
6 Cashier /ka-SHEER/ The cashier scans each item at the register. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Register /REJ-ih-ster/ Pay at the register when you are finished shopping. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Receipt /rih-SEET/ The receipt lists every item you purchased. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Coupon /KOO-pon/ Use the coupon to get one dollar off. Fingerspell COUPON
10 Shopping list /SHOP-ing LIST/ Write a shopping list before you go to the store. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Bag /BAG/ The cashier puts the groceries in a bag. Fingerspell BAG
12 Brand /BRAND/ Choose the store brand to save money. Fingerspell BRAND
13 Label /LAY-bul/ Read the label to check the ingredients. Fingerspell LABEL
14 Expiration date /ek-spuh-RAY-shun DAYT/ The expiration date tells you when food is no longer safe. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Organic /or-GAN-ik/ Organic food is grown without chemicals. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Frozen /FROH-zen/ Frozen vegetables are in the freezer section. Fingerspell FROZEN
17 Dairy /DAIR-ee/ Milk, cheese, and yogurt are in the dairy section. Fingerspell DAIRY
18 Bakery /BAY-ker-ee/ Fresh bread comes from the supermarket bakery. Fingerspell BAKERY
19 Deli /DEL-ee/ Order sliced turkey at the deli counter. Fingerspell DELI
20 Produce /PROH-doos/ Fresh fruits and vegetables are in the produce section. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Checkout /CHEK-owt/ Go to the checkout line when you're ready to pay. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Self-checkout /SELF-CHEK-owt/ The self-checkout lets you scan items yourself. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Return /rih-TURN/ Return an item if it is damaged or wrong. Fingerspell RETURN
24 Exchange /eks-CHAYNJ/ Exchange the shirt for a different size. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Refund /REE-fund/ Ask for a refund if the product doesn't work. Fingerspell REFUND
26 Sale /SAYL/ The sale sign says buy one, get one free. Fingerspell SALE
27 Price tag /PRYS TAG/ Check the price tag before putting it in the cart. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Customer /KUS-tuh-mer/ The customer asks the cashier a question. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Employee /em-PLOY-ee/ The store employee stocks the shelves. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Manager /MAN-uh-jer/ Ask for the manager if you have a complaint. Fingerspell or sign each word
🛒

Kwame's Tip:

"Before your next trip to the store, practice these words: AISLE, CASHIER, RECEIPT, COUPON, CHECKOUT. You will feel confident navigating any supermarket!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 7: Time & Clocks

The Numeria Clock Tower chimes every hour. Kwame and Octavia teach visitors to read the time.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Clock /KLOK/ The clock on the tower has two hands. Point to wrist + mime clock hands
2 Watch /WOCH/ Kwame wears a watch on his left wrist. Point to wrist (watch)
3 Hour /OW-er/ One hour equals sixty minutes. Index circles on opposite flat palm (clock hand)
4 Minute /MIN-it/ One minute equals sixty seconds. Index finger ticks on opposite palm
5 Second /SEK-und/ A second is the smallest common unit of time. Index finger flicks behind opposite palm
6 Noon /NOON/ Noon is twelve o'clock in the middle of the day. Forearm straight up on opposite forearm
7 Midnight /MID-nyt/ Midnight is twelve o'clock at night. Hand drops below opposite forearm (sun below)
8 A.M. /AY-EM/ A.M. means before noon — morning time. Fingerspell A.M.
9 P.M. /PEE-EM/ P.M. means after noon — afternoon and evening. Fingerspell P.M.
10 O'clock /oh-KLOK/ School starts at eight o'clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Half past /HAF PAST/ It's half past three — time for a snack! Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Quarter past /KWOR-ter PAST/ It's quarter past nine — class started fifteen minutes ago. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Quarter to /KWOR-ter TOO/ It's quarter to five — almost dinner time. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Hour hand /OW-er HAND/ The short hour hand points to the hour. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Minute hand /MIN-it HAND/ The long minute hand counts the minutes. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Alarm /uh-LARM/ Set the alarm for six thirty in the morning. Index taps opposite palm rapidly (ringing)
17 Timer /TY-mer/ Set the timer for ten minutes. T-hand taps wrist
18 Stopwatch /STOP-woch/ Use the stopwatch to time the race. Both S-hands: one taps other (START + WATCH)
19 Schedule /SKED-yool/ Check the schedule for the bus arrival time. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
20 On time /ON TYM/ Kwame is always on time for class. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Late /LAYT/ Octavia was late because the bus was delayed. Hand waves downward beside hip
22 Early /UR-lee/ Arriving early gives you time to prepare. Middle finger slides over fist knuckle
23 Duration /doo-RAY-shun/ The duration of the movie is two hours. D-hand slides along opposite forearm (length of time)
24 Sunrise /SUN-ryz/ Sunrise is when the sun comes up in the morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Sunset /SUN-set/ Sunset paints the sky orange and pink. Fingerspell SUNSET
⏰

Kwame's Tip:

"Find a clock right now. What time is it? Say it in English: 'It is three fifteen.' Now say it another way: 'It is quarter past three.' Two ways to say the same time!"

Land 2 of 7

🔢 Numeria

Guided by Kwame the Pattern Master & Octavia the Data Dancer

Scene 6: Weights & Measures

In the Numeria Measurement Lab, everything is weighed, measured, and compared with precision.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Inch /INCH/ One inch is about the width of your thumb. Index and thumb pinch close together
2 Foot /FOOT/ One foot equals twelve inches. Point to foot
3 Yard /YARD/ One yard equals three feet. Fingerspell YARD
4 Mile /MYL/ The school is one mile from Kwame's house. M-hand moves forward
5 Centimeter /SEN-tih-mee-ter/ A centimeter is a very small unit of length. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Meter /MEE-ter/ A meter is a little longer than a yard. Fingerspell METER
7 Kilometer /KIL-oh-mee-ter/ A kilometer is a thousand meters. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Ounce /OWNS/ An ounce is a small unit of weight. Fingerspell OUNCE
9 Pound /POWND/ One pound equals sixteen ounces. P-hand drops firmly
10 Ton /TUN/ An elephant weighs several tons. Fingerspell TON
11 Gram /GRAM/ A gram is a metric unit of weight. Fingerspell GRAM
12 Kilogram /KIL-oh-gram/ A kilogram equals about 2.2 pounds. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Cup /KUP/ Use one cup of flour for the recipe. C-hand taps opposite flat palm (cup on saucer)
14 Pint /PYNT/ A pint of ice cream is a small container. Fingerspell PINT
15 Quart /KWORT/ A quart of milk is a common size. Fingerspell QUART
16 Gallon /GAL-un/ A gallon is the biggest jug of milk. G-hand large volume shape
17 Liter /LEE-ter/ A liter is a metric unit of liquid. Fingerspell LITER
18 Tablespoon /TAY-bul-spoon/ A tablespoon holds three teaspoons. Fingerspell T-B-S or sign BIG + SPOON
19 Teaspoon /TEE-spoon/ Add one teaspoon of salt to the soup. Fingerspell T-S-P or sign SMALL + SPOON
20 Temperature /TEM-per-uh-chur/ Temperature is measured in degrees. Index slides up/down opposite index (thermometer)
21 Fahrenheit /FAIR-un-hyt/ Water boils at 212 degrees Fahrenheit. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Celsius /SEL-see-us/ Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Scale /SKAYL/ Step on the scale to measure your weight. Both flat hands balance like scale
24 Ruler /ROO-ler/ Use a ruler to measure length in inches. R-hands outline ruler
25 Tape measure /TAYP MEZH-er/ A tape measure is flexible and rolls up. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Length /LENGTH/ The length of the table is four feet. Fingerspell LENGTH
27 Width /WIDTH/ The width is how wide something is. Fingerspell WIDTH
28 Height /HYT/ Kwame's height is four feet and six inches. Fingerspell HEIGHT
29 Weight /WAYT/ The weight of the backpack is ten pounds. Fingerspell WEIGHT
30 Volume /VOL-yoom/ Volume measures how much space something fills. Fingerspell VOLUME
31 Distance /DIS-tuns/ The distance from home to school is two miles. A-hand pushes forward from opposite A-hand
32 Area /AIR-ee-uh/ The area of the room is one hundred square feet. Fingerspell AREA
33 Perimeter /per-IM-ih-ter/ The perimeter is the distance around the outside. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Speed /SPEED/ The speed limit is thirty miles per hour. Both S-hands flick to 5-hands quickly
35 Measure /MEZH-er/ Measure the room before buying new furniture. Both Y-hands tap together (SAME SIZE)
📏

Octavia's Tip:

"Grab a ruler! Measure your hand. How many INCHES long is it? Now measure your shoe. How many CENTIMETERS? You just did real math with real tools!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 19: Camping & Hiking

Silas and Vesta gear up for wilderness adventure

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Tent /TENT/ Set up the tent before dark. Fingerspell TENT
2 Sleeping bag /SLEEP-ing BAG/ Zip up inside your sleeping bag to stay warm. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Campfire /KAMP-fyr/ Gather around the campfire and tell stories. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Flashlight /FLASH-lyt/ Shine the flashlight to see in the dark. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Backpack /BAK-pak/ Carry food and water in your backpack. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Trail /TRAYL/ Follow the trail through the woods. Fingerspell TRAIL
7 Hike /HYK/ We hiked five miles to the mountain top. Fingerspell HIKE
8 Compass /KUM-pus/ Use the compass to navigate the forest. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Binoculars /buh-NOK-yoo-lerz/ Look through the binoculars to see the eagle. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Firewood /FYR-wood/ Collect firewood for the campfire. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Marshmallow /MARSH-mal-oh/ Roast a marshmallow over the campfire. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Canoe /kuh-NOO/ Paddle the canoe across the calm lake. Fingerspell CANOE
13 Cliff /KLIF/ Be careful near the edge of the cliff. Fingerspell CLIFF
14 Cabin /KAB-in/ The log cabin sits at the forest edge. Fingerspell CABIN
15 Wildlife /WYLD-lyf/ We saw a lot of wildlife on the trail. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Map /MAP/ Study the map before choosing your trail. M-hands trace on flat surface
17 Boots /BOOTS/ Wear sturdy boots on the rocky trail. B-hands stamp alternately
18 Insect repellent /IN-sekt rih-PEL-ent/ Spray insect repellent to keep mosquitoes away. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 First aid kit /FURST AYD KIT/ Always bring a first aid kit camping. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Constellation /kon-stuh-LAY-shun/ Find the Big Dipper in the night sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Bear /BAIR/ Store food properly so bears cannot find it. Crossed arms scratch chest
22 Deer /DEER/ A deer quietly crossed the trail. Open 5-hands at temples (antlers)
23 Owl /OWL/ The owl hoots in the dark forest. Fingerspell OWL
24 Nature /NAY-chur/ Nature is the greatest teacher of all. N-hand circles then settles (NATURAL)
25 Wilderness /WIL-der-nes/ The wilderness is untouched natural land. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏕

Silas's Tip:

"Camping is the best vocabulary classroom! TENT, TRAIL, CAMPFIRE!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 17: Desert & Dry Lands

Silas treks into the Terrasol Desert

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Desert /DEZ-ert/ The desert is hot and dry with very little rain. Both flat hands push outward (dry, vast)
2 Cactus /KAK-tus/ The cactus stores water inside its stem. Fingerspell CACTUS
3 Sand dune /SAND DOON/ Wind shapes the sand dune into hills. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Oasis /oh-AY-sis/ An oasis is a green spot with water. Fingerspell OASIS
5 Drought /DROWT/ A drought means no rain for a long time. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Scorpion /SKOR-pee-un/ Watch out for the scorpion under the rock. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Lizard /LIZ-erd/ The lizard basks in the sun on a rock. Fingerspell LIZARD
8 Rattlesnake /RAT-ul-snayk/ The rattlesnake shakes its tail as warning. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Vulture /VUL-chur/ The vulture circles high in the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Tumbleweed /TUM-bul-weed/ A dry tumbleweed rolls across the road. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Canyon /KAN-yun/ The Grand Canyon is a mile deep. Fingerspell CANYON
12 Mesa /MAY-suh/ A mesa is a flat-topped desert hill. Fingerspell MESA
13 Mirage /mih-RAHJ/ A mirage looks like water but is hot air. Fingerspell MIRAGE
14 Arid /AIR-id/ Arid land has very little rainfall. Fingerspell ARID
15 Shade /SHAYD/ Rest in the shade to escape the sun. Fingerspell SHADE
16 Canteen /kan-TEEN/ Fill your canteen with water before hiking. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Compass /KUM-pus/ Use a compass to find north. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Fossil /FOS-ul/ Scientists found a dinosaur fossil. Fingerspell FOSSIL
19 Erosion /ee-ROH-zhun/ Wind erosion carved the rock. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Sandstorm /SAND-storm/ A sandstorm fills the air with sand. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Sunrise /SUN-ryz/ The desert sunrise paints the sky orange. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Sunset /SUN-set/ The sunset over the desert is breathtaking. Fingerspell SUNSET
23 Stars /STARZ/ You can see thousands of stars in the desert. Both index fingers brush up alternately (repeated)
24 Heat /HEET/ Desert heat can reach over one hundred degrees. Fingerspell HEAT
25 Survive /sur-VYV/ Desert animals survive with very little water. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏜

Silas's Tip:

"The desert teaches survival! DROUGHT, SHADE, CANTEEN, SURVIVE."

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 10: Farm to Table

In Terrasol, everyone knows where their food comes from. Silas and Vesta tend their crops through every season.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Plow /PLOW/ The farmer uses a plow to turn the soil before planting. Fingerspell PLOW
2 Plant /PLANT/ Vesta plants the seeds in neat, straight rows. P-hand pushes up through opposite C-hand
3 Harvest /HAR-vest/ The harvest happens in the fall when the crops are ripe. Curved hand mimes gathering crops
4 Irrigation /ir-ih-GAY-shun/ Irrigation brings water from the river to the fields. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Orchard /OR-cherd/ Apple and pear trees fill the sunny orchard. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Grain /GRAYN/ Golden grain waves in the wind. Fingerspell GRAIN
7 Wheat /WEET/ Wheat is ground into flour to make bread. Fingerspell WHEAT
8 Corn /KORN/ Tall corn stalks grow in rows as high as Silas's head. Fingerspell CORN
9 Rice /RYS/ Rice paddies need lots of water to grow. Both cupped hands scoop up
10 Cotton /KOT-un/ Soft white cotton is picked from the plant. Fingerspell COTTON
11 Soybean /SOY-been/ Soybeans are used to make tofu and soy sauce. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Crop /KROP/ This year's corn crop is the best in ten years. Fingerspell CROP
13 Field /FEELD/ The wide green field stretches to the horizon. F-hand circles outward (open area)
14 Barn /BARN/ The harvested grain is stored in the barn. Fingerspell BARN
15 Sickle /SIK-ul/ In old times, farmers cut wheat with a curved sickle. Fingerspell SICKLE
16 Scythe /SYTH/ The long scythe cuts tall grass with each swing. Fingerspell SCYTHE
17 Basket /BAS-kit/ Vesta fills a woven basket with ripe tomatoes. Fingerspell BASKET
18 Crate /KRAYT/ Wooden crates of vegetables are loaded onto the truck. Fingerspell CRATE
19 Market /MAR-kit/ Silas and Vesta sell their produce at the village market. Both flat O-hands flick out from body
20 Season /SEE-zun/ Each season brings different crops to the farm. Fingerspell SEASON
21 Spring planting /SPRING PLAN-ting/ Spring planting begins when the ground thaws. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Summer growing /SUM-er GROH-ing/ Summer growing means long days of sunshine and water. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Fall harvest /FAWL HAR-vest/ The fall harvest is a celebration of hard work. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Winter rest /WIN-ter REST/ Winter rest lets the soil recover for the next year. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Organic /or-GAN-ik/ Organic farming uses no chemicals on the crops. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Compost /KOM-pohst/ Food scraps become compost to feed the soil. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Drought /DROWT/ A drought means no rain for a very long time. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Fertile /FUR-tul/ The dark, fertile soil is perfect for growing vegetables. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Livestock /LYV-stok/ Cows, pigs, and chickens are all livestock. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Beekeeper /BEE-keep-er/ The beekeeper collects sweet honey from the hives. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Pollination /pol-ih-NAY-shun/ Bees help with pollination so plants can make fruit. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Sprinkler /SPRING-kler/ The sprinkler waters the garden in a spinning circle. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Tiller /TIL-er/ The tiller breaks up the hard soil before planting. Fingerspell TILLER
34 Sow /SOH/ In spring, farmers sow seeds in the prepared fields. Fingerspell SOW
35 Reap /REEP/ To reap means to cut and gather the ripe crop. Fingerspell REAP
🌾

Vesta's Tip:

"Where does YOUR food come from? Trace the journey: the farm → the truck → the store → your kitchen → your plate. Every meal is a story!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 12: Fruits & Vegetables

Silas and Vesta visit the Terrasol Market stand overflowing with fresh produce from their garden.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Apple /AP-ul/ The red apple is crisp and sweet. X-knuckle twists on cheek
2 Banana /buh-NAN-uh/ Peel the yellow banana before eating it. Mime peeling banana from index finger
3 Orange /OR-inj/ Squeeze the orange to make fresh juice. C-hand squeezes near chin (like squeezing orange)
4 Grape /GRAYP/ A bunch of purple grapes hangs from the vine. Fingerspell GRAPE
5 Strawberry /STRAW-bair-ee/ The strawberry is small, red, and sweet. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Watermelon /WAW-ter-mel-un/ A slice of watermelon is perfect on a hot day. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Peach /PEECH/ The fuzzy peach is soft and juicy. Fingerspell PEACH
8 Pear /PAIR/ The green pear grows in the orchard. Fingerspell PEAR
9 Lemon /LEM-un/ A lemon is sour and yellow. Fingerspell LEMON
10 Tomato /tuh-MAY-toh/ Is a tomato a fruit or a vegetable? Fingerspell TOMATO
11 Carrot /KAIR-ut/ The orange carrot grows underground. Fingerspell CARROT
12 Potato /puh-TAY-toh/ Bake the potato in the oven for one hour. Fingerspell POTATO
13 Onion /UN-yun/ Chopping an onion can make your eyes water. Fingerspell ONION
14 Broccoli /BROK-uh-lee/ Broccoli looks like a tiny green tree. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Lettuce /LET-is/ Lettuce is the base of a good salad. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Cucumber /KYOO-kum-ber/ Cool cucumber slices are refreshing in summer. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Corn /KORN/ Corn on the cob is a summer favorite. Fingerspell CORN
18 Pepper /PEP-er/ Red and green peppers add color to the salad. Mime grinding pepper mill
19 Bean /BEEN/ Green beans grow on a vine in the garden. Fingerspell BEAN
20 Pea /PEE/ Pop open the pod to find tiny green peas inside. Fingerspell PEA
21 Spinach /SPIN-ich/ Spinach is a dark green leafy vegetable. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Celery /SEL-er-ee/ Celery is crunchy and good with peanut butter. Fingerspell CELERY
23 Pumpkin /PUMP-kin/ The big orange pumpkin sits in the fall garden. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Mushroom /MUSH-room/ Mushrooms grow in dark, damp places. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Garlic /GAR-lik/ Garlic adds strong flavor to any dish. Fingerspell GARLIC
26 Avocado /av-uh-KAH-doh/ An avocado is green inside with a big seed. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Mango /MANG-oh/ The tropical mango is sweet and orange. Fingerspell MANGO
28 Pineapple /PYN-ap-ul/ The prickly pineapple is sweet inside. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Cherry /CHAIR-ee/ Pick a red cherry from the branch. Fingerspell CHERRY
30 Blueberry /BLOO-bair-ee/ Tiny blueberries burst with sweet flavor. Fingerspell or sign each word
🍎

Vesta's Tip:

"Open the refrigerator! How many fruits and vegetables can you name in English? Point and say: 'That is a CARROT. That is a TOMATO.' You are learning new words every time you eat!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 11: Insects & Bugs

Under every leaf and stone, tiny creatures go about their busy lives. Vesta has a magnifying glass to study them all.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Insect /IN-sekt/ An insect has six legs and three body parts. Fingerspell INSECT
2 Ant /ANT/ The ant carries a crumb ten times its own weight. Fingerspell ANT
3 Bee /BEE/ The bee buzzes from flower to flower collecting pollen. Fingerspell BEE
4 Butterfly /BUT-er-fly/ The colorful butterfly was once a caterpillar. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Caterpillar /KAT-er-pil-er/ The caterpillar eats leaves before spinning a cocoon. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Ladybug /LAY-dee-bug/ The red ladybug has small black spots. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Dragonfly /DRAG-un-fly/ The dragonfly hovers over the pond. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Grasshopper /GRAS-hop-er/ The grasshopper jumps through the tall grass. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Cricket /KRIK-it/ The cricket chirps loudly on summer nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Spider /SPY-der/ The spider is not an insect — it has eight legs. Fingerspell SPIDER
11 Web /WEB/ The spider's web sparkles with morning dew. Fingerspell WEB
12 Mosquito /mus-KEE-toh/ The mosquito buzzes near your ear at night. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Fly /FLY/ A fly landed on the picnic table. Y-hand with index, soar through air
14 Beetle /BEE-tul/ The shiny beetle crawls along the branch. Fingerspell BEETLE
15 Worm /WURM/ The earthworm helps make the soil healthy. Fingerspell WORM
16 Cocoon /kuh-KOON/ Inside the cocoon, the caterpillar transforms. Fingerspell COCOON
17 Hive /HYV/ Thousands of bees live together in a hive. Fingerspell HIVE
18 Honey /HUN-ee/ Sweet honey comes from the bees' hard work. Fingerspell HONEY
19 Colony /KOL-uh-nee/ The ant colony works together underground. Fingerspell COLONY
20 Pest /PEST/ A pest is an insect that damages crops. Fingerspell PEST
21 Pollinate /POL-ih-nayt/ Bees pollinate flowers so that fruit can grow. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Larvae /LAR-vee/ The larvae of the butterfly look like tiny worms. Fingerspell LARVAE
23 Antenna /an-TEN-uh/ The butterfly uses its antenna to sense the world. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Wing /WING/ Each wing of the butterfly is painted with color. Fingerspell WING
25 Magnifying glass /MAG-nih-fy-ing GLAS/ Use a magnifying glass to see the tiny details. Fingerspell or sign each word
🐛

Silas's Tip:

"Go outside and look under a rock or a leaf. What tiny creature do you find? Say its name in English. You are now a real nature scientist!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 8: Inside the Home

Inside Silas and Vesta's cottage, every room is cozy, clean, and filled with warm light.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Kitchen /KICH-en/ Vesta cooks soup in the warm kitchen. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Living room /LIV-ing ROOM/ The family reads together in the living room. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Bedroom /BED-room/ Silas sleeps in the quiet bedroom upstairs. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Bathroom /BATH-room/ The bathroom has a sink, toilet, and bathtub. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Dining room /DY-ning ROOM/ Meals are shared at the big table in the dining room. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Hallway /HAWL-way/ The hallway connects all the rooms in the cottage. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Stairs /STAIRZ/ The wooden stairs creak softly when you climb them. Fingerspell STAIRS
8 Attic /AT-ik/ Old books and treasures are stored in the attic. Fingerspell ATTIC
9 Basement /BAYS-ment/ The cool basement keeps food fresh in summer. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Table /TAY-bul/ The round table is set for dinner. Both forearms stack, form flat surface
11 Chair /CHAIR/ Each person has their own chair at the table. Two bent fingers sit on opposite two fingers
12 Sofa /SOH-fuh/ The soft sofa is the best spot for reading. Fingerspell SOFA
13 Bookshelf /BOOK-shelf/ The bookshelf is full of stories and dictionaries. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Lamp /LAMP/ The warm lamp makes the room glow at night. Fingerspell LAMP
15 Rug /RUG/ The colorful rug covers the wooden floor. Fingerspell RUG
16 Curtain /KUR-ten/ Vesta opens the curtain to let in the morning sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Clock /KLOK/ The clock on the wall ticks softly. Point to wrist + mime clock hands
18 Mirror /MIR-er/ Silas checks his hair in the mirror by the door. Fingerspell MIRROR
19 Pillow /PIL-oh/ The fluffy pillow is white and very soft. Fingerspell PILLOW
20 Blanket /BLANG-kit/ A warm blanket keeps you cozy on cold nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Bed /BED/ The bed has clean sheets and two soft pillows. Fingerspell BED
22 Dresser /DRES-er/ Clothes are folded neatly in the dresser drawers. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Closet /KLOZ-it/ Coats and shoes are stored in the closet. Fingerspell CLOSET
24 Sink /SINGK/ Vesta washes dishes in the kitchen sink. Fingerspell SINK
25 Stove /STOHV/ The stove heats the pot of soup. S-hands mime turning burner knobs
26 Oven /UV-en/ Bread bakes slowly in the hot oven. Flat hand slides into opposite hand (putting in oven)
27 Refrigerator /rih-FRIJ-er-ay-ter/ Fresh vegetables stay cold in the refrigerator. Both hands shiver with R-hands
28 Microwave /MY-kroh-wayv/ The microwave heats food quickly. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Dishwasher /DISH-wosh-er/ The dishwasher cleans all the plates and cups. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Cabinet /KAB-ih-net/ Plates and bowls are stored in the kitchen cabinet. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Counter /KOWN-ter/ Vesta chops vegetables on the kitchen counter. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Faucet /FAW-sit/ Turn the faucet to the left for hot water. Fingerspell FAUCET
33 Bathtub /BATH-tub/ The bathtub fills with warm, bubbly water. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Shower /SHOW-er/ Silas takes a quick shower every morning. 5-hand above head opens/closes (water spraying)
35 Toilet /TOY-let/ The toilet is next to the sink in the bathroom. Fingerspell TOILET
36 Towel /TOW-ul/ Hang the wet towel on the hook to dry. Flat hands rub back and forth at sides
37 Soap /SOHP/ Use soap and warm water to wash your hands. Bend fingers on opposite palm
38 Toothbrush /TOOTH-brush/ Vesta brushes her teeth with a green toothbrush. Fingerspell or sign each word
39 Ceiling /SEE-ling/ The ceiling is painted white to make the room feel bigger. Flat hand above, pats up
40 Floor /FLOR/ The wooden floor is smooth and clean. Both flat hands horizontal, slide apart
41 Light switch /LYT SWICH/ Flip the light switch when you enter a dark room. Fingerspell or sign each word
42 Outlet /OWT-let/ The electrical outlet has two plug slots. Fingerspell OUTLET
43 Fireplace /FYR-plays/ The stone fireplace keeps the cottage warm in winter. Fingerspell or sign each word
44 Mantel /MAN-tul/ Family photos sit on the mantel above the fireplace. Fingerspell MANTEL
45 Vase /VAYS/ A glass vase with fresh flowers sits on the table. Fingerspell VASE
🏡

Vesta's Tip:

"Walk around YOUR home. How many of these words can you find? Point at each thing and say it aloud. Try to find at least 20!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 3: Pets & Companions

Every home in Terrasol has a beloved pet. Vesta runs the village pet sanctuary where she cares for animals of all kinds.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Dog /DOG/ The friendly dog wags its tail when Vesta comes home. Snap fingers + pat thigh
2 Puppy /PUP-ee/ The puppy chews on a small toy bone. Fingerspell PUPPY
3 Cat /KAT/ The cat purrs softly on Silas's lap. F-hand pulls from cheek (whiskers)
4 Kitten /KIT-en/ The playful kitten chases a ball of yarn. Fingerspell KITTEN
5 Goldfish /GOHLD-fish/ The orange goldfish swims in a round glass bowl. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Hamster /HAM-ster/ The hamster runs on its exercise wheel all night. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Guinea pig /GIN-ee PIG/ The guinea pig squeaks when it's hungry. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Rabbit /RAB-it/ The white rabbit hops around the garden. Both H-hands at head, wiggle (long ears)
9 Parakeet /PAIR-uh-keet/ The green parakeet chirps a happy song. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Canary /kuh-NAIR-ee/ The yellow canary sings in its cage every morning. Fingerspell CANARY
11 Turtle /TUR-tul/ The pet turtle moves very slowly across the floor. A-hand under opposite cupped hand, thumb peeks
12 Iguana /ih-GWAH-nuh/ The green iguana sits under a warm heat lamp. Fingerspell IGUANA
13 Gerbil /JER-bil/ The gerbil digs tunnels in its bedding. Fingerspell GERBIL
14 Ferret /FAIR-it/ The curious ferret explores every corner of the room. Fingerspell FERRET
15 Hermit crab /HER-mit KRAB/ The hermit crab carries a shell on its back. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Leash /LEESH/ Vesta clips the leash onto the dog's collar. Fingerspell LEASH
17 Collar /KOL-er/ The red collar has a tag with the dog's name. Fingerspell COLLAR
18 Bowl /BOHL/ Silas fills the water bowl for the cat. Both cupped hands form bowl shape
19 Cage /KAYJ/ The bird sings inside its clean cage. Fingerspell CAGE
20 Aquarium /uh-KWAIR-ee-um/ The aquarium has colorful fish and green plants. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Pet food /PET FOOD/ Vesta scoops pet food into the dog's dish. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Veterinarian /vet-er-ih-NAIR-ee-un/ The veterinarian checks that the pets are healthy. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Toy /TOY/ The dog's favorite toy is a squeaky rubber ball. Fingerspell TOY
24 Bed /BED/ The cat curls up on its soft, round bed. Fingerspell BED
25 Grooming /GROOM-ing/ Grooming means brushing and cleaning your pet. Fingerspell or sign each word
🐾

Vesta's Tip:

"Do you have a pet? Draw a picture of your pet next to this page. Write three words that describe your pet."

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 15: Recycling & Sustainability

Silas and Vesta teach everyone in Terrasol how to protect the land for future generations.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Recycle /ree-SY-kul/ Recycle paper, plastic, and glass. R-hand circles (going around again)
2 Reuse /ree-YOOZ/ Reuse the bag instead of throwing it away. Fingerspell REUSE
3 Reduce /rih-DOOS/ Reduce waste by buying only what you need. Fingerspell REDUCE
4 Trash /TRASH/ Put trash in the trash can, not on the ground. Fingerspell TRASH
5 Garbage /GAR-bij/ Garbage trucks pick up the bins every week. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Litter /LIT-er/ Never litter — always find a proper bin. Fingerspell LITTER
7 Bin /BIN/ The blue bin is for recycling. Fingerspell BIN
8 Compost /KOM-pohst/ Compost turns food scraps into rich soil. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Landfill /LAND-fil/ Trash goes to the landfill when it can't be recycled. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Pollution /puh-LOO-shun/ Pollution makes the air and water dirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Clean energy /KLEEN EN-er-jee/ Solar and wind are forms of clean energy. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Solar panel /SOH-ler PAN-ul/ A solar panel turns sunlight into electricity. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Wind turbine /WIND TUR-byn/ The wind turbine spins to make electricity from wind. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Water bottle /WAW-ter BOT-ul/ Use a reusable water bottle instead of plastic. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Plastic /PLAS-tik/ Too much plastic ends up in the ocean. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Paper /PAY-per/ Recycle paper to save trees. Both flat hands brush past each other
17 Glass /GLAS/ Glass can be recycled again and again. C-hand rises from opposite palm (tall glass)
18 Aluminum /uh-LOO-mih-num/ Aluminum cans are easy to recycle. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Conserve /kun-SURV/ Conserve water by turning off the faucet. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Protect /proh-TEKT/ It is our job to protect the environment. Fingerspell or sign each word
♻️

Silas's Tip:

"Here are three words to remember forever: REDUCE, REUSE, RECYCLE. Say them every day. Practice them every day. The earth will thank you!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 18: Rivers & Lakes

Vesta discovers freshwater that gives life

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 River /RIV-er/ The river flows from mountains to sea. W-hand flows forward like water
2 Stream /STREEM/ A small stream runs through the forest. Fingerspell STREAM
3 Creek /KREEK/ The children played by the creek. Fingerspell CREEK
4 Lake /LAYK/ The lake is calm and perfect for fishing. L-hands trace circle shape
5 Pond /POND/ Ducks swim in the pond at the park. Fingerspell POND
6 Waterfall /WOT-er-fall/ The waterfall drops into the pool below. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Dam /DAM/ The dam holds back the water. Fingerspell DAM
8 Bridge /BRIJ/ Walk across the bridge to the other side. V-hand bridges under opposite forearm
9 Bank /BANK/ Sit on the river bank and watch the water. B-hand taps opposite palm repeatedly (stacking money)
10 Current /KUR-ent/ The river current carries leaves downstream. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Upstream /UP-streem/ Salmon swim upstream to lay their eggs. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Downstream /DOWN-streem/ The boat floated downstream. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Canal /kuh-NAL/ The canal connects two rivers. Fingerspell CANAL
14 Rapids /RAP-idz/ The white-water rapids are exciting. Fingerspell RAPIDS
15 Kayak /KY-ak/ Paddle the kayak down the calm river. Fingerspell KAYAK
16 Canoe /kuh-NOO/ Two people can paddle a canoe together. Fingerspell CANOE
17 Fishing rod /FISH-ing ROD/ Cast the fishing rod into the deep part. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Trout /TROWT/ The trout jumps out of the stream. Fingerspell TROUT
19 Frog /FROG/ The frog sits on a lily pad. Fist under chin, flick two fingers out
20 Turtle /TUR-tul/ The turtle suns itself on a log. A-hand under opposite cupped hand, thumb peeks
21 Beaver /BEE-ver/ The beaver builds a dam with sticks. Fingerspell BEAVER
22 Marsh /MARSH/ A marsh is a wetland with tall grasses. Fingerspell MARSH
23 Swamp /SWOMP/ The swamp is wet and muddy. Fingerspell SWAMP
24 Flood /FLUD/ Heavy rain caused a flood in the valley. W-hands rise up from below
25 Fresh water /FRESH WOT-er/ Fresh water from rivers is safe to drink. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏞

Vesta's Tip:

"Water is LIFE! RIVER, LAKE, STREAM, WATERFALL!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 14: Rocks & Minerals

Deep in the Terrasol Cavern, Silas and Vesta discover a world of sparkling stones and ancient formations.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Rock /ROK/ Silas picks up a smooth rock from the riverbed. Fingerspell ROCK
2 Stone /STOHN/ The garden path is lined with flat stones. Fingerspell STONE
3 Pebble /PEB-ul/ A pebble is a very small, smooth rock. Fingerspell PEBBLE
4 Boulder /BOHL-der/ The giant boulder sits at the bottom of the hill. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Sand /SAND/ The sand on the beach is warm between your toes. Fingerspell SAND
6 Gravel /GRAV-ul/ Gravel crunches under your feet on the path. Fingerspell GRAVEL
7 Clay /KLAY/ Vesta shapes the wet clay into a bowl. Fingerspell CLAY
8 Crystal /KRIS-tul/ The purple crystal sparkles in the cave light. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Mineral /MIN-er-ul/ A mineral is a natural substance found in the earth. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Fossil /FOS-ul/ The fossil shows a fern leaf from millions of years ago. Fingerspell FOSSIL
11 Gem /JEM/ A polished gem shines like a tiny star. Fingerspell GEM
12 Quartz /KWORTS/ Quartz is one of the most common minerals. Fingerspell QUARTZ
13 Marble /MAR-bul/ The smooth marble feels cool in your hand. Fingerspell MARBLE
14 Granite /GRAN-it/ The kitchen counter is made of hard granite. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Limestone /LYM-stohn/ Limestone is used to build strong walls. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Lava /LAH-vuh/ Hot lava flows down the side of a volcano. Fingerspell LAVA
17 Magma /MAG-muh/ Magma is melted rock deep inside the earth. Fingerspell MAGMA
18 Erosion /ih-ROH-zhun/ Erosion is when wind or water wears away rock. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Sediment /SED-ih-ment/ Layers of sediment build up on the river bottom. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Stalactite /stuh-LAK-tyt/ A stalactite hangs down from the ceiling of the cave. Fingerspell or sign each word
💎

Vesta's Tip:

"Collect rocks from your yard or a park. Describe each one: Is it SMOOTH or ROUGH? Is it DARK or LIGHT? Is it HEAVY or LIGHT? Science starts with observation!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 20: Seeds & Growing

In Vesta's Greenhouse, every seed tells a story

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Seed /SEED/ Plant the seed in the soil and water it. Mime sprinkling seeds from pinched fingers
2 Sprout /SPROWT/ The sprout pushes up through the soil. Fingerspell SPROUT
3 Seedling /SEED-ling/ The seedling needs sunlight to grow strong. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Root /ROOT/ Roots grow underground and drink water. 5-hand pushes down through opposite C-hand
5 Stem /STEM/ The stem holds the flower up toward the sun. Fingerspell STEM
6 Leaf /LEEF/ Each leaf catches sunlight. Index finger hangs from opposite wrist, sways
7 Bud /BUD/ A bud is a flower before it opens. Fingerspell BUD
8 Bloom /BLOOM/ The flowers bloom in spring. Fingerspell BLOOM
9 Petal /PET-ul/ Each petal of the rose is soft and colorful. Fingerspell PETAL
10 Pollen /POL-en/ Bees carry pollen from flower to flower. Fingerspell POLLEN
11 Compost /KOM-pohst/ Add compost to make soil richer. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Fertilizer /FUR-tih-ly-zer/ Fertilizer helps plants grow bigger. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Greenhouse /GREEN-hows/ Grow plants year-round in the greenhouse. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Water /WOT-er/ Water the plants every morning. W-hand taps chin
15 Sunlight /SUN-lyt/ Plants need sunlight to make food. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Soil /SOYL/ Healthy soil is dark and rich. Fingerspell SOIL
17 Pot /POT/ Plant the herb in a small clay pot. P-hands mime stirring in pot
18 Planter /PLAN-ter/ The window planter is full of flowers. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Weed /WEED/ Pull the weeds so they do not steal nutrients. Fingerspell WEED
20 Harvest /HAR-vest/ Harvest the tomatoes when they turn red. Curved hand mimes gathering crops
21 Vine /VYN/ The vine climbs up the garden fence. Fingerspell VINE
22 Branch /BRANCH/ The bird sits on a branch of the oak tree. Fingerspell BRANCH
23 Trunk /TRUNK/ The trunk of the tree is thick and strong. Fingerspell TRUNK
24 Bark /BARK/ The bark protects the tree like a coat. Fingerspell BARK
25 Growth /GROHTH/ The growth of the plant was amazing. Fingerspell GROWTH
🌱

Vesta's Tip:

"Learning English is like growing a garden! Plant SEED, WATER it, give SUNLIGHT!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 9: Shapes in the World

Silas discovers that shapes are hidden EVERYWHERE in Terrasol — in buildings, in nature, in the stars!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Circle /SUR-kul/ The full moon makes a perfect circle in the night sky. Fingerspell CIRCLE
2 Square /SKWAIR/ The window of the cottage is a perfect square. Fingerspell SQUARE
3 Triangle /TRY-ang-gul/ The roof of the barn forms a triangle shape. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Rectangle /REK-tang-gul/ The door is a tall rectangle. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Oval /OH-vul/ The egg has a smooth oval shape. Fingerspell OVAL
6 Diamond /DY-mund/ The kite flying above the field is shaped like a diamond. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Star /STAR/ Silas traces a five-pointed star in the sand. Both index fingers brush up alternately
8 Heart /HART/ Vesta draws a heart shape in the foggy window. Middle finger taps left chest
9 Pentagon /PEN-tuh-gon/ A pentagon has five equal sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Hexagon /HEK-suh-gon/ The honeycomb is made of tiny hexagons. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Octagon /OK-tuh-gon/ A stop sign is shaped like an octagon with eight sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Cube /KYOOB/ The wooden block on the shelf is a cube. Fingerspell CUBE
13 Sphere /SFEER/ The orange is shaped like a perfect sphere. Fingerspell SPHERE
14 Cylinder /SIL-in-der/ The can of soup is shaped like a cylinder. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Cone /KOHN/ The ice cream sits on top of a waffle cone. Fingerspell CONE
16 Pyramid /PEER-uh-mid/ The sandcastle has a pyramid with four triangle sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Spiral /SPY-rul/ The snail's shell curves in a beautiful spiral. Fingerspell SPIRAL
18 Crescent /KRES-ent/ The crescent moon looks like a curved sliver of light. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Trapezoid /TRAP-uh-zoyd/ A trapezoid has only two parallel sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Parallelogram /pair-uh-LEL-oh-gram/ The tile on the floor is shaped like a parallelogram. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Line /LYN/ Draw a straight line from one dot to the other. Fingerspell LINE
22 Curve /KURV/ The road follows a gentle curve around the hillside. Fingerspell CURVE
23 Angle /ANG-gul/ The corner of the book forms a right angle. Fingerspell ANGLE
24 Edge /EJ/ Be careful near the edge of the table. Fingerspell EDGE
25 Corner /KOR-ner/ The bookshelf sits in the corner of the room. Fingerspell CORNER
26 Side /SYD/ A square has four equal sides. Fingerspell SIDE
27 Pattern /PAT-ern/ Vesta sees a repeating pattern in the garden tiles. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Symmetry /SIM-eh-tree/ A butterfly's wings show perfect symmetry. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Parallel /PAIR-uh-lel/ Railroad tracks run in parallel lines. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Perpendicular /per-pen-DIK-yoo-ler/ The wall is perpendicular to the floor — they meet at a right angle. Fingerspell or sign each word
📐

Silas's Tip:

"Look around you RIGHT NOW. Can you find a circle? A rectangle? A triangle? Shapes are everywhere! Count how many rectangles you can find in your room."

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 7: Silas & Vesta's Cottage (Outside)

The cottage sits on a gentle hillside, surrounded by flower boxes and a stone walkway. Smoke rises from the chimney.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Roof /ROOF/ The red tile roof keeps the rain out of the cottage. Fingerspell ROOF
2 Chimney /CHIM-nee/ Smoke rises from the chimney on cold mornings. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Door /DOR/ The wooden front door has a brass knocker. Both B-hands: one swings open from other
4 Doorknob /DOR-nob/ Turn the doorknob to open the door. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Window /WIN-doh/ Sunlight streams through the clean window. Both flat hands stack, top slides open
6 Shutters /SHUT-erz/ The green shutters close to block the wind. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Porch /PORCH/ Silas sits on the porch and watches the sunset. Fingerspell PORCH
8 Steps /STEPS/ Three stone steps lead up to the front door. Fingerspell STEPS
9 Walkway /WAWK-way/ The brick walkway goes from the gate to the porch. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Mailbox /MAYL-boks/ Vesta checks the mailbox for letters every afternoon. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Gate /GAYT/ The garden gate opens with a gentle push. Fingerspell GATE
12 Driveway /DRYV-way/ The tractor is parked in the driveway. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Garage /guh-RAHJ/ Tools and the wheelbarrow are stored in the garage. Fingerspell GARAGE
14 Gutter /GUT-er/ The gutter carries rainwater off the roof. Fingerspell GUTTER
15 Wall /WAWL/ The stone wall is thick and keeps the cottage warm. Both flat hands vertical, parallel
16 Foundation /fown-DAY-shun/ The foundation is the strong base under the house. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Yard /YARD/ The children play in the front yard. Fingerspell YARD
18 Garden /GAR-den/ The flower garden fills the yard with color. G-hands outline garden plot
19 Flower box /FLOW-er BOKS/ Red geraniums grow in the flower box on the window. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Deck /DEK/ The wooden deck is perfect for eating dinner outside. Fingerspell DECK
21 Awning /AW-ning/ The striped awning keeps the sun off the porch. Fingerspell AWNING
22 Antenna /an-TEN-uh/ The old antenna sits on top of the roof. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Drainpipe /DRAYN-pyp/ The drainpipe runs down the side of the house. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Doorbell /DOR-bel/ Press the doorbell and wait for someone to answer. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Welcome mat /WEL-kum MAT/ The welcome mat says "Home Sweet Home." Fingerspell or sign each word
Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 1: The Barnyard

Silas and Vesta wake up early on their farm in Terrasol. The rooster crows as the sun rises over the red barn.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Barn /BARN/ Silas opens the big red barn door each morning. Fingerspell BARN
2 Tractor /TRAK-ter/ Vesta drives the tractor across the green field. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Hay /HAY/ The hay is golden and soft for the animals to sleep on. Fingerspell HAY
4 Silo /SY-loh/ The tall silo stores grain for the winter months. Fingerspell SILO
5 Fence /FENS/ A wooden fence keeps the animals safe inside the farm. Fingerspell FENCE
6 Rooster /ROO-ster/ The rooster crows loudly when the sun comes up. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Cow /KOW/ The brown cow gives fresh milk every day. Y-hand thumb at temple, twist (horn)
8 Calf /KAF/ The little calf stays close to its mother. Fingerspell CALF
9 Goat /GOHT/ The goat climbs on rocks and eats everything! Fingerspell GOAT
