10 Pig /PIG/ The pig rolls happily in the cool mud. Flat hand flaps under chin
11 Piglet /PIG-let/ Three tiny piglets follow the mother pig. Fingerspell PIGLET
12 Horse /HORS/ Silas rides the strong horse through the meadow. Thumb at temple, two fingers flap (ear)
13 Pony /POH-nee/ Vesta brushes the pony's soft mane. Fingerspell PONY
14 Donkey /DONG-kee/ The donkey carries heavy baskets up the hill. Fingerspell DONKEY
15 Sheep /SHEEP/ The fluffy sheep graze on the hillside. V-fingers on opposite forearm, snip up (shearing)
16 Lamb /LAM/ The lamb has soft white wool. Fingerspell LAMB
17 Hen /HEN/ The hen sits on her nest of eggs. Fingerspell HEN
18 Chick /CHIK/ The yellow chick follows the hen everywhere. Fingerspell CHICK
19 Duck /DUK/ The duck waddles to the pond for a swim. Hand at mouth opens/closes (quacking)
20 Duckling /DUK-ling/ Five ducklings swim behind their mother. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Turkey /TUR-kee/ The turkey spreads its colorful tail feathers. Fingerspell TURKEY
22 Goose /GOOS/ The white goose honks loudly at visitors. Fingerspell GOOSE
23 Stable /STAY-bul/ The horses sleep inside the warm stable at night. Fingerspell STABLE
24 Pen /PEN/ The pigs live in a pen behind the barn. Pinch dominant hand, write on opposite palm
25 Coop /KOOP/ The chickens go inside the coop when it gets dark. Fingerspell COOP
26 Trough /TRAWF/ Silas fills the trough with water for the animals. Fingerspell TROUGH
27 Feed /FEED/ Vesta carries a bucket of feed to the chickens. Fingerspell FEED
28 Saddle /SAD-ul/ Silas puts the leather saddle on the horse. Fingerspell SADDLE
29 Bridle /BRY-dul/ The bridle helps Silas steer the horse gently. Fingerspell BRIDLE
30 Pasture /PAS-chur/ The cows walk slowly through the green pasture. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Corral /kuh-RAL/ The rancher brings the horses into the corral. Fingerspell CORRAL
32 Hoof /HOOF/ Vesta checks the horse's hoof for stones. Fingerspell HOOF
33 Mane /MAYN/ The horse's mane blows in the wind. Fingerspell MANE
34 Tail /TAYL/ The cow swishes its tail to keep flies away. Fingerspell TAIL
35 Wool /WOOL/ The sheep's wool is sheared in the spring. Fingerspell WOOL
36 Egg /EG/ The hen laid a brown egg in the nest. H-fingers crack apart
37 Milk /MILK/ Fresh milk comes from the cows on Silas's farm. C-hand squeezes (milking motion)
38 Hay bale /HAY BAYL/ Vesta stacks the hay bales inside the barn. Fingerspell or sign each word
39 Scarecrow /SKAIR-kroh/ A funny scarecrow stands in the middle of the field. Fingerspell or sign each word
40 Windmill /WIND-mil/ The old windmill turns slowly in the breeze. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌿

Vesta's Tip:

"Can you make the sounds these animals make? Moo, baa, oink! Say the word, then make the sound. That's two languages in one!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 6: The Environment

Terrasol has every kind of landscape — from meadows to deserts, from wetlands to coral reefs. Silas and Vesta are its caretakers.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Meadow /MED-oh/ Wildflowers dot the green meadow in springtime. Fingerspell MEADOW
2 Desert /DEZ-ert/ The desert is hot and dry with very little rain. Both flat hands push outward (dry, vast)
3 Wetland /WET-land/ The wetland is full of birds, frogs, and tall reeds. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Pond /POND/ Dragonflies hover over the still pond. Fingerspell POND
5 Stream /STREEM/ A clear stream flows over smooth, round stones. Fingerspell STREAM
6 Hill /HIL/ Vesta runs up the grassy hill to see the sunrise. Flat hand shapes hill curve
7 Valley /VAL-ee/ The green valley lies between two mountain ridges. Both flat hands dip down in center
8 Cliff /KLIF/ The cliff drops steeply to the ocean below. Fingerspell CLIFF
9 Cave /KAYV/ Bats sleep inside the dark, cool cave. Fingerspell CAVE
10 Prairie /PRAIR-ee/ The prairie stretches flat as far as the eye can see. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Marsh /MARSH/ The marsh is wet and muddy with cattails growing tall. Fingerspell MARSH
12 Jungle /JUNG-gul/ The thick jungle is warm, wet, and full of life. Fingerspell JUNGLE
13 Rainforest /RAYN-for-est/ The rainforest gets more rain than any other place. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Tundra /TUN-druh/ The frozen tundra has very few trees. Fingerspell TUNDRA
15 Coral reef /KOR-ul REEF/ The coral reef is a colorful underwater city for fish. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Volcano /vol-KAY-noh/ The volcano has been quiet for a hundred years. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Island /EYE-lund/ A small island sits in the middle of the blue lake. I-hand circles on opposite palm
18 Peninsula /pen-IN-suh-luh/ The peninsula is land surrounded by water on three sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Canyon /KAN-yun/ The deep canyon was carved by a river over millions of years. Fingerspell CANYON
20 Glacier /GLAY-sher/ The ancient glacier moves slowly down the mountain. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Ecosystem /EE-koh-sis-tem/ Every plant and animal is part of the ecosystem. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Habitat /HAB-ih-tat/ A habitat is the natural home of an animal or plant. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Conservation /kon-ser-VAY-shun/ Conservation means protecting nature for future generations. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Pollution /puh-LOO-shun/ Pollution harms the air, water, and land. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Recycle /ree-SY-kul/ Silas and Vesta recycle paper, plastic, and glass. R-hand circles (going around again)
26 Endangered /en-DAYN-jerd/ Endangered animals need our help to survive. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Extinct /ek-STINKT/ Dinosaurs are extinct — they no longer live on Earth. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Biodiversity /by-oh-dy-VUR-sih-tee/ Biodiversity means many different kinds of living things. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Renewable /rih-NOO-uh-bul/ Solar power and wind are renewable sources of energy. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Climate /KLY-mit/ The climate in Terrasol changes from season to season. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌍

Silas's Tip:

"Look at words 21–30. These are POWERFUL words. If you learn them, you can talk about protecting the Earth. Practice saying 'ecosystem' — EE-koh-sis-tem — three times fast!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 4: The Garden

Behind Silas and Vesta's cottage, a beautiful garden grows with vegetables, herbs, and flowers year-round.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Soil /SOYL/ Silas digs his hands into the dark, rich soil. Fingerspell SOIL
2 Rake /RAYK/ Vesta uses the rake to smooth the dirt. Fingerspell RAKE
3 Hoe /HOH/ The hoe breaks up hard ground for planting. Fingerspell HOE
4 Shovel /SHUV-ul/ Silas digs a hole with the shovel. Fingerspell SHOVEL
5 Watering can /WAW-ter-ing KAN/ Vesta pours water from the watering can onto the seedlings. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Hose /HOHZ/ The long green hose reaches every corner of the garden. Fingerspell HOSE
7 Compost /KOM-pohst/ Old leaves and food scraps become rich compost. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Wheelbarrow /WEEL-bair-oh/ Silas pushes the wheelbarrow full of soil. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Seed /SEED/ Vesta plants a tiny seed and covers it with soil. Mime sprinkling seeds from pinched fingers
10 Seedling /SEED-ling/ A green seedling pushes up through the dirt. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Sprout /SPROWT/ The first sprout appears after five days of rain. Fingerspell SPROUT
12 Root /ROOT/ The root grows down deep into the earth. 5-hand pushes down through opposite C-hand
13 Stem /STEM/ Water travels up through the stem to the leaves. Fingerspell STEM
14 Leaf /LEEF/ Each green leaf catches sunlight for the plant. Index finger hangs from opposite wrist, sways
15 Bud /BUD/ A tiny bud will open into a beautiful flower. Fingerspell BUD
16 Bloom /BLOOM/ The roses bloom in the warm summer months. Fingerspell BLOOM
17 Petal /PET-ul/ Each petal is soft like silk. Fingerspell PETAL
18 Thorn /THORN/ Be careful of the sharp thorn on the rose bush. Fingerspell THORN
19 Vine /VYN/ The vine climbs up the old stone wall. Fingerspell VINE
20 Weed /WEED/ Silas pulls up every weed so the vegetables can grow. Fingerspell WEED
21 Mulch /MULCH/ Vesta spreads mulch around the plants to keep the soil moist. Fingerspell MULCH
22 Fertilizer /FUR-tuh-ly-zer/ Fertilizer helps the plants grow strong and tall. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Garden bed /GAR-den BED/ Each garden bed is planted with a different vegetable. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Row /ROH/ The carrots grow in a straight row. Fingerspell ROW
25 Trellis /TREL-is/ The bean plants climb up the wooden trellis. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Pot /POT/ Herbs grow in a clay pot on the windowsill. P-hands mime stirring in pot
27 Planter /PLAN-ter/ The large planter is full of colorful flowers. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Greenhouse /GREEN-hows/ The greenhouse keeps plants warm during the winter. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Pruning shears /PROO-ning SHEERZ/ Vesta trims the bushes with her pruning shears. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Gloves /GLUVZ/ Silas wears thick gloves to protect his hands. One hand slides over other like putting on glove
31 Bucket /BUK-it/ Vesta fills a bucket with fresh vegetables from the garden. Fingerspell BUCKET
32 Sunflower /SUN-flow-er/ The tall sunflower turns its face toward the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Rose /ROHZ/ A red rose grows beside the garden gate. Fingerspell ROSE
34 Tulip /TOO-lip/ Purple tulips bloom in the spring. Fingerspell TULIP
35 Daisy /DAY-zee/ Vesta picks a white daisy and puts it in her hair. Fingerspell DAISY
🌻

Silas's Tip:

"Name five things in THIS garden that you can also find in YOUR neighborhood. Drawing connections between the story and real life makes the words stick!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 16: The Ocean & Beach

Silas and Vesta visit where land meets sea

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Ocean /OH-shun/ The ocean covers most of planet Earth. Both hands make waves, push forward
2 Wave /WAYV/ The waves crash on the sandy shore. Open hand waves side to side
3 Sand /SAND/ Build a sandcastle on the warm sand. Fingerspell SAND
4 Shell /SHEL/ Collect beautiful shells along the beach. Fingerspell SHELL
5 Tide /TYD/ The tide comes in and goes out twice a day. Fingerspell TIDE
6 Seaweed /SEE-weed/ Green seaweed washes up on the shore. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Coral /KOR-ul/ The coral reef is home to colorful fish. Fingerspell CORAL
8 Starfish /STAR-fish/ The starfish has five arms. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Jellyfish /JEL-ee-fish/ The jellyfish floats through the water. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Crab /KRAB/ The crab walks sideways on the sand. Both V-hands snap open and close (pincers)
11 Dolphin /DOL-fin/ Dolphins jump out of the sea. B-hand dips and rises forward
12 Whale /WAYL/ The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth. W-hand dips and rises (swimming)
13 Lighthouse /LYT-hows/ The lighthouse guides ships safely to shore. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Pier /PEER/ Walk to the end of the pier to watch the sunset. Fingerspell PIER
15 Surfboard /SURF-bord/ Stand on the surfboard and ride the wave. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Lifeguard /LYF-gard/ The lifeguard watches swimmers. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Sunscreen /SUN-skreen/ Put on sunscreen to protect your skin. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Towel /TOW-ul/ Dry off with a towel after swimming. Flat hands rub back and forth at sides
19 Sandcastle /SAND-kas-ul/ Decorate the sandcastle with shells. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Seagull /SEE-gul/ The seagull flies over the beach. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Horizon /hor-RY-zun/ The sun sets on the horizon. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Current /KUR-ent/ The current moves water in big circles. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Anchor /ANG-ker/ Drop the anchor to stop the boat. Fingerspell ANCHOR
24 Shore /SHOR/ The boat returned to shore. Fingerspell SHORE
25 Reef /REEF/ The reef is full of colorful sea creatures. Fingerspell REEF
🏖

Vesta's Tip:

"The beach has SO many words! WAVE, SAND, SHELL, TIDE!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 5: Trees & the Forest

The ancient forests of Terrasol are filled with trees of every kind. Silas knows every single one by name.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Oak /OHK/ The mighty oak tree provides shade for the whole meadow. Fingerspell OAK
2 Pine /PYN/ The pine tree stays green even in the winter snow. Fingerspell PINE
3 Maple /MAY-pul/ The maple tree turns bright red in autumn. Fingerspell MAPLE
4 Willow /WIL-oh/ The willow tree's branches touch the water gently. Fingerspell WILLOW
5 Birch /BURCH/ The white bark of the birch tree peels like paper. Fingerspell BIRCH
6 Cedar /SEE-der/ Cedar wood smells sweet and keeps insects away. Fingerspell CEDAR
7 Elm /ELM/ The old elm tree in the village square is 200 years old. Fingerspell ELM
8 Palm /PAHM/ The tall palm tree sways in the warm breeze. Fingerspell PALM
9 Trunk /TRUNGK/ The trunk is the thick, strong middle part of the tree. Fingerspell TRUNK
10 Branch /BRANCH/ A bird builds its nest on a high branch. Fingerspell BRANCH
11 Bark /BARK/ The rough bark protects the tree from harm. Fingerspell BARK
12 Root /ROOT/ The roots spread deep underground to find water. 5-hand pushes down through opposite C-hand
13 Canopy /KAN-uh-pee/ The forest canopy blocks out most of the sunlight. Fingerspell CANOPY
14 Stump /STUMP/ An old stump sits where a tree once stood. Fingerspell STUMP
15 Log /LOG/ Silas sits on a log to rest during his walk. Fingerspell LOG
16 Moss /MOSS/ Soft green moss covers the north side of the rocks. Fingerspell MOSS
17 Fern /FURN/ Ferns grow in the cool, shady parts of the forest. Fingerspell FERN
18 Mushroom /MUSH-room/ A red and white mushroom grows beside the path. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Acorn /AY-korn/ The squirrel buries an acorn for the winter. Fingerspell ACORN
20 Pinecone /PYN-kohn/ Vesta collects a pinecone to decorate the table. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Sap /SAP/ Sticky sap drips down from the maple tree. Fingerspell SAP
22 Forest /FOR-est/ The forest is home to many animals and plants. Forearm trees, multiple, shift sideways
23 Grove /GROHV/ A grove of apple trees grows on the hillside. Fingerspell GROVE
24 Path /PATH/ A narrow path winds through the tall trees. Fingerspell PATH
25 Clearing /KLEER-ing/ A sunny clearing opens up in the middle of the forest. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Underbrush /UN-der-brush/ Small plants and bushes make up the underbrush. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Ivy /EYE-vee/ Green ivy climbs up the side of the old stone wall. Fingerspell IVY
28 Bush /BOOSH/ Berries grow on the wild bush near the river. Fingerspell BUSH
29 Hedge /HEJ/ A tall hedge separates the garden from the road. Fingerspell HEDGE
30 Timber /TIM-ber/ Timber is wood that is cut and ready to use for building. Fingerspell TIMBER
🌲

Vesta's Tip:

"Go outside and find a tree near your home. Touch the bark. Look at the leaves. Can you identify the kind of tree? Use the words from this page!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 13: Weather & Seasons

Terrasol experiences all four seasons. Silas and Vesta watch the sky and describe what they see.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Sunny /SUN-ee/ It is sunny and warm outside today. Fingerspell SUNNY
2 Cloudy /KLOWD-ee/ The sky is gray and cloudy this afternoon. Fingerspell CLOUDY
3 Rainy /RAYN-ee/ Bring an umbrella — it's rainy today. Fingerspell RAINY
4 Windy /WIN-dee/ The windy day blows the leaves off the trees. Fingerspell WINDY
5 Snowy /SNOH-ee/ Everything looks white and beautiful on a snowy day. Fingerspell SNOWY
6 Stormy /STORM-ee/ Thunder and lightning make the night stormy. Fingerspell STORMY
7 Hot /HOT/ It is very hot in July and August. C-hand at mouth, flick away sharply
8 Cold /KOHLD/ Wear a coat because it is cold outside. Both fists shake near body (shivering)
9 Warm /WORM/ Spring days are warm but not hot. C-hand at mouth, slowly open (gentle breath)
10 Cool /KOOL/ Fall mornings are cool and crisp. Both open hands fan at body
11 Fog /FOG/ Thick fog makes it hard to see the road. Fingerspell FOG
12 Hail /HAYL/ Small balls of ice called hail fell from the sky. Fingerspell HAIL
13 Thunder /THUN-der/ The loud thunder shakes the windows. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Lightning /LYT-ning/ A flash of lightning lights up the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Rainbow /RAYN-boh/ A rainbow appears after the rain stops. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Umbrella /um-BREL-uh/ Vesta opens her umbrella when it starts to rain. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Thermometer /thur-MOM-ih-ter/ The thermometer shows the temperature outside. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Forecast /FOR-kast/ The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Spring /SPRING/ Flowers bloom and baby animals are born in spring. Fingerspell SPRING
20 Summer /SUM-er/ Summer has the longest and warmest days. Fingerspell SUMMER
21 Fall /FAWL/ Leaves change color and fall from the trees in autumn. Fingerspell FALL
22 Winter /WIN-ter/ Winter brings snow, ice, and short cold days. Fingerspell WINTER
23 Breeze /BREEZ/ A gentle breeze cools us on a warm day. Fingerspell BREEZE
24 Frost /FROST/ White frost covers the grass early in the morning. Fingerspell FROST
25 Humidity /hyoo-MID-ih-tee/ High humidity makes the air feel thick and sticky. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌦️

Silas's Tip:

"Look out the window right now! What is the weather? Say: 'Today it is ___.' Sunny? Rainy? Cloudy? Now say the temperature: 'It is ___ degrees.' You just gave a weather report!"

Land 3 of 7

🌿 Terrasol

Guided by Silas the Shape Finder & Vesta the Space Navigator

Scene 2: Wild Animals

Silas and Vesta explore the deep forests and wide savannas of Terrasol, discovering creatures from every corner of the world.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Lion /LY-un/ The lion rests under the shade of a big tree. Claw hand rakes back over head (mane)
2 Tiger /TY-ger/ An orange tiger has beautiful black stripes. Fingerspell TIGER
3 Bear /BAIR/ The brown bear catches fish in the cold river. Crossed arms scratch chest
4 Wolf /WOOLF/ The wolf howls at the full moon in Terrasol. Fingerspell WOLF
5 Fox /FOKS/ A clever red fox hides behind the bushes. Fingerspell FOX
6 Deer /DEER/ The gentle deer drinks water from the stream. Open 5-hands at temples (antlers)
7 Elk /ELK/ The elk has huge antlers on top of its head. Fingerspell ELK
8 Moose /MOOS/ A moose stands in the shallow lake eating plants. Fingerspell MOOSE
9 Zebra /ZEE-bruh/ Each zebra has its own unique pattern of stripes. Fingerspell ZEBRA
10 Giraffe /juh-RAF/ The giraffe stretches its long neck to eat leaves. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Elephant /EL-uh-funt/ The elephant sprays water with its trunk. Trace trunk from nose curving down
12 Rhinoceros /ry-NOS-er-us/ The rhinoceros has a strong horn on its nose. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Hippopotamus /hip-oh-POT-uh-mus/ The hippopotamus stays cool in the muddy river. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Gorilla /guh-RIL-uh/ The gorilla beats its chest in the jungle. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Monkey /MUNG-kee/ The monkey swings from branch to branch. Scratch sides of body going up
16 Chimpanzee /chim-pan-ZEE/ The chimpanzee uses a stick to find food. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Kangaroo /kang-guh-ROO/ The kangaroo carries its baby in a pouch. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Koala /koh-AH-luh/ The koala sleeps in its favorite eucalyptus tree. Fingerspell KOALA
19 Panda /PAN-duh/ The panda eats bamboo all day long. Fingerspell PANDA
20 Leopard /LEP-erd/ The leopard has spots and runs very fast. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Cheetah /CHEE-tuh/ The cheetah is the fastest animal on land. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Hyena /hy-EE-nuh/ The hyena laughs loudly across the savanna. Fingerspell HYENA
23 Crocodile /KROK-uh-dyl/ The crocodile waits quietly in the water. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Alligator /AL-ih-gay-ter/ The alligator has a wide, flat snout. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Snake /SNAYK/ The snake slithers silently through the tall grass. V-hand slithers forward (like fangs)
26 Lizard /LIZ-erd/ A green lizard suns itself on a warm rock. Fingerspell LIZARD
27 Frog /FROG/ The frog jumps from lily pad to lily pad. Fist under chin, flick two fingers out
28 Toad /TOHD/ The toad hides under a damp log. Fingerspell TOAD
29 Turtle /TUR-tul/ The turtle carries its home on its back. A-hand under opposite cupped hand, thumb peeks
30 Eagle /EE-gul/ The eagle soars high above the mountains. Fingerspell EAGLE
31 Hawk /HAWK/ The hawk watches for mice from a tall tree. Fingerspell HAWK
32 Owl /OWL/ The owl hunts at night with its big round eyes. Fingerspell OWL
33 Parrot /PAIR-ut/ The colorful parrot can repeat words it hears. Fingerspell PARROT
34 Penguin /PEN-gwin/ The penguin waddles on the ice and swims in the sea. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Flamingo /fluh-MING-oh/ The pink flamingo stands on one leg. Fingerspell or sign each word
36 Bat /BAT/ The bat hangs upside down in a dark cave. Fingerspell BAT
37 Squirrel /SKWIR-ul/ The squirrel hides acorns in the ground for winter. Fingerspell or sign each word
38 Raccoon /ra-KOON/ The raccoon has a black mask around its eyes. Fingerspell or sign each word
39 Skunk /SKUNGK/ The skunk has a white stripe down its back. Fingerspell SKUNK
40 Beaver /BEE-ver/ The beaver builds a dam across the stream. Fingerspell BEAVER
41 Otter /OT-er/ The otter floats on its back in the river. Fingerspell OTTER
42 Whale /WAYL/ The enormous whale sings deep in the ocean. W-hand dips and rises (swimming)
43 Dolphin /DOL-fin/ The dolphin leaps out of the waves playfully. B-hand dips and rises forward
44 Shark /SHARK/ The shark swims silently beneath the surface. Fingerspell SHARK
45 Seal /SEEL/ The seal claps its flippers on the rocky shore. Fingerspell SEAL
🔭

Silas's Tip:

"Can you sort these animals into groups? Which ones live in water? Which ones live in the forest? Which ones live on the savanna? Sorting is a superpower!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 16: Bicycle & Walking

Nerissa and Reef explore Aquaria on foot and by bike

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Bicycle /BY-sih-kul/ Ride your bicycle to the park. S-fists pedal in circles
2 Helmet /HEL-met/ Always wear a helmet when riding a bike. Both C-hands come down on head
3 Pedal /PED-ul/ Pedal faster to go up the hill. Fingerspell PEDAL
4 Chain /CHAYN/ The chain connects the pedals to the wheel. Fingerspell CHAIN
5 Tire /TYR/ Pump air into the flat tire. Fingerspell TIRE
6 Handlebar /HAN-dul-bar/ Hold the handlebar to steer the bike. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Brake /BRAYK/ Squeeze the brake to slow down. Fingerspell BRAKE
8 Kickstand /KIK-stand/ Use the kickstand to park your bike. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Bike lane /BYK LAYN/ Ride in the bike lane for safety. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Lock /LOK/ Lock your bike to the rack. Fingerspell LOCK
11 Sidewalk /SYD-wawk/ Walk on the sidewalk not in the street. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Crosswalk /KROS-wawk/ Cross the street at the crosswalk. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Pedestrian /peh-DES-tree-un/ The pedestrian waits for the walk signal. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Jogging /JOG-ing/ She goes jogging in the park every morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Stroller /STROH-ler/ Push the baby in the stroller. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Wheelchair /WEEL-chair/ The ramp is for wheelchair access. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Scooter /SKOO-ter/ The electric scooter is fun to ride. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Skateboard /SKAYT-bord/ He does tricks on his skateboard. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Path /PATH/ Follow the path through the garden. Fingerspell PATH
20 Trail /TRAYL/ The hiking trail goes to the waterfall. Fingerspell TRAIL
21 Ramp /RAMP/ Use the ramp instead of the stairs. Fingerspell RAMP
22 Stairs /STAIRZ/ Climb the stairs to the second floor. Fingerspell STAIRS
23 Elevator /EL-eh-vay-ter/ Take the elevator to the top floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Escalator /ES-kuh-lay-ter/ The escalator moves you up automatically. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Shortcut /SHORT-kut/ Take the shortcut through the park. Fingerspell or sign each word
🚲

Reef's Tip:

"Walk and bike everywhere! Name what you see: SIDEWALK, CROSSWALK, BIKE LANE!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 8: Community Places

Ronan and Nerissa explore a new town in Aquaria, finding every important place a community needs.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 School /SKOOL/ Every neighborhood has a school for children. Clap hands twice
2 Library /LY-brair-ee/ The public library lends books for free. L-hand circles in air
3 Hospital /HOS-pih-tul/ Go to the hospital for emergency medical care. H-hand draws cross on upper arm
4 Fire station /FYR STAY-shun/ The fire station has trucks and brave firefighters. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Police station /puh-LEES STAY-shun/ Report a crime at the police station. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Post office /POHST OF-is/ Mail a letter or package at the post office. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Bank /BANGK/ The bank opens at nine in the morning. B-hand taps opposite palm repeatedly (stacking money)
8 Grocery store /GROH-ser-ee STOR/ Buy food and household items at the grocery store. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Restaurant /RES-tuh-rawnt/ The restaurant serves lunch from eleven to two. R-hand touches each side of chin
10 Pharmacy /FAR-muh-see/ The pharmacy fills prescriptions and sells medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Gas station /GAS STAY-shun/ Buy fuel for your car at the gas station. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Laundromat /LAWN-droh-mat/ Wash your clothes at the laundromat. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Park /PARK/ Children play on the swings in the park. Modified P-hand taps S-hand on top
14 Playground /PLAY-grownd/ The playground has slides, swings, and monkey bars. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Community center /kuh-MYOO-nih-tee SEN-ter/ The community center hosts classes and events. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Place of worship /PLAYS uv WUR-ship/ A place of worship is where people gather for prayer. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 City hall /SIT-ee HAWL/ City hall is where the mayor works. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Courthouse /KORT-hows/ Legal cases are heard in the courthouse. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Museum /myoo-ZEE-um/ The museum has exhibits about history and art. M-hands draw building outline
20 Movie theater /MOO-vee THEE-uh-ter/ Watch a film at the movie theater. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏘️

Nerissa's Tip:

"Walk around your neighborhood in your mind. Can you name five places in English? SCHOOL, PARK, STORE, LIBRARY, RESTAURANT. Now give directions to one of them!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 6: Directions & Navigation

Ronan and Nerissa walk through the streets of a new city, asking for and giving directions.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Left /LEFT/ Turn left at the traffic light. Fingerspell LEFT
2 Right /RYT/ Turn right after the grocery store. R-hand rises (right/entitlement)
3 Straight /STRAYT/ Go straight for two blocks. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Turn /TURN/ Turn at the next corner. Fingerspell TURN
5 Block /BLOK/ The library is three blocks away. Fingerspell BLOCK
6 Corner /KOR-ner/ Meet me at the corner of Main and Oak Street. Fingerspell CORNER
7 Across from /uh-KROS FRUM/ The bank is across from the park. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Next to /NEKST TOO/ The pharmacy is next to the grocery store. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Between /bih-TWEEN/ The school is between the library and the park. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Behind /bih-HYND/ The parking lot is behind the building. Fingerspell BEHIND
11 In front of /IN FRUNT UV/ The bus stop is in front of the hospital. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Near /NEER/ The restaurant is near the train station. Fingerspell NEAR
13 Far /FAR/ The airport is far from the city center. Fingerspell FAR
14 Above /uh-BUV/ The apartment is above the shop. Fingerspell ABOVE
15 Below /bih-LOH/ The parking garage is below the mall. Fingerspell BELOW
16 Inside /in-SYD/ Go inside the building and turn left. Fingerspell INSIDE
17 Outside /owt-SYD/ Wait outside the entrance for the group. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Upstairs /UP-stairz/ The restroom is upstairs on the second floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Downstairs /DOWN-stairz/ The café is downstairs in the basement. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 GPS /JEE-PEE-ES/ Use the GPS on your phone to find the way. Fingerspell GPS
21 Landmark /LAND-mark/ The clock tower is a helpful landmark for navigation. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Location /loh-KAY-shun/ Share your location so I can find you. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Lost /LOST/ I'm lost. Can you help me find the bus station? Fingerspell LOST
24 Detour /DEE-toor/ Take a detour because the road is closed. Fingerspell DETOUR
25 Dead end /DED END/ The road is a dead end — turn around. Fingerspell or sign each word
🧭

Nerissa's Tip:

"Give directions from your seat right now! Say: 'The door is on my LEFT. The window is on my RIGHT. The teacher is IN FRONT OF me.' You just learned prepositions by looking around the room!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 13: Emergency Services

Safety is important in Aquaria. Ronan and Nerissa learn who to call and what to do in an emergency.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Emergency /ih-MUR-jen-see/ Call 911 in an emergency. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 911 /NYN-WUN-WUN/ Dial 911 for police, fire, or medical help. Fingerspell 911
3 Firefighter /FYR-fy-ter/ The brave firefighter climbs the ladder. B-hand at forehead (shield)
4 Paramedic /pair-uh-MED-ik/ The paramedic gives first aid at the scene. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Police officer /puh-LEES OF-ih-ser/ The police officer keeps the community safe. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 First aid /FURST AYD/ A first aid kit has bandages and antiseptic. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Bandage /BAN-dij/ Put a bandage on the cut to keep it clean. Mime wrapping bandage around hand
8 Fire extinguisher /FYR ek-STING-gwish-er/ The fire extinguisher is mounted on the wall. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Smoke detector /SMOHK dih-TEK-ter/ The smoke detector beeps when it senses smoke. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Escape route /es-KAYP ROOT/ Know the escape route in case of fire. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 CPR /SEE-PEE-AR/ CPR can save a life in a cardiac emergency. Fingerspell CPR
12 Stretcher /STRECH-er/ The paramedic carries the patient on a stretcher. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Siren /SY-ren/ The ambulance siren warns cars to move aside. Fingerspell SIREN
14 Safety /SAYF-tee/ Safety means protecting yourself and others. Fingerspell SAFETY
15 Hazard /HAZ-erd/ A wet floor is a slip hazard. Fingerspell HAZARD
16 Evacuate /ih-VAK-yoo-ayt/ Evacuate the building when the alarm sounds. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Alarm /uh-LARM/ Pull the alarm if you see fire or smoke. Index taps opposite palm rapidly (ringing)
18 Rescue /RES-kyoo/ The coast guard performs a water rescue. Fingerspell RESCUE
19 Dispatcher /dis-PACH-er/ The 911 dispatcher asks what the emergency is. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Safe place /SAYF PLAYS/ Go to a safe place during a tornado warning. Fingerspell or sign each word
🚒

Ronan's Tip:

"Memorize this sentence: 'In an emergency, I call 911.' Practice saying your full address out loud — the dispatcher will need it. Knowing these words could SAVE A LIFE."

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 11: Freshwater Life

Not all of Aquaria is saltwater. Ronan and Nerissa explore streams, ponds, and rivers teeming with life.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Freshwater /FRESH-waw-ter/ Lakes and rivers are freshwater — not salty. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Trout /TROWT/ The rainbow trout swims in the cold mountain stream. Fingerspell TROUT
3 Bass /BAS/ The largemouth bass hides near the lily pads. Fingerspell BASS
4 Catfish /KAT-fish/ The catfish has long whiskers near its mouth. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Salmon /SAM-un/ Salmon swim upstream to lay their eggs. Fingerspell SALMON
6 Tadpole /TAD-pohl/ A tadpole will grow into a frog. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Crayfish /KRAY-fish/ The crayfish looks like a tiny lobster. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Pond weed /POND WEED/ Pond weed gives shelter to small fish. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Lily pad /LIL-ee PAD/ The frog sits on a wide green lily pad. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Cattail /KAT-tayl/ Tall cattails grow at the edge of the marsh. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Dragonfly /DRAG-un-fly/ A blue dragonfly darts over the water. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Heron /HAIR-un/ The great blue heron stands still in the shallows. Fingerspell HERON
13 Beaver dam /BEE-ver DAM/ The beaver dam blocks the stream and makes a pond. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Minnow /MIN-oh/ Tiny minnows swim in a school near the bank. Fingerspell MINNOW
15 Algae /AL-jee/ Green algae grows on rocks in the sunlight. Fingerspell ALGAE
16 Fishing rod /FISH-ing ROD/ Ronan casts the fishing rod into the lake. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Bait /BAYT/ Use a worm as bait to catch fish. Fingerspell BAIT
18 Hook /HOOK/ The hook holds the bait for the fish. Fingerspell HOOK
19 Net /NET/ Nerissa scoops up minnows with a small net. Fingerspell NET
20 Dock /DOK/ The wooden dock stretches out over the lake. Fingerspell DOCK
🐟

Ronan's Tip:

"Compare freshwater and saltwater animals! Which live in rivers? Which live in the ocean? Make two lists and you'll see how nature sorts itself!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 2: Geography & Landforms

Ronan and Nerissa study the great map room in Aquaria, where every type of landform is modeled in miniature.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Continent /KON-tih-nent/ There are seven continents on the Earth. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Country /KUN-tree/ A country has its own government and borders. Y-hand circles elbow
3 Island /EYE-lund/ An island is land surrounded by water on all sides. I-hand circles on opposite palm
4 Peninsula /pen-IN-suh-luh/ A peninsula is land surrounded by water on three sides. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Mountain /MOWN-tun/ The snow-capped mountain rises high into the clouds. Flat hand taps fist (ROCK) + both hands peak up
6 Valley /VAL-ee/ The green valley lies between two tall mountains. Both flat hands dip down in center
7 Hill /HIL/ A hill is lower and rounder than a mountain. Flat hand shapes hill curve
8 Plain /PLAYN/ The flat plain stretches for miles with no trees. Fingerspell PLAIN
9 Plateau /pla-TOH/ A plateau is a flat area of land high above sea level. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Desert /DEZ-ert/ The desert is hot and dry with very little rain. Both flat hands push outward (dry, vast)
11 Forest /FOR-est/ A thick forest of trees covers the mountainside. Forearm trees, multiple, shift sideways
12 Jungle /JUNG-gul/ The tropical jungle has dense plants and tall trees. Fingerspell JUNGLE
13 River /RIV-er/ The river flows from the mountain to the sea. W-hand flows forward like water
14 Lake /LAYK/ The calm lake reflects the blue sky. L-hands trace circle shape
15 Pond /POND/ The small pond has ducks and lily pads. Fingerspell POND
16 Waterfall /WAW-ter-fawl/ The waterfall crashes down from the high cliff. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Stream /STREEM/ A narrow stream trickles through the forest. Fingerspell STREAM
18 Canal /kuh-NAL/ Ships travel through the canal between two bodies of water. Fingerspell CANAL
19 Bay /BAY/ The bay is a sheltered area of water along the coast. Fingerspell BAY
20 Coast /KOHST/ The coast is where the land meets the ocean. Fingerspell COAST
21 Beach /BEECH/ Nerissa walks along the sandy beach. Mime spreading towel, then waves
22 Cliff /KLIF/ The steep cliff drops straight down to the water. Fingerspell CLIFF
23 Cave /KAYV/ Ronan explores the dark cave near the shore. Fingerspell CAVE
24 Volcano /vol-KAY-noh/ A volcano can erupt with lava and ash. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Glacier /GLAY-sher/ A glacier is a massive river of ice. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Iceberg /EYS-burg/ Most of the iceberg is hidden below the water. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 North /NORTH/ The compass needle points north. N-hand moves up
28 South /SOWTH/ Travel south toward warmer weather. S-hand moves down
29 East /EEST/ The sun rises in the east every morning. E-hand moves right
30 West /WEST/ The sun sets in the west every evening. W-hand moves left
31 Equator /ih-KWAY-ter/ The equator divides the Earth into north and south halves. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Hemisphere /HEM-ih-sfeer/ The Northern Hemisphere has different seasons than the Southern. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Map /MAP/ Use a map to find your way to the destination. M-hands trace on flat surface
34 Compass /KUM-pus/ A compass shows which direction you are facing. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Border /BOR-der/ A border is the line between two countries. Fingerspell BORDER
36 Capital /KAP-ih-tul/ Washington, D.C. is the capital of the United States. Fingerspell or sign each word
37 Population /pop-yoo-LAY-shun/ The population of the city is two million people. Fingerspell or sign each word
38 Region /REE-jun/ The southwest region is hot and dry. Fingerspell REGION
39 Terrain /tuh-RAYN/ The terrain changes from mountains to desert. Fingerspell or sign each word
40 Globe /GLOHB/ Spin the globe to find a new country. Fingerspell GLOBE
🗺️

Nerissa's Tip:

"Find your country on a map! Point to it and say: 'I am from ___. My country is in the ___ hemisphere. It is near the ___.' Geography words help you tell the world WHERE you are from!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 5: Hotels & Lodging

After their journey, Ronan and Nerissa check into the Aquaria Inn. Every traveler needs a place to rest.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Hotel /hoh-TEL/ The hotel is near the beach and the train station. H-hand bounces on opposite index finger
2 Motel /moh-TEL/ The motel has rooms you can drive right up to. Fingerspell MOTEL
3 Room /ROOM/ The room has two beds, a TV, and a bathroom. R-hands form box shape
4 Reservation /rez-er-VAY-shun/ Make a reservation before you arrive. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Check-in /CHEK-in/ Check-in time is three o'clock in the afternoon. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Check-out /CHEK-owt/ Check-out time is eleven o'clock in the morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Front desk /FRUNT DESK/ Ask for extra towels at the front desk. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Lobby /LOB-ee/ Wait in the lobby while they prepare your room. Fingerspell LOBBY
9 Elevator /EL-eh-vay-ter/ Take the elevator to the fifth floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Room key /ROOM KEE/ Your room key is a plastic card. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Housekeeping /HOWS-kee-ping/ Housekeeping cleans the room every day. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Room service /ROOM SUR-vis/ Order breakfast through room service. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Suite /SWEET/ The suite has a separate living room and bedroom. Fingerspell SUITE
14 Single room /SING-gul ROOM/ A single room has one bed for one person. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Double room /DUB-ul ROOM/ A double room has two beds. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Vacancy /VAY-kun-see/ The sign says "Vacancy" meaning rooms are available. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 No vacancy /NOH VAY-kun-see/ "No vacancy" means the hotel is full. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Swimming pool /SWIM-ing POOL/ The hotel swimming pool is open until nine P.M. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Luggage rack /LUG-ij RAK/ Place your suitcase on the luggage rack. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Complimentary /kom-plih-MEN-tuh-ree/ The complimentary breakfast is free for all guests. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏨

Ronan's Tip:

"Practice checking in! Say: 'Hello, I have a RESERVATION under the name ___. What FLOOR is my room on? What time is CHECK-OUT?' You're ready for any hotel in the world!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 12: Mail & Postal Services

In Aquaria, letters and packages connect people across oceans. Ronan and Nerissa visit the post office.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Letter /LET-er/ I wrote a letter to my grandmother. Fingerspell LETTER
2 Envelope /EN-vuh-lohp/ Put the letter inside the envelope. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Stamp /STAMP/ Buy a stamp to mail the letter. Fingerspell STAMP
4 Address /AD-res/ Write the address on the front of the envelope. A-hands move up chest (like suspenders)
5 Return address /rih-TURN AD-res/ The return address is in the top left corner. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Zip code /ZIP KOHD/ The zip code helps mail get to the right place. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Package /PAK-ij/ The package arrived in a brown cardboard box. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Mail carrier /MAYL KAIR-ee-er/ The mail carrier delivers letters to every house. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Post office /POHST OF-is/ Go to the post office to send a package. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Mailbox /MAYL-boks/ Drop the letter in the blue mailbox. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Postcard /POHST-kard/ Nerissa sends a postcard from every city she visits. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Delivery /dih-LIV-er-ee/ The delivery truck brings packages to the door. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Tracking number /TRAK-ing NUM-ber/ Use the tracking number to find your package online. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Fragile /FRAJ-ul/ Mark the box "Fragile" if it contains glass. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Priority mail /pry-OR-ih-tee MAYL/ Priority mail arrives faster than regular mail. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Express /ek-SPRES/ Express delivery gets the package there by tomorrow. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Scale /SKAYL/ The clerk weighs the package on the scale. Both flat hands balance like scale
18 Certified mail /SUR-tih-fyd MAYL/ Certified mail requires a signature when delivered. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 P.O. Box /PEE-OH BOKS/ Some people receive mail at a P.O. Box. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Sort /SORT/ Workers sort the mail by zip code. Fingerspell SORT
📬

Nerissa's Tip:

"Pretend to address an envelope! Write the name, the street, the city, the state, and the ZIP CODE. Now you know all five parts of a mailing address!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 18: Map Reading & GPS

Reef learns to navigate using maps and technology

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Map /MAP/ Unfold the map to find your location. M-hands trace on flat surface
2 GPS /JEE-PEE-ES/ The GPS recalculates when you make a wrong turn. Fingerspell GPS
3 North /NORTH/ The compass needle points north. N-hand moves up
4 South /SOWTH/ Texas is in the south of the United States. S-hand moves down
5 East /EEST/ The sun rises in the east. E-hand moves right
6 West /WEST/ The sun sets in the west. W-hand moves left
7 Landmark /LAND-mark/ The Statue of Liberty is a famous landmark. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Navigate /NAV-ih-gayt/ Navigate to the store using your phone. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Compass /KUM-pus/ A compass shows magnetic north. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Legend /LEJ-end/ The map legend explains the symbols. Fingerspell LEGEND
11 Scale /SKAYL/ The scale shows one inch equals one mile. Both flat hands balance like scale
12 Coordinate /koh-OR-dih-nit/ Enter the coordinates into the GPS. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Latitude /LAT-ih-tood/ Latitude measures position north or south. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Longitude /LON-jih-tood/ Longitude measures east and west position. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Destination /des-tih-NAY-shun/ Your destination is on the right. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Intersection /in-ter-SEK-shun/ Turn left at the next intersection. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Highway /HY-way/ Take the highway for a faster route. Both hands weave forward (busy road)
18 Exit /EK-sit/ Take exit twelve to reach the mall. Fingerspell EXIT
19 Detour /DEE-toor/ Follow the detour around the construction. Fingerspell DETOUR
20 U-turn /YOO-turn/ Make a U-turn at the next light. Fingerspell U-TURN
21 Roundabout /ROWND-uh-bowt/ Go around the roundabout and take the second exit. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 One-way /WUN-way/ This is a one-way street going east. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Dead end /DED END/ The road ends at a dead end. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Scenic route /SEE-nik ROOT/ Take the scenic route to enjoy the view. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Satellite /SAT-uh-lyt/ GPS uses satellite signals to locate you. Fingerspell or sign each word
🗺

Reef's Tip:

"NORTH, SOUTH, EAST, WEST. Say directions out loud when using your GPS!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 15: Public Transportation

Nerissa rides every kind of public transit in Aquaria

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Bus /BUS/ Take the bus to school every morning. B-hand mimes long vehicle
2 Subway /SUB-way/ The subway runs underground through the city. Y-hand slides under opposite flat palm
3 Train /TRAYN/ The train travels between cities. H-hands slide along each other (tracks)
4 Station /STAY-shun/ Wait at the station for the next train. S-hands outline building
5 Platform /PLAT-form/ Stand on the platform until the train stops. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Ticket /TIK-it/ Buy a ticket before boarding the train. V-hand punches into opposite palm
7 Fare /FAIR/ The bus fare is two dollars. Fingerspell FARE
8 Transfer /TRANS-fer/ Transfer to the blue line at Main Street. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Schedule /SKEJ-ool/ Check the bus schedule for departure times. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
10 Route /ROOT/ This bus route goes to the hospital. Fingerspell ROUTE
11 Stop /STOP/ Pull the cord to request a stop. Flat hand chops down on opposite palm
12 Driver /DRY-ver/ The bus driver opens the doors. Fingerspell DRIVER
13 Passenger /PAS-en-jer/ Each passenger must pay the fare. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Seat /SEET/ Give your seat to an elderly passenger. Fingerspell SEAT
15 Standing room /STAN-ding ROOM/ The bus is full — standing room only. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Rush hour /RUSH OW-er/ Trains are crowded during rush hour. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Commute /kuh-MYOOT/ My daily commute takes forty-five minutes. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Metro /MET-roh/ The metro is fast and goes everywhere. Fingerspell METRO
19 Trolley /TROL-ee/ The trolley runs on tracks through downtown. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Ferry /FAIR-ee/ The ferry carries people across the river. F-hands carry across space
21 Terminal /TUR-mih-nul/ Meet me at the bus terminal downtown. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Pass /PAS/ Buy a monthly pass to save money. Fingerspell PASS
23 Delay /dih-LAY/ There is a ten-minute delay on the red line. Fingerspell DELAY
24 On time /ON TYM/ The bus arrived on time today. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Accessible /ak-SES-ih-bul/ All buses are accessible for wheelchairs. Fingerspell or sign each word
🚌

Nerissa's Tip:

"BUS, SUBWAY, TRAIN — say the name of every ride you take!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 19: Restaurants & Dining

Nerissa and Reef dine at the Aquaria Seaside Cafe

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Menu /MEN-yoo/ Look at the menu to choose your meal. Fingerspell MENU
2 Appetizer /AP-eh-ty-zer/ Start with an appetizer before the main course. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Entree /ON-tray/ The entree is the main dish of the meal. Fingerspell ENTREE
4 Dessert /dih-ZURT/ Save room for dessert after dinner. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Beverage /BEV-er-ij/ Would you like a beverage with your meal? Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Waiter /WAY-ter/ The waiter takes your order at the table. Fingerspell WAITER
7 Hostess /HOH-stis/ The hostess seats you at your table. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Reservation /rez-er-VAY-shun/ Make a reservation for seven o'clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Order /OR-der/ Are you ready to order? Fingerspell ORDER
10 Specials /SPESH-ulz/ The daily specials are on the board. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Check /CHEK/ May I have the check, please? Fingerspell CHECK
12 Tip /TIP/ Leave a fifteen percent tip for good service. Fingerspell TIP
13 Takeout /TAYK-owt/ Order takeout if you want to eat at home. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Drive-through /DRYV-throo/ The drive-through is open late. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Buffet /buh-FAY/ The buffet has many choices. Fingerspell BUFFET
16 Booth /BOOTH/ Sit in the booth by the window. Fingerspell BOOTH
17 Napkin /NAP-kin/ Place the napkin on your lap. Fingerspell NAPKIN
18 Silverware /SIL-ver-wair/ The silverware includes a fork, knife, and spoon. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Refill /REE-fil/ May I have a refill on my water? Fingerspell REFILL
20 Allergies /AL-er-jeez/ Tell the waiter about any food allergies. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Portion /POR-shun/ The portion size is very generous. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Gratuity /gruh-TOO-ih-tee/ Gratuity is included for large groups. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Side dish /SYD DISH/ Choose a side dish with your entree. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 To go /too GOH/ I would like my coffee to go, please. Fingerspell TO GO
25 Compliment /KOM-plih-ment/ Compliment the chef on the delicious meal. Fingerspell or sign each word
🍽

Nerissa's Tip:

"Practice ordering: TABLE FOR TWO, I WOULD LIKE, CHECK PLEASE!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 14: Road Signs & Safety

Walking or driving through Aquaria, Ronan and Nerissa learn to read the signs that keep everyone safe.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Stop /STOP/ The red stop sign means come to a complete halt. Flat hand chops down on opposite palm
2 Yield /YEELD/ Yield means slow down and let others go first. Fingerspell YIELD
3 One way /WUN WAY/ This is a one way street — traffic goes in one direction. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Do not enter /DOO NOT EN-ter/ The "Do Not Enter" sign means you cannot go that way. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Speed limit /SPEED LIM-it/ The speed limit is 25 miles per hour in a school zone. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 School zone /SKOOL ZOHN/ Drive slowly through the school zone. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Crosswalk /KROS-wawk/ Pedestrians cross at the crosswalk. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Pedestrian crossing /peh-DES-tree-un KROS-ing/ Watch for the pedestrian crossing sign. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Railroad crossing /RAYL-rohd KROS-ing/ Stop and look both ways at a railroad crossing. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Merge /MURJ/ The merge sign means two lanes become one. Fingerspell MERGE
11 Exit /EG-zit/ Take the next exit off the highway. Fingerspell EXIT
12 Detour /DEE-toor/ Follow the detour signs around road construction. Fingerspell DETOUR
13 No parking /NOH PAR-king/ You will get a ticket if you park in a no parking zone. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Handicapped parking /HAN-dee-kapt PAR-king/ Handicapped parking spaces are near the entrance. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Caution /KAW-shun/ The caution sign warns of danger ahead. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Construction zone /kun-STRUK-shun ZOHN/ Workers are ahead in the construction zone. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Dead end /DED END/ The road ends at a dead end. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 U-turn /YOO-TURN/ Make a U-turn to go back the other way. Fingerspell U-TURN
19 Curve /KURV/ Slow down at the sharp curve ahead. Fingerspell CURVE
20 Warning sign /WORN-ing SYN/ A warning sign alerts you to possible danger. Fingerspell or sign each word
⚠️

Nerissa's Tip:

"How many road signs can you spot on your next car ride? Count the STOP signs, the SPEED LIMIT signs, and the CROSSWALK markers. Signs are English in the real world!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 10: Seasons & Nature Cycles

Aquaria cycles through all four seasons. Ronan and Nerissa watch nature transform every few months.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Spring /SPRING/ Spring brings warm air and new flowers. Fingerspell SPRING
2 Summer /SUM-er/ Summer is the hottest season with the longest days. Fingerspell SUMMER
3 Fall /FAWL/ In fall, leaves turn red, orange, and gold. Fingerspell FALL
4 Autumn /AW-tum/ Autumn is another word for fall. Fingerspell AUTUMN
5 Winter /WIN-ter/ Winter is cold, and the days are shorter. Fingerspell WINTER
6 Bloom /BLOOM/ Flowers bloom in the warm spring sunshine. Fingerspell BLOOM
7 Harvest /HAR-vist/ The harvest is when farmers collect ripe crops. Curved hand mimes gathering crops
8 Migrate /MY-grayt/ Many birds migrate south for the winter. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Hibernate /HY-ber-nayt/ Bears hibernate through the cold winter months. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Bud /BUD/ A tiny green bud appears before the flower opens. Fingerspell BUD
11 Leaf /LEEF/ A single leaf falls from the tree. Index finger hangs from opposite wrist, sways
12 Branch /BRANCH/ The branch sways in the autumn wind. Fingerspell BRANCH
13 Root /ROOT/ The root anchors the tree deep underground. 5-hand pushes down through opposite C-hand
14 Seed /SEED/ Plant the seed in the soil and water it. Mime sprinkling seeds from pinched fingers
15 Soil /SOYL/ Rich dark soil helps plants grow. Fingerspell SOIL
16 Dew /DOO/ Morning dew sparkles on the grass. Fingerspell DEW
17 Frost /FROST/ Frost covers the windows on cold mornings. Fingerspell FROST
18 Thaw /THAW/ The ice begins to thaw when temperatures rise. Fingerspell THAW
19 Breeze /BREEZ/ A gentle breeze carries the smell of flowers. Fingerspell BREEZE
20 Sunshine /SUN-shyn/ Warm sunshine helps plants grow tall. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌸

Nerissa's Tip:

"What season is it where you live right now? Say: 'It is ___. In this season, I see ___. The weather is ___.' Now describe your FAVORITE season using three complete sentences!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 4: The Airport & Travel

Ronan and Nerissa are traveling to a new land! They navigate the busy Aquaria International Airport step by step.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Airport /AIR-port/ The airport is where airplanes take off and land. Y-hand with index: airplane flies
2 Terminal /TUR-mih-nul/ Go to Terminal B for your flight. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Gate /GAYT/ Your boarding gate is number twelve. Fingerspell GATE
4 Ticket /TIK-it/ Show your ticket at the gate before boarding. V-hand punches into opposite palm
5 Boarding pass /BOR-ding PAS/ The boarding pass shows your seat number. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Passport /PAS-port/ You need a passport to travel to another country. P-hand opens like small book
7 Visa /VEE-zuh/ A visa gives you permission to enter a foreign country. V-hand stamps opposite palm
8 Customs /KUS-tumz/ At customs, officers check what you bring into the country. C-hand stamps opposite palm
9 Immigration /im-ih-GRAY-shun/ Immigration officers check your passport at the border. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Luggage /LUG-ij/ Ronan places his luggage on the conveyor belt. Mime carrying suitcase (hand grips, arm curls)
11 Suitcase /SOOT-kays/ Pack your suitcase the night before the trip. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Carry-on /KAIR-ee-ON/ A carry-on bag fits under the seat or in the overhead bin. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Baggage claim /BAG-ij KLAYM/ Pick up your bags at the baggage claim area. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Security /sih-KYOOR-ih-tee/ Go through security before you reach the gates. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Check-in /CHEK-in/ Check-in at the airline counter or use the kiosk. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Departure /dih-PAR-chur/ The departure time is ten fifteen A.M. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Arrival /uh-RY-vul/ The arrival time is two thirty P.M. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Delay /dih-LAY/ The flight has a two-hour delay due to weather. Fingerspell DELAY
19 Flight /FLYT/ Flight number 247 is now boarding. Fingerspell FLIGHT
20 Pilot /PY-lut/ The pilot flies the airplane safely. Fingerspell PILOT
21 Flight attendant /FLYT uh-TEN-dunt/ The flight attendant shows you how to buckle your seatbelt. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Runway /RUN-way/ The airplane taxis down the runway before takeoff. Fingerspell RUNWAY
23 Takeoff /TAYK-off/ The airplane lifts up during takeoff. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Landing /LAND-ing/ The smooth landing means the flight is over. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Seat belt /SEET BELT/ Fasten your seat belt when the light turns on. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Overhead bin /OH-ver-hed BIN/ Store your carry-on in the overhead bin. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Aisle seat /EYL SEET/ Nerissa prefers the aisle seat for easy access. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Window seat /WIN-doh SEET/ Ronan picks the window seat to see the clouds. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Destination /des-tih-NAY-shun/ Their destination is two thousand miles away. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Round trip /ROWND TRIP/ A round trip ticket includes going and coming back. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 One-way /WUN-WAY/ A one-way ticket is for travel in one direction only. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Connecting flight /kuh-NEK-ting FLYT/ A connecting flight requires a change of planes. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Layover /LAY-oh-ver/ The layover is three hours in the middle city. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Turbulence /TUR-byoo-lens/ Turbulence makes the plane shake and bump. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 International /in-ter-NASH-un-ul/ An international flight goes to another country. Fingerspell or sign each word
✈️

Nerissa's Tip:

"Pretend you are at the airport! Practice: 'Excuse me, where is GATE twelve? When is the DEPARTURE time? Where is BAGGAGE CLAIM?' These questions help you navigate anywhere in the world!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 1: The Ocean & Marine Life

Ronan and Nerissa dive beneath the waves of Aquaria's Great Reef. Every creature tells a story of the sea.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Ocean /OH-shun/ The ocean covers more than seventy percent of the Earth. Both hands make waves, push forward
2 Sea /SEE/ The sea is calm and blue near the shore. Fingerspell SEA
3 Wave /WAYV/ Big waves crash against the rocky cliff. Open hand waves side to side
4 Tide /TYD/ The tide comes in and goes out twice each day. Fingerspell TIDE
5 Current /KUR-ent/ A strong current carries water across the ocean. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Coral /KOR-ul/ Colorful coral grows in warm, shallow water. Fingerspell CORAL
7 Coral reef /KOR-ul REEF/ The coral reef is home to thousands of fish. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Fish /FISH/ Tropical fish have bright stripes and spots. Flat hand swims forward like fish
9 Shark /SHARK/ The shark glides through the deep, dark water. Fingerspell SHARK
10 Dolphin /DOL-fin/ The friendly dolphin leaps beside the boat. B-hand dips and rises forward
11 Whale /WAYL/ The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth. W-hand dips and rises (swimming)
12 Octopus /OK-tuh-pus/ The octopus has eight long arms. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Jellyfish /JEL-ee-fish/ The transparent jellyfish floats gently in the current. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Sea turtle /SEE TUR-tul/ The sea turtle swims thousands of miles to lay eggs. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Seahorse /SEE-hors/ The tiny seahorse wraps its tail around the seaweed. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Starfish /STAR-fish/ The starfish has five arms and moves slowly on the rocks. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Crab /KRAB/ The crab walks sideways along the sandy bottom. Both V-hands snap open and close (pincers)
18 Lobster /LOB-ster/ The lobster hides among the rocks at night. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Shrimp /SHRIMP/ Tiny shrimp swim in groups near the reef. Fingerspell SHRIMP
20 Clam /KLAM/ The clam opens and closes its shell for food. Fingerspell CLAM
21 Oyster /OY-ster/ An oyster can make a pearl inside its shell. Fingerspell OYSTER
22 Seaweed /SEE-weed/ Long strands of seaweed sway in the tide. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Sand /SAND/ The sand on the ocean floor is white and soft. Fingerspell SAND
24 Shell /SHEL/ Nerissa collects a beautiful shell from the beach. Fingerspell SHELL
25 Anchor /ANG-ker/ The anchor keeps the boat from drifting away. Fingerspell ANCHOR
26 Submarine /SUB-muh-reen/ A submarine can travel deep under the sea. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Scuba diver /SKOO-buh DY-ver/ The scuba diver explores the reef up close. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Snorkel /SNOR-kul/ Ronan breathes through a snorkel near the surface. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Wetsuit /WET-soot/ The wetsuit keeps you warm in cold ocean water. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Plankton /PLANGK-tun/ Tiny plankton is food for whales and small fish. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Trench /TRENCH/ The ocean trench is the deepest part of the sea. Fingerspell TRENCH
32 Shipwreck /SHIP-rek/ An old shipwreck lies on the ocean floor. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Reef /REEF/ The reef is alive with color and movement. Fingerspell REEF
34 Saltwater /SAWLT-waw-ter/ The ocean is made of saltwater. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Marine /muh-REEN/ Marine life includes all plants and animals in the sea. Fingerspell MARINE
🌊

Ronan's Tip:

"Close your eyes and imagine you are underwater. Picture a coral reef full of colorful FISH, a JELLYFISH floating by, and a SEA TURTLE swimming past. Now open your eyes and DRAW what you saw, labeling each animal!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 3: Transportation

Ronan and Nerissa travel across Aquaria using every kind of vehicle — by land, sea, and air.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Car /KAR/ The car drives along the highway. Both C-hands mime steering wheel
2 Truck /TRUK/ The big truck carries goods across the country. Fingerspell TRUCK
3 Bus /BUS/ Take the bus to school and back home. B-hand mimes long vehicle
4 Van /VAN/ The family loads luggage into the van. Fingerspell VAN
5 Taxi /TAK-see/ Hail a taxi to get to the airport quickly. Fingerspell T-A-X-I or sign CAR + YELLOW
6 Motorcycle /MOH-ter-sy-kul/ The motorcycle weaves through city traffic. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Bicycle /BY-sih-kul/ Ronan rides his bicycle along the waterfront path. S-fists pedal in circles
8 Scooter /SKOO-ter/ The electric scooter is quiet and fast. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Train /TRAYN/ The train travels on tracks from city to city. H-hands slide along each other (tracks)
10 Subway /SUB-way/ Take the subway to travel underground across town. Y-hand slides under opposite flat palm
11 Airplane /AIR-playn/ The airplane flies above the clouds. Y-hand with index out, flies forward
12 Helicopter /HEL-ih-kop-ter/ The helicopter can hover in one place. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Boat /BOHT/ Nerissa rows the small boat across the lake. Both cupped hands form hull, bounce forward
14 Ship /SHIP/ The large ship carries passengers across the ocean. Fingerspell SHIP
15 Ferry /FAIR-ee/ The ferry takes cars and people across the bay. F-hands carry across space
16 Sailboat /SAYL-boht/ The sailboat uses wind to move through the water. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Canoe /kuh-NOO/ Ronan paddles the canoe down the calm river. Fingerspell CANOE
18 Kayak /KY-ak/ The kayak glides through the narrow waterway. Fingerspell KAYAK
19 Ambulance /AM-byoo-luns/ The ambulance rushes to the emergency with sirens on. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Fire truck /FYR TRUK/ The red fire truck races to the building. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Police car /puh-LEES KAR/ The police car patrols the neighborhood. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Tow truck /TOH TRUK/ The tow truck takes the broken car to the repair shop. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Crosswalk /KROS-wawk/ Always cross the street at the crosswalk. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Traffic light /TRAF-ik LYT/ Stop on red, go on green at the traffic light. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Stop sign /STOP SYN/ Every car must stop at the stop sign. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Speed limit /SPEED LIM-it/ The speed limit in a school zone is twenty miles per hour. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Parking lot /PAR-king LOT/ Find a space in the parking lot near the store. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Gas station /GAS STAY-shun/ Fill up the car's tank at the gas station. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Highway /HY-way/ The highway has multiple lanes for fast driving. Both hands weave forward (busy road)
30 Intersection /in-ter-SEK-shun/ Two roads meet at the intersection. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Pedestrian /peh-DES-tree-un/ A pedestrian is a person walking on foot. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Sidewalk /SYD-wawk/ Walk on the sidewalk, not on the road. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Bridge /BRIJ/ The bridge crosses over the wide river. V-hand bridges under opposite forearm
34 Tunnel /TUN-ul/ The tunnel goes through the mountain. Fingerspell TUNNEL
35 Route /ROOT/ Which route is the fastest to the airport? Fingerspell ROUTE
🚌

Ronan's Tip:

"How did you get here today? Say: 'I came by ___.' BUS? CAR? BICYCLE? WALKING? Every trip is a vocabulary lesson!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 17: Travel Documents

At the Aquaria Border Office, papers open doors

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Passport /PAS-port/ You need a passport to travel to other countries. P-hand opens like small book
2 Visa /VEE-zuh/ Apply for a visa before your trip. V-hand stamps opposite palm
3 ID /EYE-DEE/ Show your ID at the airport security. Fingerspell ID
4 Birth certificate /BURTH ser-TIF-ih-kit/ A birth certificate proves where you were born. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Social Security /SOH-shul seh-KYOOR-ih-tee/ Your Social Security number is private. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Green card /GREEN KARD/ A green card allows permanent residence. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Citizenship /SIT-ih-zen-ship/ She studied hard to earn her citizenship. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Application /ap-lih-KAY-shun/ Fill out the application completely. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Form /FORM/ Sign the form at the bottom of the page. Fingerspell FORM
10 Photo /FOH-toh/ Bring two passport photos for the application. Fingerspell PHOTO
11 Signature /SIG-nuh-chur/ Write your signature on the dotted line. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Expiration /ek-spih-RAY-shun/ Check the expiration date on your passport. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Renewal /rih-NOO-ul/ Submit a renewal before your visa expires. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Embassy /EM-buh-see/ Visit the embassy for help abroad. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Consulate /KON-suh-lut/ The consulate can replace a lost passport. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Customs /KUS-tumz/ Declare your items at customs. C-hand stamps opposite palm
17 Immigration /im-ih-GRAY-shun/ The immigration officer checks your passport. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Boarding pass /BOR-ding PAS/ Show your boarding pass to enter the plane. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Luggage /LUG-ij/ Check your luggage at the counter. Mime carrying suitcase (hand grips, arm curls)
20 Declaration /dek-luh-RAY-shun/ Fill out the customs declaration form. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Stamp /STAMP/ The officer stamps your passport. Fingerspell STAMP
22 Arrival /uh-RY-vul/ Your arrival time is three PM. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Departure /dih-PAR-chur/ The departure gate is B twelve. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 International /in-ter-NASH-un-ul/ The international terminal has flights worldwide. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Domestic /doh-MES-tik/ Domestic flights stay within the country. Fingerspell or sign each word
🛂

Nerissa's Tip:

"Know your documents: PASSPORT, VISA, ID. These words are your ticket to the world!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 9: Tricky English Words

The Aquaria Word Lab is where Ronan and Nerissa study the trickiest, most confusing words in English — the words that sound alike but mean different things.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Their / There / They're /THAIR/ Their house is there. They're coming soon. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Your / You're /YOR / YOOR/ Your book is here. You're doing great! Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Its / It's /ITS / ITS/ The dog wags its tail. It's a sunny day. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 To / Too / Two /TOO/ Go to the store. I want to come too. Buy two apples. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Here / Hear /HEER/ Come here and hear the music. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Right / Write /RYT/ Turn right and write down the address. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 No / Know /NOH/ No, I don't know the answer yet. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Buy / By / Bye /BY/ Buy bread by the bakery. Bye, see you later! Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Ate / Eight /AYT/ I ate eight grapes at lunch. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 See / Sea /SEE/ I can see the sea from the top of the hill. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Weather / Whether /WETH-er/ Check the weather to know whether to bring an umbrella. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Accept / Except /ak-SEPT / ek-SEPT/ I accept everything except the last offer. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Affect / Effect /uh-FEKT / ih-FEKT/ The rain will affect the game. The effect is a wet field. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Then / Than /THEN / THAN/ First eat, then play. She is taller than he is. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Loose / Lose /LOOS / LOOZ/ The loose button may cause you to lose it. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Quiet / Quite /KWY-it / KWYT/ The library is quiet. The book is quite good. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Advice / Advise /ad-VYS / ad-VYZ/ I give advice. I advise you to study. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Borrow / Lend /BOR-oh / LEND/ Can I borrow your pen? Can you lend me your pen? Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Bring / Take /BRING / TAYK/ Bring your book here. Take your book there. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Learn / Teach /LURN / TEECH/ Students learn. Teachers teach. Fingerspell or sign each word
🧠

Ronan's Tip:

"English has so many tricky words! The secret? Use them in sentences. 'THEIR dog is THERE. THEY'RE playing.' Write each one five times and the confusion disappears!"

Land 4 of 7

🌊 Aquaria

Guided by Ronan the Wave Rider & Nerissa the Deep Diver

Scene 7: Weather & Climate

In Aquaria, weather changes constantly as winds blow from across the ocean. Ronan and Nerissa track it all.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Weather /WETH-er/ The weather changes from sunny to cloudy. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Sunny /SUN-ee/ A sunny day is bright and warm. Fingerspell SUNNY
3 Cloudy /KLOW-dee/ The cloudy sky hides the sun. Fingerspell CLOUDY
4 Rainy /RAY-nee/ Bring an umbrella on a rainy day. Fingerspell RAINY
5 Windy /WIN-dee/ The windy day blows leaves across the path. Fingerspell WINDY
6 Snowy /SNOH-ee/ The snowy morning covers everything in white. Fingerspell SNOWY
7 Foggy /FOG-ee/ The foggy harbor makes it hard to see the ships. Fingerspell FOGGY
8 Stormy /STORM-ee/ A stormy night brings thunder and lightning. Fingerspell STORMY
9 Thunder /THUN-der/ The loud thunder echoes across the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Lightning /LYT-ning/ A flash of lightning lit up the dark sea. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Rain /RAYN/ The rain falls gently on the garden. Both 5-hands drop down repeatedly (raindrops)
12 Snow /SNOH/ Snow falls softly and covers the trees. 5-hands drift down with wiggling fingers
13 Hail /HAYL/ Small balls of hail bounce off the ground. Fingerspell HAIL
14 Ice /EYS/ Ice forms on the pond when it's very cold. Fingerspell ICE
15 Frost /FROST/ Frost covers the grass on early winter mornings. Fingerspell FROST
16 Humidity /hyoo-MID-ih-tee/ High humidity makes the air feel thick and damp. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Temperature /TEM-per-uh-chur/ The temperature today is seventy-five degrees. Index slides up/down opposite index (thermometer)
18 Forecast /FOR-kast/ The weather forecast predicts rain tomorrow. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Umbrella /um-BREL-uh/ Nerissa opens her umbrella when it starts to rain. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Rainbow /RAYN-boh/ A rainbow arches across the sky after the storm. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Drought /DROWT/ A long drought dries up the rivers. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Flood /FLUD/ The heavy rain caused a flood in the valley. W-hands rise up from below
23 Hurricane /HUR-ih-kayn/ A hurricane brings strong winds and heavy rain. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Tornado /tor-NAY-doh/ A tornado is a spinning column of wind. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Earthquake /URTH-kwayk/ The earthquake shook the buildings in the city. S-hands grab air and shake
26 Breeze /BREEZ/ A cool breeze blows in from the ocean. Fingerspell BREEZE
27 Cloud /KLOWD/ A big white cloud drifts slowly overhead. Both claw hands shape clouds, move across
28 Sunshine /SUN-shyn/ Warm sunshine makes the flowers bloom. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Overcast /OH-ver-kast/ The overcast sky looks gray from morning to night. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Climate /KLY-mit/ Climate describes the typical weather over many years. Fingerspell or sign each word
☀️

Ronan's Tip:

"Look out the window! What is the weather today? Say: 'Today is ___. The temperature is ___.' Try this every morning and you'll never run out of things to say in English!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 10: Exercise & Movement

In the Vitalis Training Grounds, Felix and Amara lead stretching, dance, and fitness exercises every morning. The body is the first instrument!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Exercise /EK-ser-syz/ Daily exercise keeps the body strong and the mind sharp. Both A-fists push up (like lifting weights)
2 Stretch /STRECH/ Stretch your muscles before and after exercise. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Warm-up /WORM-up/ A warm-up gets your body ready for harder movements. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Cool-down /KOOL-down/ A cool-down helps your heart rate return to normal. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Jump /JUMP/ Felix can jump higher than anyone in Vitalis. Fingerspell JUMP
6 Run /RUN/ Amara runs three laps around the training field. L-hands, index pulls on opposite thumb, move forward
7 Walk /WAWK/ Walking is a gentle exercise anyone can do. Both flat hands walk forward alternately
8 Skip /SKIP/ Felix skips along the path, swinging his arms. Fingerspell SKIP
9 Hop /HOP/ Hop on one foot, then switch to the other! Fingerspell HOP
10 Crawl /KRAWL/ The baby crawls quickly across the soft mat. Fingerspell CRAWL
11 Climb /KLYM/ Amara climbs the rope all the way to the top. Fingerspell CLIMB
12 Bend /BEND/ Bend at the waist and touch your toes. Fingerspell BEND
13 Twist /TWIST/ Twist your body to the left, then to the right. Fingerspell TWIST
14 Kick /KIK/ Felix kicks the ball across the field. Fingerspell KICK
15 Throw /THROH/ Throw the ball in a high arc. Fingerspell THROW
16 Catch /KACH/ Catch the ball with both hands. Fingerspell CATCH
17 Push /POOSH/ Push-ups make your arms and chest strong. Fingerspell PUSH
18 Pull /POOL/ Pull the resistance band slowly and steadily. Fingerspell PULL
19 Lift /LIFT/ Lift the light weight above your head. Fingerspell LIFT
20 Squat /SKWOT/ Squat down low and then stand back up. Fingerspell SQUAT
21 Balance /BAL-uns/ Stand on one foot to practice your balance. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Dance /DANS/ Amara loves to dance to the rhythm of the drums. Fingerspell DANCE
23 Yoga /YOH-guh/ Yoga combines breathing, stretching, and balance. Fingerspell YOGA
24 Breathe /BREETH/ Breathe in deeply through your nose, out through your mouth. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Inhale /in-HAYL/ Inhale slowly as you raise your arms. Fingerspell INHALE
26 Exhale /eks-HAYL/ Exhale gently as you lower your arms back down. Fingerspell EXHALE
27 Flexibility /flek-sih-BIL-ih-tee/ Stretching every day improves your flexibility. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Strength /STRENGTH/ Felix builds strength by doing exercises every day. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Endurance /en-DOOR-uns/ Running long distances builds endurance. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Coordination /koh-or-dih-NAY-shun/ Catching a ball while running requires coordination. Fingerspell or sign each word
💪

Amara's Tip:

"Let's do it! Stand up and follow along: JUMP! STRETCH! BEND! TWIST! BALANCE! You just exercised AND learned five vocabulary words at the same time. Music and movement make words stay in your body!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 14: Eye Care & Vision

At the Vitalis Eye Clinic, Zara learns about seeing clearly

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Eye /EYE/ Protect your eyes from bright sunlight. Point to eye
2 Vision /VIZH-un/ Good vision helps you read and drive safely. Fingerspell VISION
3 Glasses /GLAS-ez/ She wears glasses to see the board clearly. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Contacts /KON-takts/ Contact lenses sit directly on the eye. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Sunglasses /SUN-glas-ez/ Wear sunglasses to protect your eyes from UV rays. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Eye exam /EYE eg-ZAM/ Get an eye exam every two years. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Optometrist /op-TOM-eh-trist/ The optometrist checks your eyes. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Prescription /prih-SKRIP-shun/ Your glasses prescription may change over time. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Lens /LENZ/ The lens of the eye focuses light. Fingerspell LENS
10 Frame /FRAYM/ Choose a frame that fits your face. Fingerspell FRAME
