11 Pupil /PYOO-pul/ The pupil gets bigger in the dark. Fingerspell PUPIL
12 Iris /EYE-ris/ The iris is the colored part of the eye. Fingerspell IRIS
13 Retina /RET-ih-nuh/ The retina receives images at the back of the eye. Fingerspell RETINA
14 Eyelid /EYE-lid/ The eyelid protects and moistens the eye. Fingerspell EYELID
15 Eyelash /EYE-lash/ An eyelash keeps dust out of your eye. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Eyebrow /EYE-brow/ The eyebrow sits above the eye. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Tear /TEER/ A tear rolls down her cheek when she cries. Fingerspell TEAR
18 Blurry /BLUR-ee/ Without glasses everything looks blurry. Fingerspell BLURRY
19 Focus /FOH-kus/ Focus your eyes on the letters on the chart. Fingerspell FOCUS
20 Nearsighted /NEER-sy-tid/ Nearsighted means you see close things clearly. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Farsighted /FAR-sy-tid/ Farsighted means distant objects are clearer. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Blind /BLYND/ Braille helps blind people read. Fingerspell BLIND
23 Color blind /KUL-er BLYND/ Color blind people have trouble seeing certain colors. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Reading glasses /REE-ding GLAS-ez/ Reading glasses help you see small print. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Eye drops /EYE DROPS/ Use eye drops when your eyes feel dry. Fingerspell or sign each word
👁

Zara's Tip:

"Your EYES are precious! Know: GLASSES, CONTACTS, EYE EXAM."

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 13: First Aid & Injuries

At the Vitalis First Aid Station, Zara teaches emergency care

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Bandage /BAN-dij/ Wrap the bandage around the cut. Mime wrapping bandage around hand
2 Cut /KUT/ Clean the cut with soap and water. Fingerspell CUT
3 Bruise /BROOZ/ The bruise turned purple after the fall. Fingerspell BRUISE
4 Burn /BURN/ Run cold water over a minor burn. Fingerspell BURN
5 Blister /BLIS-ter/ A blister formed on her heel from new shoes. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Sprain /SPRAYN/ He sprained his ankle playing basketball. Fingerspell SPRAIN
7 Fracture /FRAK-chur/ A fracture is a broken bone. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Swelling /SWEL-ing/ Apply ice to reduce the swelling. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Ice pack /YS PAK/ Put an ice pack on the injury. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Splint /SPLINT/ A splint keeps the broken bone still. Fingerspell SPLINT
11 Wound /WOOND/ Keep the wound clean to prevent infection. Fingerspell WOUND
12 Sting /STING/ A bee sting hurts but usually heals quickly. Fingerspell STING
13 Rash /RASH/ The rash is red and itchy. Fingerspell RASH
14 Fever /FEE-ver/ A fever means your temperature is too high. Back of hand on forehead (checking temp)
15 Cough /KOF/ Cover your mouth when you cough. Fist thumps chest
16 Sneeze /SNEEZ/ Sneeze into your elbow, not your hand. Pinch nose, head jerks forward
17 Dizzy /DIZ-ee/ She felt dizzy and needed to sit down. Fingerspell DIZZY
18 Nausea /NAW-zee-uh/ Nausea means feeling sick to your stomach. Fingerspell NAUSEA
19 CPR /SEE-PEE-AR/ Learn CPR to help in an emergency. Fingerspell CPR
20 Poison /POY-zun/ Call poison control if a child swallows chemicals. Fingerspell POISON
21 Allergic reaction /uh-LUR-jik ree-AK-shun/ An allergic reaction can cause hives. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Ambulance /AM-byoo-lents/ Call an ambulance for serious injuries. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Emergency room /ee-MUR-jen-see ROOM/ Go to the emergency room for broken bones. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Stretcher /STRECH-er/ The paramedic placed the patient on a stretcher. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Recover /rih-KUV-er/ Rest and fluids help you recover faster. Fingerspell or sign each word
🩹

Zara's Tip:

"BANDAGE, ICE, SPLINT — first aid words can save a life!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 6: Healthy Habits & Hygiene

Every morning, Felix and Amara follow their Vitalis Wellness Routine. Clean body, clear mind!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Hygiene /HY-jeen/ Good hygiene keeps your body clean and healthy. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Wash /WOSH/ Wash your hands with soap for twenty seconds. Fists rub together (scrubbing)
3 Soap /SOHP/ Soap kills germs on your skin. Bend fingers on opposite palm
4 Shampoo /sham-POO/ Amara works the shampoo into her hair and rinses it out. Both claw hands scrub on head
5 Conditioner /kun-DISH-un-er/ Conditioner makes hair soft and easy to comb. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Shower /SHOW-er/ Felix takes a warm shower every morning. 5-hand above head opens/closes (water spraying)
7 Bath /BATH/ A long bath with warm water relaxes tired muscles. Both A-fists scrub up and down chest
8 Towel /TOW-ul/ Dry off with a clean towel after every shower. Flat hands rub back and forth at sides
9 Deodorant /dee-OH-der-unt/ Deodorant keeps you smelling fresh all day. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Toothbrush /TOOTH-brush/ Brush your teeth for two full minutes. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Toothpaste /TOOTH-payst/ Squeeze a pea-sized amount of toothpaste on the brush. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Floss /FLOS/ Floss reaches places the toothbrush cannot. Fingerspell FLOSS
13 Comb /KOHM/ Amara runs a wide-tooth comb through her hair. Fingerspell COMB
14 Brush /BRUSH/ A soft brush detangles hair without pulling. Mime brushing teeth
15 Nail clipper /NAYL KLIP-er/ Use a nail clipper to trim your fingernails and toenails. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Sunscreen /SUN-skreen/ Apply sunscreen before going outside in the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Lotion /LOH-shun/ Lotion keeps skin soft and prevents dryness. Fingerspell LOTION
18 Tissue /TISH-oo/ Cover your nose with a tissue when you sneeze. Fingerspell TISSUE
19 Hand sanitizer /HAND SAN-ih-ty-zer/ Use hand sanitizer when soap and water aren't available. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Sleep /SLEEP/ Eight to ten hours of sleep helps children grow strong. Open hand draws down face, eyes close
21 Rest /REST/ Your body needs rest after a long day of activity. Arms cross over chest (resting)
22 Stretch /STRECH/ Stretch your arms and legs when you wake up. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Posture /POS-chur/ Good posture means sitting and standing up straight. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Hydrate /HY-drayt/ Drink water throughout the day to stay hydrated. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Germs /JURMZ/ Germs are tiny organisms that can make you sick. Fingerspell GERMS
26 Immune system /ih-MYOON SIS-tem/ The immune system protects the body from illness. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Wellness /WEL-nes/ Wellness means taking care of your body AND your mind. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Routine /roo-TEEN/ A daily routine helps you build healthy habits. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Laundry /LAWN-dree/ Put dirty clothes in the laundry basket to be washed. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Clean /KLEEN/ A clean home keeps everyone in the family healthy. Flat hand slides across opposite palm
🧼

Amara's Tip:

"Let's practice a routine! Say: 'First, I wake up. Then, I brush my teeth. Next, I wash my face. After that, I eat breakfast.' When you say it in order, it becomes easy to remember!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 2: Inside the Body

Amara opens her big illustrated anatomy book. Inside, diagrams show how the body works, organ by organ.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Brain /BRAYN/ The brain controls everything your body does. Tap temple with index
2 Heart /HART/ The heart pumps blood through the whole body. Middle finger taps left chest
3 Lungs /LUNGZ/ The lungs fill with air every time you breathe in. Fingerspell LUNGS
4 Stomach /STUM-uk/ Food goes to the stomach to be broken down. Pat stomach
5 Liver /LIV-er/ The liver filters toxins and helps digest food. Fingerspell LIVER
6 Kidneys /KID-neez/ The kidneys clean the blood and make urine. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Intestines /in-TES-tinz/ The intestines absorb nutrients from digested food. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Bone /BOHN/ The body has 206 bones that form the skeleton. Crossed arms tap shoulders (like skeleton)
9 Skeleton /SKEL-uh-tun/ The skeleton is the framework that holds the body up. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Muscle /MUS-ul/ The muscle gets stronger when you exercise. Flex bicep, point to it
11 Tendon /TEN-dun/ A tendon connects muscle to bone. Fingerspell TENDON
12 Ligament /LIG-uh-ment/ A ligament connects one bone to another bone at a joint. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Joint /JOYNT/ The knee is a joint that lets the leg bend. Fingerspell JOINT
14 Blood /BLUD/ Blood carries oxygen to every part of the body. Red wiggling fingers drip down opposite hand
15 Blood vessel /BLUD VES-ul/ Blood vessels are the tubes that carry blood. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Artery /AR-ter-ee/ An artery carries blood away from the heart. Fingerspell ARTERY
17 Vein /VAYN/ A vein carries blood back to the heart. Fingerspell VEIN
18 Nerve /NURV/ A nerve sends signals between the brain and the body. Fingerspell NERVE
19 Skull /SKUL/ The skull is the hard bone that protects the brain. Fingerspell SKULL
20 Rib cage /RIB KAYJ/ The rib cage protects the heart and lungs. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Pelvis /PEL-vis/ The pelvis is the wide bone at the base of the spine. Fingerspell PELVIS
22 Cartilage /KAR-tih-lij/ Cartilage is softer than bone and cushions the joints. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Diaphragm /DY-uh-fram/ The diaphragm is the muscle that helps you breathe. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Spleen /SPLEEN/ The spleen helps the body fight infections. Fingerspell SPLEEN
25 Bladder /BLAD-er/ The bladder stores urine until you use the restroom. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Oxygen /OK-sih-jen/ We breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide. Fingerspell OXYGEN
27 Cell /SEL/ Every living thing is made of tiny cells. Fingerspell CELL
28 Tissue /TISH-oo/ Muscle tissue is a group of similar cells working together. Fingerspell TISSUE
29 Organ /OR-gun/ The heart is an organ that pumps blood. Fingerspell ORGAN
30 Pulse /PULS/ Amara presses two fingers to her wrist to feel her pulse. Fingerspell PULSE
💓

Amara's Tip:

"Put your hand on your chest. Can you feel your heartbeat? That's your heart pumping blood! Now jump 10 times and feel it again — it's faster!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 9: Meal Time

The table is set, the food is ready, and the whole Vitalis community gathers to eat and talk together.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Breakfast /BREK-fust/ Breakfast gives your body energy to start the day. Flat O to mouth + arm rises (EAT + MORNING)
2 Lunch /LUNCH/ Felix eats lunch at noon in the village square. Flat O to mouth + L-arm (EAT + NOON)
3 Dinner /DIN-er/ Dinner brings the whole family together at the table. Flat O to mouth + hand curves down (EAT + EVENING)
4 Snack /SNAK/ An apple is a healthy snack between meals. Fingerspell SNACK
5 Plate /PLAYT/ Each person has a clean plate at their place. Both hands outline circle (plate shape)
6 Bowl /BOHL/ Amara ladles soup into a deep bowl. Both cupped hands form bowl shape
7 Cup /KUP/ Felix drinks water from a tall glass cup. C-hand taps opposite flat palm (cup on saucer)
8 Glass /GLAS/ The glass of juice is cold and refreshing. C-hand rises from opposite palm (tall glass)
9 Mug /MUG/ Amara wraps her hands around a warm mug of tea. Fingerspell MUG
10 Fork /FORK/ Use the fork to pick up pieces of food. V-hand stabs opposite palm
11 Knife /NYF/ The butter knife spreads jam on the toast. Index finger slides along opposite index (cutting)
12 Spoon /SPOON/ Stir your tea with a small spoon. Curved H scoops from opposite palm to mouth
13 Chopsticks /CHOP-stiks/ Felix practices eating rice with chopsticks. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Napkin /NAP-kin/ Place the napkin on your lap before eating. Fingerspell NAPKIN
15 Placemat /PLAYS-mat/ The colorful placemat protects the table. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Bread /BRED/ Warm bread with butter melts in your mouth. Slice back of hand with opposite hand
17 Rice /RYS/ Fluffy white rice goes with many dishes. Both cupped hands scoop up
18 Pasta /PAH-stuh/ Long strands of pasta twirl around the fork. Fingerspell PASTA
19 Soup /SOOP/ The hot soup warms you up on a cold day. Curved hand scoops up to mouth
20 Salad /SAL-ud/ A fresh green salad starts the meal. Both claw hands toss upward (mixing)
21 Sandwich /SAND-wich/ Felix makes a sandwich with turkey and lettuce. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Eggs /EGZ/ Scrambled eggs are Felix's favorite breakfast. H-fingers crack apart (repeated)
23 Cheese /CHEEZ/ A slice of yellow cheese tops the burger. Palms press and twist together
24 Chicken /CHIK-en/ Grilled chicken is a good source of protein. G-hand pecks from mouth
25 Fish /FISH/ Fish is healthy for the brain and the body. Flat hand swims forward like fish
26 Beef /BEEF/ Beef stew cooks slowly in the big pot. Fingerspell BEEF
27 Beans /BEENZ/ Black beans are rich in protein and fiber. Fingerspell BEANS
28 Tortilla /tor-TEE-yuh/ Amara wraps beans and rice in a warm tortilla. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Cereal /SEER-ee-ul/ Felix pours cereal and milk for a quick breakfast. Fingerspell CEREAL
30 Yogurt /YOH-gurt/ Yogurt with fruit is a healthy snack. Fingerspell YOGURT
31 Juice /JOOS/ Fresh-squeezed orange juice is full of vitamin C. J-hand + DRINK motion
32 Water /WAW-ter/ Drink plenty of water throughout the day. W-hand taps chin
33 Milk /MILK/ Cold milk goes perfectly with warm cookies. C-hand squeezes (milking motion)
34 Tea /TEE/ Chamomile tea helps you relax before bed. F-hand stirs in opposite C-hand (stirring cup)
35 Dessert /dih-ZURT/ Fruit salad is a light and sweet dessert. Fingerspell or sign each word
🍽️

Felix's Tip:

"At your next meal, name EVERYTHING on the table. Say it, spell it, use it in a sentence. 'I eat my soup with a SPOON. S-P-O-O-N. The spoon is silver.' You just learned that word forever!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 12: Mental Health & Emotions

In Vitalis, the heart matters as much as the body. Felix and Amara learn to name feelings and care for their minds.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Stress /STRES/ Deep breathing helps reduce stress. Fingerspell STRESS
2 Anxiety /ang-ZY-uh-tee/ Anxiety is when you feel worried about many things. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Calm /KAHM/ Yoga and music help Felix feel calm. Both flat hands push down slowly
4 Relax /rih-LAKS/ Take a break and relax your mind and body. Fingerspell RELAX
5 Mindfulness /MYND-ful-nes/ Mindfulness means paying attention to the present moment. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Self-care /SELF-KAIR/ Self-care includes rest, exercise, and healthy eating. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Therapy /THAIR-uh-pee/ Therapy is talking to a professional about your feelings. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Counselor /KOWN-sel-er/ A school counselor helps students solve problems. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Support /suh-PORT/ Friends and family give you emotional support. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Resilience /rih-ZIL-yuns/ Resilience means bouncing back after a hard time. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Gratitude /GRAT-ih-tood/ Writing a gratitude list helps you see the good things. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Confidence /KON-fih-dens/ Practice builds confidence in speaking English. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Overwhelmed /oh-ver-WELMD/ Amara feels overwhelmed when there's too much to do. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Meditation /med-ih-TAY-shun/ Meditation means sitting quietly and focusing on breathing. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Well-being /WEL-BEE-ing/ Well-being includes feeling good in body AND mind. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Cope /KOHP/ Exercise and music help Felix cope with hard days. Fingerspell COPE
17 Journal /JUR-nul/ Writing in a journal helps you understand your feelings. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Patience /PAY-shuns/ Learning a new language requires patience. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Self-esteem /SELF-es-TEEM/ High self-esteem means believing in yourself. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Rest /REST/ Your mind needs rest just as much as your body. Arms cross over chest (resting)
🧠

Amara's Tip:

"Name your feeling right now! 'I feel ___.' Happy? Tired? Nervous? Calm? When you NAME your emotion, you take CONTROL of it."

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 11: Nutrition & Food Groups

In the Vitalis Nutrition Lab, Felix and Amara learn about the five food groups and how to build a balanced plate.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Nutrition /noo-TRISH-un/ Good nutrition means eating foods that help your body. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Protein /PROH-teen/ Chicken, beans, and eggs are rich in protein. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Carbohydrate /kar-boh-HY-drayt/ Bread and rice are carbohydrates that give you energy. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Fiber /FY-ber/ Fruits, vegetables, and whole grains have lots of fiber. Fingerspell FIBER
5 Calcium /KAL-see-um/ Milk and cheese have calcium that makes bones strong. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Iron /EYE-urn/ Spinach and red meat contain iron for healthy blood. Fingerspell IRON
7 Vitamin /VY-tuh-min/ Oranges are full of Vitamin C. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Calorie /KAL-uh-ree/ A calorie is a unit that measures energy in food. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Sugar /SHOOG-er/ Too much sugar is bad for your teeth and body. Fingers brush down chin twice
10 Fat /FAT/ Healthy fats come from nuts, fish, and avocados. Fingerspell FAT
11 Dairy /DAIR-ee/ Milk, yogurt, and cheese belong to the dairy group. Fingerspell DAIRY
12 Grain /GRAYN/ Bread, oatmeal, and pasta are made from grains. Fingerspell GRAIN
13 Whole grain /HOHL GRAYN/ Whole grain bread is healthier than white bread. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Serving size /SUR-ving SYZ/ The serving size tells you how much to eat at one time. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Balanced meal /BAL-unst MEEL/ A balanced meal has protein, grains, and vegetables. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Sodium /SOH-dee-um/ Too much sodium, or salt, raises blood pressure. Fingerspell SODIUM
17 Organic /or-GAN-ik/ Organic vegetables are grown without pesticides. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Portion /POR-shun/ A small portion of rice is about the size of your fist. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Nutrient /NOO-tree-unt/ Every nutrient has a job inside the body. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Food label /FOOD LAY-bul/ Read the food label to know what's inside the package. Fingerspell or sign each word
🥗

Felix's Tip:

"Look at your plate! Is it colorful? A plate full of different colors means different NUTRIENTS. Green spinach, red tomatoes, orange carrots — eat the rainbow!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 16: Personal Care Products

At the Vitalis Hygiene Market, Zara names every product

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Shampoo /sham-POO/ Use shampoo to wash your hair. Both claw hands scrub on head
2 Conditioner /kun-DISH-un-er/ Conditioner makes hair soft and smooth. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Soap /SOHP/ Wash your hands with soap and water. Bend fingers on opposite palm
4 Body wash /BOD-ee WOSH/ Use body wash in the shower. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Toothpaste /TOOTH-payst/ Squeeze toothpaste onto your toothbrush. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Toothbrush /TOOTH-brush/ Replace your toothbrush every three months. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Floss /FLOS/ Floss between your teeth every day. Fingerspell FLOSS
8 Mouthwash /MOWTH-wosh/ Gargle with mouthwash after brushing. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Deodorant /dee-OH-der-unt/ Apply deodorant under your arms. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Lotion /LOH-shun/ Put lotion on your hands to keep them soft. Fingerspell LOTION
11 Sunscreen /SUN-skreen/ Apply sunscreen before going outside. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Razor /RAY-zer/ Use a razor carefully to shave. Fingerspell RAZOR
13 Comb /KOHM/ Comb your hair to remove tangles. Fingerspell COMB
14 Brush /BRUSH/ Brush your hair before leaving the house. Mime brushing teeth
15 Hair dryer /HAIR DRY-er/ Use the hair dryer on a low setting. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Cotton ball /KOT-un BAWL/ Use a cotton ball to apply medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Band-Aid /BAND-ayd/ Put a Band-Aid on the small cut. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Tissues /TISH-ooz/ Grab a tissue when you need to sneeze. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Hand sanitizer /HAND SAN-ih-ty-zer/ Use hand sanitizer when soap is not available. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Nail clipper /NAYL KLIP-er/ Trim your nails with a nail clipper. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Mirror /MEER-er/ Check your appearance in the mirror. Fingerspell MIRROR
22 Towel /TOW-ul/ Dry your face with a clean towel. Flat hands rub back and forth at sides
23 Washcloth /WOSH-kloth/ Scrub your face gently with a washcloth. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Tweezers /TWEE-zerz/ Use tweezers to remove a splinter. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Q-tip /KYOO-tip/ Use a Q-tip carefully to clean your ears. Fingerspell Q-TIP
🧴

Zara's Tip:

"Name every product in your bathroom! SHAMPOO, TOOTHPASTE, DEODORANT!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 15: Sleep & Rest

In the Vitalis Dream Chamber, rest is medicine

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Sleep /SLEEP/ Get eight hours of sleep every night. Open hand draws down face, eyes close
2 Bed /BED/ Make your bed every morning. Fingerspell BED
3 Pillow /PIL-oh/ Fluff the pillow before going to sleep. Fingerspell PILLOW
4 Blanket /BLANK-it/ Pull the blanket up when you feel cold. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Mattress /MAT-res/ A good mattress supports your back. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Alarm clock /uh-LARM KLOK/ The alarm clock wakes you up at six. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Nap /NAP/ A short nap can recharge your energy. Fingerspell NAP
8 Dream /DREEM/ She had a wonderful dream last night. Fingerspell DREAM
9 Nightmare /NYT-mair/ A nightmare is a scary dream. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Rest /REST/ Rest your body after a long day. Arms cross over chest (resting)
11 Yawn /YAWN/ A yawn means you are tired. Fingerspell YAWN
12 Snore /SNOR/ He snores loudly when he sleeps. Fingerspell SNORE
13 Drowsy /DROW-zee/ Feeling drowsy means you are getting sleepy. Fingerspell DROWSY
14 Insomnia /in-SOM-nee-uh/ Insomnia means having trouble falling asleep. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Pajamas /puh-JAH-muhz/ Put on pajamas before going to bed. P-hand at cheek (SLEEP) + CLOTHES
16 Lullaby /LUL-uh-by/ Sing a lullaby to help the baby sleep. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Bedtime /BED-tym/ Bedtime is at nine o'clock on school nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Wake up /WAYK UP/ Wake up early to have a good morning. Pinch at eyes, flick open
19 Stretch /STRECH/ Stretch your body when you first wake up. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Rested /REST-ed/ I feel rested after a good night of sleep. Fingerspell RESTED
21 Toss and turn /TOS and TURN/ She tossed and turned all night. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Night light /NYT LYT/ The night light glows softly in the room. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Sleepy /SLEEP-ee/ The baby looks sleepy after lunch. Fingerspell SLEEPY
24 Relax /rih-LAKS/ Relax your muscles before bed. Fingerspell RELAX
25 Routine /roo-TEEN/ A bedtime routine helps you fall asleep faster. Fingerspell or sign each word
😴

Zara's Tip:

"Sleep is MEDICINE! BED, PILLOW, REST, DREAM — healthy bodies need healthy sleep."

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 17: Sports & Fitness

At the Vitalis Arena, healthy bodies are built through sport

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Soccer /SOK-er/ Soccer is the most popular sport worldwide. Mime kicking ball to side
2 Basketball /BAS-kit-bawl/ Dribble and shoot in basketball. Both hands mime shooting basketball
3 Baseball /BAYS-bawl/ Hit the baseball with the bat. Both hands mime swinging bat
4 Football /FOOT-bawl/ The quarterback throws the football. Both hands mime catching football
5 Tennis /TEN-is/ Hit the tennis ball over the net. Mime swinging tennis racket
6 Swimming /SWIM-ing/ Swimming is great exercise for the whole body. Both hands mime swim strokes
7 Running /RUN-ing/ Running helps build strong legs. L-hands, thumb pulls on index, move forward fast
8 Volleyball /VOL-ee-bawl/ Spike the volleyball over the net. Both hands push up and forward (setting/spiking)
9 Golf /GOLF/ Swing the golf club to hit the ball. Mime golf swing
10 Gymnastics /jim-NAS-tiks/ Gymnastics requires balance and flexibility. V on opposite palm, flip over (somersault)
11 Referee /ref-er-EE/ The referee blows the whistle to start the game. Both flat hands make T-shape (time out)
12 Score /SKOR/ The final score was three to one. Both hands tally in air
13 Champion /CHAM-pee-un/ The champion wins the tournament. Modified A on opposite fist (WINNER)
14 Trophy /TROH-fee/ The team celebrated with the trophy. Both Y-hands stack high
15 Medal /MED-ul/ She won a gold medal at the Olympics. Mime placing medal around neck
16 Stadium /STAY-dee-um/ The stadium holds fifty thousand fans. Both hands outline dome shape
17 Court /KORT/ Play basketball on the indoor court. C-hands outline rectangle
18 Field /FEELD/ The soccer field is one hundred yards long. F-hand circles outward (open area)
19 Pool /POOL/ Swim laps in the pool for exercise. P-hands mime swimming
20 Track /TRAK/ Run four laps around the track for one mile. Both index fingers trace track lanes forward
21 Uniform /YOO-nih-form/ Wear your team uniform to the game. U-hands brush down chest
22 Coach /KOHCH/ The coach designs the practice drills. C-hand taps shoulder (CAPTAIN)
23 Warm up /WARM UP/ Always warm up before exercising. Both hands rub together, then stretch
24 Cool down /KOOL DOWN/ Cool down with stretches after the game. Both hot hands cool, wave gently
25 Sportsmanship /SPORTS-mun-ship/ Good sportsmanship means playing fair. S on S (FAIR + PLAY)
⚽

Zara's Tip:

"What sport do YOU play? SOCCER, BASKETBALL, SWIMMING? Say it in English!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 4: The Dentist

Amara visits Dr. Sona's dental office in Vitalis. Her teeth are checked, cleaned, and polished sparkling white.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Dentist /DEN-tist/ The dentist examines Amara's teeth carefully. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Dental hygienist /DEN-tul hy-JEE-nist/ The dental hygienist cleans Amara's teeth with special tools. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Dental chair /DEN-tul CHAIR/ Amara leans back in the comfortable dental chair. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Tooth /TOOTH/ Each tooth has a different shape and job. Fingerspell TOOTH
5 Molar /MOH-ler/ The flat molar in the back grinds food into small pieces. Fingerspell MOLAR
6 Canine /KAY-nyn/ The pointed canine tooth tears food apart. Fingerspell CANINE
7 Incisor /in-SY-zer/ The sharp incisor at the front bites into an apple. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Gum /GUM/ Healthy gums are pink and firm around each tooth. Fingerspell GUM
9 Cavity /KAV-ih-tee/ A cavity is a small hole caused by sugar and plaque. Fingerspell CAVITY
10 Filling /FIL-ing/ The dentist puts a filling in the cavity to fix it. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Crown /KROWN/ A crown covers and protects a damaged tooth. Fingerspell CROWN
12 Braces /BRAY-siz/ Braces straighten teeth slowly over time. Fingerspell BRACES
13 Retainer /rih-TAY-ner/ A retainer keeps teeth in their new position after braces. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Dental X-ray /DEN-tul EKS-ray/ The dental X-ray shows what's happening under the gums. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Fluoride /FLOR-eyed/ Fluoride treatment makes teeth stronger. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Plaque /PLAK/ Plaque is a sticky film of bacteria on the teeth. Fingerspell PLAQUE
17 Floss /FLOS/ Use floss to clean between your teeth every day. Fingerspell FLOSS
18 Toothbrush /TOOTH-brush/ Amara brushes with a soft toothbrush twice a day. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Toothpaste /TOOTH-payst/ Mint toothpaste makes your mouth feel fresh and clean. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Mouthwash /MOWTH-wash/ Swish mouthwash around for thirty seconds, then spit. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Dental mirror /DEN-tul MIR-er/ The dental mirror lets the dentist see the back teeth. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Drill /DRIL/ The small drill removes the decayed part of the tooth. Fingerspell DRILL
23 Numbing gel /NUM-ing JEL/ Numbing gel prevents pain during the procedure. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Rinse /RINS/ Rinse your mouth with water after the cleaning. Fingerspell RINSE
25 Wisdom tooth /WIZ-dum TOOTH/ Wisdom teeth usually appear in the late teens. Fingerspell or sign each word
😁

Amara's Tip:

"How do you take care of your teeth? Say these three sentences: 'I brush my teeth. I floss my teeth. I visit the dentist.' Now YOU are in charge of your smile!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 3: The Doctor's Office

Felix visits Dr. Esperanza in Vitalis for his yearly checkup. Everything in the office helps keep people healthy.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Doctor /DOK-ter/ The doctor asks Felix how he feels today. D-hand or M-hand taps opposite wrist
2 Nurse /NURS/ The nurse takes Felix's temperature first. N-hand taps opposite wrist
3 Patient /PAY-shunt/ Felix is the patient visiting the doctor today. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Waiting room /WAY-ting ROOM/ Felix reads a magazine in the waiting room. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Appointment /uh-POYNT-ment/ The appointment is at ten o'clock in the morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Examination room /ig-zam-ih-NAY-shun ROOM/ The examination room has a high table covered in paper. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Stethoscope /STETH-uh-skohp/ The doctor listens to Felix's heart with a stethoscope. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Thermometer /ther-MOM-ih-ter/ The thermometer shows that Felix has no fever. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Blood pressure cuff /BLUD PRESH-er KUF/ The blood pressure cuff wraps around Felix's arm. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Scale /SKAYL/ Felix stands on the scale to find out his weight. Both flat hands balance like scale
11 Height chart /HYT CHART/ The height chart on the wall shows Felix has grown two inches. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Syringe /suh-RINJ/ The nurse prepares the syringe for the vaccination. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Bandage /BAN-dij/ A small bandage covers the spot where Felix got his shot. Mime wrapping bandage around hand
14 Cotton ball /KOT-un BAWL/ The nurse dabs the skin with a soft cotton ball. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Latex gloves /LAY-teks GLUVZ/ The doctor wears latex gloves during the examination. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Tongue depressor /TUNG dih-PRES-er/ The tongue depressor helps the doctor look at the throat. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Eye chart /EYE CHART/ Felix reads the letters on the eye chart across the room. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Reflex hammer /REE-fleks HAM-er/ The reflex hammer taps gently on the knee. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Otoscope /OH-tuh-skohp/ The otoscope has a light to look inside the ear. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 X-ray /EKS-ray/ The X-ray shows the bones inside Felix's hand. Fingerspell X-RAY
21 Prescription /prih-SKRIP-shun/ The doctor writes a prescription for medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Diagnosis /dy-ag-NOH-sis/ The diagnosis is the doctor's explanation of what is wrong. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Symptom /SIMP-tum/ A cough is a common symptom of a cold. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Fever /FEE-ver/ A fever means your body temperature is higher than normal. Back of hand on forehead (checking temp)
25 Allergy /AL-er-jee/ Felix has an allergy to pollen that makes him sneeze. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Vaccine /vak-SEEN/ A vaccine helps the body fight serious diseases. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Check-up /CHEK-up/ A yearly check-up keeps you healthy. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Insurance card /in-SHOOR-uns KARD/ Amara shows her insurance card at the front desk. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Referral /rih-FUR-ul/ The doctor gives a referral to see a specialist. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Vital signs /VY-tul SYNZ/ Vital signs include heart rate, breathing, and temperature. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Blood test /BLUD TEST/ A blood test checks for infections and nutrients. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Cast /KAST/ A plaster cast keeps a broken bone in place while it heals. Fingerspell CAST
33 Crutches /KRUCH-ez/ Crutches help you walk when your leg is injured. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Wheelchair /WEEL-chair/ A wheelchair provides mobility for people who cannot walk. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 First aid kit /FURST AYD KIT/ The first aid kit has bandages, antiseptic, and tape. Fingerspell or sign each word
36 Emergency /ih-MUR-jun-see/ Call 911 in an emergency for immediate help. Fingerspell or sign each word
37 Ambulance /AM-byoo-luns/ The ambulance rushes to the hospital with its sirens on. Fingerspell or sign each word
38 Hospital /HOS-pih-tul/ The hospital is where people go for serious medical care. H-hand draws cross on upper arm
39 Surgery /SUR-jer-ee/ Surgery is an operation to fix something inside the body. Fingerspell or sign each word
40 Recovery /rih-KUV-er-ee/ Recovery is the time it takes to get better after being sick. Fingerspell or sign each word
🩺

Felix's Tip:

"Practice saying these words to someone you trust. If you ever need to visit a doctor, knowing these words will help you describe what you feel. 'I have a symptom. I have a fever.' See? You can do it!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 1: The Human Body

Felix stands in front of Amara's full-length mirror. Together, they name every part of the body from head to toe.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Head /HED/ Felix balances a book on top of his head. Bent hand touches temple, then chin
2 Forehead /FOR-hed/ Amara feels her forehead to check for a fever. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Temple /TEM-pul/ The temple is the flat area on the side of the forehead. Fingerspell TEMPLE
4 Eyebrow /EYE-brow/ Felix raises one eyebrow when he's curious. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Eyelid /EYE-lid/ The eyelid closes to protect the eye. Fingerspell EYELID
6 Eyelash /EYE-lash/ A tiny eyelash fell on Amara's cheek. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Eye /EYE/ Felix has bright brown eyes that see everything. Point to eye
8 Pupil /PYOO-pul/ The black pupil in the center of the eye gets bigger in the dark. Fingerspell PUPIL
9 Iris /EYE-ris/ The iris is the colorful part of the eye. Fingerspell IRIS
10 Nose /NOHZ/ Amara wrinkles her nose when she laughs. Point to nose
11 Nostril /NOS-trul/ Air flows in and out through each nostril. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Cheek /CHEEK/ Felix's cheeks turn red when he runs fast. Fingerspell CHEEK
13 Ear /EER/ The ear catches sound waves from every direction. Point to ear
14 Earlobe /EER-lohb/ The soft earlobe hangs at the bottom of the ear. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Mouth /MOWTH/ Open your mouth wide and say "Ahh." Point to mouth
16 Lip /LIP/ Amara puts balm on her dry lip. Fingerspell LIP
17 Tongue /TUNG/ The tongue helps you taste and speak. Point to tongue
18 Tooth /TOOTH/ Felix lost his first baby tooth last week. Fingerspell TOOTH
19 Gum /GUM/ The gums hold your teeth in place. Fingerspell GUM
20 Chin /CHIN/ Felix rests his chin on his hand while thinking. Fingerspell CHIN
21 Jaw /JAW/ The jaw moves up and down when you chew food. Fingerspell JAW
22 Neck /NEK/ Amara wraps a warm scarf around her neck in winter. Bent hand touches side of neck
23 Throat /THROHT/ A sore throat makes it hard to swallow. Fingerspell THROAT
24 Shoulder /SHOHL-der/ Felix puts his backpack over one shoulder. Palm taps opposite shoulder
25 Arm /ARM/ Amara stretches both arms above her head. Hand runs down opposite arm
26 Elbow /EL-boh/ The elbow is the joint in the middle of the arm. Fingerspell ELBOW
27 Forearm /FOR-arm/ The forearm connects the elbow to the wrist. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Wrist /RIST/ Amara wears a colorful bracelet on her wrist. Fingerspell WRIST
29 Hand /HAND/ Felix waves his hand to say hello. Flat hand chops opposite wrist
30 Palm /PAHM/ Place the seed in the palm of your hand. Fingerspell PALM
31 Finger /FING-ger/ Point your finger at the word and say it aloud. Point to and wiggle finger
32 Thumb /THUM/ The thumb helps you grip and hold things. Point to thumb
33 Knuckle /NUK-ul/ Each finger has three small knuckles. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Fingernail /FING-ger-nayl/ Keep your fingernails clean and trimmed. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Chest /CHEST/ Take a deep breath and feel your chest expand. Pat chest
36 Rib /RIB/ The ribs protect the heart and lungs inside the chest. Fingerspell RIB
37 Back /BAK/ Amara stretches her back after sitting for a long time. Pat back with hand over shoulder
38 Spine /SPYN/ The spine is the long bone that runs down the center of the back. Fingerspell SPINE
39 Waist /WAYST/ Felix ties a belt around his waist. Fingerspell WAIST
40 Hip /HIP/ The hip joint allows the leg to move in many directions. Fingerspell HIP
41 Abdomen /AB-doh-men/ The abdomen is the area between the chest and the hips. Fingerspell or sign each word
42 Thigh /THY/ The thigh is the strong upper part of the leg. Fingerspell THIGH
43 Knee /NEE/ Felix bends his knee when he stretches. Point to knee
44 Shin /SHIN/ The shin is the front part of the leg below the knee. Fingerspell SHIN
45 Calf /KAF/ The calf muscle at the back of the lower leg helps you jump. Fingerspell CALF
46 Ankle /ANG-kul/ The ankle connects the foot to the leg. Fingerspell ANKLE
47 Foot /FOOT/ Each foot has five toes at the end. Point to foot
48 Heel /HEEL/ The heel is the back part of the foot. Fingerspell HEEL
49 Toe /TOH/ Wiggle each toe to wake up your feet! Point to toe area
50 Skin /SKIN/ Skin is the largest organ of the body. Pinch skin on hand
🤸

Felix's Tip:

"Stand up! Touch your head, then your shoulders, then your knees, then your toes. Say each word as you touch it. Head! Shoulders! Knees! Toes! Now — faster!"

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 8: The Kitchen

Back home, Amara and Felix prepare a healthy meal from scratch. Every tool in the kitchen has a name and a job.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Stove /STOHV/ Amara turns on the stove to heat the pot. S-hands mime turning burner knobs
2 Oven /UV-en/ The oven preheats to three hundred and fifty degrees. Flat hand slides into opposite hand (putting in oven)
3 Pot /POT/ The large pot holds soup for the whole family. P-hands mime stirring in pot
4 Pan /PAN/ Felix cracks an egg into the hot frying pan. Mime holding pan handle, flip
5 Skillet /SKIL-it/ The cast-iron skillet cooks vegetables evenly. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Spatula /SPACH-oo-luh/ Use the spatula to flip the pancake. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Whisk /WISK/ Amara uses the whisk to mix the batter smooth. Fingerspell WHISK
8 Ladle /LAY-dul/ The ladle scoops soup into each bowl. Fingerspell LADLE
9 Tongs /TONGZ/ Tongs grip hot food without burning your fingers. Fingerspell TONGS
10 Cutting board /KUT-ing BORD/ Felix chops vegetables on the wooden cutting board. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Knife /NYF/ A sharp knife slices the tomato neatly. Index finger slides along opposite index (cutting)
12 Peeler /PEE-ler/ The peeler removes the skin from the potato. Fingerspell PEELER
13 Grater /GRAY-ter/ Amara grates cheese on top of the pasta. Fingerspell GRATER
14 Colander /KOL-un-der/ The colander drains hot water from the pasta. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Measuring cup /MEZH-er-ing KUP/ The measuring cup shows exactly one cup of flour. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Measuring spoon /MEZH-er-ing SPOON/ One teaspoon of salt is all you need. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Mixing bowl /MIK-sing BOHL/ Combine the ingredients in the big mixing bowl. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Blender /BLEN-der/ The blender makes fruit smoothies in seconds. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Toaster /TOH-ster/ The bread pops up golden from the toaster. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Can opener /KAN OH-pen-er/ The can opener turns around the edge of the can. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Rolling pin /ROH-ling PIN/ Amara rolls out dough flat with the rolling pin. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Baking sheet /BAY-king SHEET/ Place the cookies on the baking sheet. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Pot holder /POT HOHL-der/ Always use a pot holder to pick up something hot. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Apron /AY-prun/ Felix ties his apron before he starts cooking. Fingerspell APRON
25 Timer /TY-mer/ Set the timer for fifteen minutes. T-hand taps wrist
26 Recipe /RES-ih-pee/ The recipe says to add two eggs and one cup of sugar. Fingerspell RECIPE
27 Ingredient /in-GREE-dee-unt/ Each ingredient is measured before mixing. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Boil /BOYL/ Bring the water to a boil before adding the pasta. Fingerspell BOIL
29 Simmer /SIM-er/ Let the sauce simmer on low heat for twenty minutes. Fingerspell SIMMER
30 Stir /STUR/ Stir the soup slowly so it doesn't stick to the bottom. Fingerspell STIR
31 Chop /CHOP/ Chop the onion into small, even pieces. Fingerspell CHOP
32 Dice /DYS/ Dice the tomatoes into tiny cubes. Fingerspell DICE
33 Slice /SLYS/ Slice the bread into thick pieces for sandwiches. Fingerspell SLICE
34 Grill /GRIL/ Felix grills chicken outside on the barbecue. Fingerspell GRILL
35 Bake /BAYK/ Bake the muffins at three hundred and seventy-five degrees. Fingerspell BAKE
36 Fry /FRY/ Fry the plantains in a pan with a little oil. Fingerspell FRY
37 Steam /STEEM/ Steam the broccoli to keep it bright green and crunchy. Fingerspell STEAM
38 Roast /ROHST/ Roast the vegetables in the oven until golden brown. Fingerspell ROAST
39 Season /SEE-zun/ Season the food with salt, pepper, and herbs. Fingerspell SEASON
40 Serve /SURV/ Amara serves dinner on colorful plates. Fingerspell SERVE
🍳

Amara's Tip:

"Cooking is a language too! Practice: 'First, I chop. Then, I boil. Next, I stir. Finally, I serve.' Sequence words are MAGIC words."

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 5: The Pharmacy

After the doctor visit, Felix and Amara walk next door to the pharmacy to pick up medicine and supplies.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Pharmacy /FAR-muh-see/ The pharmacy is where you pick up prescribed medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Pharmacist /FAR-muh-sist/ The pharmacist explains how to take the medicine safely. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Prescription /prih-SKRIP-shun/ Felix hands the prescription to the pharmacist. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Medicine /MED-ih-sin/ Take your medicine at the same time every day. Middle finger rocks on opposite palm
5 Pill /PIL/ Felix swallows the small pill with a glass of water. Mime popping pill into mouth with thumb
6 Tablet /TAB-let/ The chewable tablet tastes like grape. Fingerspell TABLET
7 Capsule /KAP-sul/ The capsule has powder inside a smooth shell. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Liquid medicine /LIK-wid MED-ih-sin/ Liquid medicine is measured with a tiny cup. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Dosage /DOH-sij/ The label tells you the correct dosage for your age. Fingerspell DOSAGE
10 Side effect /SYD ih-FEKT/ Drowsiness is a common side effect of this medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Vitamins /VY-tuh-minz/ Amara takes her daily vitamins each morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Bandage /BAN-dij/ A clean bandage protects a cut from infection. Mime wrapping bandage around hand
13 Adhesive tape /ad-HEE-siv TAYP/ Adhesive tape holds gauze in place over a wound. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Gauze /GAWZ/ Clean gauze pads absorb blood from a small cut. Fingerspell GAUZE
15 Antiseptic /an-tih-SEP-tik/ Apply antiseptic to a scrape to prevent infection. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Ointment /OYNT-ment/ The ointment soothes the rash and helps it heal. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Cough syrup /KAWF SIR-up/ Cough syrup helps calm a persistent cough. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Eye drops /EYE DROPS/ Eye drops relieve dry, itchy eyes. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Inhaler /in-HAY-ler/ An inhaler helps people with asthma breathe easier. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Thermometer /ther-MOM-ih-ter/ Use a thermometer to check your temperature at home. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Ice pack /EYS PAK/ An ice pack reduces swelling on a sore ankle. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Heating pad /HEE-ting PAD/ A heating pad soothes sore, tight muscles. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Sling /SLING/ The sling holds a hurt arm still so it can heal. Fingerspell SLING
24 Over-the-counter /OH-ver thuh KOWN-ter/ Some medicines are over-the-counter and don't need a prescription. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Refill /REE-fil/ Amara calls the pharmacy for a refill on her vitamins. Fingerspell REFILL
💊

Felix's Tip:

"NEVER take medicine without a grown-up's help. The pharmacist is your friend — they make sure the medicine is right for YOU."

Land 5 of 7

❤️ Vitalis

Guided by Felix the Body Guardian & Amara the Wellness Guide

Scene 7: The Produce Market

Felix and Amara visit the outdoor Vitalis Produce Market every Saturday morning. The stalls overflow with colorful fruits and vegetables.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Apple /AP-ul/ Felix picks a crisp red apple from the basket. X-knuckle twists on cheek
2 Banana /buh-NAN-uh/ The yellow banana is a perfect snack for energy. Mime peeling banana from index finger
3 Orange /OR-inj/ Amara peels an orange and shares the sections. C-hand squeezes near chin (like squeezing orange)
4 Grape /GRAYP/ Purple grapes grow in clusters on the vine. Fingerspell GRAPE
5 Strawberry /STRAW-bair-ee/ The sweet strawberry is small, red, and juicy. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Blueberry /BLOO-bair-ee/ Tiny blueberries are packed with vitamins. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Watermelon /WAW-ter-mel-un/ A big slice of watermelon cools you down in summer. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Pineapple /PYN-ap-ul/ The pineapple has a rough outside and sweet inside. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Mango /MANG-goh/ The ripe mango is soft, sweet, and bright orange. Fingerspell MANGO
10 Peach /PEECH/ The fuzzy peach is a favorite summer fruit. Fingerspell PEACH
11 Pear /PAIR/ The green pear is smooth and sweet. Fingerspell PEAR
12 Cherry /CHAIR-ee/ Two red cherries hang together on a tiny stem. Fingerspell CHERRY
13 Lemon /LEM-un/ Felix squeezes a lemon to make fresh lemonade. Fingerspell LEMON
14 Lime /LYM/ The lime is small, green, and very sour. Fingerspell LIME
15 Avocado /av-uh-KAH-doh/ Amara mashes the avocado to make guacamole. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Tomato /tuh-MAY-toh/ A ripe tomato is bright red and full of juice. Fingerspell TOMATO
17 Carrot /KAIR-ut/ The orange carrot grows underground in the soil. Fingerspell CARROT
18 Broccoli /BROK-uh-lee/ Broccoli looks like a tiny green tree. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Spinach /SPIN-itch/ Dark green spinach leaves are full of iron. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Lettuce /LET-us/ Crisp lettuce is the base of a fresh salad. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Cucumber /KYOO-kum-ber/ The cool cucumber is mostly water inside. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Onion /UN-yun/ Cutting an onion makes Amara's eyes water. Fingerspell ONION
23 Garlic /GAR-lik/ A clove of garlic adds strong flavor to any dish. Fingerspell GARLIC
24 Potato /puh-TAY-toh/ The brown potato can be baked, mashed, or fried. Fingerspell POTATO
25 Sweet potato /SWEET puh-TAY-toh/ The orange sweet potato is naturally sweet and nutritious. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Bell pepper /BEL PEP-er/ Bell peppers come in red, yellow, and green. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Corn /KORN/ Felix bites into a buttery ear of corn. Fingerspell CORN
28 Green beans /GREEN BEENZ/ Fresh green beans snap when you bend them. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Celery /SEL-er-ee/ Crunchy celery sticks are great with peanut butter. Fingerspell CELERY
30 Mushroom /MUSH-room/ Sliced mushrooms go perfectly in soup and stir-fry. Fingerspell or sign each word
31 Zucchini /zoo-KEE-nee/ The green zucchini grows quickly in the summer garden. Fingerspell or sign each word
32 Eggplant /EG-plant/ The purple eggplant is shiny and shaped like an egg. Fingerspell or sign each word
33 Cauliflower /KAW-lih-flow-er/ White cauliflower looks like broccoli's pale cousin. Fingerspell or sign each word
34 Cabbage /KAB-ij/ A head of cabbage has many layers of crunchy leaves. Fingerspell or sign each word
35 Radish /RAD-ish/ The small red radish has a peppery bite. Fingerspell RADISH
36 Beet /BEET/ The dark red beet stains everything it touches. Fingerspell BEET
37 Squash /SKWOSH/ Butternut squash makes a creamy, warm soup. Fingerspell SQUASH
38 Peas /PEEZ/ Tiny green peas pop out of their pods. Fingerspell PEAS
39 Herbs /URBZ/ Fresh herbs like basil and cilantro add flavor to food. Fingerspell HERBS
40 Basil /BAZ-ul/ The aromatic basil leaves top the tomato salad. Fingerspell BASIL
41 Cilantro /sih-LAN-troh/ Cilantro is a key ingredient in many Latin American dishes. Fingerspell or sign each word
42 Mint /MINT/ Fresh mint leaves make tea taste cool and refreshing. Fingerspell MINT
43 Parsley /PAR-slee/ A sprig of parsley decorates the plate. Fingerspell or sign each word
44 Ginger /JIN-jer/ Ginger root adds a warm, spicy zing to food. Fingerspell GINGER
45 Coconut /KOH-kuh-nut/ The coconut has hard brown shell and sweet white flesh. Fingerspell or sign each word
46 Plantain /PLAN-tin/ A ripe plantain is fried until golden and crispy. Fingerspell or sign each word
47 Vendor /VEN-der/ The friendly vendor weighs the apples on a scale. Fingerspell VENDOR
48 Bag /BAG/ Amara puts the vegetables in a reusable bag. Fingerspell BAG
49 Scale /SKAYL/ The vendor puts the oranges on the scale to weigh them. Both flat hands balance like scale
50 Price /PRYS/ The price of the strawberries is two dollars per box. F-hand strikes opposite palm downward
🍎

Felix's Tip:

"Next time you go to the store, see how many of these fruits and vegetables you can find! Can you say their names? Can you describe their colors and shapes?"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 17: Banking & Online Services

At the Sophia Digital Bank, Dara manages money online

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Account /uh-KOWNT/ Open a checking account at the bank. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Deposit /dih-POZ-it/ Deposit your paycheck into savings. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Withdraw /with-DRAW/ Withdraw cash from the ATM. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Transfer /TRANS-fer/ Transfer money between your accounts. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Balance /BAL-ents/ Check your account balance online. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Statement /STAYT-ment/ Review your monthly bank statement. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 ATM /AY-TEE-EM/ The ATM is outside the bank. Fingerspell ATM
8 PIN /PIN/ Never share your PIN with anyone. Fingerspell PIN
9 Password /PAS-wurd/ Create a strong password for online banking. P-hand taps opposite palm (SECRET + WORD)
10 Mobile app /MOH-bul AP/ Use the mobile app to check your balance. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Direct deposit /dih-REKT dih-POZ-it/ Set up direct deposit to get paid faster. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Check /CHEK/ Write a check to pay the landlord. Fingerspell CHECK
13 Wire transfer /WYR TRANS-fer/ A wire transfer sends money electronically. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Savings account /SAY-vingz uh-KOWNT/ A savings account earns interest. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Checking account /CHEK-ing uh-KOWNT/ Use your checking account for daily expenses. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Credit card /KRED-it KARD/ Pay with a credit card and pay the balance monthly. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Debit card /DEB-it KARD/ A debit card takes money directly from your account. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Loan /LOHN/ Apply for a loan to buy a car. Fingerspell LOAN
19 Mortgage /MOR-gij/ A mortgage is a loan to buy a house. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Payment /PAY-ment/ Make your car payment on time every month. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Due date /DOO DAYT/ The payment due date is the fifteenth. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Overdue /OH-ver-doo/ The bill is overdue by two weeks. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Late fee /LAYT FEE/ Avoid the late fee by paying on time. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Fraud /FROD/ Report fraud to your bank immediately. Fingerspell FRAUD
25 Security /seh-KYOOR-ih-tee/ Online security protects your personal information. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏦

Dara's Tip:

"ACCOUNT, DEPOSIT, TRANSFER — banking English is ESSENTIAL!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 13: Childcare & Parenting

In Sophia, parents and caregivers learn the vocabulary of raising children — from infancy to school age.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Daycare /DAY-kair/ Drop off the baby at daycare before going to work. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Babysitter /BAY-bee-sit-er/ The babysitter watches the children on Friday nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Diaper /DY-per/ Change the baby's diaper every two hours. Fingerspell DIAPER
4 Bottle /BOT-ul/ Warm the baby's bottle to the right temperature. Fingerspell BOTTLE
5 Formula /FOR-myoo-luh/ Baby formula provides nutrition when breastfeeding isn't possible. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Crib /KRIB/ The baby sleeps safely in the crib. Fingerspell CRIB
7 Stroller /STROH-ler/ Push the stroller through the park on sunny days. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Pacifier /PAS-ih-fy-er/ The pacifier soothes the baby when she cries. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Car seat /KAR SEET/ Every child must ride in a car seat until a certain age. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Highchair /HY-chair/ The baby eats in the highchair at the table. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Sippy cup /SIP-ee KUP/ The toddler drinks milk from a sippy cup. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Teething /TEE-thing/ Teething means the baby's first teeth are coming in. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Lullaby /LUL-uh-by/ Sing a soft lullaby to help the baby fall asleep. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Nap /NAP/ The toddler takes a nap after lunch. Fingerspell NAP
15 Potty training /POT-ee TRAY-ning/ Potty training teaches toddlers to use the toilet. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Pediatrician /pee-dee-uh-TRISH-un/ The pediatrician is a doctor for children. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Immunization /im-yoo-nih-ZAY-shun/ Immunization shots protect children from diseases. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Play date /PLAY DAYT/ The children have a play date at the park. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Preschool /PREE-skool/ Preschool prepares children for kindergarten. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Bedtime /BED-tym/ Bedtime is eight o'clock on school nights. Fingerspell or sign each word
👶

Athena's Tip:

"Parenting words are LIFE words! Practice: 'The baby needs a DIAPER change. It's time for a NAP. We have a PEDIATRICIAN appointment.' Knowing these words helps you care for your family."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 9: Community Helpers & Services

The people of Sophia take care of each other. Every helper has a title and a task.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Firefighter /FYR-fy-ter/ Call a firefighter if you see smoke or fire. B-hand at forehead (shield)
2 Paramedic /pair-uh-MED-ik/ A paramedic arrives quickly in an ambulance. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Police officer /puh-LEES OF-ih-ser/ A police officer helps keep the community safe. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Mail carrier /MAYL KAIR-ee-er/ The mail carrier delivers letters to every house. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Garbage collector /GAR-bij kuh-LEK-ter/ The garbage collector picks up trash every week. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Crossing guard /KROS-ing GARD/ The crossing guard helps children cross the street safely. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Librarian /ly-BRAIR-ee-un/ The librarian helps you find the right book. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Coach /KOHCH/ The coach teaches teamwork and sportsmanship. C-hand taps shoulder (CAPTAIN)
9 Tutor /TOO-ter/ The volunteer tutor helps adults learn English. Fingerspell TUTOR
10 Counselor /KOWN-sel-er/ A counselor listens and helps you work through problems. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Interpreter /in-TUR-preh-ter/ An interpreter translates spoken words in real time. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Case worker /KAYS WUR-ker/ The case worker helps families access social services. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Dispatcher /dis-PACH-er/ The 911 dispatcher sends help to the right location. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Lifeguard /LYF-gard/ The lifeguard watches swimmers at the pool. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Security guard /sih-KYOOR-ih-tee GARD/ A security guard monitors the building entrance. Fingerspell or sign each word
🤝

Ezra's Tip:

"Community helpers are everywhere! Next time you see one, say: 'Thank you!' in English. 'Thank you, Officer. Thank you, Mail Carrier. Thank you, Librarian.' Gratitude is a language that everyone speaks."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 11: Forms & Documents

Living in Sophia means knowing your paperwork. Ezra and Athena practice filling out the forms that matter most.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Form /FORM/ Fill out the form with your personal information. Fingerspell FORM
2 Signature /SIG-nuh-chur/ Sign your signature on the line at the bottom. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Social Security number /SOH-shul sih-KYOOR-ih-tee NUM-ber/ Your Social Security number is private — never share it. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Birth certificate /BURTH sur-TIF-ih-kit/ A birth certificate proves where and when you were born. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Driver's license /DRY-verz LY-sens/ A driver's license is a common form of photo ID. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 State ID /STAYT EYE-DEE/ A state ID works as identification if you don't drive. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Passport /PAS-port/ A passport is required for international travel. P-hand opens like small book
8 Green card /GREEN KARD/ A green card allows a person to live and work in the U.S. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Work permit /WURK PUR-mit/ A work permit gives you legal permission to be employed. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Tax return /TAKS rih-TURN/ File your tax return by April fifteenth. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 W-2 form /DUB-ul-yoo TOO FORM/ The W-2 form shows how much you earned last year. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Application /ap-lih-KAY-shun/ Fill out the application completely and neatly. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Notary /NOH-tuh-ree/ A notary stamps official documents to verify them. Fingerspell NOTARY
14 Photocopy /FOH-toh-kop-ee/ Make a photocopy of every important document. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Translate /TRANS-layt/ Ask someone to translate the form into your language. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Print /PRINT/ Print your name clearly in capital letters. Fingerspell PRINT
17 Date of birth /DAYT UV BURTH/ Write your date of birth as month, day, year. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Marital status /MAIR-ih-tul STAT-us/ Marital status means single, married, or divorced. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Emergency contact /ih-MUR-jen-see KON-takt/ List an emergency contact person and their phone number. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Legal name /LEE-gul NAYM/ Write your full legal name as it appears on your ID. Fingerspell or sign each word
📋

Ezra's Tip:

"Practice filling out forms! Write your full name, your address, your date of birth, and your phone number. These four things appear on EVERY form."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 6: Government & Civics

In the Sophia Hall of Governance, Athena explains how communities organize and make decisions together.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Government /GUV-ern-ment/ The government makes laws to protect citizens. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 President /PREZ-ih-dent/ The president is the leader of the country. Both C-hands at temples, rise to close as fists
3 Governor /GUV-er-ner/ The governor leads the state government. G-hand circles at temple
4 Mayor /MAY-er/ The mayor leads the city government. M-hand at shoulder, like official
5 Law /LAW/ A law is a rule that everyone must follow. L-hand taps opposite flat palm (LAW or RULE)
6 Right /RYT/ Every person has the right to free speech. R-hand rises (right/entitlement)
7 Vote /VOHT/ Citizens vote to choose their leaders. F-hand drops into opposite O-hand (putting ballot in box)
8 Election /ih-LEK-shun/ An election is when people vote for their leaders. E-hand + VOTE motion
9 Constitution /kon-stih-TOO-shun/ The Constitution is the highest law of the land. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Amendment /uh-MEND-ment/ An amendment is a change or addition to the Constitution. A-hand rewrites on opposite palm
11 Court /KORT/ Legal disputes are settled in court. C-hands outline rectangle
12 Judge /JUJ/ The judge makes decisions based on the law. Fingerspell JUDGE
13 Jury /JUR-ee/ A jury is a group of citizens who decide a court case. Fingerspell JURY
14 Trial /TRY-ul/ The trial determines if the person is guilty or not guilty. Fingerspell TRIAL
15 Citizen /SIT-ih-zen/ A citizen has both rights and responsibilities. Both C-hands touch shoulders then meet at center
16 Tax /TAKS/ Citizens pay taxes to fund public services. T-hand or X-hand taps opposite palm
17 Community /kuh-MYOO-nih-tee/ A community is a group of people living in the same area. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Neighborhood /NAY-ber-hood/ Athena knows everyone in her neighborhood. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Democracy /deh-MOK-ruh-see/ In a democracy, the people choose their leaders. D-hands circle each other
20 Freedom /FREE-dum/ Freedom means the ability to act and speak without restraint. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Justice /JUS-tis/ Justice means fairness and equal treatment for everyone. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Liberty /LIB-er-tee/ The Statue of Liberty symbolizes freedom. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Flag /FLAG/ The flag of a country represents its people and values. Fingerspell FLAG
24 Pledge /PLEJ/ Students recite the Pledge of Allegiance each morning. Fingerspell PLEDGE
25 National anthem /NASH-un-ul AN-them/ Stand respectfully when the national anthem plays. Fingerspell or sign each word
⚖️

Athena's Tip:

"Knowing civics words makes you a powerful citizen! Practice: 'I VOTE. I have RIGHTS. I follow the LAW. I live in a DEMOCRACY.' These words are your tools for belonging."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 12: Housing & Renting

Finding a place to live in Sophia means learning the language of landlords, leases, and home life.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Lease /LEES/ A lease is a contract to rent a home for a set time. Fingerspell LEASE
2 Tenant /TEN-unt/ A tenant is the person who rents the apartment. Fingerspell TENANT
3 Landlord /LAND-lord/ The landlord owns the building and collects rent. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Security deposit /sih-KYOOR-ih-tee dih-POZ-it/ The security deposit is returned when you move out. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Rent /RENT/ Rent is due on the first day of every month. Fingerspell RENT
6 Apartment /uh-PART-ment/ The apartment has two bedrooms and one bathroom. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Studio /STOO-dee-oh/ A studio apartment has one room for living and sleeping. Fingerspell STUDIO
8 Maintenance /MAYN-tuh-nuns/ Call maintenance if the sink is leaking. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Eviction /ih-VIK-shun/ An eviction is when a landlord forces you to move out. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Move in /MOOV IN/ Move in day is the first of September. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Move out /MOOV OWT/ Clean the apartment before you move out. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Utilities /yoo-TIL-ih-teez/ Utilities like water and gas may be included in rent. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Furnished /FUR-nisht/ A furnished apartment comes with beds and a couch. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Unfurnished /un-FUR-nisht/ An unfurnished apartment is empty — bring your own furniture. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Application fee /ap-lih-KAY-shun FEE/ The application fee is twenty-five dollars. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Credit check /KRED-it CHEK/ The landlord runs a credit check before approving you. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Roommate /ROOM-mayt/ A roommate shares the apartment and splits rent. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Neighborhood /NAY-ber-hood/ Choose a safe neighborhood close to school or work. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Repair /rih-PAIR/ The landlord must repair broken appliances. Fingerspell REPAIR
20 Inspection /in-SPEK-shun/ The inspection checks the apartment's condition. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏠

Athena's Tip:

"Know your renting words! Practice: 'I am a TENANT. I signed a LEASE. My RENT is due on the first. I call MAINTENANCE for repairs.' These sentences make you a confident renter."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 15: Job Interview Skills

At the Sophia Career Center, Dara practices for the big interview

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Interview /IN-ter-vyoo/ Prepare for the job interview. I-hands alternate near mouth
2 Resume /REZ-oo-may/ Submit your resume with the application. Fingerspell RESUME
3 Qualifications /kwol-ih-fih-KAY-shunz/ List your qualifications on your resume. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Experience /ek-SPEER-ee-ents/ She has five years of work experience. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Reference /REF-er-ents/ Provide three references who know your work. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Strengths /STRENGTHS/ My strengths include teamwork and communication. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Weakness /WEEK-nes/ Describe a weakness and how you overcame it. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Punctual /PUNK-choo-ul/ Be punctual and arrive five minutes early. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Professional /proh-FESH-un-ul/ Dress in professional clothes for the interview. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Handshake /HAND-shayk/ Give a firm handshake when you meet the interviewer. Clasp hands together, mime shaking
11 Eye contact /EYE KON-takt/ Make eye contact when answering questions. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Confident /KON-fih-dent/ Speak in a confident and clear voice. Both C-hands pull down from chest firmly
13 Nervous /NUR-vus/ It is normal to feel nervous before an interview. Both 5-hands shake in front
14 Hire /HYR/ The company will hire the best candidate. H-hand sweeps in toward body
15 Position /poh-ZISH-un/ What position are you applying for? Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Salary /SAL-er-ee/ The salary for this job is competitive. Flat hand scoops off opposite palm toward body
17 Benefits /BEN-ih-fits/ Benefits include health insurance and vacation. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Full-time /FULL-tym/ A full-time job is forty hours per week. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Part-time /PART-tym/ She works part-time while attending school. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Shift /SHIFT/ The morning shift starts at seven. Fingerspell SHIFT
21 Background check /BAK-grownd CHEK/ The employer runs a background check. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Follow up /FOL-oh UP/ Follow up with a thank-you email. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Offer /OF-er/ She received a job offer on Friday. Fingerspell OFFER
24 Accept /ak-SEPT/ He decided to accept the position. Fingerspell ACCEPT
25 Decline /dih-KLYN/ She had to decline the offer. Fingerspell or sign each word
💼

Dara's Tip:

"Practice: TELL ME ABOUT YOURSELF, WHAT ARE YOUR STRENGTHS? Confidence starts with words!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 14: Legal & Court Terms

Ezra and Athena visit the Sophia Courthouse to learn the vocabulary of justice, rights, and the legal system.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Attorney /uh-TUR-nee/ An attorney is a person trained in the law. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Plaintiff /PLAYN-tif/ The plaintiff is the person who brings a case to court. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Defendant /dih-FEN-dunt/ The defendant is the person accused in court. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Evidence /EV-ih-dens/ Evidence includes facts and objects that prove a point. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Witness /WIT-nes/ A witness tells the court what they saw or heard. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Testimony /TES-tih-moh-nee/ The witness gives testimony under oath. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Verdict /VUR-dikt/ The verdict is the jury's decision — guilty or not guilty. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Sentence /SEN-tens/ The judge gives a sentence after a guilty verdict. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Fine /FYN/ A fine is money you pay as a penalty. Thumb of 5-hand taps chest
10 Bail /BAYL/ Bail is money paid to be released before trial. Fingerspell BAIL
11 Guilty /GIL-tee/ Guilty means the person committed the crime. Fingerspell GUILTY
12 Not guilty /NOT GIL-tee/ Not guilty means the person is found innocent. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Oath /OHTH/ An oath is a promise to tell the truth. Fingerspell OATH
14 Summons /SUM-unz/ A summons orders you to appear in court. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Subpoena /suh-PEE-nuh/ A subpoena requires a person to testify or bring documents. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Arraignment /uh-RAYN-ment/ The arraignment is the first court appearance. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Misdemeanor /mis-dih-MEE-ner/ A misdemeanor is a minor crime with lighter punishment. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Felony /FEL-uh-nee/ A felony is a serious crime with heavy punishment. Fingerspell FELONY
19 Probation /proh-BAY-shun/ Probation means supervision instead of prison time. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Legal aid /LEE-gul AYD/ Legal aid provides free lawyers for people who can't afford one. Fingerspell or sign each word
⚖️

Ezra's Tip:

"Knowing legal words protects you. Practice: 'I have the right to an ATTORNEY. I understand my RIGHTS.' If you ever need help, contact LEGAL AID — it's free."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 7: Money Management & Bills

Ezra and Athena sit at the kitchen table in Sophia, organizing their monthly expenses.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Bill /BIL/ Pay the electricity bill before the due date. Fingerspell BILL
2 Rent /RENT/ Rent is the monthly payment for your apartment. Fingerspell RENT
3 Mortgage /MOR-gij/ A mortgage is a monthly payment for a house. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Utilities /yoo-TIL-ih-teez/ Utilities include water, gas, and electricity. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Electricity /ee-lek-TRIS-ih-tee/ The electricity bill goes up in the summer. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Water bill /WAW-ter BIL/ The water bill is paid every two months. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Gas bill /GAS BIL/ The gas bill is higher in the winter for heating. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Insurance /in-SHOOR-uns/ Health insurance helps pay for doctor visits. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Car insurance /KAR in-SHOOR-uns/ Car insurance is required by law in most states. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Due date /DOO DAYT/ Pay before the due date to avoid a late fee. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Late fee /LAYT FEE/ A late fee is charged if you pay after the due date. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Income /IN-kum/ Income is the total money you earn. Hand scoops money toward body
13 Expense /ek-SPENS/ Rent is the biggest monthly expense. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Budget /BUJ-it/ A budget tracks what you earn and what you spend. B-hands on opposite palm (MONEY + PLAN)
15 Debt /DET/ Pay off your debt as quickly as you can. Fingerspell DEBT
16 Credit score /KRED-it SKOR/ A high credit score helps you get better loan rates. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Minimum payment /MIN-ih-mum PAY-ment/ Always pay at least the minimum payment on time. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Paycheck /PAY-chek/ The paycheck arrives every two weeks. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Direct deposit /dih-REKT dih-POZ-it/ Direct deposit puts money straight into your bank. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Savings /SAY-vingz/ Put ten percent of every paycheck into savings. Both hands scoop toward body (gathering)
🧾

Ezra's Tip:

"Adulting in English means knowing money words. Practice: 'I pay RENT. I pay my BILLS. I have a BUDGET. I put money in SAVINGS.' These are power sentences for grown-up life!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 1: Occupations & Careers

Ezra and Athena visit the Sophia Career Fair, where workers from every field share what they do.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Job /JOB/ A job is work you do to earn money. S-hand taps opposite wrist (WORK)
2 Career /kuh-REER/ A career is a long-term path in a field you love. Fingerspell CAREER
3 Boss /BOS/ The boss manages the workers in the office. B-hand taps shoulder (military stripes)
4 Employee /em-PLOY-ee/ An employee works for a company or business. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Employer /em-PLOY-er/ An employer is the person or company that hires you. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Salary /SAL-uh-ree/ A salary is the money paid for a full year of work. Flat hand scoops off opposite palm toward body
7 Wages /WAY-jez/ Wages are money paid by the hour. Fingerspell WAGES
8 Benefits /BEN-ih-fits/ Benefits may include health insurance and vacation days. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Resume /REZ-oo-may/ A resume lists your skills, education, and work experience. Fingerspell RESUME
10 Interview /IN-ter-vyoo/ Dress professionally for the job interview. I-hands alternate near mouth
11 Application /ap-lih-KAY-shun/ Fill out the application form to apply for the job. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Hire /HYR/ The company will hire two new employees next month. H-hand sweeps in toward body
13 Promotion /proh-MOH-shun/ A promotion means moving to a higher position. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Part-time /PART-tym/ A part-time job is fewer than forty hours a week. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Full-time /FOOL-tym/ A full-time job is usually forty hours per week. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Shift /SHIFT/ Ezra works the morning shift from six to two. Fingerspell SHIFT
17 Overtime /OH-ver-tym/ Working extra hours is called overtime. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Coworker /KOH-wur-ker/ A coworker is a person you work with. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Manager /MAN-uh-jer/ The manager assigns tasks to the team. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Retire /rih-TYR/ Grandfather plans to retire at age sixty-five. Both R-hands relax downward
21 Volunteer /vol-un-TEER/ A volunteer works without pay to help the community. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Skill /SKIL/ Typing is a useful skill for office work. Fingerspell SKILL
23 Experience /ek-SPEER-ee-ens/ Three years of experience is required for this position. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Qualification /kwol-ih-fih-KAY-shun/ A qualification proves you are trained for the job. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Deadline /DED-lyn/ The deadline for the project is Friday. Fingerspell or sign each word
💼

Ezra's Tip:

"What job do YOU want? Say: 'I want to be a ___.' Now say WHY: 'Because I like ___.' Having a career dream in two languages doubles your chances!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 16: Resume & Applications

Dara builds a winning resume at the Sophia Writing Lab

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Application /ap-lih-KAY-shun/ Fill out the job application completely. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Cover letter /KUV-er LET-er/ Write a cover letter to introduce yourself. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Objective /ob-JEK-tiv/ The objective states what kind of job you want. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Education /ej-oo-KAY-shun/ List your education history. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Degree /dih-GREE/ She earned a degree in nursing. Fingerspell DEGREE
6 Certificate /ser-TIF-ih-kit/ A certificate proves you completed training. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Skills /SKILZ/ List your skills such as bilingual or computer. Fingerspell SKILLS
8 Achievement /uh-CHEEV-ment/ Describe your achievements in past jobs. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Employer /em-PLOY-er/ Your previous employer can give a reference. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Employee /em-PLOY-ee/ Every employee must attend the meeting. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Deadline /DED-lyn/ Submit the application before the deadline. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Online /ON-lyn/ Apply online through the company website. Fingerspell ONLINE
13 Upload /UP-lohd/ Upload your resume as a PDF file. S-fist pushes up, opens to claw
14 Contact info /KON-takt IN-foh/ Include your contact info at the top. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Work history /WURK HIS-ter-ee/ List your work history from most recent. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Volunteer /vol-un-TEER/ Include volunteer experience on your resume. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Training /TRAYN-ing/ Complete the required training before starting. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Certification /ser-tih-fih-KAY-shun/ Get your CPR certification for the job. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Licensed /LY-sensd/ She is a licensed cosmetologist. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Bilingual /by-LING-gwul/ Being bilingual is a valuable skill. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Proofread /PROOF-reed/ Proofread your resume for spelling errors. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Font /FONT/ Use a clean professional font. Fingerspell FONT
23 Format /FOR-mat/ Keep the format simple and easy to read. Fingerspell FORMAT
24 Summary /SUM-er-ee/ Write a brief career summary at the top. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Portfolio /port-FOH-lee-oh/ Bring your portfolio to show your work. Fingerspell or sign each word
📄

Dara's Tip:
