"Your resume is your FIRST IMPRESSION. Make every word count!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 10: Rights & Responsibilities

In the Sophia Town Hall, Athena leads a workshop on what it means to be a responsible citizen.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Right /RYT/ You have the right to speak freely. R-hand rises (right/entitlement)
2 Responsibility /rih-spon-sih-BIL-ih-tee/ Paying taxes is a responsibility of every citizen. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Duty /DOO-tee/ Jury duty is serving on a jury when called. Fingerspell DUTY
4 Respect /rih-SPEKT/ Respect other people's opinions and beliefs. R-hand arcs from forehead outward
5 Equal /EE-kwul/ All people are created equal. Both bent hands tap together evenly
6 Fair /FAIR/ A fair system treats everyone the same. Fingerspell FAIR
7 Obey /oh-BAY/ Citizens must obey the law. Fingerspell OBEY
8 Protect /proh-TEKT/ It is everyone's duty to protect the environment. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Serve /SURV/ Many people serve their community through volunteer work. Fingerspell SERVE
10 Cooperate /koh-OP-er-ayt/ Cooperate with others to solve big problems. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Participate /par-TIS-ih-payt/ Participate in school events and community meetings. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Honest /ON-est/ Being honest means telling the truth. Fingerspell HONEST
13 Tolerance /TOL-er-uns/ Tolerance means accepting people who are different from you. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Diversity /dih-VUR-sih-tee/ Diversity means having people of many backgrounds together. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Inclusion /in-KLOO-zhun/ Inclusion means making sure everyone feels welcome. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌍

Athena's Tip:

"Big words, big ideas! Say: 'I believe in EQUALITY. I practice TOLERANCE. I value DIVERSITY.' These words describe the world we want to build — together."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 5: Safety & Emergencies

In the Sophia Safety Center, Ezra and Athena learn the words that can save a life.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Emergency /ih-MUR-jun-see/ In an emergency, call 911 immediately. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Danger /DAYN-jer/ The "Danger" sign warns you to stay away. Fingerspell DANGER
3 Caution /KAW-shun/ Use caution when crossing the busy street. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Warning /WOR-ning/ The warning light flashes when there is a problem. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Exit /EK-sit/ Find the nearest exit in case of emergency. Fingerspell EXIT
6 Fire escape /FYR es-KAYP/ Use the fire escape if the stairs are blocked. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Fire extinguisher /FYR ek-STING-gwish-er/ The fire extinguisher puts out small fires. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Smoke detector /SMOHK dih-TEK-ter/ The smoke detector beeps loudly when it senses smoke. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 First aid /FURST AYD/ First aid is immediate help before the doctor arrives. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 CPR /SEE-PEE-AR/ CPR can save a life by keeping blood flowing. Fingerspell CPR
11 Poison /POY-zun/ Keep poison and chemicals away from children. Fingerspell POISON
12 Hazard /HAZ-erd/ A wet floor is a slipping hazard. Fingerspell HAZARD
13 Evacuate /ih-VAK-yoo-ayt/ Evacuate the building calmly and quickly. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Rescue /RES-kyoo/ Firefighters rescue people from burning buildings. Fingerspell RESCUE
15 Safe /SAYF/ Stay in a safe place during a storm. S-hands cross at wrists, then pull apart
16 Helmet /HEL-met/ Wear a helmet when riding a bicycle. Both C-hands come down on head
17 Seat belt /SEET BELT/ Always buckle your seat belt in the car. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Life jacket /LYF JAK-it/ Wear a life jacket when you are on a boat. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Crosswalk /KROS-wawk/ Use the crosswalk to cross the street safely. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Stop, drop, and roll /STOP DROP and ROHL/ If your clothes catch fire: stop, drop, and roll. Fingerspell or sign each word
🚨

Ezra's Tip:

"Safety words can SAVE YOUR LIFE. Memorize your address and phone number in English. Practice: 'My address is ___. My phone number is ___. I need HELP.' You are always safer when you can communicate."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 8: School Subjects & Education

The Sophia Academy teaches every subject. Athena walks through the halls naming each one.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Math /MATH/ Math teaches you to solve problems with numbers. Fingerspell MATH
2 Science /SY-ens/ Science explores how the world works. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 History /HIS-tor-ee/ History teaches us about the past. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 English /ING-lish/ English class focuses on reading and writing. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Reading /REE-ding/ Reading opens doors to new worlds. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Writing /RY-ting/ Practice writing sentences every day. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Art /ART/ Art class lets you draw, paint, and create. Fingerspell ART
8 Music /MYOO-zik/ Music class teaches rhythm, melody, and instruments. Fingerspell MUSIC
9 Physical education /FIZ-ih-kul ej-oo-KAY-shun/ Physical education keeps your body active. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Geography /jee-OG-ruh-fee/ Geography studies land, water, and people. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Social studies /SOH-shul STUD-eez/ Social studies covers communities and cultures. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Computer science /kum-PYOO-ter SY-ens/ Computer science teaches coding and technology. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Foreign language /FOR-in LANG-gwij/ Learning a foreign language connects you to new cultures. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Health /HELTH/ Health class teaches about nutrition and safety. Fingerspell HEALTH
15 Diploma /dih-PLOH-muh/ A diploma proves you completed school. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Degree /dih-GREE/ A college degree opens doors to more careers. Fingerspell DEGREE
17 GED /JEE-EE-DEE/ The GED is an alternative to a high school diploma. Fingerspell GED
18 Certificate /ser-TIF-ih-kit/ A certificate shows you completed a training program. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Tutor /TOO-ter/ A tutor gives extra help with difficult subjects. Fingerspell TUTOR
20 Scholarship /SKOL-er-ship/ A scholarship pays for part or all of college. Fingerspell or sign each word
📖

Athena's Tip:

"What is your favorite subject? Say: 'My favorite subject is ___. I like it because ___.' Education has its own vocabulary, and knowing it helps you succeed!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 18: Taxes & Filing

At the Sophia Tax Office, Dara learns that taxes support community

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Tax /TAKS/ Pay your income tax every year. T-hand or X-hand taps opposite palm
2 Tax return /TAKS rih-TURN/ File your tax return by April fifteenth. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Refund /REE-fund/ You may get a refund if you overpaid taxes. Fingerspell REFUND
4 W-2 /DUB-ul-yoo TOO/ Your employer sends a W-2 form each January. Fingerspell W-2
5 Income /IN-kum/ Report all income on your tax return. Hand scoops money toward body
6 Deduction /dih-DUK-shun/ A deduction reduces the tax you owe. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Dependent /dih-PEN-dent/ Claim your children as dependents. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Filing status /FY-ling STAT-us/ Choose your filing status: single or married. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Federal /FED-er-ul/ Federal taxes go to the national government. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 State tax /STAYT TAKS/ State tax rates vary by state. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Sales tax /SAYLZ TAKS/ Sales tax is added at the register. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Property tax /PROP-er-tee TAKS/ Property tax is based on home value. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Audit /AW-dit/ An audit reviews your tax records. Fingerspell AUDIT
14 Receipt /rih-SEET/ Keep receipts for tax deductions. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Accountant /uh-KOWN-tent/ Hire an accountant to prepare your taxes. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 IRS /EYE-AR-ES/ The IRS collects federal taxes. Fingerspell IRS
17 Extension /ek-STEN-shun/ File an extension if you need more time. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Withholding /with-HOHL-ding/ Withholding is tax taken from your paycheck. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Exempt /eg-ZEMPT/ Some items are exempt from sales tax. Fingerspell EXEMPT
20 Penalty /PEN-ul-tee/ A penalty is charged for late filing. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Gross pay /GROHS PAY/ Gross pay is your total earnings before taxes. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Net pay /NET PAY/ Net pay is what you take home after taxes. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Tax bracket /TAKS BRAK-it/ Your tax bracket determines your rate. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Volunteer tax help /VOL-un-TEER TAKS HELP/ Free volunteer tax help is available. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 E-file /EE-fyl/ E-file your tax return for faster processing. Fingerspell E-FILE
📋

Dara's Tip:

"TAX, REFUND, W-2, DEDUCTION — these words put money BACK in your pocket!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 3: The Workplace

Ezra shows the inside of a typical Sophia office. Everything has a name and a function.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Office /OF-is/ The office has desks, computers, and a copier. O-hands outline box shape
2 Desk /DESK/ Ezra keeps his files organized on his desk. Sign TABLE (forearms stacked, then flat)
3 Computer /kum-PYOO-ter/ The computer is the main tool for most office work. C-hand bounces up opposite forearm
4 Printer /PRIN-ter/ The printer makes paper copies of digital documents. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Copier /KOP-ee-er/ Use the copier to make duplicates of the report. Fingerspell COPIER
6 File cabinet /FYL KAB-ih-net/ Old documents are stored in the file cabinet. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Stapler /STAY-pler/ Use the stapler to hold pages together. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Paper clip /PAY-per KLIP/ A paper clip holds a few pages together. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Sticky notes /STIK-ee NOHTS/ Write reminders on sticky notes and post them on your monitor. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Meeting /MEE-ting/ The team meeting is at ten o'clock in the conference room. Both 5-hands come together, fingertips meet
11 Conference room /KON-fer-ens ROOM/ The conference room has a long table and a projector. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Break room /BRAYK ROOM/ Eat lunch and relax in the break room. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Cubicle /KYOO-bih-kul/ Each worker has a cubicle with walls for privacy. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Supervisor /SOO-per-vy-zer/ The supervisor checks in with the team each morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Project /PROJ-ekt/ The team is working on a big project this month. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Report /rih-PORT/ Write a report at the end of every week. Fingerspell REPORT
17 Email /EE-mayl/ Send the document by email. Index shoots into opposite C-hand (mail + computer)
18 Schedule /SKED-yool/ Check the schedule for today's meetings. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
19 Paycheck /PAY-chek/ Ezra receives his paycheck every two weeks. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Time card /TYM KARD/ Swipe your time card to clock in and out. Fingerspell or sign each word
🏢

Ezra's Tip:

"Even if you've never been in an office, knowing these words prepares you. Practice: 'I work at a DESK. I use a COMPUTER. I go to MEETINGS.' Every word brings you closer to your goals."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 4: Tools & Construction

The Sophia Build Yard is where Ezra and Athena learn the names of every tool used to create and repair.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Hammer /HAM-er/ Use a hammer to drive nails into wood. Fingerspell HAMMER
2 Nail /NAYL/ The nail holds two boards together. Fingerspell NAIL
3 Screw /SKROO/ A screw twists into wood for a tight hold. Fingerspell SCREW
4 Screwdriver /SKROO-dry-ver/ Turn the screw with a screwdriver. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Wrench /RENCH/ The wrench tightens and loosens bolts. Fingerspell WRENCH
6 Pliers /PLY-erz/ Pliers grip and pull things with their jaws. Fingerspell PLIERS
7 Drill /DRIL/ The drill makes holes in wood, metal, or walls. Fingerspell DRILL
8 Saw /SAW/ Use the saw to cut boards to the right length. Fingerspell SAW
9 Level /LEV-ul/ The level checks if a surface is flat. Fingerspell LEVEL
10 Tape measure /TAYP MEZH-er/ Measure twice with the tape measure, cut once. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Ladder /LAD-er/ Climb the ladder to reach the high shelf. Fingerspell LADDER
12 Paint roller /PAYNT ROH-ler/ The paint roller covers large areas quickly. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Paintbrush /PAYNT-brush/ Use a paintbrush for edges and small details. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Bucket /BUK-it/ Fill the bucket with soapy water for cleaning. Fingerspell BUCKET
15 Shovel /SHUV-ul/ Use a shovel to dig a hole in the ground. Fingerspell SHOVEL
16 Rake /RAYK/ Rake the leaves into a pile in the fall. Fingerspell RAKE
17 Wheelbarrow /WEEL-bair-oh/ The wheelbarrow carries heavy loads of dirt. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Hard hat /HARD HAT/ Wear a hard hat on the construction site for safety. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Safety goggles /SAYF-tee GOG-ulz/ Safety goggles protect your eyes from dust and debris. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Work gloves /WURK GLUVZ/ Work gloves protect your hands from sharp objects. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Blueprint /BLOO-print/ The blueprint shows the plan for the new building. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Cement /sih-MENT/ Cement hardens into a strong, solid surface. Fingerspell CEMENT
23 Brick /BRIK/ Stack the bricks to build a wall. Fingerspell BRICK
24 Lumber /LUM-ber/ Lumber is wood cut into boards for building. Fingerspell LUMBER
25 Wire /WYR/ The electrician runs wire through the walls. Fingerspell WIRE
26 Pipe /PYP/ Water flows through a copper pipe. Fingerspell PIPE
27 Bolt /BOHLT/ Tighten the bolt with a wrench. Fingerspell BOLT
28 Nut /NUT/ The nut screws onto the bolt to hold it. Fingerspell NUT
29 Sandpaper /SAND-pay-per/ Sandpaper makes rough wood smooth. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Toolbox /TOOL-boks/ Keep all your tools organized in the toolbox. Fingerspell or sign each word
🔨

Athena's Tip:

"Tools have FUN names! Say them aloud: HAMMER, DRILL, WRENCH, SANDPAPER. Now pick up an object near you and describe it: 'This is a ___. It is made of ___. We use it to ___."

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 2: Types of Workers

In every corner of Sophia, people work to keep the community running. Athena lists them all.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Doctor /DOK-ter/ A doctor diagnoses and treats illnesses. D-hand or M-hand taps opposite wrist
2 Nurse /NURS/ A nurse cares for patients in the hospital. N-hand taps opposite wrist
3 Teacher /TEE-cher/ A teacher helps students learn new things. Both flat hands at temples, move outward + PERSON
4 Firefighter /FYR-fy-ter/ A firefighter rescues people and puts out fires. B-hand at forehead (shield)
5 Police officer /puh-LEES OF-ih-ser/ A police officer protects and serves the community. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Lawyer /LOY-er/ A lawyer helps people understand and follow the law. Fingerspell LAWYER
7 Chef /SHEF/ A chef creates delicious meals in a restaurant kitchen. Flip hand on opposite palm (COOK) + PERSON
8 Mechanic /meh-KAN-ik/ A mechanic fixes cars and engines. S-fists twist opposite directions (wrench)
9 Electrician /ee-lek-TRISH-un/ An electrician installs and repairs electrical wiring. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Plumber /PLUM-er/ A plumber fixes pipes, sinks, and toilets. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Carpenter /KAR-pen-ter/ A carpenter builds and repairs things made of wood. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Painter /PAYN-ter/ A painter covers walls with fresh coats of paint. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Farmer /FAR-mer/ A farmer grows crops and raises animals. Fingerspell FARMER
14 Bus driver /BUS DRY-ver/ The bus driver safely takes passengers to their stops. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Postal worker /POHS-tul WUR-ker/ The postal worker delivers mail and packages. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Cashier /ka-SHEER/ The cashier rings up purchases at the store. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Janitor /JAN-ih-ter/ The janitor keeps the building clean and tidy. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Accountant /uh-KOWN-tant/ An accountant manages money and financial records. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Pharmacist /FAR-muh-sist/ A pharmacist prepares and gives out medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Dentist /DEN-tist/ A dentist takes care of your teeth and gums. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Veterinarian /vet-er-ih-NAIR-ee-un/ A veterinarian is a doctor for animals. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Engineer /en-juh-NEER/ An engineer designs buildings, roads, and machines. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Architect /AR-kih-tekt/ An architect designs the plans for new buildings. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Programmer /PROH-gram-er/ A programmer writes code for computers and apps. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Artist /AR-tist/ An artist creates paintings, sculptures, or music. Fingerspell ARTIST
26 Journalist /JUR-nuh-list/ A journalist writes news stories for the newspaper. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Translator /TRANS-lay-ter/ A translator converts words from one language to another. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Social worker /SOH-shul WUR-ker/ A social worker helps families in difficult situations. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Paramedic /pair-uh-MED-ik/ A paramedic provides emergency medical care. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Scientist /SY-en-tist/ A scientist studies the world through experiments. Fingerspell or sign each word
👷

Athena's Tip:

"Ask someone what they do! Say: 'What is your JOB? What do you DO for work?' Listen for the answer. Then say: 'That sounds interesting!' Making conversation is a SKILL too!"

Land 6 of 7

⚖️ Sophia

Guided by Ezra the Builder & Athena the Wise

Scene 19: Voting & Elections

At the Sophia Civic Center, Dara exercises the power of the vote

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Vote /VOHT/ Every citizen has the right to vote. F-hand drops into opposite O-hand (putting ballot in box)
2 Ballot /BAL-ut/ Mark your choices on the ballot. Fingerspell BALLOT
3 Candidate /KAN-dih-dayt/ The candidate gave a speech at the rally. C-hand shakes in space or fingerspell
4 Election /ee-LEK-shun/ The election is held on the first Tuesday of November. E-hand + VOTE motion
5 Register /REJ-ih-ster/ Register to vote before the deadline. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Polling place /POHL-ing PLAYS/ Go to your polling place to cast your vote. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Campaign /kam-PAYN/ The campaign lasted several months. C-hand pumps forward repeatedly
8 Debate /dih-BAYT/ The candidates debated on television. Index fingers point at each other alternately
9 Platform /PLAT-form/ The party platform outlines their goals. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Democrat /DEM-oh-krat/ The Democrat ran for city council. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Republican /rih-PUB-lih-kun/ The Republican candidate won the local race. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Independent /in-dih-PEN-dent/ An independent candidate has no party. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Issue /ISH-oo/ Education is an important issue this election. Fingerspell ISSUE
14 Polling /POHL-ing/ Polling shows the race is very close. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Absentee ballot /AB-sen-tee BAL-ut/ An absentee ballot lets you vote by mail. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Primary /PRY-mair-ee/ The primary election narrows the candidates. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 General election /JEN-er-ul ee-LEK-shun/ The general election determines the winner. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Inauguration /in-aw-gyoo-RAY-shun/ The inauguration ceremony is in January. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Governor /GUV-er-ner/ The governor leads the state government. G-hand circles at temple
20 Mayor /MAY-er/ The mayor is the leader of the city. M-hand at shoulder, like official
21 Senator /SEN-uh-ter/ Each state elects two senators. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Representative /rep-rih-ZEN-tuh-tiv/ Your representative speaks for your district. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Amendment /uh-MEND-ment/ An amendment changes the constitution. A-hand rewrites on opposite palm
24 Democracy /dih-MOK-ruh-see/ Democracy means government by the people. D-hands circle each other
25 Civic duty /SIV-ik DOO-tee/ Voting is a civic duty. Fingerspell or sign each word
🗳

Dara's Tip:

"VOTE, BALLOT, CANDIDATE — your voice matters! Use it!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 12: Ages & Life Stages

The Garden of Time grows a tree for every stage of life. Elias and Selene walk along its rings.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Baby /BAY-bee/ A baby is a child from birth to about one year old. Rock arms like cradling baby
2 Toddler /TOD-ler/ A toddler is learning to walk and talk. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Child /CHYLD/ A child is a young person between two and twelve. Pat downward in air (showing height)
4 Teenager /TEEN-ay-jer/ A teenager is between thirteen and nineteen years old. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Adult /uh-DULT/ An adult is a fully grown person, usually eighteen or older. A-hand moves from forehead to chest
6 Senior /SEEN-yer/ A senior citizen is over sixty-five years old. Fingerspell SENIOR
7 Age /AYJ/ What is your age? I am thirty-two years old. Pull chin down with C-hand
8 Birthday /BURTH-day/ Your birthday is the day you were born. Middle finger touches chin, then chest
9 Anniversary /an-ih-VUR-suh-ree/ An anniversary marks years since a special event. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Generation /jen-er-AY-shun/ A generation is about twenty-five to thirty years. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Youth /YOOTH/ Youth is the time of life when you are young. Fingerspell YOUTH
12 Elderly /EL-der-lee/ The elderly deserve our respect and care. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Grow up /GROH UP/ What do you want to be when you grow up? Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Milestone /MYL-stohn/ A first word is a milestone in every baby's life. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Lifespan /LYF-span/ The average human lifespan is about seventy-five years. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Mature /muh-CHOOR/ To mature means to grow older and wiser. Fingerspell MATURE
17 Infant /IN-funt/ An infant needs constant care from a parent. Fingerspell INFANT
18 Middle-aged /MID-ul AYJD/ A middle-aged person is usually between forty and sixty. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Aging /AY-jing/ Aging is a natural part of every living thing. Fingerspell AGING
20 Memory /MEM-or-ee/ A memory is something you remember from the past. Fingerspell MEMORY
🌳

Selene's Tip:

"Draw your own 'Tree of Life!' Label the rings: INFANT, TODDLER, CHILD, TEENAGER, ADULT, SENIOR. Where are YOU on the tree? Say: 'I am a ___ . I am ___ years old."

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 15: Digital Time & Screens

Selene discovers how technology changed how we experience time

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Digital clock /DIJ-ih-tul KLOK/ The digital clock shows numbers instead of hands. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Timer /TY-mer/ Set the timer for ten minutes. T-hand taps wrist
3 Alarm /uh-LARM/ The alarm rings at six thirty every morning. Index taps opposite palm rapidly (ringing)
4 Countdown /KOWNT-down/ The countdown starts at ten, nine, eight... Fingers count down 5-4-3-2-1
5 Stopwatch /STOP-wotch/ Use the stopwatch to time the race. Both S-hands: one taps other (START + WATCH)
6 Snooze /SNOOZ/ Press snooze for five more minutes of sleep. Fingerspell SNOOZE
7 Reminder /rih-MYN-der/ Set a reminder for your doctor appointment. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Calendar app /KAL-en-der AP/ Add events to your calendar app. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Notification /noh-tih-fih-KAY-shun/ A notification pops up on your screen. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Screen time /SKREEN TYM/ Limit screen time to two hours per day. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Streaming /STREEM-ing/ Streaming a movie takes about two hours. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Loading /LOH-ding/ The page is still loading. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Buffering /BUF-er-ing/ The video keeps buffering because the internet is slow. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Download /DOWN-lohd/ Download the file in about three minutes. Claw hand pulls down to S-fist
15 Upload /UP-lohd/ Upload the photo to the cloud. S-fist pushes up, opens to claw
16 Update /up-DAYT/ The software update takes fifteen minutes. Fingerspell UPDATE
17 Real-time /REEL-tym/ GPS gives real-time directions. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Instant /IN-stunt/ The message was delivered in an instant. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Lag /LAG/ A lag means there is a delay in the connection. Fingerspell LAG
20 Sync /SINK/ Sync your watch with your phone for accuracy. Fingerspell SYNC
21 Timestamp /TYM-stamp/ The timestamp shows when the photo was taken. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Log in /LOG IN/ Log in to your account to check the schedule. Fingerspell LOG IN
23 Schedule /SKEJ-ool/ Schedule the meeting for tomorrow at two. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
24 Time zone /TYM ZOHN/ Adjust for the time zone when calling overseas. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 24-hour clock /TWEN-tee-for OW-er KLOK/ The military uses a 24-hour clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
📱

Selene's Tip:

"TIMER, ALARM, COUNTDOWN — your phone is a time machine!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 3: Energy & Forces

The Celestia Power Lab explores how energy moves, changes, and makes everything work.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Energy /EN-er-jee/ Energy is the ability to do work or cause change. E-hands flex at biceps (STRONG + E)
2 Heat /HEET/ Heat energy makes things warmer. Fingerspell HEAT
3 Light /LYT/ Light energy lets us see the world around us. Both flat hands rise up easily
4 Sound /SOWND/ Sound energy travels in waves through the air. Fingerspell SOUND
5 Electricity /ee-lek-TRIS-ih-tee/ Electricity powers lights, computers, and machines. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Solar energy /SOH-ler EN-er-jee/ Solar energy comes from the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Wind energy /WIND EN-er-jee/ Wind energy is captured by windmills and turbines. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Battery /BAT-er-ee/ A battery stores electrical energy. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Magnet /MAG-net/ A magnet attracts objects made of iron or steel. Fingerspell MAGNET
10 Force /FORS/ A force is a push or a pull on an object. Fingerspell FORCE
11 Friction /FRIK-shun/ Friction slows things down when they rub together. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Motion /MOH-shun/ An object in motion keeps moving until a force stops it. Fingerspell MOTION
13 Speed /SPEED/ Speed is how fast something moves. Both S-hands flick to 5-hands quickly
14 Acceleration /ak-sel-er-AY-shun/ Acceleration is the rate of getting faster. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Weight /WAYT/ Weight is the measure of gravity pulling on an object. Fingerspell WEIGHT
16 Mass /MAS/ Mass is the amount of matter in an object. Fingerspell MASS
17 Matter /MAT-er/ Everything you can touch or see is made of matter. Fingerspell MATTER
18 Solid /SOL-id/ Ice is water in its solid form. Fingerspell SOLID
19 Liquid /LIK-wid/ Water is a liquid that flows and takes the shape of its container. Fingerspell LIQUID
20 Gas /GAS/ Steam is water in its gas form. Fingerspell GAS
21 Evaporation /ih-vap-or-AY-shun/ Evaporation turns liquid water into water vapor. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Condensation /kon-den-SAY-shun/ Condensation turns water vapor back into liquid drops. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Precipitation /prih-sip-ih-TAY-shun/ Rain, snow, and hail are all forms of precipitation. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Water cycle /WAW-ter SY-kul/ The water cycle is how water moves from Earth to sky and back. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Temperature /TEM-per-uh-chur/ Temperature measures how hot or cold something is. Index slides up/down opposite index (thermometer)
⚡

Elias's Tip:

"Energy is EVERYWHERE! Clap your hands — that's SOUND energy. Feel the sun — that's HEAT energy. Turn on a lamp — that's LIGHT energy. Now name three types of energy you use every day!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 9: Environment & Sustainability

Elias and Selene lead the Celestia Green Initiative, teaching everyone how to protect the planet.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Environment /en-VY-run-ment/ The environment is everything around us — air, water, land. E-hand circles around index (AROUND + E)
2 Climate change /KLY-mit CHAYNJ/ Climate change is the long-term shift in weather patterns. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Global warming /GLOH-bul WOR-ming/ Global warming is the gradual increase in Earth's temperature. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Greenhouse gas /GREEN-hows GAS/ Carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas that traps heat. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Carbon footprint /KAR-bun FOOT-print/ Your carbon footprint is the amount of CO2 your activities produce. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Recycle /ree-SY-kul/ Recycle cans, bottles, and paper. R-hand circles (going around again)
7 Reuse /ree-YOOZ/ Reuse bags and containers instead of throwing them away. Fingerspell REUSE
8 Reduce /rih-DOOS/ Reduce waste by buying only what you need. Fingerspell REDUCE
9 Compost /KOM-pohst/ Compost food scraps to make rich soil for the garden. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Biodegradable /by-oh-dih-GRAY-duh-bul/ Biodegradable materials break down naturally. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Endangered /en-DAYN-jerd/ Endangered species are animals at risk of disappearing. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Extinct /ek-STINGKT/ Dinosaurs are extinct — they no longer exist. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Deforestation /dee-for-es-TAY-shun/ Deforestation is cutting down forests in large areas. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Renewable energy /rih-NOO-uh-bul EN-er-jee/ Solar and wind are types of renewable energy. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Sustainability /sus-tayn-uh-BIL-ih-tee/ Sustainability means meeting today's needs without harming the future. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Organic /or-GAN-ik/ Organic farming avoids harmful chemicals. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Pollution /puh-LOO-shun/ Air pollution comes from cars and factories. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Clean energy /KLEEN EN-er-jee/ Clean energy does not produce pollution. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Conservation /kon-ser-VAY-shun/ Conservation protects natural habitats and wildlife. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Ecosystem /EE-koh-sis-tem/ Every ecosystem depends on balance to survive. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌱

Selene's Tip:

"YOU can help the planet! Learn and say these THREE words every day: REDUCE, REUSE, RECYCLE. Now teach them to someone else — that's how change spreads!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 14: Historical Timekeeping

In the Celestia Time Museum, Elias explores how humans measured time throughout history

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Sundial /SUN-dy-ul/ The sundial uses shadows to tell time. S-hand + index traces shadow on palm
2 Hourglass /OW-er-glas/ Flip the hourglass to start the timer. Both C-hands stack, flip (turning hourglass)
3 Pendulum /PEN-joo-lum/ The pendulum swings back and forth in the clock. Index swings back and forth below fist
4 Candle clock /KAN-dul KLOK/ A candle clock burns down to mark the hours. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Water clock /WOT-er KLOK/ The water clock drips to measure time passing. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Pocket watch /POK-it WOTCH/ Grandfather keeps a pocket watch on a chain. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Wristwatch /RIST-wotch/ Check the wristwatch to see if we are late. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Grandfather clock /GRAND-fah-ther KLOK/ The grandfather clock chimes every hour. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Cuckoo clock /KOO-koo KLOK/ The cuckoo clock has a little bird that pops out. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Clocktower /KLOK-tow-er/ Big Ben is a famous clocktower. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Timekeeper /TYM-kee-per/ The timekeeper records the runners' times. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Chronometer /kroh-NOM-eh-ter/ A chronometer is an extremely accurate clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Dial /DY-ul/ The dial shows the numbers one through twelve. Fingerspell DIAL
14 Hands /HANDZ/ The clock hands point to the hour and minute. Flat hand chops opposite wrist (repeated)
15 Tick /TIK/ The clock goes tick tock every second. Fingerspell TICK
16 Chime /CHYM/ The clock chimes at noon and midnight. Fingerspell CHIME
17 Ancient /AYN-shent/ Ancient civilizations measured time with the stars. S-hand pulls wisdom from chin repeatedly
18 Modern /MOD-ern/ Modern clocks use quartz crystals. M-hand flicks forward from chin (NEW)
19 Invention /in-VEN-shun/ The clock was one of history's greatest inventions. 4-hand pulls from forehead (IDEA + MAKE)
20 Accurate /AK-yur-it/ Atomic clocks are the most accurate. Both F-hands touch precisely
21 Timezone /TYM-zohn/ The world has twenty-four timezones. T + Z, then point across horizon
22 Greenwich /GREN-itch/ Greenwich Mean Time is the world standard. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 AM /AY-EM/ AM means before noon. Fingerspell AM
24 PM /PEE-EM/ PM means after noon. Fingerspell PM
25 Era /EER-uh/ Each era of history has its own timekeeping method. E-hand circles in time motion
⏳

Elias's Tip:

"SUNDIAL, HOURGLASS, PENDULUM — time has been measured for thousands of years!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 16: Holidays & Celebrations Calendar

The Celestia Festival Calendar marks every special day

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 New Year /NOO YEER/ Happy New Year! January first is a fresh start. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Valentine's Day /VAL-en-tynz DAY/ Exchange cards on Valentine's Day. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Presidents' Day /PREZ-ih-dents DAY/ Presidents' Day honors past leaders. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Easter /EES-ter/ Children hunt for Easter eggs in the park. Fingerspell EASTER
5 Mother's Day /MUTH-erz DAY/ Celebrate Mother's Day with flowers. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Father's Day /FAH-therz DAY/ Father's Day is in June. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Independence Day /in-dih-PEN-dents DAY/ Fireworks on Independence Day light up the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Labor Day /LAY-ber DAY/ Labor Day honors all workers. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Halloween /HAL-oh-ween/ Dress in costume on Halloween night. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Veterans Day /VET-er-unz DAY/ Veterans Day honors those who served. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Thanksgiving /THANKS-giv-ing/ The family gathers for Thanksgiving dinner. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Christmas /KRIS-mus/ Decorate the tree for Christmas. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Hanukkah /HAH-nuh-kuh/ Light the menorah during Hanukkah. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Kwanzaa /KWAN-zuh/ Kwanzaa celebrates African heritage. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Ramadan /RAH-muh-don/ Ramadan is a month of fasting. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Eid /EED/ Eid marks the end of Ramadan. Fingerspell EID
17 Diwali /dih-WAH-lee/ Diwali is the festival of lights. Fingerspell DIWALI
18 Lunar New Year /LOO-ner NOO YEER/ Lunar New Year celebrations last many days. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Birthday /BURTH-day/ Blow out the candles on your birthday cake. Middle finger touches chin, then chest
20 Anniversary /an-ih-VUR-ser-ee/ An anniversary marks a special date each year. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Memorial Day /meh-MOR-ee-ul DAY/ Memorial Day remembers fallen soldiers. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Martin Luther King Day /MAR-tin LOO-ther KING DAY/ MLK Day celebrates equality. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Earth Day /URTH DAY/ Plant a tree on Earth Day, April twenty-second. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Graduation day /graj-oo-AY-shun DAY/ Graduation day is a proud milestone. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Back to school /BAK too SKOOL/ Back to school means new supplies and new friends. Fingerspell or sign each word
🎄

Elias's Tip:

"HOLIDAYS mark time! NEW YEAR, INDEPENDENCE DAY, THANKSGIVING — the calendar tells stories!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 5: Inventions & Discoveries

The Celestia Museum of Innovation celebrates the greatest inventions and the people who made them.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Invention /in-VEN-shun/ An invention is something new that someone creates. 4-hand pulls from forehead (IDEA + MAKE)
2 Discovery /dis-KUV-er-ee/ A discovery is finding something for the first time. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Inventor /in-VEN-ter/ An inventor thinks of new ideas and builds them. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Scientist /SY-en-tist/ A scientist studies the natural world. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Experiment /ek-SPER-ih-ment/ An experiment tests an idea to see if it works. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Hypothesis /hy-POTH-ih-sis/ A hypothesis is a prediction based on what you know. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Laboratory /LAB-ruh-tor-ee/ Scientists work in a laboratory with special equipment. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Microscope /MY-kroh-skohp/ A microscope makes tiny things look big enough to study. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Telescope /TEL-ih-skohp/ A telescope makes faraway things look closer. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Electricity /ee-lek-TRIS-ih-tee/ Benjamin Franklin studied electricity with a kite. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Light bulb /LYT BULB/ Thomas Edison invented the practical light bulb. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Telephone /TEL-ih-fohn/ Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Airplane /AIR-playn/ The Wright Brothers built and flew the first airplane. Y-hand with index out, flies forward
14 Computer /kum-PYOO-ter/ Computers have changed how we work, learn, and communicate. C-hand bounces up opposite forearm
15 Internet /IN-ter-net/ The internet connects billions of people worldwide. 5-hands, middle fingers touch, twist
16 Printing press /PRIN-ting PRES/ The printing press made books available to everyone. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Vaccine /vak-SEEN/ The vaccine has saved millions of lives from disease. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Wheel /WEEL/ The wheel is one of the oldest and most important inventions. Fingerspell WHEEL
19 Steam engine /STEEM EN-jin/ The steam engine powered the Industrial Revolution. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Solar panel /SOH-ler PAN-ul/ A solar panel converts sunlight into electricity. Fingerspell or sign each word
💡

Elias's Tip:

"What would YOU invent? Say: 'I would invent a ___ because ___.' Every invention starts with an IDEA and the words to describe it!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 2: Planet Earth

Elias and Selene study a giant model of the Earth in the Celestia Science Museum, exploring its layers and systems.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Earth /URTH/ Earth is our home planet. Fingerspell EARTH
2 Core /KOR/ The core is the hot center of the Earth. Fingerspell CORE
3 Crust /KRUST/ The crust is the thin outer layer we walk on. Fingerspell CRUST
4 Mantle /MAN-tul/ The mantle is the thick layer between the crust and the core. Fingerspell MANTLE
5 Tectonic plate /tek-TON-ik PLAYT/ Tectonic plates are huge slabs of rock that move slowly. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Continent /KON-tih-nent/ There are seven continents on the Earth. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Ocean /OH-shun/ Oceans cover about 71 percent of the Earth's surface. Both hands make waves, push forward
8 Mountain /MOWN-tun/ Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth. Flat hand taps fist (ROCK) + both hands peak up
9 Volcano /vol-KAY-noh/ A volcano erupts when hot lava bursts through the crust. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Earthquake /URTH-kwayk/ An earthquake happens when tectonic plates shift. S-hands grab air and shake
11 Lava /LAH-vuh/ Hot lava flows from the opening of the volcano. Fingerspell LAVA
12 Magma /MAG-muh/ Magma is melted rock beneath the Earth's surface. Fingerspell MAGMA
13 Fossil /FOS-ul/ A fossil is the preserved remains of an ancient plant or animal. Fingerspell FOSSIL
14 Mineral /MIN-er-ul/ A mineral is a natural solid found in rock. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Rock /ROK/ There are three types of rock: igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic. Fingerspell ROCK
16 Erosion /ih-ROH-zhun/ Erosion wears away land by water, wind, or ice. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Soil /SOYL/ Healthy soil has nutrients for plants to grow. Fingerspell SOIL
18 Natural resource /NACH-er-ul REE-sors/ Water, air, and sunlight are natural resources. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Renewable /rih-NOO-uh-bul/ Solar and wind energy are renewable resources. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Nonrenewable /non-rih-NOO-uh-bul/ Coal and oil are nonrenewable resources that can run out. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Pollution /puh-LOO-shun/ Pollution harms the air, water, and land. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Recycle /ree-SY-kul/ Recycle paper, plastic, and glass to reduce waste. R-hand circles (going around again)
23 Conservation /kon-ser-VAY-shun/ Conservation means using resources wisely to protect nature. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Ecosystem /EE-koh-sis-tem/ An ecosystem is a community of living things and their environment. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Habitat /HAB-ih-tat/ The forest is a natural habitat for many animals. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌎

Selene's Tip:

"Our planet has layers — like an onion! From the outside in: CRUST, MANTLE, CORE. Can you draw the Earth and label each layer? Now you're a GEOLOGIST who speaks English!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 17: School Schedules & Timetables

Selene maps out every period, break, and deadline

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Period /PEER-ee-ud/ First period starts at eight o'clock. Fingerspell PERIOD
2 Bell /BEL/ The bell rings to start and end each class. Fingerspell BELL
3 Homeroom /HOHM-room/ Check in with your teacher during homeroom. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Advisory /ad-VY-zor-ee/ Advisory is a short class for announcements. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Break /BRAYK/ We have a ten-minute break between classes. Fingerspell BREAK
6 Lunch period /LUNCH PEER-ee-ud/ Lunch period is from noon to twelve thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 After school /AF-ter SKOOL/ Stay for after school tutoring. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Early dismissal /UR-lee dis-MIS-ul/ Early dismissal is at one o'clock today. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Late start /LAYT START/ Late start means school begins at ten. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Snow day /SNOH DAY/ A snow day means no school due to weather. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Substitute /SUB-stih-toot/ A substitute teacher fills in today. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Syllabus /SIL-uh-bus/ The syllabus outlines the course. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Due date /DOO DAYT/ The project due date is Friday. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Midterm /MID-turm/ Study hard for the midterm exam. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Final /FY-nul/ The final exam covers the entire semester. Fingerspell FINAL
16 Quarter /KWOR-ter/ Report cards are issued every quarter. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Trimester /try-MES-ter/ The school year has three trimesters. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 School year /SKOOL YEER/ The school year runs from August to June. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Summer break /SUM-er BRAYK/ Summer break lasts about ten weeks. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Spring break /SPRING BRAYK/ Spring break is one week in March or April. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Winter break /WIN-ter BRAYK/ Winter break includes two weeks off. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Tardy /TAR-dee/ Three tardies equal one absence. Fingerspell TARDY
23 Absent /AB-sent/ Mark the student absent if they are not here. Fingerspell ABSENT
24 Present /PREZ-ent/ Raise your hand to be marked present. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Attendance /uh-TEN-dents/ The teacher takes attendance every morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
📅

Selene's Tip:

"PERIOD, BELL, SCHEDULE — say your school day out loud in English!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 13: Seasons & Time in Nature

The Garden of Time blooms and fades with the turning seasons. Nature keeps its own clock.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Spring /SPRING/ Flowers bloom and baby animals are born in spring. Fingerspell SPRING
2 Summer /SUM-er/ Summer brings long, hot days and school vacation. Fingerspell SUMMER
3 Fall /FAWL/ In fall, the leaves change color and drop from trees. Fingerspell FALL
4 Winter /WIN-ter/ Winter is the coldest season with short days. Fingerspell WINTER
5 Season /SEE-zun/ A season is one of four parts of the year. Fingerspell SEASON
6 Harvest /HAR-vest/ Harvest is the time when farmers pick their crops. Curved hand mimes gathering crops
7 Bloom /BLOOM/ Roses bloom in late spring and early summer. Fingerspell BLOOM
8 Migrate /MY-grayt/ Birds migrate south when winter comes. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Hibernate /HY-ber-nayt/ Bears hibernate through the entire winter. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Sunrise /SUN-ryz/ Sunrise is earlier in summer and later in winter. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Sunset /SUN-set/ The sunset paints the sky orange and pink. Fingerspell SUNSET
12 Annual /AN-yoo-ul/ An annual event happens once every year. Fingerspell ANNUAL
13 Monthly /MUNTH-lee/ Rent is a monthly expense — paid every month. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Weekly /WEEK-lee/ The weekly schedule is posted on the bulletin board. Fingerspell WEEKLY
15 Daily /DAY-lee/ Daily exercise keeps your body and mind strong. Fingerspell DAILY
16 Hourly /OW-er-lee/ The bus runs on an hourly schedule. Fingerspell HOURLY
17 Cycle /SY-kul/ The seasons move in a cycle, year after year. Fingerspell CYCLE
18 Change /CHAYNJ/ Nature teaches us that change is constant. A-fists switch positions (swap)
19 Rhythm /RITH-um/ The rhythm of the seasons guides all living things. R-hand bounces forward rhythmically
20 Eternal /ee-TUR-nul/ The Garden of Time reminds us that time is eternal. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌸

Elias & Selene's Tip:

"Time moves in circles — like the SEASONS! Say: 'In SPRING I ___. In SUMMER I ___. In FALL I ___. In WINTER I ___.' Fill in YOUR activities and you've just described your year!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 11: Telling Time & Clocks

In the Celestia Clock Tower, Elias and Selene learn to read every kind of timekeeper — from sundials to smartphones.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Clock /KLOK/ The clock on the wall shows it is ten fifteen. Point to wrist + mime clock hands
2 Watch /WOCH/ Selene wears a watch on her wrist to tell time. Point to wrist (watch)
3 Hour /OW-er/ An hour is sixty minutes long. Index circles on opposite flat palm (clock hand)
4 Minute /MIN-it/ Each minute is sixty seconds long. Index finger ticks on opposite palm
5 Second /SEK-und/ A second is the smallest common unit of time. Index finger flicks behind opposite palm
6 Quarter past /KWOR-ter PAST/ It is quarter past three — that means 3:15. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Half past /HAF PAST/ Half past six means 6:30. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Quarter to /KWOR-ter TOO/ Quarter to nine means 8:45. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Noon /NOON/ Noon is twelve o'clock in the daytime. Forearm straight up on opposite forearm
10 A.M. /AY-EM/ A.M. means the morning hours, before noon. Fingerspell A.M.
11 P.M. /PEE-EM/ P.M. means the afternoon and evening hours. Fingerspell P.M.
12 Alarm /uh-LARM/ Set the alarm for seven A.M. to wake up for school. Index taps opposite palm rapidly (ringing)
13 Timer /TY-mer/ Set a timer for twenty minutes to bake the cookies. T-hand taps wrist
14 Stopwatch /STOP-woch/ Use the stopwatch to time the race. Both S-hands: one taps other (START + WATCH)
15 Sundial /SUN-dy-ul/ A sundial uses the sun's shadow to tell time. S-hand + index traces shadow on palm
16 Hourglass /OW-er-glas/ Sand flows through the hourglass in exactly one hour. Both C-hands stack, flip (turning hourglass)
17 Time zone /TYM ZOHN/ New York and Los Angeles are in different time zones. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 On time /ON TYM/ Always try to be on time for appointments. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Late /LAYT/ If you leave at eight, you will be late for class. Hand waves downward beside hip
20 Early /UR-lee/ Arriving early gives you time to prepare. Middle finger slides over fist knuckle
⏰

Elias's Tip:

"Practice telling time all day! Look at a clock and say: 'It is ___.' Try: 'quarter past,' 'half past,' and 'quarter to.' Time is the one English skill you use EVERY SINGLE DAY."

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 8: The Calendar & Cycles

The Celestia Great Calendar shows how days, months, and years cycle endlessly.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Day /DAY/ A day is twenty-four hours long. Index arm rests on opposite, arcs like sun
2 Night /NYT/ Night is the dark time when the moon is out. Bent hand drops over opposite forearm wrist
3 Dawn /DAWN/ Dawn is the first light of morning. Fingerspell DAWN
4 Dusk /DUSK/ Dusk is the fading light just before night. Fingerspell DUSK
5 Morning /MOR-ning/ Morning is the time from sunrise to noon. Arm rises from opposite forearm (sunrise)
6 Afternoon /af-ter-NOON/ Afternoon is the time from noon to evening. Forearm tilts forward from opposite forearm
7 Evening /EEV-ning/ Evening is the time from sunset to bedtime. Hand curves down on opposite forearm (sunset)
8 Midnight /MID-nyt/ Midnight marks the middle of the night. Hand drops below opposite forearm (sun below)
9 Leap year /LEEP YEER/ A leap year has 366 days instead of 365. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Solstice /SOL-stis/ The summer solstice is the longest day of the year. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Equinox /EE-kwih-noks/ At the equinox, day and night are equal in length. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Phase /FAYZ/ The moon goes through phases from new to full. Fingerspell PHASE
13 New moon /NOO MOON/ The new moon is invisible to us in the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Full moon /FOOL MOON/ The full moon lights up the night brightly. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Crescent /KRES-ent/ A crescent moon is a thin sliver in the sky. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌙

Selene's Tip:

"Look at the moon tonight! What PHASE is it? Is it a CRESCENT? A FULL MOON? A NEW MOON? Track it every night for a month and you'll SEE the lunar cycle with your own eyes!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 10: The Future & Dreams

At the top of the Celestia Lighthouse, Elias and Selene look toward the horizon and dream about tomorrow.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Dream /DREEM/ A dream is a wish your heart makes for the future. Fingerspell DREAM
2 Goal /GOHL/ Set a goal and work toward it every day. Fingerspell GOAL
3 Plan /PLAN/ Make a plan to reach your goal step by step. Fingerspell PLAN
4 Hope /HOHP/ Hope is believing that good things are possible. Fingerspell HOPE
5 Imagine /ih-MAJ-in/ Imagine a world where everyone speaks kindly. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Create /kree-AYT/ Create something new that helps your community. Fingerspell CREATE
7 Inspire /in-SPYR/ A good story can inspire someone to change the world. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Believe /bih-LEEV/ Believe in yourself and you can do anything. Touch forehead then clasp hands together
9 Achieve /uh-CHEEV/ You can achieve your goals with hard work and patience. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Grow /GROH/ Every day you grow stronger, smarter, and more capable. C-hand pushes up through opposite C-hand, opens
11 Change /CHAYNJ/ Change begins with one person making a decision. A-fists switch positions (swap)
12 Courage /KUR-ij/ Courage means doing the right thing even when it's hard. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Persevere /pur-suh-VEER/ Persevere through challenges and never give up. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Succeed /suk-SEED/ You succeed when preparation meets opportunity. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Together /too-GETH-er/ Together, we can build a better world. Fingerspell or sign each word
✨

Elias & Selene's Tip:

"You have traveled through ALL SEVEN LANDS. You have learned hundreds of words. But the most important word? TOGETHER. Say it: 'TOGETHER, we can do ANYTHING.' Now go — use every word you've learned — and change the world!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 7: The Scientific Method

In the Celestia Lab, Elias and Selene follow the steps of scientific thinking.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Observe /ob-ZURV/ Observe the world around you carefully. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Question /KWES-chun/ Ask a question about something you want to understand. Index finger draws question mark in air
3 Hypothesis /hy-POTH-ih-sis/ A hypothesis is your educated guess. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Experiment /ek-SPER-ih-ment/ Design an experiment to test your hypothesis. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Variable /VAIR-ee-uh-bul/ A variable is the thing you change in an experiment. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Control /kun-TROHL/ The control group stays the same for comparison. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Data /DAY-tuh/ Collect data by measuring and recording results. D-hand moves along opposite palm (reading data)
8 Results /rih-ZULTS/ The results show what happened in the experiment. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Conclusion /kun-KLOO-zhun/ The conclusion tells what you learned from the experiment. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Theory /THEER-ee/ A theory is a well-tested explanation backed by evidence. Fingerspell THEORY
11 Fact /FAKT/ A fact is something that has been proven true. Fingerspell FACT
12 Opinion /oh-PIN-yun/ An opinion is a personal belief, not a fact. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Evidence /EV-ih-dents/ Evidence supports your conclusion. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Measure /MEZH-er/ Measure the temperature every hour. Both Y-hands tap together (SAME SIZE)
15 Record /rih-KORD/ Record your observations in a science journal. Fingerspell RECORD
🧪

Elias's Tip:

"YOU are a scientist! Follow the steps: OBSERVE, QUESTION, HYPOTHESIS, EXPERIMENT, CONCLUSION. This process works for ANY problem, in ANY language!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 1: The Solar System

Elias and Selene gaze through the Celestia Observatory telescope. The night sky is a map of wonder.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Sun /SUN/ The sun is the star at the center of our solar system. C-hand above, opens to 5-hand (radiating)
2 Moon /MOON/ The moon orbits the Earth once every 27 days. Modified C at temple, arcs up
3 Star /STAR/ A star is a ball of burning gas far away in space. Both index fingers brush up alternately
4 Planet /PLAN-it/ Earth is the third planet from the sun. Fingerspell PLANET
5 Mercury /MUR-kyoo-ree/ Mercury is the closest planet to the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Venus /VEE-nus/ Venus is the hottest planet because of its thick atmosphere. Fingerspell VENUS
7 Earth /URTH/ Earth is the only planet known to support life. Fingerspell EARTH
8 Mars /MARZ/ Mars is called the Red Planet. Fingerspell MARS
9 Jupiter /JOO-pih-ter/ Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Saturn /SAT-urn/ Saturn has beautiful rings made of ice and rock. Fingerspell SATURN
11 Uranus /YOOR-uh-nus/ Uranus rotates on its side, unlike other planets. Fingerspell URANUS
12 Neptune /NEP-toon/ Neptune is the farthest planet from the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Orbit /OR-bit/ The Earth takes one year to orbit the sun. Fingerspell ORBIT
14 Rotate /ROH-tayt/ The Earth rotates once every twenty-four hours. Fingerspell ROTATE
15 Galaxy /GAL-uk-see/ Our sun is one of billions of stars in the Milky Way galaxy. Fingerspell GALAXY
16 Constellation /kon-steh-LAY-shun/ A constellation is a group of stars forming a pattern. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Comet /KOM-it/ A comet leaves a glowing tail as it flies past the sun. Fingerspell COMET
18 Asteroid /AS-ter-oyd/ An asteroid is a rocky object that orbits the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Meteor /MEE-tee-or/ A meteor streaks across the night sky like a shooting star. Fingerspell METEOR
20 Telescope /TEL-ih-skohp/ Selene looks at the moon through the telescope. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Astronaut /AS-troh-nawt/ An astronaut travels to space in a rocket. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Rocket /ROK-it/ The rocket launches from the pad with a roar. Fingerspell ROCKET
23 Space station /SPAYS STAY-shun/ Astronauts live and work on the space station. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Gravity /GRAV-ih-tee/ Gravity pulls everything toward the ground. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Lunar /LOO-ner/ A lunar eclipse happens when Earth blocks the sun's light on the moon. Fingerspell LUNAR
26 Solar /SOH-ler/ A solar eclipse happens when the moon blocks the sun. Fingerspell SOLAR
27 Satellite /SAT-eh-lyt/ A satellite orbits Earth and sends information to us. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Light-year /LYT-yeer/ A light-year is the distance light travels in one year. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Universe /YOO-nih-vers/ The universe contains everything that exists. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Atmosphere /AT-muh-sfeer/ Earth's atmosphere protects us from the sun's harmful rays. Fingerspell or sign each word
🔭

Elias's Tip:

"Tonight, look up at the sky! Can you see the MOON? Can you find a STAR? A CONSTELLATION? Say the words aloud and point. You are now a STARGAZER — in TWO languages!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 4: The Water Cycle & Weather Systems

Selene explains the water cycle using a beautiful glass model in the Celestia Weather Station.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Water cycle /WAW-ter SY-kul/ The water cycle recycles Earth's water endlessly. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Evaporation /ih-vap-or-AY-shun/ Evaporation lifts water from lakes into the air. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Condensation /kon-den-SAY-shun/ Condensation forms clouds from water vapor. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Precipitation /prih-sip-ih-TAY-shun/ Precipitation falls as rain, snow, sleet, or hail. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Collection /kuh-LEK-shun/ Collection is when water gathers in rivers, lakes, and oceans. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Cloud /KLOWD/ White clouds drift slowly across the blue sky. Both claw hands shape clouds, move across
7 Cumulus /KYOO-myoo-lus/ Cumulus clouds are big, white, and puffy. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Stratus /STRAT-us/ Gray stratus clouds cover the sky like a blanket. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Cirrus /SEER-us/ Thin, wispy cirrus clouds form high in the sky. Fingerspell CIRRUS
10 Thunderstorm /THUN-der-storm/ A thunderstorm brings lightning, thunder, and heavy rain. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Blizzard /BLIZ-erd/ A blizzard is a severe snowstorm with strong winds. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Hurricane /HUR-ih-kayn/ A hurricane forms over warm ocean water. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Tornado /tor-NAY-doh/ A tornado spins in a funnel from the clouds to the ground. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Drought /DROWT/ A long drought causes water shortages. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Flood /FLUD/ A flood covers dry land with water. W-hands rise up from below
16 Barometer /buh-ROM-ih-ter/ A barometer measures air pressure. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Wind vane /WIND VAYN/ A wind vane shows which direction the wind is blowing. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Anemometer /an-eh-MOM-ih-ter/ An anemometer measures wind speed. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Rain gauge /RAYN GAYJ/ A rain gauge measures how much rain has fallen. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Meteorologist /mee-tee-or-OL-oh-jist/ A meteorologist studies and predicts the weather. Fingerspell or sign each word
🌧️

Selene's Tip:

"Draw the water cycle! Label each step: EVAPORATION (up), CONDENSATION (cloud), PRECIPITATION (rain), COLLECTION (river). Congratulations — you just drew SCIENCE in English!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 6: Time Concepts & History

In the Celestia Timeline Hall, Elias and Selene walk along a path that spans from ancient times to the future.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Past /PAST/ The past is everything that already happened. Fingerspell PAST
2 Present /PREZ-ent/ The present is the time happening right now. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Future /FYOO-chur/ The future is the time that hasn't happened yet. Fingerspell FUTURE
4 Ancient /AYN-shunt/ Ancient civilizations lived thousands of years ago. S-hand pulls wisdom from chin repeatedly
5 Modern /MOD-ern/ Modern technology includes smartphones and computers. M-hand flicks forward from chin (NEW)
6 Century /SEN-chur-ee/ A century is one hundred years. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Decade /DEK-ayd/ A decade is ten years. Fingerspell DECADE
8 Era /EER-uh/ The space era began in the 1960s. E-hand circles in time motion
9 Timeline /TYM-lyn/ A timeline shows events in the order they happened. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Cause /KAWZ/ Every event in history has a cause. Fingerspell CAUSE
11 Effect /ih-FEKT/ The effect of the invention was a changed world. Fingerspell EFFECT
12 Revolution /rev-oh-LOO-shun/ A revolution is a big, sudden change. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Independence /in-dih-PEN-dents/ Independence Day celebrates a nation's freedom. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 War /WOR/ War brings conflict and suffering. Fingerspell WAR
15 Peace /PEES/ Peace is the absence of war and conflict. Fingerspell PEACE
16 Treaty /TREE-tee/ A treaty is an agreement between nations. Fingerspell TREATY
17 Culture /KUL-chur/ Culture includes traditions, art, food, and language. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Civilization /siv-ih-lih-ZAY-shun/ A civilization is a complex society with its own culture. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Heritage /HAIR-ih-tij/ Heritage is the history and traditions passed down. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Legacy /LEG-uh-see/ Every person leaves a legacy for the next generation. Fingerspell LEGACY
⏳

Selene's Tip:

"Make YOUR timeline! Write three events from YOUR life in order: PAST (when you were born), PRESENT (where you are now), FUTURE (what you dream of). Tell your story in English!"

Land 7 of 7

🔭 Celestia

Guided by Elias the Star Gazer & Selene the Time Keeper

Scene 18: Time in Music & Rhythm

Where Celestia meets Harmonia! Time and music are one

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Beat /BEET/ Clap on every beat of the music. Fist taps chest rhythmically
2 Tempo /TEM-poh/ A fast tempo makes you want to dance. Index bounces at varying speeds
3 Measure /MEZH-er/ Each measure has four beats. Both Y-hands tap together (SAME SIZE)
4 Rest /REST/ A rest is a moment of silence in the music. Arms cross over chest (resting)
5 Duration /dur-AY-shun/ The duration of the song is three minutes. D-hand slides along opposite forearm (length of time)
6 Whole note /HOHL NOHT/ A whole note lasts four beats. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Half note /HAF NOHT/ A half note lasts two beats. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Quarter note /KWOR-ter NOHT/ A quarter note gets one beat. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Eighth note /AYTTH NOHT/ An eighth note is half a beat. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Time signature /TYM SIG-nuh-chur/ The time signature tells how many beats per measure. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Metronome /MET-roh-nohm/ The metronome clicks to keep steady time. Index swings side to side steadily
12 Rhythm /RITH-um/ Feel the rhythm in your body. R-hand bounces forward rhythmically
13 Syncopation /sin-koh-PAY-shun/ Syncopation puts the beat in unexpected places. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Upbeat /UP-beet/ The upbeat comes before the downbeat. Fingerspell UPBEAT
15 Downbeat /DOWN-beet/ The downbeat is the first beat of the measure. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Repeat /rih-PEET/ Repeat the chorus one more time. Fingerspell REPEAT
17 Verse /VURS/ The verse tells the story of the song. Fingerspell VERSE
18 Chorus /KOR-us/ The chorus is the part everyone sings along to. Fingerspell CHORUS
19 Bridge /BRIJ/ The bridge connects the verse to the chorus. V-hand bridges under opposite forearm
20 Finale /fih-NAL-ee/ The finale is the exciting ending of the performance. Both 5-hands shake and drop (FINISH + BIG)
21 Encore /ON-kor/ The crowd shouted encore for one more song. Index circles then points forward (MORE)
22 Crescendo /kreh-SHEN-doh/ A crescendo means the music gets louder. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Pause /PAWZ/ There was a dramatic pause before the last note. Both flat hands freeze in air
24 Timing /TY-ming/ Good timing means playing the note at the right moment. T-hand taps wrist precisely
25 Countdown /KOWNT-down/ The conductor gives a countdown before the first note. Fingers count down 5-4-3-2-1
🎶

Elias's Tip:

"Music IS time! BEAT, TEMPO, MEASURE — clap the rhythm of English!"

Back Matter

💪 Action Verbs Gallery

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: Body Movement Verbs

Every action starts with a VERB. These are the words that make things happen!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Run /RUN/ Run to the finish line as fast as you can. L-hands, index pulls on opposite thumb, move forward
2 Walk /WAWK/ Walk carefully on the wet floor. Both flat hands walk forward alternately
3 Jump /JUMP/ Jump over the puddle! Fingerspell JUMP
4 Sit /SIT/ Sit down in your chair, please. Bent H-hooks sit on opposite bent H
5 Stand /STAND/ Stand up when the teacher enters. V-fingers stand on opposite flat palm
6 Climb /KLYM/ Climb the stairs to the second floor. Fingerspell CLIMB
7 Crawl /KRAWL/ The baby can crawl across the room. Fingerspell CRAWL
8 Bend /BEND/ Bend your knees when you lift heavy things. Fingerspell BEND
9 Stretch /STRECH/ Stretch your arms above your head. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Push /PUSH/ Push the door open gently. Fingerspell PUSH
11 Pull /PUL/ Pull the handle to open the drawer. Fingerspell PULL
12 Lift /LIFT/ Lift the box with both hands. Fingerspell LIFT
13 Carry /KAIR-ee/ Carry the groceries into the kitchen. Fingerspell CARRY
14 Throw /THROH/ Throw the ball to your partner. Fingerspell THROW
15 Catch /KACH/ Catch the ball with two hands. Fingerspell CATCH
16 Kick /KIK/ Kick the soccer ball into the goal. Fingerspell KICK
17 Swim /SWIM/ I like to swim at the community pool. Fingerspell SWIM
18 Dance /DANS/ Dance to the rhythm of the music! Fingerspell DANCE
19 Clap /KLAP/ Clap your hands to the beat. Fingerspell CLAP
20 Wave /WAYV/ Wave goodbye to your friends. Open hand waves side to side
21 Point /POYNT/ Point to the correct answer on the board. Fingerspell POINT
22 Touch /TUCH/ Touch your toes without bending your knees. Fingerspell TOUCH
23 Hold /HOHLD/ Hold the baby carefully. Fingerspell HOLD
24 Drop /DROP/ Be careful not to drop the glass. Fingerspell DROP
25 Shake /SHAYK/ Shake hands when you meet someone new. Fingerspell SHAKE
Back Matter

💪 Action Verbs Gallery

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: Everyday Life Verbs

From morning to night, these verbs describe everything you do.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Eat /EET/ Eat a healthy breakfast every morning. Flat O taps mouth repeatedly
2 Drink /DRINK/ Drink eight glasses of water a day. C-hand tips to mouth
3 Cook /KOOK/ I cook dinner for my family every night. Flip hand on opposite palm (like flipping pancake)
4 Clean /KLEEN/ Clean your room before you go out. Flat hand slides across opposite palm
5 Wash /WOSH/ Wash your hands before eating. Fists rub together (scrubbing)
6 Open /OH-pen/ Open the window to let fresh air in. B-hands together, swing apart
7 Close /KLOHZ/ Close the door behind you. B-hands apart, swing together
8 Turn on /TURN ON/ Turn on the light, please. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Turn off /TURN OFF/ Turn off the TV before bed. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Buy /BY/ Buy milk at the grocery store. Flat hand on opposite palm, lift away
11 Pay /PAY/ Pay for the groceries at the register. Index flicks off opposite palm
12 Give /GIV/ Give the book to your friend. Both flat O-hands push forward opening
13 Take /TAYK/ Take your medicine twice a day. 5-hand grabs from air, pulls to body as S-hand
14 Put /PUT/ Put the dishes in the sink. Flat O-hands drop forward, open
15 Get /GET/ Get ready for school by seven thirty. Fingerspell GET
16 Make /MAYK/ Make your bed every morning. S-fists stack, twist and stack again
17 Read /REED/ Read a book to your child before bed. V-fingers move down opposite flat palm
18 Write /RYT/ Write your name at the top of the page. Pinch fingers, write on opposite palm
19 Listen /LIS-en/ Listen carefully to the instructions. Cup hand behind ear
20 Speak /SPEEK/ Speak slowly so I can understand. 4-fingers tap near chin repeatedly
21 Ask /ASK/ Ask the teacher if you have a question. Fingerspell ASK
22 Answer /AN-ser/ Answer the phone when it rings. Index from chin points forward
23 Help /HELP/ Can you help me carry this box? Fist on flat palm, lift both up
24 Try /TRY/ Try your best on every test. T-hands push forward with effort
25 Learn /LURN/ I want to learn English fluently. Flat hand lifts from opposite palm to forehead
Back Matter

🎨 Descriptive Words (Adjectives)

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: Feelings, Colors & Qualities

Words that describe how things look, feel, taste, and make us feel.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Beautiful /BYOO-tih-ful/ The sunset is beautiful tonight. Circle face with 5-hand, close to flat O
2 Ugly /UG-lee/ The old building looks ugly but is strong. Fingerspell UGLY
3 Delicious /dih-LISH-us/ The homemade bread smells delicious. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Terrible /TAIR-ih-bul/ The storm last night was terrible. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Important /im-POR-tant/ Education is very important for your future. F-hands rise from center to top
6 Interesting /IN-ter-es-ting/ The book about space was very interesting. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Boring /BOR-ing/ The long meeting was boring. Fingerspell BORING
8 Tired /TYRD/ I am tired after a long day of work. Bent hands on chest, fall forward
9 Hungry /HUNG-gree/ The children are hungry after playing outside. Fingerspell HUNGRY
10 Thirsty /THUR-stee/ I am thirsty — may I have some water? Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Sick /SIK/ She stayed home because she was sick. Middle finger on forehead + stomach
12 Healthy /HEL-thee/ Eating vegetables keeps you healthy. Both claw hands on chest pull to fists (strong)
13 Strong /STRONG/ The strong man lifted the heavy box. Fingerspell STRONG
14 Weak /WEEK/ After being sick, he felt weak. Fingerspell WEAK
15 Brave /BRAYV/ The brave firefighter ran into the building. Claw hands at shoulders pull to fists at chest
16 Afraid /uh-FRAYD/ The child was afraid of the dark. Fingerspell AFRAID
17 Proud /PROWD/ Her parents are proud of her success. Thumb slides up chest
18 Nervous /NUR-vus/ I feel nervous before a job interview. Both 5-hands shake in front
19 Excited /ek-SY-ted/ The children are excited about the field trip. Middle fingers brush up chest alternately
20 Grateful /GRAYT-ful/ I am grateful for my family and friends. Flat hand from chin sweeps forward
21 Kind /KYND/ A kind word can change someone's day. Fingerspell KIND
22 Friendly /FREND-lee/ The new neighbor is very friendly. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Careful /KAIR-ful/ Be careful when crossing the street. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Ready /RED-ee/ Are you ready for the test? Fingerspell READY
25 Free /FREE/ The concert in the park is free. F-hands cross, then spread apart
Back Matter

🎨 Descriptive Words (Adjectives)

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: Opposite Pairs

Descriptive words come in pairs! Learning opposites doubles your vocabulary instantly.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Big /BIG/ The elephant is big. Both L-hands spread apart (expanding)
2 Small /SMAWL/ The ant is very small. Both flat hands come close together
3 Hot /HOT/ Be careful — the soup is hot. C-hand at mouth, flick away sharply
4 Cold /KOHLD/ Wear a coat when it is cold outside. Both fists shake near body (shivering)
5 Fast /FAST/ The rabbit runs fast. L-hands pull back quickly (snapping)
6 Slow /SLOH/ The turtle moves slow and steady. Hand drags slowly up opposite forearm
7 Happy /HAP-ee/ The children are happy at the party. Flat hand brushes up chest twice
8 Sad /SAD/ She felt sad when her friend moved away. Both open hands slide down face
9 Old /OHLD/ The old tree has been here for a hundred years. S-hand pulls down from chin (beard)
10 New /NOO/ I bought a new pair of shoes. Curved hand scoops across opposite palm
11 Full /FOOL/ The glass is full of water. Flat hand slides across top of opposite fist
12 Empty /EMP-tee/ The refrigerator is empty — time to go shopping. Middle finger draws across back of opposite hand
13 Long /LONG/ The long road stretches for miles. Index draws along opposite arm (length)
14 Short /SHORT/ The short answer is yes. Bent hand pushes down
15 Tall /TAWL/ The tall building has fifty floors. Index draws upward on opposite palm
16 Heavy /HEV-ee/ The suitcase is too heavy to carry. Both flat hands push down slowly
17 Light /LYT/ This bag is light enough for a child. Both flat hands rise up easily
18 Loud /LOWD/ The music is too loud — please turn it down. Point to ear, then both fists shake out
19 Quiet /KWY-et/ The library is a quiet place to study. Index finger on lips, hands cross and separate
20 Hard /HARD/ The math problem was very hard. Bent V hits opposite palm (solid)
21 Easy /EE-zee/ The first question was easy. Curved hand brushes up off opposite fingers
22 Clean /KLEEN/ Keep your workspace clean and organized. Flat hand slides across opposite palm
23 Dirty /DUR-tee/ Wash the dirty dishes after dinner. 5-hand under chin, wiggle fingers
24 Safe /SAYF/ This neighborhood is safe for children. S-hands cross at wrists, then pull apart
25 Dangerous /DAYN-jer-us/ Swimming alone is dangerous. A-fist pushes up past opposite A-fist
Back Matter

🤟 ASL Alphabet & Numbers

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: ASL Alphabet A–M

Every letter has a handshape. Learn the ASL Manual Alphabet — your bridge to fingerspelling any English word!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 A /AY/ The letter A starts the alphabet. Fist with thumb at side
2 B /BEE/ B is for Book, Bus, and Banana. Four fingers up, thumb across palm
3 C /SEE/ C is for Cat, City, and Cup. Curved hand like the letter C
4 D /DEE/ D is for Dog, Day, and Door. Index up, other fingers touch thumb
5 E /EE/ E is for Eat, Eye, and Energy. Fingertips curled to thumb, thumb tucked
6 F /EF/ F is for Family, Fish, and Flower. Three fingers up, index and thumb circle
7 G /JEE/ G is for Good, Green, and Garden. Index and thumb point sideways
8 H /AYCH/ H is for Happy, Home, and Help. Index and middle point sideways, stacked
9 I /EYE/ I is for I, Ice, and Important. Pinky finger up, rest closed
10 J /JAY/ J is for Juice, Job, and January. Pinky up, trace letter J in air
11 K /KAY/ K is for Kitchen, Key, and Kind. Index and middle up, thumb between
12 L /EL/ L is for Love, Lake, and Library. L-shape: index up, thumb out
13 M /EM/ M is for Mother, Map, and Music. Three fingers over thumb, fingers down
Back Matter

🤟 ASL Alphabet & Numbers

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: ASL Alphabet N–Z

Complete your fingerspelling alphabet — now you can spell any word in the dictionary!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 N /EN/ N is for Name, Night, and Number. Two fingers over thumb, fingers down
2 O /OH/ O is for Ocean, Open, and Orange. All fingers curve to make O shape
3 P /PEE/ P is for Please, Park, and People. K-hand points downward
4 Q /KYOO/ Q is for Question, Quick, and Quiet. G-hand points downward
5 R /AR/ R is for Read, River, and Respect. Cross index and middle fingers
6 S /ES/ S is for School, Smile, and Sun. Fist with thumb over fingers
7 T /TEE/ T is for Teacher, Time, and Tree. Thumb between index and middle
8 U /YOO/ U is for Uncle, Up, and Under. Index and middle up together
9 V /VEE/ V is for Vote, Visit, and Vegetable. Index and middle spread apart (peace sign)
10 W /DUB-ul-yoo/ W is for Water, Walk, and Welcome. Index, middle, ring spread apart
11 X /EKS/ X is for X-ray and eXcited. Index finger bent like a hook
12 Y /WY/ Y is for Yes, Year, and Yellow. Thumb and pinky out, rest closed
13 Z /ZEE/ Z is for Zero, Zoo, and Zone. Index draws letter Z in air
Back Matter

🤟 ASL Alphabet & Numbers

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 3: ASL Numbers 0–10

Numbers are everywhere — now learn to show them with your hands!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Zero /ZEER-oh/ Zero means nothing or none. O-hand shape
2 One /WUN/ One is a single item. Index finger up
3 Two /TOO/ Two means a pair. Index and middle up
4 Three /THREE/ Three comes after two. Thumb, index, and middle up
5 Four /FOR/ Four fingers but no thumb. Four fingers up, thumb folded
6 Five /FYV/ Five is a whole hand. All five fingers spread open
7 Six /SIKS/ Six is five plus one. Pinky meets thumb, three fingers up
8 Seven /SEV-en/ Seven days in a week. Ring finger meets thumb, three up
9 Eight /AYT/ Eight legs on a spider. Middle finger meets thumb, three up
10 Nine /NYN/ Nine is almost ten. Index meets thumb, four fingers up
11 Ten /TEN/ Ten fingers, ten toes. A-hand (thumb up), shake
Back Matter

🤟 100 Essential ASL Signs

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 3: Essential ASL — Actions & Daily Life (25 Signs)
