The verbs that make your day — eat, drink, go, come, work, play!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Eat /EET/ Time to eat breakfast. Flat-O hand taps mouth
2 Drink /DRINK/ Drink some water. C-hand tilts to mouth
3 Sleep /SLEEP/ I need to sleep. 5-hand draws down face, closing
4 Go /GOH/ Let's go to school. Both index fingers flick forward
5 Come /KUM/ Come here, please. Both index fingers beckon toward body
6 Want /WONT/ I want to learn. Both claw hands pull toward body
7 Need /NEED/ I need help. X-hand bends down firmly
8 Like /LYK/ I like this book. Thumb and middle pull from chest
9 Know /NOH/ I know the answer. Flat hand taps side of forehead
10 Learn /LURN/ I want to learn ASL. Flat hand from palm to forehead (absorbing)
11 Work /WURK/ Time to work hard. S-fist taps on opposite S-fist
12 Play /PLAY/ Let's play a game. Y-hands shake in front of body
13 Read /REED/ Read the book carefully. V-fingers scan down opposite palm
14 Write /RYT/ Write your name here. Mime writing on opposite palm
15 Walk /WAWK/ Walk to school every day. Both flat hands walk forward
16 Run /RUN/ Run fast to the finish line. L-hands pull forward quickly
17 Sit /SIT/ Please sit down. Bent V sits on opposite bent V
18 Stand /STAND/ Stand up straight. V-fingers stand on opposite palm
19 Open /OH-pen/ Open the door. B-hands together, swing apart
20 Close /KLOHS/ Close the window. B-hands apart, swing together
21 Give /GIV/ Give me the book. Flat-O hands push forward, opening
22 Take /TAYK/ Take this with you. 5-hand grabs, pulls to S-fist
23 Look /LOOK/ Look at the picture. V-hand from eyes points outward
24 Listen /LIS-en/ Listen carefully to the teacher. Cup hand at ear
25 Think /THINK/ Think about it first. Index finger circles at temple
Back Matter

🤟 100 Essential ASL Signs

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: Essential ASL — Family & People (25 Signs)

The people in your life — learn to sign them all.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Mother /MUTH-er/ My mother takes care of me. 5-hand, thumb taps chin
2 Father /FAH-ther/ My father works hard. 5-hand, thumb taps forehead
3 Sister /SIS-ter/ My sister is my best friend. A-hand at chin drops to L-hand
4 Brother /BRUTH-er/ My brother helps me study. A-hand at forehead drops to L-hand
5 Baby /BAY-bee/ The baby is sleeping. Rock arms like cradling
6 Family /FAM-uh-lee/ Family is everything. F-hands circle outward from center
7 Boy /BOY/ The boy plays in the park. Flat hand snaps at forehead
8 Girl /GURL/ The girl reads her book. Thumb traces down jawline
9 Man /MAN/ The man wears a hat. 5-hand forehead to chest center
10 Woman /WOO-mun/ The woman drives the bus. 5-hand chin to chest center
11 Teacher /TEECH-er/ The teacher helps us learn. Flat-O hands at temples move out + PERSON
12 Student /STOO-dent/ Every student works hard. Flat hand from opposite palm to forehead
13 Doctor /DOK-ter/ The doctor checks my health. M or D-hand taps opposite wrist
14 Police /poh-LEES/ The police keep us safe. C-hand taps left chest like badge
15 Neighbor /NAY-ber/ My neighbor waves hello. Flat hand near other flat hand, both move
16 Person /PUR-sun/ Every person is important. P-hands drop down sides of body
17 Children /CHIL-dren/ Children love to play. Flat hands pat down repeatedly (heights)
18 Grandma /GRAN-mah/ Grandma tells great stories. 5 at chin, arc forward twice
19 Grandpa /GRAN-pah/ Grandpa has wise advice. 5 at forehead, arc forward twice
20 Husband /HUZ-band/ Her husband cooks dinner. Clasp hands (MARRY), sign at forehead
21 Wife /WYF/ His wife is a teacher. Clasp hands (MARRY), sign at chin
22 Friend /FREND/ Friends help each other. Linked index fingers swap
23 People /PEE-pul/ All people deserve respect. P-hands circle alternately in front
24 Deaf /DEF/ Deaf culture is rich and beautiful. Index touches ear, then chin (or reverse)
25 Hearing /HEER-ing/ Hearing and Deaf people communicate together. Index circles away from mouth
Back Matter

🤟 100 Essential ASL Signs

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 4: Essential ASL — Feelings, Food & Places (25 Signs)

Express how you feel, name what you eat, and sign where you go!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Happy /HAP-ee/ I am so happy today. Flat hand brushes up chest twice
2 Sad /SAD/ The movie made me sad. Open hands slide down face
3 Angry /ANG-ree/ Don't get angry, stay calm. Claw hand pulls from face
4 Scared /SKAIRD/ The thunder made me scared. Fists open outward at chest (startled)
5 Tired /TYRD/ I am very tired tonight. Bent hands at chest fall forward
6 Sick /SIK/ I feel sick today. Middle finger on forehead + stomach
7 Hungry /HUNG-ree/ I am hungry for lunch. C-hand slides down chest
8 Thirsty /THUR-stee/ I am thirsty for water. Index drags down throat
9 Food /FOOD/ The food is delicious. Flat-O taps mouth
10 Water /WOT-er/ Drink plenty of water. W-hand taps chin
11 Milk /MILK/ Pour some milk in the glass. C-hand squeezes (milking)
12 Bread /BRED/ Slice the fresh bread. Slice back of hand with opposite
13 Apple /AP-ul/ Eat an apple every day. X-knuckle twists on cheek
14 Home /HOHM/ There is no place like home. Flat-O touches near mouth, then cheek
15 School /SKOOL/ I go to school every day. Clap hands twice
16 Store /STOR/ Let's go to the store. Flat-O hands flick outward from chest
17 Hospital /HOS-pih-tul/ The hospital is nearby. H-hand draws cross on upper arm
18 Library /LY-brair-ee/ Read books at the library. L-hand circles in air
19 Park /PARK/ Play at the park after school. P-hand taps S-hand
20 Car /KAR/ Drive the car to work. Both C-hands mime steering
21 Bus /BUS/ Take the bus to school. Mime holding overhead bus strap
22 Bathroom /BATH-room/ Where is the bathroom? T-hand shakes between fists
23 Phone /FOHN/ Call me on the phone. Y-hand at ear
24 Book /BOOK/ Open the book and read. Palms together, open like book
25 Money /MUN-ee/ Save your money wisely. Flat-O taps opposite palm twice
Back Matter

🤟 100 Essential ASL Signs

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: Essential ASL — Greetings & Social (25 Signs)

The most important signs you will use every single day. Start here!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Hello /heh-LOH/ Say hello to everyone you meet. Flat hand salutes from forehead outward
2 Goodbye /good-BY/ Wave goodbye when you leave. Open hand waves fingers down
3 Please /PLEEZ/ Always say please when you ask. Flat hand circles on chest clockwise
4 Thank you /THANK yoo/ Show gratitude with thank you. Flat hand from chin moves outward
5 Sorry /SOR-ee/ Say sorry when you make a mistake. A-fist circles on chest
6 Yes /YES/ Nod yes with your hand. S-fist nods up and down (like head nodding)
7 No /NOH/ Shake no with two fingers. Index and middle snap to thumb
8 Help /HELP/ Ask for help when you need it. A-fist on flat palm, both rise up
9 More /MOR/ Sign more when you want extra. Both flat-O hands tap fingertips together
10 Done /DUN/ Sign done when you finish. Both 5-hands flip from palms-in to palms-out
11 Stop /STOP/ Sign stop to set a boundary. Flat hand chops down on opposite flat palm
12 Wait /WAYT/ Sign wait to be patient. Both 5-hands face up, wiggle fingers
13 Again /uh-GEN/ Sign again to repeat something. Curved hand flips into opposite flat palm
14 Understand /un-der-STAND/ Do you understand? Flick index finger up near temple
15 Don't understand /DOHNT/ I don't understand yet. Index at temple, flick down (negative)
16 Name /NAYM/ What is your name? H-fingers tap crosswise
17 Nice /NYS/ Nice to meet you! Flat hand slides across opposite palm
18 Friend /FREND/ You are my friend. Hook index fingers, then swap positions
19 Love /LUV/ I love you. Cross arms over chest, squeeze
20 I love you /EYE LUV YOO/ The universal ILY sign! Thumb, index, pinky up (ILY handshape)
21 Good /GOOD/ That is very good! Flat hand from chin to opposite palm
22 Bad /BAD/ That was bad. Flat hand from chin, flip palm down
23 Welcome /WEL-kum/ You are welcome here. Open hand sweeps inward toward body
24 Excuse me /ek-SKYOOZ/ Excuse me, please. Fingertips brush forward on opposite palm
25 Congratulations /kun-GRACH/ Congratulations on your success! Both hands clap, then shake fists
Back Matter

🔤 A–Z Word Index

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: A–C Words Across All Lands

The A–Z Index is your master key. Every word from every Land, organized by letter.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Abbreviation /uh-BREE-vee-AY-shun/ An abbreviation is a shortened form of a word, like Dr. for Doctor. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Accent /AK-sent/ An accent is the way a person pronounces words. Fingerspell ACCENT
3 Accomplish /uh-KOM-plish/ You can accomplish any goal with practice. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Accurate /AK-yur-it/ Make sure your answer is accurate before submitting. Both F-hands touch precisely
5 Admire /ad-MYR/ I admire people who work hard to learn English. Fingerspell ADMIRE
6 Advantage /ad-VAN-tij/ Speaking two languages is a great advantage. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Agree /uh-GREE/ I agree with your idea. Fingerspell AGREE
8 Amount /uh-MOWNT/ What amount of money do you need? Fingerspell AMOUNT
9 Announce /uh-NOWNS/ The principal will announce the winners today. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Appear /uh-PEER/ Stars appear in the sky at night. Fingerspell APPEAR
11 Apply /uh-PLY/ Apply for the job by filling out the form. Fingerspell APPLY
12 Arrange /uh-RAYNJ/ Arrange the books in alphabetical order. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Available /uh-VAYL-uh-bul/ Is this seat available? Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Balance /BAL-uns/ Keep your balance when you walk on ice. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Bargain /BAR-gun/ A sale price is a good bargain. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Benefit /BEN-ih-fit/ Exercise has many health benefits. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Borrow /BOR-oh/ May I borrow your pencil? Fingerspell BORROW
18 Budget /BUJ-it/ A budget helps you plan your spending. B-hands on opposite palm (MONEY + PLAN)
19 Calendar /KAL-en-der/ Mark the date on the calendar. C-hands mime turning page
20 Cancel /KAN-sul/ I need to cancel my appointment. Fingerspell CANCEL
21 Capable /KAY-puh-bul/ You are capable of learning anything. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Celebrate /SEL-eh-brayt/ We celebrate birthdays with cake and candles. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Challenge /CHAL-enj/ Learning a new language is a worthy challenge. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Choose /CHOOZ/ Choose the best answer on the test. Fingerspell CHOOSE
25 Citizen /SIT-ih-zen/ A citizen has rights and responsibilities. Both C-hands touch shoulders then meet at center
26 Combine /kum-BYN/ Combine the flour and sugar in a bowl. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Communicate /kuh-MYOO-nih-kayt/ We communicate through words, gestures, and writing. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Compare /kum-PAIR/ Compare prices before buying something. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Complete /kum-PLEET/ Complete the form with your personal information. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Connect /kuh-NEKT/ Connect the charger to your phone. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

🔤 A–Z Word Index

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: D–K Words Across All Lands

From D to K — the words that describe actions, decisions, and daily life.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Damage /DAM-ij/ The storm caused damage to the roof. Fingerspell DAMAGE
2 Deadline /DED-lyn/ The deadline for applications is Friday. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Decide /dih-SYD/ Decide which classes you want to take. Fingerspell DECIDE
4 Deliver /dih-LIV-er/ The mail carrier will deliver your package. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Demonstrate /DEM-un-strayt/ The teacher will demonstrate the experiment. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Depend /dih-PEND/ Children depend on their parents for care. Fingerspell DEPEND
7 Describe /dih-SKRYB/ Describe what you see in the picture. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Design /dih-ZYN/ She will design a new logo for the company. Fingerspell DESIGN
9 Develop /dih-VEL-up/ Scientists develop new medicines every year. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Differ /DIF-er/ People differ in their opinions and beliefs. Fingerspell DIFFER
11 Discuss /dih-SKUS/ Let's discuss the homework as a class. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Document /DOK-yoo-ment/ Bring your identification document to the office. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Earn /URN/ You can earn money by working part-time. Fingerspell EARN
14 Educate /EJ-oo-kayt/ Schools educate children in reading and math. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Effort /EF-ert/ Good grades require effort and dedication. Fingerspell EFFORT
16 Encourage /en-KUR-ij/ Teachers encourage students to ask questions. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Establish /eh-STAB-lish/ Establish a daily routine for homework. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Evaluate /ee-VAL-yoo-ayt/ Evaluate your progress at the end of each week. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Expect /ek-SPEKT/ We expect the bus to arrive at three o'clock. Fingerspell EXPECT
20 Experience /ek-SPEER-ee-ents/ Work experience helps you get a better job. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Familiar /fuh-MIL-yer/ This word looks familiar — I've seen it before. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Guarantee /gair-un-TEE/ The product comes with a one-year guarantee. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Improve /im-PROOV/ Practice every day to improve your English. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Include /in-KLOOD/ The price includes tax and shipping. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Indicate /IN-dih-kayt/ Raise your hand to indicate you have a question. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Inform /in-FORM/ Please inform the office if you will be absent. Fingerspell INFORM
27 Investigate /in-VES-tih-gayt/ The police investigate the cause of the accident. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Journey /JUR-nee/ Learning English is a journey, not a race. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Justify /JUS-tih-fy/ Justify your answer with evidence from the text. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Knowledge /NOL-ij/ Knowledge is power — and words are the keys. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

🔤 A–Z Word Index

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 3: L–R Words Across All Lands

From L to R — the words that power storytelling, problem-solving, and community.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Language /LANG-gwij/ How many languages do you speak? L-hands wiggle apart from center
2 Limit /LIM-it/ There is a speed limit on this road. Fingerspell LIMIT
3 Locate /LOH-kayt/ Can you locate the library on this map? Fingerspell LOCATE
4 Maintain /mayn-TAYN/ Maintain a positive attitude every day. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Manage /MAN-ij/ She manages the front desk at the hospital. Fingerspell MANAGE
6 Mention /MEN-shun/ Did the teacher mention the homework? Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Negative /NEG-uh-tiv/ Stay away from negative thoughts. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Notice /NOH-tis/ Did you notice the new sign on the door? Fingerspell NOTICE
9 Obtain /ob-TAYN/ Obtain a library card for free books. Fingerspell OBTAIN
10 Occur /uh-KUR/ Earthquakes can occur without warning. Fingerspell OCCUR
11 Operate /OP-er-ayt/ Do you know how to operate this machine? Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Organize /OR-guh-nyz/ Organize your notes by subject. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Participate /par-TIS-ih-payt/ Everyone should participate in class discussions. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Permit /per-MIT/ You need a permit to park in this lot. Fingerspell PERMIT
15 Positive /POZ-ih-tiv/ A positive attitude helps you succeed. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Practice /PRAK-tis/ Practice makes progress — not perfection. A-hand brushes back and forth on opposite index
17 Predict /prih-DIKT/ Can you predict what will happen next? Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Prepare /prih-PAIR/ Prepare your materials before class begins. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Process /PROS-es/ Learning is a process that takes time. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Produce /pruh-DOOS/ Farmers produce food for the community. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Protect /proh-TEKT/ Sunscreen protects your skin from the sun. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Provide /proh-VYD/ The school provides free lunch for students. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Receive /rih-SEEV/ You will receive your test results by email. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Recognize /REK-ug-nyz/ I recognize that word from yesterday's lesson. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Recommend /rek-uh-MEND/ I recommend this book to all new learners. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Relate /rih-LAYT/ Can you relate this lesson to your own life? Fingerspell RELATE
27 Remain /rih-MAYN/ Please remain seated until the bus stops. Fingerspell REMAIN
28 Require /rih-KWYR/ This job requires two years of experience. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Resolve /rih-ZOLV/ We can resolve this problem together. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Respond /rih-SPOND/ Respond to the question in a complete sentence. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

🔤 A–Z Word Index

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 4: S–Z Words Across All Lands

The final stretch — from S to Z, the words that seal your mastery.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Schedule /SKEJ-ool/ Check the schedule for bus departure times. 4-hand slides down, then across opposite palm
2 Search /SURCH/ Search for the answer on the internet. Fingerspell SEARCH
3 Select /sih-LEKT/ Select the correct answer from the list. Fingerspell SELECT
4 Separate /SEP-er-ayt/ Separate the laundry into whites and colors. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Serve /SURV/ Volunteers serve meals at the shelter. Fingerspell SERVE
6 Signal /SIG-nul/ A green light is a signal to go. Fingerspell SIGNAL
7 Skill /SKIL/ Reading is an important skill for every job. Fingerspell SKILL
8 Solution /suh-LOO-shun/ Every problem has a solution. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Sort /SORT/ Sort the papers by date. Fingerspell SORT
10 Source /SORS/ Books and websites are good sources of information. Fingerspell SOURCE
11 Specific /spih-SIF-ik/ Give specific examples in your answer. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Strategy /STRAT-eh-jee/ A good study strategy includes review and practice. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Suggest /suh-JEST/ I suggest studying for thirty minutes each night. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Supply /suh-PLY/ The teacher will supply pencils and paper. Fingerspell SUPPLY
15 Support /suh-PORT/ Family and friends support each other. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Symbol /SIM-bul/ The heart is a symbol of love. Fingerspell SYMBOL
17 Task /TASK/ Complete each task before starting the next one. Fingerspell TASK
18 Transfer /TRANS-fer/ Transfer the money to your savings account. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Translate /TRANS-layt/ Can you translate this sentence into your language? Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Understand /un-der-STAND/ I understand the directions now. Flick index finger up near forehead
21 Update /up-DAYT/ Update your contact information at the front desk. Fingerspell UPDATE
22 Utilize /YOO-tih-lyz/ Utilize the dictionary to find new words. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Value /VAL-yoo/ Education is something of great value. Fingerspell VALUE
24 Vary /VAIR-ee/ Prices vary from store to store. Fingerspell VARY
25 Verify /VAIR-ih-fy/ Verify your spelling before submitting. Fingerspell VERIFY
26 Volunteer /vol-un-TEER/ Volunteer at a food bank to help your community. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 Warn /WORN/ The sign warns drivers of a sharp curve. Fingerspell WARN
28 Withdraw /with-DRAW/ Withdraw cash from the ATM machine. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Yield /YEELD/ Yield to pedestrians at the crosswalk. Fingerspell YIELD
30 Zone /ZOHN/ The school is in a no-parking zone. Fingerspell ZONE
Back Matter

🍎 Parent & Teacher Guide

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: Classroom Language & Commands

These are the words teachers say every day. Knowing them helps you follow along in any classroom.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Attendance /uh-TEN-dents/ The teacher takes attendance every morning. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Assignment /uh-SYN-ment/ Turn in your assignment by Friday. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Homework /HOHM-wurk/ Do your homework before watching TV. Sign HOME + WORK
4 Quiz /KWIZ/ We have a spelling quiz tomorrow. Fingerspell QUIZ
5 Test /TEST/ Study hard for the math test. X-hands draw question marks in air, then move down
6 Grade /GRAYD/ She got a good grade on her report card. Fingerspell GRADE
7 Report card /rih-PORT KARD/ Report cards are sent home every quarter. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Permission slip /per-MISH-un SLIP/ Bring a signed permission slip for the field trip. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Absence /AB-sents/ An excused absence requires a note from a parent. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Tardy /TAR-dee/ Students who arrive late are marked tardy. Fingerspell TARDY
11 Raise your hand /RAYZ yor HAND/ Raise your hand before speaking in class. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Pay attention /PAY uh-TEN-shun/ Please pay attention to the instructions. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Turn to page /TURN too PAYJ/ Turn to page forty-seven in your textbook. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Copy /KOP-ee/ Copy the notes from the whiteboard. Fingerspell COPY
15 Repeat /rih-PEET/ Please repeat the question slowly. Fingerspell REPEAT
16 Pronounce /proh-NOWNS/ How do you pronounce this word? Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Spell /SPEL/ Can you spell your last name for me? Fingerspell SPELL
18 Define /dih-FYN/ Define the word in your own words. Fingerspell DEFINE
19 Explain /ek-SPLAYN/ Can you explain the answer in English? Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Translate /TRANS-layt/ Translate the sentence into your home language. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Review /rih-VYOO/ Let's review yesterday's vocabulary. Fingerspell REVIEW
22 Practice /PRAK-tis/ Practice reading aloud for ten minutes. A-hand brushes back and forth on opposite index
23 Correct /kuh-REKT/ Check your work and correct any mistakes. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Submit /sub-MIT/ Submit your essay by the end of the day. Fingerspell SUBMIT
25 Participate /par-TIS-ih-payt/ Everyone must participate in the group activity. Fingerspell or sign each word
26 Enroll /en-ROHL/ Enroll in the ESL class at the community center. Fingerspell ENROLL
27 Register /REJ-ih-ster/ Register for classes before the deadline. Fingerspell or sign each word
28 Tutor /TOO-ter/ A tutor can help you with reading and writing. Fingerspell TUTOR
29 Conference /KON-fer-ens/ The parent-teacher conference is next Thursday. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Graduate /GRAJ-oo-ayt/ She will graduate from the program in June. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

🍎 Parent & Teacher Guide

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: Parent Engagement Vocabulary

These words help parents communicate with schools and support their children's learning.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Principal /PRIN-suh-pul/ The principal is the leader of the school. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Counselor /KOWN-suh-ler/ The school counselor helps students with problems. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 PTA /PEE-TEE-AY/ Join the PTA to be involved in your child's school. Fingerspell PTA
4 Volunteer /vol-un-TEER/ Parents can volunteer in their child's classroom. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Field trip /FEELD TRIP/ The class is going on a field trip to the museum. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Lunch menu /LUNCH MEN-yoo/ Check the lunch menu for the week online. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Backpack /BAK-pak/ Pack your backpack with books and supplies. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Supply list /suh-PLY LIST/ Buy everything on the school supply list. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Uniform /YOO-nih-form/ Students must wear the school uniform every day. U-hands brush down chest
10 Pick up /PIK UP/ Pick up your child from school at three fifteen. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Drop off /DROP OFF/ Drop off your child at the front entrance. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Car line /KAR LYN/ Wait in the car line during morning drop-off. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Bus route /BUS ROOT/ Find your child's bus route number on the website. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 After-school /AF-ter SKOOL/ The after-school program runs until five thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Progress report /PROG-res rih-PORT/ The progress report shows how your child is doing. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Reading level /REE-ding LEV-ul/ Ask the teacher about your child's reading level. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Behavior /bih-HAYV-yer/ Good behavior in class is expected of all students. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Discipline /DIS-ih-plin/ The school has a fair discipline policy. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Special needs /SPESH-ul NEEDZ/ Students with special needs receive extra support. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Bilingual /by-LING-gwul/ The school offers a bilingual education program. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Interpreter /in-TUR-prih-ter/ Request an interpreter if you need language support. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Scholarship /SKOL-er-ship/ Apply for a scholarship to help pay for college. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Diploma /dih-PLOH-muh/ A high school diploma is required for many jobs. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Transcript /TRAN-skript/ Request your transcript for college applications. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Milestone /MYL-stohn/ Your child's first day of school is a big milestone. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

🍎 Parent & Teacher Guide

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 3: Study Skills & Learning Strategies

These are the words that turn you from a student into a LEARNER.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Outline /OWT-lyn/ Make an outline before writing your essay. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Brainstorm /BRAYN-storm/ Brainstorm ideas before starting the project. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Highlight /HY-lyt/ Highlight the important words in the text. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Summarize /SUM-uh-ryz/ Summarize the story in three sentences. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Memorize /MEM-oh-ryz/ Memorize the spelling of these ten words. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Flashcard /FLASH-kard/ Use flashcards to study vocabulary. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Dictionary /DIK-shun-air-ee/ Look up unknown words in the dictionary. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Notebook /NOHT-book/ Write new words in your vocabulary notebook. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Pencil /PEN-sul/ Sharpen your pencil before the test begins. Thumb on lips, then write on palm
10 Eraser /ih-RAY-ser/ Use the eraser to fix your mistakes. Fingerspell ERASER
11 Folder /FOHL-der/ Keep your papers organized in a folder. Fingerspell FOLDER
12 Textbook /TEKST-book/ Open your textbook to chapter four. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Worksheet /WURK-sheet/ Complete the worksheet and turn it in. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Research /REE-surch/ Research your topic using books and the internet. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Proofread /PROOF-reed/ Proofread your writing before turning it in. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Revise /rih-VYZ/ Revise your essay to make it stronger. Fingerspell REVISE
17 Draft /DRAFT/ Write a rough draft first, then improve it. Fingerspell DRAFT
18 Paragraph /PAIR-uh-graf/ Each paragraph should have one main idea. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Sentence /SEN-tens/ A sentence begins with a capital letter. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Vocabulary /voh-KAB-yoo-lair-ee/ Building vocabulary is the key to fluency. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Comprehension /kom-preh-HEN-shun/ Reading comprehension improves with practice. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Fluency /FLOO-en-see/ Fluency means speaking smoothly and naturally. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Pronunciation /proh-nun-see-AY-shun/ Practice pronunciation by listening and repeating. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Context /KON-tekst/ Use context clues to guess the meaning of new words. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Goal /GOHL/ Set a goal to learn five new words every day. Fingerspell GOAL
Back Matter

📖 Sight Words & High-Frequency Words

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: Essential Sight Words — Group A

Every language has words that appear everywhere. These are the 50 most common words in English — the building blocks of every sentence.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 The /THUH/ The sun rises over Harmonia every morning. Fingerspell THE
2 Is /IZ/ Felix is a doctor at the Vitalis clinic. Fingerspell IS
3 Are /AR/ The children are playing in the park. Fingerspell ARE
4 Was /WUZ/ The weather was sunny yesterday. Fingerspell WAS
5 Were /WUR/ Ezra and Athena were at the library. Fingerspell WERE
6 Have /HAV/ I have two brothers and one sister. Bent hands touch chest
7 Has /HAZ/ She has a new job at the hospital. Fingerspell HAS
8 Had /HAD/ He had breakfast before leaving for school. Fingerspell HAD
9 Do /DOO/ What do you do for work? Fingerspell DO
10 Does /DUZ/ Does the bus stop here? Fingerspell DOES
11 Did /DID/ Did you finish your homework? Fingerspell DID
12 Will /WIL/ I will go to the store after school. Flat hand at cheek moves forward
13 Would /WOOD/ Would you like some water? Fingerspell WOULD
14 Can /KAN/ I can speak two languages. S-fists push down firmly
15 Could /KOOD/ Could you help me, please? Fingerspell COULD
16 Should /SHOOD/ You should eat breakfast every morning. X-hand bends down from shoulder repeatedly
17 Must /MUST/ You must wear a seat belt in the car. X-hand bends down firmly once
18 May /MAY/ May I use the bathroom? Fingerspell MAY
19 Might /MYT/ It might rain this afternoon. Fingerspell MIGHT
20 Not /NOT/ I do not understand the question. Fingerspell NOT
21 And /AND/ Bread and butter are on the table. Fingerspell AND
22 But /BUT/ I like math, but I love science. Fingerspell BUT
23 Or /OR/ Do you want coffee or tea? Fingerspell OR
24 If /IF/ If it rains, bring an umbrella. Fingerspell IF
25 Then /THEN/ First study, then play. Fingerspell THEN
26 Because /bih-KUZ/ I stayed home because I was sick. Fingerspell or sign each word
27 So /SOH/ It was cold, so I wore a jacket. Fingerspell SO
28 Very /VAIR-ee/ The test was very difficult. Fingerspell VERY
29 Also /AWL-soh/ She speaks Spanish and also English. Fingerspell ALSO
30 Just /JUST/ I just finished eating dinner. Fingerspell JUST
Back Matter

📖 Sight Words & High-Frequency Words

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: Essential Sight Words — Group B

The journey continues with 30 more power words that unlock reading fluency.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 This /THIS/ This is my house. Fingerspell THIS
2 That /THAT/ That book is on the shelf. Fingerspell THAT
3 These /THEEZ/ These shoes are too small. Fingerspell THESE
4 Those /THOHZ/ Those flowers are beautiful. Fingerspell THOSE
5 Here /HEER/ Come here, please. Fingerspell HERE
6 There /THAIR/ The store is over there. Fingerspell THERE
7 Where /WAIR/ Where is the nearest bus stop? Fingerspell WHERE
8 When /WEN/ When does the class start? Fingerspell WHEN
9 What /WUT/ What is your name? Fingerspell WHAT
10 Who /HOO/ Who is your teacher? Fingerspell WHO
11 How /HOW/ How do you say this in English? Fingerspell HOW
12 Why /WY/ Why are you late today? Fingerspell WHY
13 Which /WICH/ Which color do you prefer? Fingerspell WHICH
14 All /AWL/ All students must attend class. Fingerspell ALL
15 Each /EECH/ Each child gets a book. Fingerspell EACH
16 Every /EV-ree/ Every morning I drink water. Fingerspell EVERY
17 Many /MEN-ee/ There are many people at the park. Fingerspell MANY
18 Some /SUM/ Would you like some milk? Fingerspell SOME
19 Any /EN-ee/ Do you have any questions? Fingerspell ANY
20 No /NOH/ There is no school on Saturday. Index and middle finger snap to thumb
21 More /MOR/ I need more time to finish. Fingerspell MORE
22 Most /MOHST/ Most students passed the test. Fingerspell MOST
23 Other /UTH-er/ The other answer is correct. Fingerspell OTHER
24 Another /uh-NUTH-er/ May I have another chance? Fingerspell or sign each word
25 About /uh-BOWT/ Tell me about your family. Fingerspell ABOUT
26 After /AF-ter/ We eat dessert after dinner. Fingerspell AFTER
27 Before /bih-FOR/ Wash your hands before eating. Fingerspell BEFORE
28 Between /bih-TWEEN/ The park is between the school and the library. Fingerspell or sign each word
29 Through /THROO/ Walk through the door carefully. Fingerspell or sign each word
30 Again /uh-GEN/ Please say that again slowly. Fingerspell AGAIN
Back Matter

📖 Sight Words & High-Frequency Words

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 3: Essential Sight Words — Group C

The final set of high-frequency words completes your reading foundation.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Always /AWL-wayz/ Always say please and thank you. Index finger circles continuously
2 Never /NEV-er/ Never give up on your dreams. S-hand swoops down and away
3 Sometimes /SUM-tymz/ Sometimes the bus is late. Index finger bounces on opposite palm slowly
4 Usually /YOO-zhoo-uh-lee/ I usually wake up at six thirty. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Often /OF-en/ How often do you exercise? Fingerspell OFTEN
6 Already /awl-RED-ee/ I already finished my homework. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Still /STIL/ Are you still waiting? Fingerspell STILL
8 Yet /YET/ I haven't eaten lunch yet. Fingerspell YET
9 Now /NOW/ We need to leave now. Fingerspell NOW
10 Soon /SOON/ The doctor will see you soon. F-hand on chin tilts forward slightly
11 Today /too-DAY/ Today is a beautiful day. Both Y-hands drop in front of body
12 Tomorrow /too-MOR-oh/ Tomorrow we have a test. Thumb of A-hand bounces forward from cheek
13 Yesterday /YES-ter-day/ Yesterday I went to the store. Y-hand or A-thumb touches cheek, then ear
14 Together /too-GETH-er/ Let's work together on this project. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Only /OHN-lee/ I have only five dollars left. Fingerspell ONLY
16 Even /EE-ven/ Even children can learn this. Fingerspell EVEN
17 Really /REE-lee/ I really like your new shirt. Fingerspell REALLY
18 Too /TOO/ This soup is too hot. Fingerspell TOO
19 Enough /ee-NUF/ Do you have enough money? Fingerspell ENOUGH
20 Almost /AWL-mohst/ I almost missed the bus. Fingerspell ALMOST
21 Both /BOHTH/ Both answers are correct. Fingerspell BOTH
22 Either /EE-ther/ You can sit in either chair. Fingerspell EITHER
23 Neither /NEE-ther/ Neither answer is wrong. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Else /ELS/ What else do you need? Fingerspell ELSE
25 Own /OHN/ I want my own room someday. Fingerspell OWN
26 Such /SUCH/ She is such a kind person. Fingerspell SUCH
27 Much /MUCH/ Thank you very much! Fingerspell MUCH
28 Well /WEL/ She speaks English very well. Fingerspell WELL
29 Quite /KWYT/ The movie was quite good. Fingerspell QUITE
30 Perhaps /per-HAPS/ Perhaps we can try again tomorrow. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

👁️ Visual Glossary

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 1: Around the House — Visual Vocabulary

Point to each item. Say the word. Use it in a sentence. Your home is your first classroom!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Doorbell /DOR-bel/ Press the doorbell and wait for someone to answer. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Mailbox /MAYL-boks/ Check the mailbox for letters and packages. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Porch /PORCH/ Sit on the porch and enjoy the evening breeze. Fingerspell PORCH
4 Garage /guh-RAHJ/ Park the car inside the garage. Fingerspell GARAGE
5 Driveway /DRYV-way/ The driveway leads from the street to the garage. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Curtain /KUR-tun/ Close the curtain to block the sunlight. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Pillow /PIL-oh/ Put your head on the pillow and rest. Fingerspell PILLOW
8 Blanket /BLANK-it/ The warm blanket keeps you cozy at night. Fingerspell or sign each word
9 Shelf /SHELF/ Place the books on the shelf. Fingerspell SHELF
10 Drawer /DRAW-er/ Your socks are in the top drawer. Fingerspell DRAWER
11 Faucet /FAW-sit/ Turn the faucet to start the water flowing. Fingerspell FAUCET
12 Pantry /PAN-tree/ Store canned goods in the pantry. Fingerspell PANTRY
13 Microwave /MY-kroh-wayv/ Heat the soup in the microwave for two minutes. Fingerspell or sign each word
14 Dishwasher /DISH-wosh-er/ Load the dirty plates into the dishwasher. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Vacuum /VAK-yoom/ Vacuum the carpet to remove dust and crumbs. Fingerspell VACUUM
16 Laundry /LAWN-dree/ Sort the laundry before putting it in the machine. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Trash can /TRASH KAN/ Throw the wrapper in the trash can. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Light switch /LYT SWICH/ Flip the light switch when you enter a room. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Outlet /OWT-let/ Plug the charger into the wall outlet. Fingerspell OUTLET
20 Thermostat /THUR-moh-stat/ Adjust the thermostat to control the temperature. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Doorknob /DOR-nob/ Turn the doorknob to open the door. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Hallway /HAWL-way/ Walk down the hallway to the last room on the right. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Basement /BAYS-ment/ The basement is the lowest floor of the house. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Attic /AT-ik/ Old boxes are stored in the attic. Fingerspell ATTIC
25 Fence /FENS/ The wooden fence surrounds the backyard. Fingerspell FENCE
Back Matter

👁️ Visual Glossary

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 4: At the Doctor — Visual Vocabulary

Knowing medical words helps you take care of yourself and your family.

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Waiting room /WAY-ting ROOM/ Please sit in the waiting room until the doctor calls you. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Appointment /uh-POYNT-ment/ I have a doctor's appointment at two o'clock. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Insurance card /in-SHOOR-ents KARD/ Show your insurance card at the front desk. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Clipboard /KLIP-bord/ Fill out the forms on the clipboard. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Blood pressure /BLUD PRESH-er/ The nurse checks your blood pressure first. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Stethoscope /STETH-oh-skohp/ The doctor listens to your heart with a stethoscope. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Thermometer /ther-MOM-ih-ter/ The thermometer shows your body temperature. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Scale /SKAYL/ Step on the scale to check your weight. Both flat hands balance like scale
9 Exam table /eg-ZAM TAY-bul/ Sit on the exam table and wait for the doctor. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Prescription /prih-SKRIP-shun/ The doctor writes a prescription for your medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Syringe /sih-RINJ/ The nurse uses a syringe to give the injection. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Bandage /BAN-dij/ Put a bandage on the cut to keep it clean. Mime wrapping bandage around hand
13 X-ray /EKS-ray/ The X-ray shows the bones inside your body. Fingerspell X-RAY
14 Symptom /SIMP-tum/ A cough is a symptom of a cold. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Diagnosis /dy-ug-NOH-sis/ The diagnosis tells you what illness you have. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Treatment /TREET-ment/ The treatment includes rest and medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Follow-up /FOL-oh-UP/ Schedule a follow-up visit in two weeks. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Referral /rih-FUR-ul/ The doctor gave me a referral to see a specialist. Fingerspell or sign each word
19 Co-pay /KOH-pay/ The co-pay for each visit is twenty dollars. Fingerspell CO-PAY
20 Pharmacy /FAR-muh-see/ Pick up your medicine at the pharmacy. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Dosage /DOH-sij/ Take the correct dosage as directed on the label. Fingerspell DOSAGE
22 Side effect /SYD ih-FEKT/ Drowsiness is a common side effect of this medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
23 Allergic /uh-LUR-jik/ Tell the doctor if you are allergic to any medicine. Fingerspell or sign each word
24 Emergency /ee-MUR-jen-see/ Call 911 in an emergency. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Recovery /rih-KUV-er-ee/ Recovery from surgery takes several weeks. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

👁️ Visual Glossary

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 2: At the Store — Visual Vocabulary

Every shopping trip is a vocabulary lesson. Name what you see!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Cart /KART/ Put your groceries in the shopping cart. Fingerspell CART
2 Basket /BAS-kit/ Use a basket if you only need a few items. Fingerspell BASKET
3 Aisle /YL/ The cereal is in aisle five. Fingerspell AISLE
4 Checkout /CHEK-owt/ Go to the checkout counter to pay. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Receipt /rih-SEET/ Keep your receipt in case you need to return something. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Coupon /KOO-pon/ Use the coupon to save two dollars. Fingerspell COUPON
7 Cashier /kash-EER/ The cashier scans your items and tells you the total. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Sale /SAYL/ These shoes are on sale — twenty percent off. Fingerspell SALE
9 Brand /BRAND/ Which brand of toothpaste do you prefer? Fingerspell BRAND
10 Label /LAY-bul/ Read the label to check the ingredients. Fingerspell LABEL
11 Expiration /ek-spih-RAY-shun/ Check the expiration date before buying milk. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Price tag /PRYS TAG/ The price tag shows how much the item costs. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Refund /REE-fund/ Ask for a refund if the product is defective. Fingerspell REFUND
14 Exchange /eks-CHAYNJ/ You can exchange the shirt for a different size. Fingerspell or sign each word
15 Barcode /BAR-kohd/ The cashier scans the barcode to ring up the item. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Produce section /PROH-doos SEK-shun/ Fresh fruits and vegetables are in the produce section. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Frozen food /FROH-zen FOOD/ Pizza and ice cream are in the frozen food section. Fingerspell or sign each word
18 Dairy /DAIR-ee/ Milk, cheese, and yogurt are dairy products. Fingerspell DAIRY
19 Bakery /BAY-ker-ee/ Fresh bread is available at the bakery counter. Fingerspell BAKERY
20 Deli /DEL-ee/ Order sliced turkey at the deli counter. Fingerspell DELI
21 Bag /BAG/ Would you like paper or plastic bags? Fingerspell BAG
22 Scale /SKAYL/ Weigh the apples on the scale. Both flat hands balance like scale
23 Sample /SAM-pul/ Try a free sample before you buy. Fingerspell SAMPLE
24 Register /REJ-ih-ster/ The register shows the total amount due. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Manager /MAN-ih-jer/ Ask the manager if you have a complaint. Fingerspell or sign each word
Back Matter

👁️ Visual Glossary

Guided by All Rhythm Quest Heroes

Scene 3: On the Street — Visual Vocabulary

Step outside your door and the world becomes a picture dictionary!

# Word Pronunciation In the Story… ASL Sign 🤟 My Language
1 Sidewalk /SYD-wawk/ Walk on the sidewalk, not in the street. Fingerspell or sign each word
2 Crosswalk /KROS-wawk/ Use the crosswalk to cross the street safely. Fingerspell or sign each word
3 Traffic light /TRAF-ik LYT/ Stop at the red traffic light. Fingerspell or sign each word
4 Stop sign /STOP SYN/ Come to a complete stop at the stop sign. Fingerspell or sign each word
5 Parking meter /PAR-king MEE-ter/ Put coins in the parking meter. Fingerspell or sign each word
6 Fire hydrant /FYR HY-drant/ Never park in front of a fire hydrant. Fingerspell or sign each word
7 Streetlight /STREET-lyt/ The streetlight turns on automatically at dusk. Fingerspell or sign each word
8 Bench /BENCH/ Sit on the bench and wait for the bus. Fingerspell BENCH
9 Manhole /MAN-hohl/ Workers go underground through the manhole cover. Fingerspell or sign each word
10 Telephone pole /TEL-eh-fohn POHL/ Power lines hang between the telephone poles. Fingerspell or sign each word
11 Intersection /in-ter-SEK-shun/ The accident happened at the intersection. Fingerspell or sign each word
12 Pedestrian /peh-DES-tree-un/ The pedestrian waited for the walk signal. Fingerspell or sign each word
13 Curb /KURB/ Step off the curb carefully. Fingerspell CURB
14 Gutter /GUT-er/ Rainwater flows into the gutter. Fingerspell GUTTER
15 Speed bump /SPEED BUMP/ Slow down at the speed bump. Fingerspell or sign each word
16 Overpass /OH-ver-pas/ The highway overpass crosses above the local road. Fingerspell or sign each word
17 Tunnel /TUN-ul/ The train goes through the tunnel under the mountain. Fingerspell TUNNEL
18 Bridge /BRIJ/ Cross the bridge to get to the other side of the river. V-hand bridges under opposite forearm
19 Billboard /BIL-bord/ The billboard advertises a new movie. Fingerspell or sign each word
20 Mailbox /MAYL-boks/ Drop the letter in the blue public mailbox. Fingerspell or sign each word
21 Newspaper stand /NOOZ-pay-per STAND/ Buy a newspaper at the newspaper stand. Fingerspell or sign each word
22 Awning /AW-ning/ Stand under the awning to stay dry in the rain. Fingerspell AWNING
23 Alley /AL-ee/ The alley runs between two buildings. Fingerspell ALLEY
24 Playground /PLAY-grownd/ Children play on the swings at the playground. Fingerspell or sign each word
25 Fountain /FOWN-tun/ Toss a coin into the fountain and make a wish. Fingerspell or sign each word
